docs.title: ℹ️ About This Site
docs.text: Farming Game Guides is a comprehensive guide database and interactive tool hub focused on farming, simulation, and life-management games. We help players optimize their farms, maximize profits, and get the most out of their favorite games.

docs.title: What We Offer
docs.text: Feature Description 📚 In-Depth Guides Step-by-step tutorials for every stage of each game 🧮 Interactive Tools Profit calculators, crop comparators, trackers, and planners 📊 Data Tables Comprehensive reference data — crops, fish, NPCs, items 🆕 Latest Updates Coverage of patches, DLC, and new releases

docs.title: Games Covered
docs.text: Game Focus Area Steam Rating 🌿 Stardew Valley Crops, fishing, mining, relationships, perfection 98% 🚜 Farming Simulator 25 Crops, machinery, animals, forestry, contracts 89% 🏹 Palia Hunting, fishing, cooking, housing, friendship 82% 🌴 Coral Island Crops, diving, mining, romance, artisan 91% ✨ Fields of Mistria Crops, mining, fishing, museum, housing 94% 🏜️ My Time at Sandrock Workshop, combat, romance, cooking 92% ☀️ Sun Haven Farming, fishing, mining, combat, romance 88%

docs.title: Why This Site Exists
docs.text:
Farming and simulation games have exploded in popularity. With hundreds of crops, fish, NPCs, and crafting recipes across dozens of games, keeping track of everything is overwhelming. This site was built to be the one-stop reference — no more alt-tabbing to the wiki every 30 seconds.

Every guide is: - Hand-checked against in-game data - Updated for the latest game versions - Tool-integrated — calculators and trackers work alongside the guides

docs.title: What Are Affiliate Links?
docs.text:
Some of the links on Farming Games Help are "affiliate links." This means that if you click on one of these links and make a purchase, we may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.

These commissions help us maintain and grow the site — covering hosting costs, research time, and tool development so we can keep creating free, high-quality farming game guides.

docs.title: About the Game
docs.text: Coral Island is a vibrant tropical farming simulation game developed by Stairway Games, released in October 2023 (version 1.0). Set on a gorgeous Indonesian-inspired archipelago, you inherit your grandmother's old farm and work alongside diverse villagers to restore the island's ecosystem and community.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Details 🎮 Genre Farming Sim / Life Sim 👥 Mode Single-player 🏭 Developer Stairway Games 📅 Release October 2023 (1.0) 🖥️ Platform PC, PS5, Xbox Series X/S

docs.title: 🌴 Farming & Ranching
docs.text: Coral Island's farming system is built around seasonal crops suited to a tropical climate. The soil starts depleted and must be cleared of trash and debris — reflecting the game's core theme of environmental restoration. You can raise animals including cows, chickens, goats, and sheep, each requiring outdoor pasture and daily care.

docs.title: Unique Mechanics
docs.text:
• Diving — Explore the ocean floor to clean trash, discover artifacts, and gather resources
  • Pollination — Bees and insect farms boost crop yields organically
  • Automation — Sprinklers and auto-feeders unlock as you progress through the town rank system

docs.title: 🤿 Diving & Ocean Restoration
docs.text: A standout feature of Coral Island is its underwater exploration. Using diving gear, you descend into the coral reefs surrounding the island to collect trash, salvage artifacts for the museum, and find rare gems. Cleaning the ocean raises your "Ocean Rank," which unlocks better diving equipment and reveals new areas.

docs.title: Ocean Rank Tiers
docs.text:
Rank Requirement Reward 🥉 Bronze C Clean 20 trash Basic fins upgrade 🥈 Silver B Clean 100 trash Oxygen tank upgrade 🥇 Gold A Donate 10 artifacts Underwater fast-travel

Tip: Prioritize upgrading your diving gear before your fishing rod. Diving provides significantly more early-game income and materials than fishing in Coral Island, and cleaning the ocean unlocks new areas packed with valuable forage.

docs.title: 🐄 Coral Island — Complete Animal Husbandry Guide
docs.text: Coral Island's animal system is more involved than Stardew Valley's: four quality tiers (Normal → Silver → Gold → Osmium), friendship directly impacts product quality, and there's a separate pet mechanic. This guide breaks down every animal's real ROI from an economic perspective.

docs.title: 🐔 Coop Animals
docs.text:
Animal Purchase Price Maturity Days Daily Output Base Price Processed Product Processed Price 🐔 Chicken 800g 3 days 1× Egg 50g Mayonnaise 150g 🦆 Duck 1,200g 4 days 1× Duck Egg 70g Duck Mayonnaise 200g 🐰 Rabbit 2,000g 6 days Wool / Rabbit's Foot 80g (Wool) Cloth 240g (Cloth) 🦚 Peacock 3,000g 5 days 1× Peacock Feather 150g — — 🐣 Ostrich 4,000g 7 days 1× Ostrich Egg 200g Ostrich Egg Mayonnaise 450g

Tip: Ostriches have the highest purchase price among coop animals, but each egg sells for 200g raw or 450g processed into mayonnaise. Per-tile daily profit beats every other coop animal — the 7-day wait to maturity is well worth it.

docs.title: 🐄 Barn Animals
docs.text:
Animal Purchase Price Maturity Days Daily Output Base Price Processed Product Processed Price 🐄 Cow 1,500g 3 days 1× Milk 100g Cheese (200g) / Butter (150g) / Pudding (250g) See below 🐑 Sheep 2,000g 4 days 1× Wool 120g Cloth 240g 🐐 Goat 2,500g 3 days 1× Goat Milk 140g Goat Cheese 280g 🐖 Pig 4,000g 5 days 1-3× Truffle 200g each Truffle Oil 400g each 🦌 Alpaca 5,000g 6 days 1× Alpaca Wool 250g High-Quality Cloth 400g

Tip: Pigs are the profit kings of the animal world. Truffles are foraged items (rooted up by pigs), yielding 1-3 per day. Process each into Truffle Oil for 400g. 3 Truffle Oils = 1,200g/day. But pigs need free-range grazing (can't be locked in the barn) and produce nothing in winter.

docs.title: Friendship Levels
docs.text: Level Name Hearts Effect 0 Stranger ❌0❤️ Normal quality output 1 Friendly ❤️❤️🤍🤍🤍 10% chance of Silver quality 2 Close ❤️❤️❤️🤍🤍 25% Silver + 5% Gold 3 Buddy ❤️❤️❤️❤️🤍 40% Gold + 10% Osmium 4 Best Friend ❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️ 50% Osmium + 30% Gold

docs.title: Friendship Gains
docs.text: Action Friendship Gain Limit 🫱 Petting (once daily) +15 1/day 🧀 Feeding (auto or manual) +5 each — 🌿 Grazing (eating grass) +10/day — 🎶 Radio (coop decor) +5/day Requires radio purchase 250g ❄️ Heater (winter) +10/day Requires heater purchase 500g

docs.title: Quality Upgrade Formula
docs.text:
Final Quality = Base Quality × (1 + Friendship Modifier + Husbandry Skill Bonus + Feed Bonus)

Factor Bonus Notes Base Quality 100% Newly purchased or low friendship Max Friendship +50% At 5❤️ Husbandry Skill Lv.5 +10% Unlocks "Quality Products" perk Husbandry Skill Lv.10 +25% Unlocks "Master Herder" perk Premium Feed +20% Premium feed via auto-feeder

docs.title: 🏗️ Coop/Barn Upgrades & Auto-Feeder ROI
docs.text:
Building Upgrade Cost Materials Max Capacity Auto-Feeder Price Unlocks 🐔 Basic Coop 2,000g Wood×50, Stone×30 4 animals N/A Basic chickens 🐔 Medium Coop 5,000g Hardwood×30, Silver Bar×5 8 animals 3,000g Ducks, Rabbits 🐔 Deluxe Coop 10,000g Gold Bar×8, Refined Quartz×5 12 animals 3,000g Peacocks, Ostriches 🐮 Basic Barn 3,000g Wood×80, Stone×40 4 animals N/A Basic cows, sheep 🐮 Medium Barn 6,000g Hardwood×40, Silver Bar×8 8 animals 4,000g Goats, Pigs 🐮 Deluxe Barn 12,000g Gold Bar×10, Refined Quartz×8 12 animals 4,000g Alpacas

Auto-Feeder ROI Analysis:

Device Cost Time Saved/Day Days to Break Even Long-Term Value Coop Auto-Feeder 3,000g ~3 min Not monetary Hands-free feeding, no daily hay refills Barn Auto-Feeder 4,000g ~4 min Same as above Fully automated with Auto-Grabber

Tip: Buy the Auto-Grabber (2,000g, unlocks at Medium Coop) before the Auto-Feeder — it collects all eggs and wool daily, saving you from clicking each one. Auto-Feeder + Auto-Grabber = true passive income.

docs.title: 🐾 Pet System
docs.text: Pet How to Get Cost Benefits 🐕 Dog (Corgi/German Shepherd/Golden Retriever) Pet shop (Sundays) 1,000g Brings 1 gift daily, boosts animal friendship 🐈 Cat (Orange/Black/Calico) Pet shop (Sundays) 1,000g Brings 1 gift daily, scares away birds 🐇 Bunny Pet shop 800g No special function, just companionship 🦎 Lizard Adopt after diving discovery Free No special function, collector's item 🐢 Turtle Beach event trigger Free Roams your yard, eats weeds

docs.title: Pet Friendship
docs.text: Level Effect 1-3❤️ 1 random small gift daily (seeds/forage/shells) 4❤️ Petting bonus unlocked: +1 stamina regen all day after petting 5❤️ Pet skill unlocked: Dog scares off wildlife attacking animals, Cat catches mice to protect feed

docs.title: Dairy Profit Table
docs.text: Product Ingredient Machine Processing Time Sell Price Profit Increase 🥛 Milk (base) — — — 100g — 🧀 Cheese 1× Milk Cheese Press 6 hours 200g +100g (+100%) 🧈 Butter 1× Milk Butter Churn 4 hours 150g +50g (+50%) 🍮 Pudding 1× Milk Pudding Maker 8 hours 250g +150g (+150%) 🥛 Goat Milk — — — 140g — 🧀 Goat Cheese 1× Goat Milk Cheese Press 6 hours 280g +140g (+100%) 🥚 Ostrich Egg — — — 200g — 🫙 Ostrich Egg Mayo 1× Ostrich Egg Mayo Machine 4 hours 450g +250g (+125%)

docs.title: Egg Processing Profit Table
docs.text:
Product Ingredient Machine Processing Time Sell Price Profit Increase 🥚 Egg — — — 50g — 🫙 Mayonnaise 1× Egg Mayo Machine 3 hours 150g +100g (+200%) 🥚 Duck Egg — — — 70g — 🫙 Duck Mayonnaise 1× Duck Egg Mayo Machine 3 hours 200g +130g (+186%) 🧶 Wool / Rabbit Wool — — — 80~120g — 🧥 Cloth 1× Wool Loom 4 hours 240g +120~160g

Tip: Processing priority: Pudding > Mayonnaise > Cheese > Butter. Pudding has the highest profit increase (+150%) but takes the longest — perfect for loading before bed. If machines are limited, Mayonnaise's +200% in just 3 hours is the most efficient option.

docs.title: 💰 Daily Profit: Animals vs. Crops
docs.text: Category Per-Tile Daily Profit (Base) Processed Post-Process Daily Profit per Tile Consistency 🌱 Spring Crops (avg) 8~15g/tile ~2× processed 16~30g/tile ❌ Season-dependent 🐔 Chicken (1) 50g Mayo 150g 150g/day ✅ Year-round 🦆 Duck (1) 70g Duck Mayo 200g 200g/day ✅ Year-round 🐄 Cow (1) 100g Pudding 250g 250g/day ✅ Year-round 🐖 Pig (1) 400~600g Truffle Oil 800~1,200g 800~1,200g/day ❌ No winter output 🐰 Rabbit (1) 80g Cloth 240g 240g/day ✅ Year-round

docs.title: Verdict
docs.text:
• Pigs have the highest per-tile profit (800-1,200g/day) but stop in winter — best as a summer/fall primary income
  • Cow + Pudding is the most stable passive income, running all year
  • Animals outpace crops overall (excluding processing), but upfront costs are higher. Recommended hybrid: spring crops to build capital → summer barn expansion into cows/pigs → fall lean on animal products through winter

docs.title: ❄️ Seasonal Farming Strategies
docs.text:
Season Focus Notes 🌸 Spring Start with chickens (low cost, fast returns) → save to upgrade coop to Medium → buy first duck Spring crops are main income; animals are a side line ☀️ Summer Barn upgrade → buy first cow → buy pigs (truffles peak in summer) Pigs need free-range grazing — reserve grass space 🍂 Fall Deluxe barn → buy ostriches/alpacas → mass-process animal products for fall prices Stockpile truffle oil and pudding for winter ❄️ Winter Pigs stop producing; rely on cows/chickens/alpacas for income → upgrade auto-feeders and auto-grabbers Winter grazing doesn't work — stockpile hay

Tip: In winter, all animals must stay indoors on hay. Stockpile at least 100 bales of hay (each animal eats 1/day) before winter. Buy hay from Macy's general store at 10g/bale, or harvest wild grass with a scythe in fall. One animal costs 28 bales for winter (28 days). 6 animals = 168 bales × 10g = 1,680g — worth preparing early.

docs.title: 🏭 Coral Island — Complete Artisan Equipment & Processing Profit Guide
docs.text: Processing is the engine of wealth in Coral Island — a 50g egg through the Mayo Machine becomes 150g (+200%), a 260g melon through the Keg becomes 740g Melon Wine (+185%). This guide breaks down every machine's economics so you can make the best processing decisions.

docs.title: ⚙️ All Artisan Equipment Overview
docs.text: Machine Unlock Condition Materials Processing Time Input Output 🫙 Preserve Jar Farming Lv.3 Wood×30 + Stone×15 + Copper Bar×3 12 hours Vegetables/Fruits Pickles/Jam 🍷 Keg Farming Lv.6 Wood×40 + Copper Bar×5 + Silver Bar×2 24h (Fruit) / 48h (Veg) Fruits/Veg/Grain Wine/Juice/Vinegar/Sake 🧀 Cheese Press Husbandry Lv.2 Copper Bar×3 + Stone×20 6 hours Milk/Goat Milk Cheese/Goat Cheese 🧈 Butter Churn Husbandry Lv.3 Silver Bar×2 + Hardwood×15 4 hours Milk Butter 🍮 Pudding Maker Husbandry Lv.5 Gold Bar×3 + Refined Quartz×2 8 hours Milk Pudding 🫙 Mayo Machine Husbandry Lv.1 Copper Bar×2 + Wood×15 3 hours Eggs Mayonnaise 🧥 Loom Husbandry Lv.4 Silver Bar×3 + Hardwood×20 4 hours Wool/Rabbit Wool Cloth 🥩 Dehydrator Farming Lv.8 Gold Bar×3 + Refined Quartz×3 8 hours Hot Pepper/Kale/Broccoli Dried Vegetables 🫒 Oil Press Farming Lv.5 Silver Bar×2 + Stone×15 6 hours Truffle/Sunflower/Corn Truffle Oil/Cooking Oil 🐝 Bee House Mid-game quest Wood×20 + Iron Bar×2 + Queen Bee 72 hours (near flowers) Flower+Nectar Honey/Special Honey 🎣 Fermenter Farming Lv.10 Gold Bar×5 + Osmium Bar×3 36~48 hours Wine Aged Wine

docs.title: Preserve Jar — 12 hours
docs.text: Input Crop Base Price Jarred Price Profit Increase Profit per Hour 🍉 Watermelon 300g 700g +400g (+133%) 33.3g/h 🎃 Pumpkin 280g 660g +380g (+136%) 31.7g/h 🍈 Melon 260g 620g +360g (+138%) 30.0g/h 🥦 Cauliflower 250g 590g +340g (+136%) 28.3g/h 🥦 Broccoli 185g 470g +285g (+154%) 23.8g/h 🥬 Cabbage 120g 320g +200g (+167%) 16.7g/h 🍅 Tomato 110g 280g +170g (+155%) 14.2g/h 🫐 Blueberry 140g 340g +200g (+143%) 16.7g/h 🥒 Eggplant 120g 310g +190g (+158%) 15.8g/h

docs.title: Keg — 24 / 48 hours
docs.text: Input Crop Base Price Wine/Juice Price Time Profit Increase Profit per Hour 🍈 Melon 260g 740g (Melon Wine) 24h +480g (+185%) 20.0g/h 🎃 Pumpkin 280g 690g (Pumpkin Wine) 24h +410g (+146%) 17.1g/h 🍉 Watermelon 300g 700g (Watermelon Wine) 24h +400g (+133%) 16.7g/h 🥦 Cauliflower 250g 620g 24h +370g (+148%) 15.4g/h 🍇 Grape 85g 340g (Wine) 24h +255g (+300%) 10.6g/h 🌾 Rice 130g 390g (Sake) 24h +260g (+200%) 10.8g/h ☕ Coffee Bean 65g 250g (Coffee) 24h +185g (+285%) 7.7g/h 🥕 Carrot 68g 200g (Carrot Juice) 24h +132g (+194%) 5.5g/h 🌽 Corn 120g 310g (Corn Juice) 48h +190g (+158%) 4.0g/h

docs.title: Dehydrator — 8 hours
docs.text:
Input Crop Base Price Dried Price Profit Increase Profit per Hour 🌶️ Hot Pepper 85g 230g +145g (+171%) 18.1g/h 🥬 Kale 100g 270g +170g (+170%) 21.3g/h 🥦 Broccoli 185g 470g +285g (+154%) 35.6g/h

Tip: Broccoli in the Dehydrator has the highest per-hour profit of any processing route in the game — 35.6g/h beats the Preserve Jar's 33.3g/h. But the Dehydrator only handles these three crops, so its use is limited. When broccoli and kale are in season during fall, prioritize the dehydrator route.

docs.title: 🔄 Best Processing Route per Crop
docs.text: Crop Season Preserve Jar Keg Dehydrator Best Choice 🍉 Watermelon ☀️ Summer 700g ✓ 700g ✓ — Preserve Jar (12h vs 24h, same price, faster) 🍈 Melon ☀️ Summer 620g 740g ✓ — Keg (highest premium) 🎃 Pumpkin 🍂 Fall 660g 690g ✓ — Keg or Preserve Jar (30g difference, depends on machine count) 🥦 Cauliflower 🌸 Spring 590g ✓ 620g — Keg (30g more, worth the 24h wait) 🥦 Broccoli 🌸 Spring 470g — 470g ✓ Preserve Jar or Dehydrator (same price, Dehydrator faster) 🌶️ Hot Pepper ☀️ Summer — — 230g ✓ Dehydrator (only route) 🍅 Tomato ☀️ Summer 280g ✓ — — Preserve Jar 🫐 Blueberry ☀️ Summer 340g ✓ — — Preserve Jar 🍇 Grape 🍂 Fall — 340g ✓ — Keg → Wine 🌾 Rice 🍂 Fall — 390g ✓ — Keg → Sake ☕ Coffee Bean ☀️ Summer — 250g ✓ — Keg → Coffee 🍓 Strawberry 🌸 Spring 360g — — Preserve Jar (strawberries can't go in Keg)

docs.title: How Many Machines You Need
docs.text: Machine Type Early Game (50 tiles) Mid Game (150 tiles) Late Game (300+ tiles) 🫙 Preserve Jar 3~4 8~12 20~30 🍷 Keg 2~3 6~10 15~25 🧀 Cheese Press 1 2 4~6 🫙 Mayo Machine 1~2 3~5 8~12 🥩 Dehydrator 1~2 3~4 6~8

docs.title: Layout Principles
docs.text:
1. Group in batches: Place 5 machines per row, leave 1 tile for walking
  2. Near storage: Keep machines close to your chest to minimize running time
  3. Preserve Jars before Kegs: With limited machines early on, build Preserve Jars first (12h vs 24h, faster capital turnover)
  4. Animal product machines inside the barn: Cheese Presses, Butter Churns and Mayo Machines go inside the barn — milk → process in one step
  5. Outdoor vs. indoor: Kegs and Preserve Jars can go outside; consider a roof overhang for tidiness (no damage mechanic in the game, but looks cleaner)

Tip: Once you have 10 Preserve Jars and 5 Kegs, stop building basic machines. Invest in Fermenters instead — age your regular wine from Kegs into Aged Wine, selling for 1.5~2× more. A Melon Wine (740g) aged is worth about 1,200g.

docs.title: 🌟 Artisan Skill Tree
docs.text: Skill Level Skill Name Effect Must-Have? Lv.1 🥘 Basic Cooking Unlocks Preserve Jar ✅ Lv.3 🫙 Preservation Tech Preserve Jar output +10% sell price ✅ Lv.5 🧈 Quality Boost 15% chance of double artisan output ✅✅ Core Lv.7 ⚡ Efficiency Expert All machine processing time -15% ✅✅ Core Lv.9 💰 Premium Negotiation Artisan product sell price +20% ✅✅ Core Lv.10 🏆 Master Artisan Artisan goods can reach Osmium quality ✅ (Endgame)

docs.title: Recommended Order
docs.text:
Early priority: Lv.1 → Lv.3 (Preserve Jar +10%) → Lv.5 (Double output)

Mid-game Keg pivot: Lv.7 (Speed) → Lv.9 (+20% price)

Endgame: Lv.10 unlocks Osmium-quality artisan goods — single-item price jumps 1.5× again

docs.title: Ideal Configuration (300+ tile farm)
docs.text: Item Quantity Per-Cycle Output Notes 🍷 Keg 20 20 wines/day Primary processing 🫙 Preserve Jar 15 30 jars/day Fast turnover supplement 🥩 Dehydrator 8 24 units/day Broccoli specialist 🧀 Cheese Press / Pudding Maker 6 12 units/day Animal product processing 🫙 Mayo Machine 8 24 bottles/day Egg processing 🎣 Fermenter 6 3~6 aged wines/day Highest-end route

docs.title: Estimated Daily Income
docs.text:
Season Daily Income Estimate Income Sources 🌸 Spring 15,000~25,000g Cauliflower Wine + Dried Broccoli + Mayonnaise ☀️ Summer 25,000~40,000g Melon Wine + Watermelon Preserves + Tomato Preserves + Truffle Oil 🍂 Fall 30,000~50,000g Pumpkin Wine + Grape Wine + Aged Ferments ❄️ Winter 5,000~10,000g Animal products + Aged wine inventory sell-off

Tip: Age a batch of Melon Wine or Pumpkin Wine before winter. Wines sitting in Fermenters will finish aging during winter — the best time to clear inventory at high prices since there are no fresh crops to process. Aged wine becomes your only processing income during the cold months.

docs.title: 📊 Raw Sale vs. Processing Quick Reference
docs.text:
Input Raw Price Best Processed Product Processed Price Multiplier Machine Time 🥚 Egg 50g Mayonnaise 150g ×3.0 Mayo Machine 3h 🥛 Milk 100g Pudding 250g ×2.5 Pudding Maker 8h 🍇 Grape 85g Wine 340g ×4.0 Keg 24h ☕ Coffee Bean 65g Coffee 250g ×3.8 Keg 24h 🥦 Broccoli 185g Dried/Preserved 470g ×2.5 Dehydrator/Jar 8h 🍈 Melon 260g Melon Wine 740g ×2.8 Keg 24h 🐖 Truffle 200g Truffle Oil 400g ×2.0 Oil Press 6h 👕 Wool 120g Cloth 240g ×2.0 Loom 4h

Golden rule: Any item that can be processed — never sell it raw. Even if you only have one Mayo Machine, process those eggs before selling. 3 hours for +100g is an ROI no other activity can touch.

docs.title: 🍳 Coral Island Cooking Guide — All Recipes, Ingredients, and Meal Benefits
docs.text:
Cooking in Coral Island is essential for restoring energy, getting temporary buffs, and building relationships. Unlike some farming sims, cooking here also contributes to a dedicated Cooking Skill with its own perk tree. This guide covers every recipe, the best dishes for every situation, and how to level up your cooking fast.

Tip: Don't sell raw ingredients for pocket change. Even the simplest cooked meal is worth 2-3× the raw ingredients. Cooking is your best profit multiplier outside of artisan machines.

docs.title: 🔥 Cooking Overview
docs.text:
You start with a basic campfire stove. Upgrading your kitchen unlocks more cooking stations and better recipes.

Station Unlock Function 🔥 Basic Stove Starter tent Simple dishes (boiled, grilled) 🍳 Kitchen Stove Wood Cabin upgrade All intermediate recipes 🧑‍🍳 Gourmet Stove Stone House upgrade Advanced recipes, buff bonuses 🧊 Fridge Stone House Ingredient storage, extends food shelf life 🔬 Kitchen Lab Brick House Fermentation, aging, special recipes

docs.title: Ingredient Sources
docs.text: Source What You Get 🌱 Farm Crops Vegetables, fruits, grains 🐄 Animal Products Eggs, milk, cheese, butter 🐟 Fishing All seafood types 🤿 Diving Shells, seaweed, rare ocean ingredients 🌿 Foraging Wild greens, mushrooms, berries 🏪 Store Bought Spices, oil, sugar, flour

docs.title: Spring Dishes
docs.text: Dish Ingredients Energy Restored Sell Price Buff 🥗 Spring Salad Lettuce×2 + Tomato + Cucumber 80 300g +20 Stamina 🥣 Veggie Soup Carrot + Potato + Onion + Water 100 250g +10% Defense (4h) 🥟 Spring Rolls Rice×2 + Cabbage + Soy Sauce 120 350g +15% Movement (3h) 🍓 Berry Parfait Strawberry×2 + Milk + Sugar 90 420g +10% Fishing (5h)

docs.text: Dish Ingredients Energy Restored Sell Price Buff 🍲 Hearty Stew Meat×2 + Potato + Carrot + Spice 200 580g +15% Attack (6h) 🍵 Hot Cocoa Cocoa + Milk + Sugar 80 250g +20% Cold Resist (3h) 🥩 Roast Meat Meat×3 + Potato + Onion + Spice 250 750g +25% Attack (6h) 🥟 Dumplings Wheat + Meat + Cabbage + Soy 160 420g +10% Luck (5h) 🎂 Winter Cake Wheat + Egg + Butter + Sugar + Berry 220 800g None (best winter gift)

docs.title: Year-Round / Special Dishes
docs.text:
Dish Ingredients Energy Restored Sell Price Notes 🥚 Fried Egg Egg + Oil 40 120g Simplest recipe 🧀 Cheese Plate Cheese×2 + Fruit 100 350g Quick energy 🥪 Sandwich Wheat×2 + Tomato + Lettuce + Cheese 140 380g Portable, no buff 🥣 Seafood Stew Any Fish×2 + Shell×2 + Spice + Water 220 650g Best mining food 🫙 Pickled Veggies Any Veg×3 + Vinegar + Salt 80 280g Ferments 2 days 🍷 Fruit Wine Any Fruit×3 + Sugar + Yeast — 500-1,200g Ferments 7 days

Tip: Recipes marked with "ferments" need time in the Kitchen Lab. Plan ahead — Fruit Wine takes 7 days but sells for way more than any other processed fruit product.

docs.title: ⚡ Best Meals for Energy
docs.text:
When you're deep in the mines or spending a full day diving, energy management is everything. These dishes give you the best energy-per-slot:

Dish Energy Ingredients Source Energy Per Ingredient Verdict 🍲 Hearty Stew 200 Meat 2× + Potato + Carrot + Spice 40/ingredient ✅ Best all-around 🥣 Seafood Stew 220 Fish 2× + Shell 2× + Spice 36/ingredient Great if you fish 🥩 Roast Meat 250 Meat 3× + Potato + Onion + Spice 42/ingredient ✅ Highest single item 🥟 Spring Rolls 120 Rice 2× + Cabbage + Soy 30/ingredient Cheap to mass-produce 🥚 Fried Egg 40 Egg + Oil 20/ingredient Emergencies only

Best Energy Strategy: Mass-produce Spring Rolls early game (rice and cabbage are cheap). Mid-game, switch to Hearty Stew or Seafood Stew. Late-game, Roast Meat stacks give you 250 energy per slot, enough for full-day mining runs.

docs.title: 💝 Best Meals for Gifting
docs.text:
Not all dishes make good gifts. These are the ones villagers actually love:

Dish Universal Love Best Recipient Season 🍓 Berry Parfait ✅ Loved Most villagers Spring 🧊 Fruit Ice Cream ✅ Loved Kids, Lily, Kenny Summer 🎃 Pumpkin Pie ✅ Loved Rafael, Suki Fall 🍎 Apple Crumble ❌ Liked Mark, Noah Fall 🎂 Winter Cake ✅ Loved Everyone (almost) Winter 🧀 Cheese Plate ❌ Liked Alice, Eva All year 🍷 Fruit Wine ✅ Loved Adults All year (needs aging)

Tip: Fruit Wine is universally loved by all adult NPCs and ages in 7 days. If you have spare fruits, ferment them into wine for the ultimate universal gift. Just don't give it to kids!

docs.title: 🎯 Cooking Skill Progression
docs.text:
Every dish you cook gives Cooking XP. The amount depends on the complexity and tier of the recipe.

Level XP Needed Perk Unlocked Effect 1 0 Basic Recipes Access to campfire recipes 2 100 Quick Hands 10% faster cooking 3 250 Kitchen Intuition 5% chance to double a dish 4 500 Gourmet Touch Unlock Gourmet Stove recipes 5 800 Quality Cooking +1 quality tier to all cooked dishes 6 1,200 Efficient Chef 10% ingredient reduction 7 1,800 Double Batch 15% chance to cook 2× 8 2,500 Master Chef Unlock all recipes 9 3,500 Perfect Presentation +50% sell price on cooked dishes 10 5,000 Culinary Legend 25% chance to cook osmium quality

Fastest XP Strategy: Cook the highest-tier recipe you can consistently make. XP is based on recipe complexity, not quantity. One Roast Meat (200 XP) = four Fried Eggs (50 XP each). Always cook the best dish you can, not the cheapest.

docs.title: Cooking Skill Perk Priority
docs.text: Priority Perk Why 🥇 1st Quality Cooking (Lv.5) +1 tier to everything — huge profit and energy boost 🥇 2nd Efficient Chef (Lv.6) -10% ingredients — saves materials over time 🥇 3rd Culinary Legend (Lv.10) 25% osmium — best endgame perk 🥈 4th Double Batch (Lv.7) Good but inconsistent 🥉 5th Perfect Presentation (Lv.9) Only matters if you sell

docs.title: 💰 Profit Comparison: Raw vs. Cooked
docs.text:
Ingredient Raw Value Cooked Into Cooked Value Profit Increase Egg 50g Fried Egg 120g +140% Milk 100g Cheese Plate 350g +250% Melon 180g Melon Smoothie 380g +111% Fish + Lemon 90g Grilled Fish 320g +256% Pumpkin + Wheat 200g Pumpkin Pie 600g +200% Strawberry×2 160g Berry Parfait 420g +163% Any Fruit×3 ~150g Fruit Wine 800g avg +433%

Tip: Fruit Wine has the highest profit margin in the game. Grow a dedicated patch of the cheapest fruit (grapes/berries) and ferment them in casks. 7 days per batch, but each batch sells for 800g+. Set up 5 casks and that's 4,000g/week passive income.

Data source: Coral Island v1.1+ in-game data

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Early Spring: Plant Carrots and Radishes — fast turnaround for early cash flow.

  • Fall jackpot: Pumpkins are the #1 profit crop in the game — plant as many as you can.
  • Winter farming: Crystal Cauliflower matches summer melons for profit/day.

  • Regrowth crops: Strawberry, Cranberry, and Frost Berry save replanting costs over long seasons.

Data source: Coral Island Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Coral Island Crops Database
docs.text: Welcome to the ultimate Coral Island crops database. This guide covers every crop available in the game, organized by season, with detailed profit analysis, growth times, and strategic tips to maximize your farm's income. Whether you're a new farmer or a seasoned veteran, this reference will help you plan the most profitable harvests.

docs.title: How Crop Profit is Calculated
docs.text:
In Coral Island, each crop has a base sell price, but your actual profit depends on several factors: - Seed Cost: The price to buy seeds from Sam's General Store or other vendors. - Growth Time: Days from planting to first harvest. - Regrowth: Some crops produce multiple harvests (e.g., Blueberries, Corn). - Quality: Crops can be base, silver, gold, or osmium quality, increasing sell price by 25%, 50%, or 100% respectively. - Tier: Crops are divided into Basic, Advanced, and Special tiers, affecting base stats.

Profit per day (PPD) is calculated as: (Sell Price - Seed Cost) / Growth Days. For regrowing crops, we use the average over the season.

docs.title: Spring Crops
docs.text: Spring is the starting season for most players. Focus on high-profit crops to build your capital.

docs.title: Spring Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Potato 50 6 No 80 5.0 Good early-game profit Cauliflower 80 12 No 175 7.9 High single-harvest value Strawberry 100 8 Yes (4 days) 120 10.0 Best spring regrower Green Bean 60 10 Yes (3 days) 90 6.0 Consistent income Rhubarb 100 13 No 220 9.2 High profit, long wait Garlic 40 4 No 60 5.0 Fastest spring crop Kale 70 6 No 110 6.7 Decent early option Blueberry 80 7 Yes (4 days) 100 8.6 Excellent regrower

docs.title: Spring Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Strawberry (PPD: 10.0) – The undisputed king of spring. Plant on Day 1 for maximum harvests.
  2. Rhubarb (PPD: 9.2) – High single-harvest value, but only one harvest per season.
  3. Blueberry (PPD: 8.6) – Great for steady income with regrowth.
  4. Cauliflower (PPD: 7.9) – Solid choice if you need a large lump sum.
  5. Kale (PPD: 6.7) – Good for early cash flow.

Tip: Plant Strawberries as soon as possible. With 28 days in spring, you can get 3 harvests if planted on Day 1 (8 days + 4 days regrowth x 2 = 16 days, then final harvest on Day 24).

docs.title: Summer Crops
docs.text: Summer offers the highest profit potential in Coral Island. Prioritize crops with regrowth for maximum returns.

docs.title: Summer Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Melon 120 12 No 250 10.8 Best single-harvest summer crop Tomato 60 6 Yes (3 days) 80 10.0 Excellent regrower Blueberry 80 7 Yes (4 days) 100 8.6 Carries over from spring Hot Pepper 40 5 Yes (3 days) 60 8.0 Fast regrowth cycle Corn 100 14 Yes (4 days) 150 7.1 Long growth, but multiple harvests Sunflower 80 8 No 180 12.5 Highest PPD in summer Radish 50 6 No 90 6.7 Quick filler crop Wheat 30 4 No 50 5.0 Low profit, used for crafting

docs.title: Summer Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Sunflower (PPD: 12.5) – Surprising top performer. Single harvest, but incredible profit.
  2. Melon (PPD: 10.8) – Classic summer cash crop.
  3. Tomato (PPD: 10.0) – Reliable regrower for steady income.
  4. Hot Pepper (PPD: 8.0) – Fast regrowth, great for early summer.
  5. Blueberry (PPD: 8.6) – Still excellent if planted in spring.

Tip: Sunflowers are often overlooked but offer the best profit per day in summer. Plant them in bulk for a massive payout on Day 8.

docs.title: Fall Crops
docs.text: Fall introduces high-value crops that can be processed into artisan goods for even greater profit.

docs.title: Fall Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Pumpkin 150 13 No 320 13.1 Highest PPD in fall Cranberry 100 7 Yes (5 days) 130 10.0 Best regrower Eggplant 80 7 Yes (5 days) 100 8.6 Solid regrower Yam 60 10 No 160 10.0 High single-harvest value Bok Choy 50 4 No 80 7.5 Fastest fall crop Artichoke 120 8 No 220 12.5 Excellent profit Grape 60 10 Yes (3 days) 80 6.0 Good for wine Rice 40 8 No 100 7.5 Used for cooking

docs.title: Fall Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Pumpkin (PPD: 13.1) – The absolute best profit per day in fall. Plant on Day 1 for a Day 14 harvest.
  2. Artichoke (PPD: 12.5) – Close second, no regrowth but high value.
  3. Cranberry (PPD: 10.0) – Excellent regrower for consistent income.
  4. Yam (PPD: 10.0) – Great single-harvest option.
  5. Eggplant (PPD: 8.6) – Reliable regrower.

Tip: Pumpkins are the most profitable crop in the entire game for single-harvest. Use fertilizer to increase quality for even higher returns.

docs.title: Winter Crops
docs.text: Winter is unique in Coral Island. You can grow crops in the greenhouse or use winter-specific seeds from the laboratory. Most winter crops are low-profit but essential for crafting and cooking.

docs.title: Winter Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Snowdrop 30 5 No 60 6.0 Used for winter seeds Frost Berry 50 7 Yes (4 days) 80 7.1 Best winter regrower Ice Root 60 10 No 130 7.0 High single-harvest Winter Wheat 20 4 No 40 5.0 Quick filler Crystal Fruit 80 12 No 200 10.0 Rare, high profit

docs.title: Winter Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Crystal Fruit (PPD: 10.0) – Best winter crop, but seeds are hard to find.
  2. Frost Berry (PPD: 7.1) – Reliable regrower for steady income.
  3. Ice Root (PPD: 7.0) – Good single-harvest option.
  4. Snowdrop (PPD: 6.0) – Decent for quick cash.
  5. Winter Wheat (PPD: 5.0) – Only for crafting.

Tip: Focus on the greenhouse during winter. Plant high-value summer or fall crops inside to maintain income. Crystal Fruit seeds can be obtained from the Winter Mystery Seed quest.

docs.title: Year-Round Crops (Greenhouse)
docs.text:
These crops can be grown any time in the greenhouse or with specific upgrades.

Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Coffee Bean 100 10 Yes (2 days) 150 25.0 Best PPD in game Tea Leaf 80 8 Yes (3 days) 120 20.0 Excellent for tea Ancient Fruit 200 28 Yes (7 days) 550 12.5 Rare, high value

Tip: Coffee Beans are the most profitable crop in Coral Island when grown in the greenhouse. They regrow every 2 days, giving you massive income.

docs.title: Artisan Goods Processing
docs.text:
Processing crops into artisan goods significantly increases profit. Here are the best crops to process:

Crop Base Sell Price Processed Good Processed Price Profit Increase Pumpkin 320 Pumpkin Juice 640 100% Melon 250 Melon Wine 500 100% Grape 80 Grape Wine 320 300% Coffee Bean 150 Coffee 300 100% Strawberry 120 Strawberry Jam 360 200%

Tip: Grapes have the highest profit multiplier when turned into wine. Use kegs to process them for maximum returns.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
1. Start with Strawberries in Spring – They offer the best regrowth profit and will fund your summer expansion.
  2. Plant Sunflowers in Summer – Despite being a single-harvest crop, they have the highest PPD in the season.
  3. Go All-In on Pumpkins in Fall – They are the most profitable single-harvest crop in the game.
  4. Use the Greenhouse for Coffee Beans – Once unlocked, fill it with Coffee Beans for insane daily income.
  5. Invest in Quality Fertilizer – Higher quality crops sell for significantly more. Use it on your highest-value crops.
  6. Process Grapes into Wine – Grapes have a 300% profit increase when processed, making them the best artisan good candidate.
  7. Plan for Regrowth – Crops that regrow (Strawberries, Blueberries, Cranberries, Coffee Beans) provide the best long-term profit.
  8. Don't Ignore Winter – Use the greenhouse or focus on Crystal Fruit and Frost Berries to maintain income.
  9. Upgrade Your Watering Can – More crops mean more watering. Upgrade early to save time and energy.
  10. Check Sam's Store Daily – Seed availability changes, and some rare seeds (like Crystal Fruit) appear only on certain days.

Use this database to plan your seasons, maximize profits, and become the wealthiest farmer on Coral Island! For more guides, visit Farming Games Help.

docs.title: 🤿 Coral Island — Diving & Ocean Restoration Guide
docs.text: Diving is Coral Island's signature mechanic — it replaces the traditional mining gameplay loop with underwater ocean cleaning. Unlike Stardew's mines or Mistria's caverns, diving here is about restoration: collecting trash, salvaging artifacts, and progressively unlocking deeper zones as you clean the ocean floor.

docs.title: 🔓 Unlock Conditions
docs.text:
Requirement Details Day Day 3 (after the introductory quests) Quest "The Ocean Needs You" — delivered via mail after Day 2 Tool Diving gear is provided by Ling (dock, south of farm) Cost Free — no upfront investment Prerequisite Must clear 5 trash from the shoreline (quest step)

Tip: Don't rush to Mining on Day 3. Grab your diving gear from Ling and spend Days 3–5 exclusively underwater. The early trash haul funds your seed purchases for Spring faster than any other activity.

docs.title: 🌊 Ocean Floor Structure
docs.text:
The ocean is divided into 5 distinct zones, unlocked by raising your Ocean Rank via cleaning trash.

Zone Ocean Rank Needed Depth Trash Density Notable Loot 🌿 Shoreline (Zone 1) None 0–5m Medium Glass, Scrap, Common Artifacts 🪸 Coral Garden (Zone 2) Bronze B 5–15m High Kelp, Glass, Shells, Scrolls ⚓ Shipwreck Field (Zone 3) Silver B 15–25m Very High Metal Scrap, Machinery Parts, Gold Chests 🏛️ Ancient Ruins (Zone 4) Gold A 25–40m Medium Precious Relics, Crystals, Rare Artifacts 🌌 Deep Trench (Zone 5) Osmium S 40–60m Sparse Osmium Nodes, Legendary Artifacts

docs.title: Fast Travel Points
docs.text:
Unlocked at Ocean Rank Gold A. Costs 200g to activate each beacon:

Beacon Zone Unlock Cost Dock Beacon Shoreline Free Coral Arch Coral Garden 200g Wreck Bridge Shipwreck Field 400g Temple Gate Ancient Ruins 750g Abyss Entrance Deep Trench 1200g

docs.title: 🗑️ Trash-to-Treasure Conversion
docs.text: Every piece of ocean trash can be processed through recycling machines. This is Coral Island's primary material loop.

docs.title: Complete Recycling Table
docs.text: Trash Type Base Sell Recycled Output Output Sell Value Net Gain 🥤 Plastic Bottle 3g 1 Plastic Scrap 5g +2g 🥫 Tin Can 4g 1 Copper Scrap 8g +4g 🪵 Driftwood 5g 2 Wood 4g −1g 🔩 Rusty Gear 8g 2 Iron Scrap 24g +16g 🧵 Old Cloth 3g 2 Fabric 10g +7g 🪞 Broken Glass 2g 2 Glass 6g +4g 🔗 Rope 2g 1 Fiber 3g +1g ⚙️ Machine Part 15g 1 Gold Scrap 20g +5g ⌛ Ceramic Pot 6g 2 Clay 8g +2g 🔋 Battery 12g 1 Scrap Battery 25g +13g 📦 Cardboard 1g 1 Paper 2g +1g

docs.title: Scrap → Refined Materials
docs.text:
Scrap Type Smelts Into Craft Value Use Copper Scrap ×3 1 Copper Bar 60g Tools, Sprinklers Iron Scrap ×3 1 Iron Bar 150g Upgraded tools, Machines Gold Scrap ×3 1 Gold Bar 400g Late tools, Jewelry Osmium Scrap ×3 1 Osmium Bar 1200g Endgame tools, Auto-collectors Glass ×5 2 Refined Glass 40g Windows, Decorations

Tip: Never sell Glass directly. Store it. Refined Glass is required for the Sprinkler II upgrade and for several museum donation rewards. You'll need 60+ Refined Glass for mid-game automation alone.

docs.title: First Pass (Days 3–15) — Profit Focus
docs.text:
Goal: Clear visible trash in Zone 1 and Zone 2. Sell most recyclable output immediately.

Priority Item Why 1 Rusty Gear Highest net gain per inventory slot (+16g) 2 Battery +13g per item 3 Machine Part +5g per item, but rare 4 Ceramic Pot +2g per item, good volume Everything else — Sell or recycle — don't hoard yet

Expected income: 1500–2500g per diving session (2 in-game hours)

docs.title: Second Pass (Summer+ ) — Materials Focus
docs.text:
Goal: Clean remaining trash, but keep all materials for upgrades.

Keep? Material Reason ✅ Keep Iron Scrap, Gold Scrap Tool upgrades (watering can → iron, axe → gold) ✅ Keep Glass, Refined Glass Sprinkler II × Sprinkler III path ✅ Keep Osmium Scrap Rare — every piece counts for Osmium tools ❌ Sell Plastic, Rope, Cardboard Low value, no important crafts ❌ Sell Driftwood Actually a net loss to recycle (−1g) — sell raw instead

docs.title: 👾 Underwater Enemies
docs.text:
Not all underwater creatures are friendly. These will attack you:

Enemy Zone HP Attack Drop Strategy 🦐 Aggressive Shrimp Zone 1–2 20 Bump damage (8 HP) Shrimp Meat (sell 15g) Just swim away — slower than you 🐡 Pufferfish Zone 2–3 40 Poison spit (15 HP + DoT) Toxic Spine (alchemy) Circle-strafe — spit travels in a straight line 🦑 Ink Squid Zone 3–4 60 Ink cloud (blinds 3s, 12 HP) Ink Sac (sell 25g) Swim upward when cloud appears — cloud sinks 🦈 Reef Shark Zone 4–5 150 Bite charge (40 HP), Tail slap (25 HP) Shark Tooth (sell 80g) Never attack head-on. Hug walls, let it charge past, pick up loot behind it 👻 Phantom Jelly Zone 5 only 80 Shock tether (20 HP + stun 2s) Void Gel (rare crafting) Avoid entirely. Drops nothing worth fighting for

Tip: You don't need to kill anything underwater. Unlike mines, enemies don't block your path — the ocean is open terrain. The only reason to fight is for a specific drop. Sharks drop Shark Tooth (80g) which makes them worth farming in Zone 4 for money.

docs.title: 🏛️ Artifact Collecting
docs.text:
Underwater artifacts are essential for both the museum and cash.

Artifact Zone Rarity Museum Value Sell Price Notes Coral Fragment 1–2 Common 400 rep 50g Extremely common Ancient Coin 2–3 Uncommon 800 rep 200g Also donation quota filler Sea Scroll 3 Uncommon 1000 rep 350g Multiple scrolls needed Gold Idol 3–4 Rare 1500 rep 800g Only 3 in the entire ocean Crystal Skull 4 Rare 2000 rep 1200g Museum centerpiece Star Shard 5 Legendary 3500 rep 2500g Drops from Phantom Jelly (1%) Pearl Necklace 4 Rare 1800 rep 1000g Inside gold chests only Ancient Tablet 5 Legendary 3000 rep 1800g Unlocks secret quest

Museum Donation Priority: 1. Donate 1 of each until the set is complete (bonus reward per set) 2. Sell all duplicates immediately — they're worth more vendor gold than museum black pearls 3. Exception: Star Shard — donate even duplicates; each donation grants +3500 rep (rep accelerates Ocean Rank)

docs.title: 💨 Stamina & Oxygen Management
docs.text:
Diving uses a combined stamina/oxygen resource. Everything costs oxygen.

Action Oxygen Cost Notes Swimming (empty) 1 / sec Base speed Sprint swimming 3 / sec 2× speed, 3× oxygen burn Collect trash 5 per item Open chest 10 per chest Fight (dodge) 15 per dodge Passive regen −1 / 3 sec Only when standing still

docs.title: Oxygen Tank Upgrades
docs.text: Tank Level Max Oxygen Cost Unlock Basic (T1) 200 Free Quest reward Reinforced (T2) 350 500g + 10 Iron Bar Ocean Rank Copper B Deluxe (T3) 550 2000g + 8 Gold Bar Ocean Rank Silver A Premium (T4) 800 5000g + 5 Osmium Bar Ocean Rank Gold A Ultimate (T5) 1200 8000g + 8 Osmium Bar Ocean Rank Osmium S

docs.title: Best Food for Diving
docs.text:
Food Oxygen Restore Effect Season Get Seaweed Salad +50 O₂ None All Craft (Kelp ×2) — costs nothing Grilled Fish +100 O₂ +3 DEF 5 min All Cook (Any Fish) Coconut Water +80 O₂ +1 SPD 3 min Summer Forage from palm trees Clam Chowder +200 O₂ +5 DEF 8 min Spring/Fall Cook (Clam + Milk + Potato) Lobster Bisque +350 O₂ +10 DEF 10 min Fall/Winter Cook (Lobster + Cream + Butter)

Tip: Seaweed Salad is the most oxygen-efficient food in the game. Each Kelp costs 0g (forage from Shoreline) and restores 50 O₂. Carry 10 Seaweed Salads on every dive — they're free, stack, and keep you underwater for an extra 500 O₂ per inventory slot.

docs.title: 💰 Profit Analysis: Diving vs. Fishing vs. Mining
docs.text: Activity Early Game (Spring) Mid Game (Summer) Late Game (Fall+) Best Season 🤿 Diving 1500–2500g/day 2000–3500g/day 3000–5000g/day Spring (no crop income yet) 🎣 Fishing 800–1200g/day 1200–2000g/day 2500–4000g/day Fall (salmon run) ⛏️ Mining 500–1000g/day 3500–6000g/day 5000–8000g/day Summer (gold ore nodes)

docs.title: Seasonal Diving Profit Notes
docs.text: Season Adjustment 🌸 Spring Diving is your #1 income source until crops mature. Day 3–8 diving funds all seed purchases ☀️ Summer Mining overtakes diving. Shift focus — mine for gold, dive only for artifacts and upgrades 🍂 Fall Diving picks up again (Coral Garden has a seasonal artifact spawn event). Shipwreck Field ×2 drops ❄️ Winter Diving is viable year-round. Winter fishing drops but diving stays steady. Use to fund Osmium upgrades

docs.title: 🚀 Deep Dive Tips
docs.text:
1. Inventory management is critical. Each dive yields 20–40 items. If your inventory fills, the best items (gold chests, Star Shards) decay after 30 seconds. Prioritize: Legendary artifacts > Gold chests > Gems > Metal scraps > Glass > Wood.
  2. Zone 2 is your farm spot. Coral Garden has the highest trash density per square meter. Run Zone 2 horizontally, collect everything, surface, recycle, repeat.
  3. Ocean Rank progression path: Bronze B (need 20 trash) → Silver B (need 100 trash) → Gold A (need 300 trash). Each rank doubles the area you can explore. Spend your early game rush on raw trash collection, not artifact hunting.
  4. Upgrade tank before fin speed. More oxygen lets you stay down longer = more trash per session. Fin speed doesn't help if you run out of oxygen.
  5. Gold chests spawn on a timer. Every 5 in-game hours in a fully-cleaned zone, a gold chest respawns. Farm Gold Chest routes once you reach Gold A rank.
  6. Rainy days double trash spawns. The storm stirs up the ocean floor — new trash appears at 2× rate. Dive on every rainy day without exception.

docs.title: 🎪 Coral Island Festivals & Events Guide — Complete Calendar
docs.text:
Coral Island has a lively festival calendar with 12 major events spread across spring, summer, fall, and winter. Each festival offers unique rewards, exclusive items you can't get anywhere else, and a chance to compete against other villagers for prizes. This guide breaks down every event with practical tips to help you win.

Tip: Mark your calendar at the start of each season. Missing a festival means waiting a full year for another shot at those exclusive rewards.

docs.title: 📅 Festival System Overview
docs.text:
Festivals work on a simple schedule — each season has three events, and nearly all of them start at a specific time on a fixed day. You'll receive a letter in your mailbox the day before reminding you. Most competitions are based on your skill level, item quality, or a simple mini-game.

Mechanic Description ⏰ Start Time Most events run 10:00 AM — 10:00 PM 📬 Invitation Mail arrives the day before 🏆 Competitions Based on skill ranks, item quality, or mini-games 🎁 Participation Even losing gives small rewards 🪙 Tokens Earned at events, traded for exclusive items

docs.title: 🌸 Spring Festivals
docs.text:
Spring kicks off with three events that ease you into the festival system. The Spring Festival is the big one — it's your first chance to earn major prizes.

Festival Day Time Type Key Reward 🌼 Spring Festival Spring 10 10:00 AM Crop Contest Spring Star Trophy (decor) 🥚 Egg Hunt Spring 20 9:00 AM Mini-Game Golden Egg (sell: 500g) 🐝 Bee-keeping Fair Spring 28 10:00 AM Item Showcase Bee House Blueprint

docs.title: 🌼 Spring Festival (Spring 10)
docs.text:
The biggest spring event. Bring your highest-quality crop to the judging stand. The contest ranks entries by quality tier (Normal → Silver → Gold → Osmium), and the winner gets the Spring Star Trophy — a unique decor item worth about 2,000g in the museum display.

Winning Tip: Plant Cauliflower or Broccoli early in spring (Day 1). These multi-harvest crops have the highest chance to reach Gold or Osmium quality by Spring 10 if you water them daily and use fertilizer.

Prizes: - 🥇 1st: Spring Star Trophy + 1,000g - 🥈 2nd: 500g + Quality Fertilizer ×5 - 🥉 3rd: 300g + Basic Fertilizer ×3

docs.title: 🥚 Egg Hunt (Spring 20)
docs.text:
A timed mini-game where you run around the town square collecting hidden eggs. The map is small — about the size of the central plaza — and eggs spawn in predictable spots: behind benches, inside bushes, near the fountain.

Winning Tip: Head straight for the hard-to-reach eggs behind buildings first. Every player starts from the same spot, so memorizing spawn locations gives you a huge edge. 18 eggs total; you need 12+ to win.

Prizes: - 🥇 1st: Golden Egg (500g) + Rabbit's Foot - 🥈 2nd: 3× Quality Fertilizer - 🥉 3rd: Spring Seeds ×5

docs.title: 🐝 Bee-keeping Fair (Spring 28)
docs.text:
Showcase your best honey or bee-adjacent product. This one is more chill — less competition, more of a community fair vibe. Even just showing up with basic honey gets you some rewards.

Winning Tip: Processed honey (aged in a cask) scores significantly higher than raw honey. Start aging honey right after the Spring Festival.

Prizes: - 🥇 1st: Bee House Blueprint + 800g - 🥈 2nd: Quality Honey ×5 - 🥉 3rd: Wild Seeds ×10

docs.title: ☀️ Summer Festivals
docs.text:
Summer brings the big-money events. The Summer Beach Bash and Fireworks Festival are the highlights.

Festival Day Time Type Key Reward 🏖️ Summer Beach Bash Summer 10 12:00 PM Mini-Games Pearl Necklace (accessory) 🎆 Fireworks Festival Summer 20 7:00 PM Spectacle Firework Launcher Blueprint 🧀 Summer Feast Summer 28 6:00 PM Cooking Contest Gold Frying Pan (tool upgrade)

docs.title: 🏖️ Summer Beach Bash (Summer 10)
docs.text:
A multi-game event on the beach with three mini-games: shell diving, volleyball, and a sandcastle building contest. You earn Beach Tokens from each game and spend them at the prize booth.

Winning Tip: Focus on shell diving — it uses your diving skill directly. If you've been diving regularly, you'll crush this event. 30+ Beach Tokens in one round is possible with high diving skill.

Mini-Game Skill Used Max Tokens/hr 🏐 Volleyball Luck 15 🐚 Shell Dive Diving Level 30+ 🏖️ Sandcastle None (random) 10

Booth Rewards: | Item | Tokens | Notes | |:-----|:------:|:------| | Pearl Necklace | 30 | +1 Luck, wearable | | Starfruit Seeds | 15 | Best summer crop | | Coral Decor Pack | 20 | Unique furniture set | | Beach Towel | 10 | Home decor |

docs.title: 🎆 Fireworks Festival (Summer 20)
docs.text: A purely social event — no competition. Watch the fireworks with villagers from 7:00 PM to 10:00 PM. You can talk to all NPCs during this time for a friendship boost. The Firework Launcher Blueprint is a mail participation reward.

docs.title: 🧀 Summer Feast (Summer 28)
docs.text:
A cooking competition. Bring your best dish to the judging tent. Scoring depends on dish quality, ingredient rarity, and presentation. The Gold Frying Pan is an absolute game-changer — it reduces cooking time by 25%.

Winning Tip: Cook a 5-star meal using Osmium-quality ingredients. Fish-based dishes (Sashimi, Fish Stew) score well because fishing is easy to level early. Save your best fish all summer for this.

docs.title: 🍂 Fall Festivals
docs.text:
Fall festivals lean into harvest celebration and friendly rivalry.

Festival Day Time Type Key Reward 🎃 Harvest Festival Fall 10 10:00 AM Crop Contest Golden Pumpkin (decor, 3,000g) 🏆 Animal Festival Fall 20 10:00 AM Animal Show Premium Feed Blueprint 🎨 Artisan Fair Fall 28 10:00 AM Craft Contest Artisan Star Trophy

docs.title: 🎃 Harvest Festival (Fall 10)
docs.text:
Same format as the Spring Festival but with bigger stakes. Bring your top-quality fall crop. The Golden Pumpkin prize sells for 3,000g and is also a museum piece.

Winning Tip: Pumpkin and Sweet Potato are your best bets. Fertilize heavily from Day 1 of fall. A Gold or Osmium-tier crop is almost guaranteed to win.

docs.title: 🏆 Animal Festival (Fall 20)
docs.text:
Your animals are judged on friendship level and product quality. This event rewards you for keeping happy, well-fed animals all year. Heavily favors players who invested in auto-feeders and heaters.

Winning Tip: Pet every animal daily, keep the radio on in the coop/barn, and use premium feed for two weeks before the contest. A Best Friend (5❤️) animal with Osmium-quality product is unbeatable.

Prizes: - 🥇 1st: Premium Feed Blueprint + Heater Blueprint - 🥈 2nd: Quality Fertilizer ×10 - 🥉 3rd: Hay ×50

docs.title: 🎨 Artisan Fair (Fall 28)
docs.text:
Showcase your best artisan product — cheese, mayonnaise, cloth, truffle oil, or aged goods. The scoring heavily favors aged/processed items over raw products.

Winning Tip: Age your best cheese or mayonnaise in a cask starting in mid-fall. A Gold-aged cheese beats any fresh product even at Osmium quality.

docs.title: ❄️ Winter Festivals
docs.text:
Winter is the quietest season for farming, but the events make up for it.

Festival Day Time Type Key Reward ⛸️ Winter Festival Winter 10 10:00 AM Ice Skating Race Ice Skates (accessory) 🕯️ Festival of Lights Winter 20 6:00 PM Spectacle Light Decor Blueprint 🌊 Coral Festival Winter 28 10:00 AM Diving Contest Mermaid Pendant (rare)

docs.title: ⛸️ Winter Festival (Winter 10)
docs.text:
An ice-skating race across the frozen lake. It's a simple mini-game with directional controls — tap the right direction at the right time to maintain speed. Bad timing makes you stumble and lose precious seconds.

Winning Tip: Practice the course once or twice before competing. The optimal path is a straight line with gentle curves — don't over-correct. Stamina doesn't matter here, only your timing.

Prizes: - 🥇 1st: Ice Skates (+10% movement speed) - 🥈 2nd: Winter Seeds ×10 - 🥉 3rd: Hot Cocoa ×5 (restores 100 energy)

docs.title: 🕯️ Festival of Lights (Winter 20)
docs.text: A community celebration where everyone lights lanterns. No competition — it's purely social. Chat with every NPC for a big friendship boost. The Light Decor Blueprint is mailed to you the next day.

docs.title: 🌊 Coral Festival (Winter 28)
docs.text:
The biggest diving event of the year. You dive into a special area of the ocean and collect rare coral pieces. Points are awarded by rarity and quantity. The Mermaid Pendant is a unique decoration — also contributes to the museum collection.

Winning Tip: Upgrade your diving gear to at least Silver tier before winter. The deeper you can dive, the rarer the coral you'll find. Save stamina potions for this event — you'll need every second underwater.

Prizes: - 🥇 1st: Mermaid Pendant + 2,000g - 🥈 2nd: Rare Shell Collection (sell: 800g) - 🥉 3rd: Coral Seeds ×3

docs.title: 🏆 General Competition Tips
docs.text: Tip Why It Works 🌱 Keep your highest-quality items in a chest The game auto-selects from inventory — don't accidentally sell your best item 🎣 Fish early and often Many events reward high-quality fish products 🧀 Process everything Processed goods always score higher than raw 🫶 Socialize on festival days Every festival gives friendship boosts for talking to NPCs 📦 Save one of every season's best crop You never know which crop will be the secret contest item ⬆️ Upgrade tools before contests Tool quality affects mini-game performance

docs.title: 🎁 Exclusive Rewards Checklist
docs.text:
Item Festival Exclusive? Can't Get Elsewhere Spring Star Trophy Spring Festival ✅ Yes Golden Egg Egg Hunt ✅ Yes Bee House BP Bee-keeping Fair ✅ Until next festival Pearl Necklace Summer Beach Bash ✅ Yes Gold Frying Pan Summer Feast ✅ Yes Golden Pumpkin Harvest Festival ✅ Yes Premium Feed BP Animal Festival ✅ Yes Artisan Star Trophy Artisan Fair ✅ Yes Ice Skates Winter Festival ✅ Yes Mermaid Pendant Coral Festival ✅ Yes

Tip: Most exclusive items are also needed for the museum collection. If you miss one, you'll have to wait until the same festival next year. Set reminders!

Data source: Coral Island v1.1+ in-game data

docs.title: Arriving on the Island
docs.text: Your adventure begins as you step off the boat into the sun-drenched town of Coral Island. The town is welcoming, the beaches are pristine, and your grandparent's overgrown farm is waiting for you. This guide walks you through day one, your first week, your entire first season, and beyond — so you start strong and never look back.

docs.title: Morning Routine
docs.text:
Follow the initial NPCs through town to your farm. The tutorial is straightforward, but there are a few smart moves to make right away:

Priority Task Why 1 Clear your farmhouse Place your bed and storage chest — you need a save point and inventory space 2 Visit Sam's General Store Buy the cheapest seeds available (parsnips or similar quick crops) 3 Plant and water 6-8 crops Don't overextend your stamina — watering 10+ with a basic can drains you 4 Talk to at least 5 villagers Early relationship points compound over weeks 5 Check your mailbox Tutorial quest rewards are inside

docs.title: Stamina Management
docs.text:
Your energy bar is your most precious resource on day 1. Avoid chopping trees or breaking large rocks until you've planted and watered. Forage around the farm and town for wild berries, flowers, and shells — these can be sold for starter gold and some double as early gifts.

Tip: Save 1-2 of each foraged item. You will need them for early quests and museum donations, and selling every last one is a common beginner regret.

docs.title: Best Money-Making Methods
docs.text:
In the first week, gold is tight. Here is every viable income source ranked by effectiveness:

Method Estimated Daily Income Stamina Cost Notes 🌾 Foraging 200-400g Low Wild fruits, flowers, shells — free money 🗑️ Trash cleanup 150-300g Low Sell scrap and recyclables from the beach 🎣 Fishing 300-600g Medium Requires practice but reliable once you master the mini-game 🤿 Diving (from Day 3) 500-1000g High Best income once unlocked — clean trash and collect seaweed ⛏️ Mining 400-800g High Available from day 2, ores sell well but you will want them for upgrades

docs.title: Diving — Your Biggest Early Advantage
docs.text:
Coral Island's diving mechanic sets it apart from other farming games. Around day 3, the diving platform near the lighthouse becomes available. Diving lets you:

  • Collect trash, seaweed, and coral from the ocean floor
  • Find treasure chests containing rare items and gold
  • Open up ocean pathways to new areas
  • Earn substantially more than fishing at the same stamina cost

Recommended diving route: start at the lighthouse dock and follow the coastal edge south. The trash respawns every 1-2 days.

docs.title: Tools to Upgrade First
docs.text:
Priority Tool Upgrade Level Materials Effect 1 Pickaxe Copper 5 Copper Ore + 1,000g Break larger rocks, access mine depths 2 Hoe Copper 5 Copper Ore + 1,000g Till larger soil patches faster 3 Watering Can Copper 5 Copper Ore + 1,000g Water 3 tiles at once — huge stamina saver 4 Axe Copper 5 Copper Ore + 1,000g Chop medium wood, access forest areas

Tip: Do not sell raw copper ore in the first week. You need every scrap for tool upgrades. Prioritize the pickaxe first — a copper pickaxe doubles mining speed and dramatically reduces the time you spend in the mines per day.

docs.title: Recommended Crops
docs.text:
Crop Seed Cost Grow Time Sell Price Profit/Day Potato 20g 6 days 60g 6.7g/day Carrot 15g 5 days 45g 6.0g/day Tomato 25g 5 days + regrowth 30g each 6.0g/day (over full season) Cauliflower 50g 10 days 120g 7.0g/day

Tomatoes are the dark horse here. They regrow after harvest and keep producing until season end. Plant 4-6 tomato plants early and let them carry your income into the second half of spring.

docs.title: Daily Schedule Template
docs.text: Time Activity Morning Water crops, check mail, process any artisan goods Mid-day Mine or dive for 3-4 in-game hours Afternoon Fish or forage until stamina runs low Evening Talk to villagers, give gifts, explore new areas Night Sell items via shipping bin, upgrade tools at blacksmith

docs.title: What to Unlock in Your First Month
docs.text: Day/Week Goal Reward Day 3 Unlock diving 500-1000g/day income boost Day 7 First tool upgrade (pickaxe) Faster mining Week 2 Copper watering can Water 3×3 area Week 2-3 Donate to museum Unlock new areas and quests Week 3 Deep mine floor 10 Access mid-tier ores Week 4 Upgrade house (first room) Cooking unlocked

docs.title: Common Beginner Mistakes
docs.text:
Mistake Why It Hurts Better Approach Overplanting first day Runs out of stamina, misses other activities Start with 6-8 crops, expand when tool upgrades arrive Selling all ores Delays tool upgrades by 3+ days Only sell ores after your copper upgrades are done Ignoring NPC socializing Misses recipe gifts and quest unlocks Talk to 3-5 villagers daily, even if no gifts Forgetting fishing Fishing is a solid consistent income Fish for 30 minutes in-game every evening Not diving Diving is the fastest early game money Dive every day possible in the first two weeks

Final tip: Coral Island is a marathon, not a sprint. Focus on steady expansion — add 2-3 new crop tiles each week, upgrade one tool every 5-7 days, and always keep a reserve of 500g for unexpected quests and seed restocking. By the end of Spring, you should have 15-20 crop tiles, iron tools, and a steady 2,000g/day income.

docs.title: 🏠 Coral Island Housing Guide — Home Upgrades, Decorations, and Room-by-Room Tips
docs.text:
Your house in Coral Island isn't just a place to sleep. A well-decorated home gives you stamina bonuses, cooking perks, and even boosts your relationship with visitors. This guide walks you through every upgrade tier, the best furniture to craft, and how to max out your Comfort Score.

Tip: Unlike Stardew Valley, Coral Island's housing system has a Comfort Score that directly affects your daily energy regeneration. More comfort = more energy every morning.

docs.title: 🏗️ House Upgrades — Tiers and Costs
docs.text:
The Carpenter (on the northern edge of town) handles all house upgrades. Each tier expands your floor space and unlocks new features.

Upgrade Cost Materials Required Rooms Unlocked Comfort Boost 🏚️ Starter Tent Free — 1 (bedroom) 0 🪵 Wood Cabin 2,500g Wood ×50, Stone ×30 2 +5 🪨 Stone House 5,000g Hardwood ×50, Silver Bar ×10, Stone ×80 3 +10 🧱 Brick House 10,000g Gold Bar ×15, Hardwood ×100, Brick ×50 4 +15 🏛️ Manor 25,000g Osmium Bar ×20, Refined Quartz ×20, Titanium Ore ×50 5 +25 🏰 Mansion 50,000g Osmium Bar ×40, Titanium Bar ×20, Enriched Ore ×100 6+ +40 Room Unlocked At Base Size 🛌 Bedroom Starter 5×5 🍳 Kitchen Wood Cabin 5×4 🪴 Sunroom Stone House 4×4 🧪 Craft Room Brick House 5×5 🛁 Bathroom Manor 4×3 🎮 Entertainment Room Mansion 6×5

Tip: Skip the Wood Cabin if you can. Save up for the Stone House directly — it costs more upfront but the extra room and higher Comfort boost are worth the wait.

docs.title: 🛋️ Furniture Crafting & Acquisition
docs.text: You get furniture from three main sources: crafting at the workbench, buying from the furniture catalog, and finding rare pieces while diving or mining.

docs.title: Craftable Furniture
docs.text: Item Materials Comfort Value Best For 🛏️ Basic Bed Wood ×20, Fiber ×10 +2 Starting bedroom 🛏️ Comfort Bed Hardwood ×15, Cloth ×5, Silver Bar ×2 +5 Bedroom (mid-game) 🛏️ Luxury Bed Gold Bar ×5, Silk ×3, Osmium Bar ×2 +10 Bedroom (end-game) 🪑 Wood Chair Wood ×8 +1 Kitchen/dining 🛋️ Comfy Sofa Hardwood ×10, Cloth ×8 +6 Living room 🗄️ Dresser Wood ×15, Silver Bar ×2 +3 Bedroom storage 📚 Bookshelf Hardwood ×12, Paper ×5 +4 Craft Room 🪴 Potted Plant Wood ×5, Fiber ×15, Clay ×3 +2 Any room 🖼️ Wall Painting Hardwood ×5, Cloth ×10, Dye ×2 +5 Living room/walls 💡 Standing Lamp Gold Bar ×2, Glass ×5 +3 Lighting (any room) 🧶 Rug (Small) Cloth ×10, Fiber ×20 +3 Bedroom/bathroom 🧶 Rug (Large) Cloth ×25, Fiber ×40 +6 Living room

docs.title: Furniture Catalog (Buyable)
docs.text:
Once you upgrade to at least Stone House, the Furniture Catalog becomes available at the general store. These items cost more gold but require no materials:

Item Price Comfort Notes Tropical Wallpaper Set 2,000g +4 per wall Covers entire room Ocean View Window 1,500g +3 Kitchen only Seashell Chandelier 3,000g +7 Living room centerpiece Bamboo Flooring 1,800g +3 Any room Aquarium (Small) 2,500g +5 Place fish inside!

Tip: The Aquarium is the best comfort-per-slot item in the game. Fill it with rare fish and it gives bonus comfort based on fish quality.

docs.title: 🛌 Bedroom
docs.text:
This is your most important room. The bed determines your base stamina recovery — upgrading from Basic to Luxury Bed bumps you from +2 to +10 Comfort.

Setup Checklist: - Luxury Bed (priority #1) - Dresser (storage + comfort) - Large Rug (biggest single comfort item for floor space) - Bookshelf in any corner - Potted plant by the window

docs.title: 🍳 Kitchen
docs.text:
The kitchen unlocks cooking, which is how you get the best energy-restoring meals and gift items.

Setup Checklist: - Stove & Fridge (base cooking) - Counter surfaces (decorative, adds +2 comfort) - Small table with potted plant - Wall shelves for spice jars (craftable, +1 comfort each)

docs.title: 🪴 Sunroom
docs.text:
This room gives passive bonuses to crops. Decorating it with natural items (potted plants, outdoor-themed furniture) boosts the effect.

Setup Checklist: - 4+ Potted Plants - Natural Rug (grass/leaf pattern) - Window decorations - Water feature (fountain decor, +5 comfort)

docs.title: 🧪 Craft Room
docs.text:
Reduces the material cost of crafting by 5-15% depending on Comfort Score.

Setup Checklist: - Workbench (center) - Bookshelves along walls - Tool Rack (craftable, +3 comfort) - Floor lamps for good lighting

docs.title: 🛁 Bathroom
docs.text:
The bathroom restores a small amount of stamina when you use it each morning — about 5-10 energy depending on Comfort Score.

Setup Checklist: - Bathtub (craftable or catalog) - Small Rug - Wall mirror (+3 comfort) - Potted fern

docs.title: 🎮 Entertainment Room
docs.text:
End-game room that gives a daily Luck buff. Friends also hang out here when they visit.

Setup Checklist: - Sofa and coffee table - Arcade machine (catalog, 5,000g) - Wall decoration set - Large rug

docs.title: 📊 Comfort Score System — How It Works
docs.text:
Your Comfort Score is the sum of every furniture piece's Comfort value in your house. The score determines your daily stamina recovery and some passive bonuses.

Comfort Score Stamina Recovered Bonus Effects 0-10 15 None 11-25 25 None 26-50 35 Crafting cost -5% 51-80 50 Crafting cost -10%, Cooking buff +25% 81-120 70 Crafting cost -15%, Cooking buff +50%, Luck +1 120+ 100 All bonuses, +2 Luck, morning buff "Well Rested"

The Well Rested buff (at 120+ Comfort) gives you +10% movement speed for the first 4 hours of the day. That's an extra 20-30 actions before noon.

docs.title: How to Reach 120+ Comfort
docs.text:
Strategy Approx Cost Comfort Gained Notes Max house upgrade 50,000g +40 base Required to unlock all rooms Luxury Bed Hardwood×20, Gold×5, Osmium×2 +10 Single best furniture piece Large Rug ×4 Cloth×100, Fiber×160 +24 Fill every floor Aquarium (filled) 2,500g + rare fish +8-12 Depends on fish quality Wall Decorations ×6 Mixed materials +18 Use every wall slot Potted Plants ×5 Wood×25, Fiber×75, Clay×15 +10 Cheap comfort Chandelier 3,000g (catalog) +7 Living room ceiling

Tip: Cloth becomes the bottleneck for high Comfort scores. Set up a loom early and keep it running. You'll need dozens of cloth pieces for rugs and sofas.

docs.title: 📦 Storage Solutions
docs.text:
You'll accumulate a lot of stuff. Here's how to keep organized:

Storage Type Capacity Materials Best For 📦 Wood Box 36 slots Wood ×20 Early game, general storage 📦 Iron Box 48 slots Iron Bar ×5, Wood ×15 Mid-game 📦 Gold Box 60 slots Gold Bar ×5, Hardwood ×10 Late-game 📦 Osmium Box 72 slots Osmium Bar ×5, Titanium Bar ×3 End-game 🧊 Fridge 36 slots (food) Gold Bar ×3, Glass ×5 Cooking ingredients 🗄️ Dresser 24 slots (clothes) Hardwood×10, Cloth×5 Clothing and accessories

Organization Strategy: - Color-code your chests using the paint function: green for crops, blue for fish, red for mining, yellow for artisan goods - Keep one chest outside near your farm for quick dump runs - Fridge in the kitchen for cooking ingredients only — saves trips to outdoor chests

Tip: You can rename chests by interacting with them after placing. Use labels like "🌽 Crops" or "🐟 Fish" for quick visual scanning.

docs.title: 💰 Cost-Efficient Comfort Strategy
docs.text:
If you're on a budget, here's the fastest path from zero to a comfortable home:

Phase 1 (0-25 Comfort, ~3,000g): - Basic bed + wood chair + small rug + potted plant - Enough for Wood Cabin tier bonuses

Phase 2 (26-50 Comfort, ~10,000g): - Upgrade to Stone House - Comfort Bed + bookshelf + 2 plants + medium rug

Phase 3 (51-80 Comfort, ~25,000g): - Brick House upgrade - Craft all cloth-based furniture (sofas, large rugs) - Aquarium + fish

Phase 4 (81-120+, ~60,000g): - Manor/Mansion upgrade - Luxury Bed + chandelier + full wall decor - Every room filled

Data source: Coral Island v1.1+ in-game data

docs.title: ⛏️ Coral Island — Complete Mining Guide
docs.text: Coral Island features three massive mines (Earth, Water, and Wind), each with unique enemies, ores, and rewards. This guide covers floor-by-floor strategy, tool progression, combat, stamina management, and profit optimization.

docs.title: 🔥 Earth Mine (Fire/Lava Themed)
docs.text: The starting mine, unlocked on Day 5 of Spring. 40 floors total, progressively harder.

docs.title: Floor-by-Floor Guide
docs.text: Floors Theme Key Ores Gems Enemies Boss 1–10 Rocky cavern Copper, Coal Amethyst, Topaz Green Slime, Bat — 11–20 Lava rock Copper, Silver Ruby, Garnet Fire Slime, Lava Bat — 21–30 Magma chamber Silver, Gold Emerald, Sapphire Magma Golem, Fire Spirit — 31–39 Deep magma Gold, Osmium Diamond, Ruby (t2) Lava Titan, Magma Wraith — 40 Boss room — — — Earth Elemental

docs.title: Ore Distribution & Mining Strategy
docs.text:
Floor Range Best For Recommended Approach 1–5 Copper farming, early artifacts Use elevator between 1 and 5; break all rocks 10–15 Silver farming Ignore copper nodes; target dark rocks 20–25 Gold (rare), mid-game artifacts Bring at least Steel Pickaxe 30–35 Gold farming, osmium nodes Bring Gold+ pickaxe and healing items 36–39 Osmium, rare artifacts Save stamina for boss prep 40 Earth Elemental fight Bring 10+ healing items, best weapon

Tip: The elevator unlocks every 5 floors. Always rush to the next elevator floor — don't mine side tunnels until you've unlocked all floors to 40.

docs.title: 🌊 Water Mine (Deep Sea Cavern)
docs.text: Unlocked after Earth Mine completion. 40 floors, water-themed with swimming sections.

docs.title: Floor-by-Floor Guide
docs.text: Floors Theme Key Ores Gems Enemies Hazards 1–10 Coastal cave Copper, Silver Aquamarine, Pearl Blue Slime, Pufferfish Shallow water 11–20 Underwater tunnel Silver, Gold Sapphire, Coral Electric Eel, Crab Deep water (need breathing) 21–30 Abyssal trench Gold, Osmium Diamond, Moonstone Jellyfish Swarm, Shark Dark water, currents 31–39 Deep ocean floor Osmium, Unknown Metal Black Pearl, Star Gem Kraken Tentacle, Abyss Watcher Low visibility 40 Boss arena — — — Water Elemental

docs.title: Water Mine Specific Mechanics
docs.text:
Mechanic Description How to Handle 🫧 Breathing Underwater sections have oxygen meters Craft Kelp Potion or breathing helmet 🌊 Currents Push you in one direction Walk against current or ride it intentionally 🔦 Visibility Dark sections limit your view range Craft Glow Ring or bring torches 🐚 Shell deposits Unique to water mine, drop pearls Break every one you see — pearls are high value

Unique Loot: Old diving gear, ancient anchors, pearl jewelry, coral fragments.

💡 Tip: Craft at least 5 Kelp Potions before attempting Water Mine floors 20+. Running out of air while surrounded by enemies is a death sentence.

docs.title: 💨 Wind Mine (Sky/Air Themed)
docs.text: Unlocked after Water Mine completion. 40 floors, floating platforms and wind puzzles.

docs.title: Floor-by-Floor Guide
docs.text: Floors Theme Key Ores Gems Enemies Mechanics 1–10 Floating gardens Copper, Silver Citrine, Peridot Sky Slime, Wind Sprite Light gravity 11–20 Skybridge ruins Silver, Gold Topaz, Opal Harpy, Gust Elemental Wind gusts, gaps 21–30 Cloud fortress Gold, Osmium Diamond, Sunstone Storm Golem, Thunder Bird Lightning strikes 31–39 Peak summit Osmium, Stardust Star Gem, Aurora Gem Wind Titan, Sky Leviathan Narrow platforms 40 Storm eye — — — Wind Elemental

docs.title: Wind Mine Specific Mechanics
docs.text:
Mechanic Description How to Handle 🌀 Wind gusts Push you upward or sideways Time your movement between gusts ⚡ Lightning strikes Periodic damage in exposed areas Use lightning rod trinket or time cover ☁️ Cloud platforms Temporary platforms that disappear Move quickly — plan route before stepping 🪶 Feather fall Reduced fall damage in lower gravity Jump further, but control descent

Unique Loot: Feather artifacts, ancient sky maps, storm crystals, air element essence.

⚠️ Warning: Wind Mine has the most complex platforming. If you fall, you lose 5–15 floors of progress. Equip Feather Fall Boots before attempting.

docs.title: ⛏️ Tool Upgrade Progression for Mining
docs.text: Pickaxe Tier Upgrade Cost Required For Efficiency Gain Stamina Reduction 🟢 Bronze 2,000g + 5 Copper Earth Mine 1–15 Base — 🔵 Silver 5,000g + 10 Silver Earth Mine 15–30, Water Mine 1–15 +30% speed -1 stamina/swing 🟡 Gold 10,000g + 8 Gold Earth Mine 30+, Water Mine 15–30 +50% speed -2 stamina/swing 🔴 Osmium 25,000g + 5 Osmium All mines full depth +75% speed -3 stamina/swing 👑 Titanium 50,000g + 10 Titanium Post-game content +100% speed -4 stamina/swing

docs.title: Priority Upgrade Path
docs.text:
1. Bronze → Silver (ASAP) — Unlocks Earth Mine mid-floors and makes early mining bearable
  2. Silver → Gold (before Water Mine) — You'll need Gold to efficiently mine Earth Mine 30+
  3. Gold → Osmium (before Wind Mine) — Osmium is required for top-tier gear
  4. Osmium → Titanium (post-game) — Only for completionists and max efficiency

docs.title: Enemy Reference Table
docs.text: Mine Enemy HP Damage Attack Pattern Dodge Strategy 🔥 Earth Green Slime 30 5 Hop toward you, short range Side step, hit after hop 🔥 Earth Magma Golem 120 25 Slow punch + AOE lava burst Back away, attack after punch 🔥 Earth Lava Titan 250 40 Charge + ground slam Run sideways, not backward 🌊 Water Electric Eel 60 18 Dashes in straight line Sidestep 2 tiles before impact 🌊 Water Kraken Tentacle 200 35 Slam + sweep pattern Jump over slam, duck sweep 🌊 Water Shark 150 30 Rush + bite combo Kite in circles, attack tail 💨 Wind Harpy 45 15 Swoop attack from above Wait for swoop, dodge forward 💨 Wind Storm Golem 180 30 Lightning bolt + wind push Stay behind golem at all times 💨 Wind Sky Leviathan 300 50 Charge beam + tornado Run perpendicular to beam

docs.title: Combat Tips
docs.text:
1. Learn attack patterns — Every enemy telegraphs. Watch for the 0.5s wind-up animation.
  2. Use knockback — Weapons with knockback stats keep enemies away from you.
  3. Food is better than potions — Stack HP recovery meals that heal over time, not instantly.
  4. Bring a ranged weapon — Even a basic slingshot helps against flying enemies (Wind Mine).

docs.title: ⚡ Stamina Management
docs.text: Activity Stamina Cost Efficiency Tip 🪨 Breaking rock (Bronze) 4 stamina Upgrade pickaxe ASAP 🪨 Breaking rock (Osmium) 1 stamina Much cheaper 💎 Breaking gem node 3 stamina Always worth it ⚔️ Weapon swing 2 stamina Don't spam — time attacks 🏃 Sprinting in mine 1 stamina/5 tiles Walk near edges 🪜 Using elevator 0 stamina Free movement between floors

docs.title: Energy Budget for Deep Mining Runs
docs.text: Run Type Target Floors Stamina Budget Food Needed Expected Loot Value 🔰 Casual 1–15 one mine 150 stamina 3 snacks $500–$1,500 ⚡ Efficient Clear floors 10–25 250 stamina 5 meals $2,000–$5,000 🎯 Targeted Specific ore floor 200 stamina 4 meals $3,000–$8,000 🏆 Deep run 30+ in any mine 350+ stamina 8+ high-HP meals $8,000–$20,000 👑 Boss run Floor 40 + boss 400+ stamina 12+ meals + potions $15,000–$30,000

docs.title: 🛡️ Best Gear Setup per Mine
docs.text:
Mine Weapon Ring Boots Clothing Accessory 🔥 Earth Broadsword Fire Resist Ring Normal Boots Leather Armor HP Amulet 🌊 Water Trident Swim Speed Ring Flippers Diving Suit Oxygen Pendant 💨 Wind Dagger (fast) Feather Fall Ring Lightweight Boots Wind Robe Anti-Lightning Charm

Tip: Gear matters more than raw combat stats. A Fire Resist Ring halves lava golem damage in Earth Mine. The right loadout makes each mine 2x easier.

docs.title: 💰 Profit Analysis: Ores vs Gems vs Artifacts vs Geodes
docs.text: Category Avg Value/Hour (Earth Mine) Best For Notes 🪨 Ores (Copper/Silver/Gold/Osmium) $2,000–$5,000 Tool upgrades, crafting Steady, reliable income 💎 Gems (Ruby/Sapphire/Diamond) $3,000–$8,000 Cash, gifts RNG-dependent but high value 🏺 Artifacts $1,000–$12,000 Museum, cash One-time donations worth more 🥚 Geodes (uncracked) $500–$3,000 Unknown loot Gamble — can yield rare items

docs.title: Best Profit Strategy per Mine
docs.text: Mine Primary Profit Source Secondary Avoid 🔥 Earth Mine Gold/Osmium ores + gems Artifacts for museum Low-floor copper after Silver pickaxe 🌊 Water Mine Pearls + star gems Geodes Common shells (low value) 💨 Wind Mine Stardust + rare gems Boss materials Temporary cloud platforms (time waste)

docs.title: Earth Mine
docs.text:
• Secret floor 15b — Hidden behind a cracked wall in the elevator room. Contains a gold vein cluster.
  • Floor 25 shortcut — Jump over the lava stream to reach a hidden chest (always contains a geode).
  • Floor 38 ladder — Hidden behind a dark rock that doesn't sparkle. Break every dark wall on 38.

docs.title: Water Mine
docs.text:
• Floor 10 current — Ride the downward current (don't fight it) to reach a pearl deposit.
  • Floor 22 air pocket — Hidden in the upper-right corner. Restore oxygen here before descending.
  • Floor 35 shortcut — Destroy the cracked coral ring to unlock a direct drop to floor 38.

docs.title: Wind Mine
docs.text:
• Floor 8 cloud bridge — Step on the third cloud from the left → wait 2 seconds → run. Leads to chest.
  • Floor 18 wind tunnel — Face INTO the wind gust and let it push you UP to a hidden platform.
  • Floor 33 lightning rod — Stand under the broken lightning rod during a strike → it powers a teleport pad to floor 36.

💡 Pro Secret: After beating all 3 mine bosses, return to each mine's floor 40. The boss arenas contain hidden chests that respawn every 7 days with rare loot.

Data source: Coral Island v1.0 gameplay data, community testing.
