docs.title: 💕 Coral Island — Complete Romance, Socializing & Marriage Guide
docs.text: Coral Island features 14 romanceable characters + 2 unique routes, with a richer interaction system than Stardew including multi-dating mechanics and separation/divorce options. This guide helps you plan the best strategy.

docs.title: 🌸 Female Candidates (7)
docs.text: Character Residence Occupation Birthday Daily Area Difficulty 💃 Nina Starter Town Tailor Shop Clerk Spring 12 Tailor Shop / Town Square ⭐⭐ 🌿 Surya Starter Town Botanist Spring 22 Research Lab / Garden ⭐⭐ 🐟 Alice Beach Area Fishing Shop Owner Summer 7 Pier / Fishing Shop ⭐ 🥧 Lily Starter Town Baker Summer 24 Bakery ⭐⭐⭐ 🎨 Macy Starter Town General Store Owner Fall 5 General Store ⭐⭐ 📚 Eva Starter Town Librarian Fall 19 Library / Community Center ⭐ 🎭 Pablo Starter Town Theater Agent Winter 3 Theater / Town Square ⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: 🌸 Male Candidates (7)
docs.text: Character Residence Occupation Birthday Daily Area Difficulty 🌊 Rafael Beach Area Diving Instructor Spring 8 Beach / Dive Shop ⭐ 🎸 Charles Starter Town Guitarist / Musician Spring 15 Town Square / Bar ⭐⭐ 🔧 Mark Starter Town Craftsman / Repair Shop Summer 10 Repair Shop ⭐⭐ 🏥 Kenny Starter Town Doctor Summer 20 Hospital ⭐⭐⭐ ⚓ Theo Beach Area Fisherman Fall 8 Pier / Boat ⭐ 🚜 Scott Starter Town Farm Supply Store Owner Fall 15 Farm Supply / Farm ⭐ ☕ Ben Starter Town Cafe Owner Winter 14 Cafe ⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: 🌸 Unique Routes (2)
docs.text:
Character Unlock Condition Special Mechanic Notes 🗿 Ling (Diving Coach) Triggered after diving unlocks Not marriageable, but has unique friendship story Full friendship unlocks special accessory rewards 👻 Jake Specific story choices Non-traditional romance route Unlocked later in the game

Tip: Alice (Fishing Shop) and Theo (Fisherman) are the easiest to romance for both genders. Their loved gifts can both be obtained from fishing (Alice loves sashimi, Theo loves rare fish), and they're the easiest to find day-to-day. If you want to speed-run the marriage achievement, pick one of them as your gift route.

docs.title: Gift-Giving Rules
docs.text: Rule Details 📊 Max 2 gifts per week Beyond that, only 30% friendship gain 🧠 Max 1 loved gift per week Second loved gift in same week gives half friendship 🎂 Birthday gift Friendship ×8 — always give a loved gift on birthdays 💍 Flowers/Perfume Unlocked only after dating, ×2 friendship multiplier 📈 Weekly friendship cap 800 XP/week (about 1~1.5 hearts)

docs.title: Complete Gift Preference Reference
docs.text:
Character 🏆 Loved ❤️ 👍 Liked ❌ Disliked Alice 🐟 Sashimi, Tropical Fish Seafood, Shells Mushrooms Surya 🌿 Rare Seeds, Orchids Flowers, Potted Plants Meat Products Nina 💃 Cloth, Silk Clothing Materials Bait Lily 🥧 Strawberry Cake, Desserts Fruits, Sugar Fish (all) Macy 🛒 Coffee Beans, Exotic Spices Groceries Trash Eva 📚 Ancient Books, Mysterious Scrolls Handicrafts Noisy Items Pablo 🎭 Perfume, Feather Decorations Luxury Goods Cheap Items Rafael 🌊 Diving Equipment, Coral Ocean Products Synthetic Items Charles 🎸 Music Records, Handmade Instruments Handicrafts Meat Mark 🔧 Metal Bars, Tools Ores, Stone Decorations Kenny 🏥 Medicinal Plants, Rare Herbs Vegetables Sweets Theo ⚓ Rare Fish, Fish Bait Seafood Flowers Scott 🚜 Machine Parts, Engine Oil Ores, Wood Flowers Ben ☕ Rare Coffee, Tea Beverages Seafood

Tip: Easiest loved gifts to source in bulk: Alice's «Tropical Fish» (fishing, abundant in summer), Rafael's «Coral» (plentiful in Dive Zones 1-2), Theo's «Rare Fish» (fishing bobber mechanic). If multi-dating, strongly consider these three to keep gift costs low.

docs.title: General Trigger Rules
docs.text: Condition Requirement 📅 Time Any time except late night (22:00-6:00) 🌤️ Weather Sunny (some events need specific weather) 📍 Location Character's current daily location 💕 Heart Threshold One event every 2~4 hearts ❌ No overlap Only one heart event active at a time

docs.title: Key Heart Event Choices
docs.text: Character Event Recommended Choice Reason Alice 4❤️ Pier Help ✅ Help fix the boat +30 friendship, unlocks late-game fishing story Alice 8❤️ Confession ✅ Answer honestly Either choice triggers confession; honest route is best Rafael 6❤️ Diving Accident ✅ Jump in to save him Major friendship boost, triggers follow-up story Lily 6❤️ Baking Contest ✅ Let her choose the recipe Respecting her choice → max friendship gain Scott 4❤️ Farm Equipment Exhibition ✅ Help set up Unlocks equipment discount Theo 8❤️ Boat Confession ✅ Accept the invitation Highest friendship gain; missing it means waiting another week Surya 6❤️ Botanical Garden ✅ Choose rare plant +50 friendship, unlocks rare seed purchases Mark 4❤️ Workshop ✅ Help with repairs —

docs.title: Bouquet System
docs.text: Stage Item How to Get Effect 💐 Confession Bouquet Pierre's Flower Shop 200g Confirms dating relationship 💍 Engagement Ring Blacksmith 1,000g + Gold Bar×2 Engaged, triggers 10❤️ proposal 🫂 Breakup — Choose in dialogue Friendship drops 5❤️, can't date for 1 week

docs.title: Jealousy Mechanics
docs.text:
Situation Effect Penalty Giving bouquets to 2+ candidates ❌ Triggers jealousy dialogue −100 friendship each Caught dating A by B ❌ −50 friendship Need gift to recover Married & 8❤️+ with another ❌ Spouse dialogue warning −200 friendship Pre-bouquet (before 7❤️) ✅ No jealousy Safe to multi-gift

Tip: Pull everyone's gifts to the same tier before 7❤️, then pick your favorite to give the bouquet. There's no penalty before the bouquet — it's your safe window to cast a wide net. After the bouquet, every flower you give someone else can make your partner jealous, so once you've chosen, don't stray.

docs.title: Marriage Requirements
docs.text: Condition Specific Requirement 💕 Friendship At least 10❤️ (max hearts) 💐 Bouquet Status Bouquet given, dating confirmed 💍 Engagement Ring Ring given and accepted 🏠 House Upgrade At least Tier 2 (includes spouse room) 💰 Wedding Cost Wedding planning 5,000g

docs.title: Wedding Cost Breakdown
docs.text: Item Cost Notes 💍 Engagement Ring 1,000g + Gold Bar×2 Non-negotiable 💐 Wedding Planning 5,000g Pay at Community Center 🏡 House Upgrade (Tier 2) 10,000g + Hardwood×50 + Gold Bar×10 Must have separate spouse room 🎀 Optional: Wedding Decor 1,000g+ Cosmetic only

docs.title: Roommate Route
docs.text: Comparison Marriage Roommate Friendship Threshold 10❤️ 8❤️ Cost 5,000g + Ring + House Upgrade Free Living Together ✅ Moves onto farm ✅ Moves onto farm (separate bedrooms) Spouse Daily Help ✅ Cooks/waters/feeds animals/repairs fences ❌ No help Breakfast ✅ Spouse cooks daily breakfast ❌ Children ✅ Possible ❌ Not possible Divorce/Ending Requires Mayor (5,000g) Can opt out

docs.title: Children
docs.text: Stage Trigger Condition Duration Notes 🍼 Pregnancy 14 days after marriage 14 days Spouse tells you the news 👶 Baby Stage After 14-day pregnancy 14 days In the crib 👧 Toddler Stage 14 days after baby stage Permanent Runs around the farm, interacts 👦 Growth Doesn't grow further — Toddler form is permanent

docs.title: Child Limits
docs.text: Question Answer 📊 Max number of children 2 (can have both at once) ♂️♀️ Gender Random, not selectable 👶 Name Player custom 🛌 Separate room needed? Requires Tier 3 House Upgrade (10,000g + Hardwood×75 + Gold Bar×15) 🪄 Function Cosmetic/emotional only — won't help with farm work

docs.title: House Upgrade Cost Table
docs.text:
Upgrade Level Cost Materials Unlocks 🏠 Tier 1 Default None Basic 1-bedroom 🏠 Tier 2 10,000g Hardwood×50 + Gold Bar×10 Spouse room (required for marriage) 🏠 Tier 3 15,000g Hardwood×75 + Gold Bar×15 + Refined Quartz×10 Child's room (required for kids) 🏠 Tier 4 20,000g Osmium Bar×10 + Refined Quartz×15 Basement + Luxury Kitchen

Tip: Don't wait until you've saved 10,000g to upgrade the house if you want to marry quickly. First upgrade your tools to Iron/Gold tier for better income, while giving your target character their loved gift daily. Start gifting in spring, propose by late summer. The house upgrade can wait until after you've given the bouquet.

docs.title: ⏱️ Speedrun Marriage Route (Fastest Timeline)
docs.text:
Time Action Cumulative Days Day 1 Pick target character, learn loved gift D1 Days 2~10 Daily liked gifts, at least 2/week D10 Day 14 First 2❤️ reached, trigger first heart event D14 Day 21 Daily gifts + conversation → 4❤️ D21 Day 30 Unlock 6❤️ event D30 Day 40 Reach 8❤️ → ready to give bouquet D40 Day 42 Give bouquet → dating phase D42 Day 55 Reach 10❤️ + Tier 2 house ready D55 Day 56 Propose → Wedding! D56 (~2 seasons)

Fastest marriage record: about 8 weeks (56 in-game days), from early summer to early fall.

docs.title: 📅 Coral Island Seasonal Strategy — What to Do Each Season
docs.text:
Coral Island runs on a four-season cycle with 28 days each. Every season has its own crops, fish, forageables, and events. Knowing what to prioritize each season is the difference between scraping by and having a thriving farm by Winter Year 1. This guide gives you a season-by-season breakdown with the best crops, activities, and money-making strategies.

Tip: Coral Island's seasons are longer than Stardew's in terms of content density. Use the first few days of each season to plan — don't just rush into planting everything.

docs.title: 🌸 Spring — Getting Established
docs.text: Spring is all about building capital. You start with limited gold, basic tools, and a modest patch of tillable land. Every gold piece you earn here funds your summer expansion.

docs.title: Best Spring Crops
docs.text:
Crop Seed Cost Growth Regrowth Harvest Price Daily Profit Notes 🥔 Potato 25g 6 days No 80g 9.1g Starter staple 🥦 Broccoli 50g 8 days Every 4 days 140g 11.2g ✅ Best multi-harvest 🥬 Cauliflower 60g 10 days No 200g 14g ✅ Best single-profit 🥕 Carrot 20g 5 days No 60g 8g Quick cash 🍓 Strawberry 80g 8 days Every 3 days 120g 10g Festival favorite 🌿 Lettuce 15g 4 days No 40g 6.2g Cheap filler 🫘 Green Bean 30g 7 days Every 5 days 90g 8.6g Trellis crop

Spring Crop Verdict: Plant Potatoes early for quick cash (6 days to harvest). Transition to Cauliflower and Broccoli for better profit. Broccoli's regrowth mechanic makes it the best long-term spring crop — one planting keeps producing all season.

Money Strategy: - Week 1: Clear 30-40 tiles near your house. Plant 50% Potatoes, 50% Carrots - Week 2: Harvest carrots, replant with Broccoli. Sell excess potatoes - Week 3: Plant Cauliflower in any remaining space - Week 4: Harvest everything. Save the best cauliflower for the Spring Festival (Day 10)

docs.title: 🤿 Diving Tips for Spring
docs.text:
Spring is the easiest time to start diving. The waters are calm and the shallow zones are full of easy-to-collect items.

Activity Priority Why 🐚 Shell collecting High Easy gold early on. A full inventory of shells = 500-800g 🌿 Seaweed gathering Medium Needed for fertilizer recipes 🧽 Sponge removal High Clear trash from the ocean floor to unlock new diving areas 🔧 Trash collection Medium Scrap materials can be recycled into bars

Tip: Spend at least Days 5-10 focusing on diving. Clearing trash from the ocean floor unlocks new areas with better loot. The earlier you start, the faster you can reach the deeper zones with osmite deposits.

docs.title: Other Spring Priorities
docs.text: Task When Why 🌲 Chop trees for wood Days 1-5 You need 50+ wood for basic coops 🪨 Break rocks for stone Days 1-5 Stone for paths and buildings 🐔 Buy a chicken Day 8+ Chickens produce daily. Start early for festival 🎣 Fish the river Evenings Spring fish fetch good prices at the store 🧑‍🌾 Meet villagers Daily Say hi to everyone. Early friendships pay off 🪴 Build compost bin Day 3+ Start making fertilizer for cauliflower

docs.title: ☀️ Summer — Scaling Up
docs.text: Summer is the most profitable season. The beach opens up, festival rewards are generous, and crop diversity peaks.

docs.title: Best Summer Crops
docs.text:
Crop Seed Cost Growth Regrowth Harvest Price Daily Profit Notes 🌽 Corn 40g 7 days Every 4 days 100g 8.6g Reliable multi-harvest 🍅 Tomato 50g 6 days Every 3 days 110g 10g ✅ Best multi-harvest 🫑 Pepper 30g 5 days Every 4 days 80g 10g ✅ Fast regrow 🍉 Watermelon 100g 10 days No 350g 25g ✅ Highest single-profit 🌻 Sunflower 40g 7 days No 120g 11.4g Good for gifting 🌶️ Hot Pepper 30g 5 days Every 3 days 80g 10g Same as pepper 🍇 Grape 60g 8 days Every 5 days 140g 10g Trellis + wine material

Tip: Watermelon is the best single-crop profit in summer at 25g/day per tile. But it doesn't regrow — plant it on Day 1 for a Day 10 harvest, then again for a second wave. Tomato is better for hands-off profit since it regrows every 3 days.

Summer Crop Verdict: Plant Watermelon ×20 for big lump sums, surround them with Tomato ×30 for daily harvests. Corn is also worth planting for the multi-season mechanic — it survives into fall!

docs.title: 🏖️ Beach Activities
docs.text: Activity Best Time Reward 🐚 Beach foraging Morning tide Shells, starfish, coral (good gold) 🎣 Ocean fishing Anytime Summer-exclusive fish (tuna, snapper) 🤿 Deep diving After tool upgrade Osmite, rare gems 🏐 Beach volleyball Festival day only Beach Tokens

docs.title: Summer Money Strategy
docs.text:
Summer is where you hit your first real income milestones. Aim for:

  • Week 1: Clear beach trash. Dive every day. Buy barn upgrade materials
  • Week 2: First Watermelon harvest = 7,000g from 20 plants. Buy cow
  • Week 3: Tomato + Pepper daily harvests start rolling in (400-600g/day)
  • Week 4: Save up for Fall seeds. Aim for 10,000g+ in the bank

docs.title: 🍂 Fall — Harvest Season
docs.text: Fall is the second most profitable season and your last chance to stockpile before winter. Every crop counts.

docs.title: Best Fall Crops
docs.text:
Crop Seed Cost Growth Regrowth Harvest Price Daily Profit Notes 🎃 Pumpkin 80g 8 days No 280g 25g ✅ Best profit 🍠 Sweet Potato 40g 5 days Every 3 days 90g 10g Reliable regrow 🌽 Corn (survived) Free — Every 4 days 100g — Free money from summer 🫘 Bean 35g 7 days Every 4 days 100g 9.3g Trellis 🥬 Cabbage 60g 8 days No 180g 15g Good alternative 🍄 Mushroom Log 200g — Every 5 days 80-150g Setup cost Passive forage 🍎 Apple Tree 1,000g 28 days Every year 160g each Yearly Long-term investment

Tip: Pumpkin is the undisputed king of fall crops. 25g/day per tile beats everything else. Plant it Day 1 for a Day 8 harvest, then replant for a second harvest. Two pumpkin harvests = 14,000g from 25 plants.

docs.title: 🎃 Fall Priorities
docs.text: Task When Why Plant Pumpkins Day 1 Your main income for the season Save for winter All season You need minimum 5,000g Upgrade tools Mid-fall Winter is for mining — need better pickaxe Stockpile hay Late fall Animals can't graze in winter Process everything Ongoing Fall artisan prices are at seasonal peak Buy heater for barn Before Winter 1 Animals need heaters in winter

docs.title: Winter Prep Checklist
docs.text: Item Quantity Needed Cost 🌾 Hay 28 per animal 10g each (or self-harvested) ❄️ Heater 1 per coop/barn 500g each 🔥 Fuel 50+ Free (wood) ⛏️ Tool Upgrades Pickaxe + Axe ≥ Silver Variable 💰 Emergency Gold 2,000g+ For seeds in Spring Year 2

docs.title: ❄️ Winter — Mining and Crafting Focus
docs.text: Winter is slow for farming but it's your best season for progression. No crops grow outdoors, which frees up every day for mining, diving, and skill grinding.

docs.title: 🪨 Mining Focus
docs.text:
The mines are the main attraction in winter. With no crops to water, you can spend entire days underground.

Mine Level Best Loot Danger Level Recommended Gear 1-20 Copper, Silver, Basic Gems Low Copper Pickaxe 21-40 Gold, Rubies, Emeralds Medium Silver Pickaxe 41-60 Osmite, Diamonds, Titanium High Gold Pickaxe 61-80 Enriched Ore, Mythic Gems Very High Osmium Pickaxe + Armor

Winter Mining Strategy: - Days 1-10: Clear floors 1-30. Focus on collecting ores for tool upgrades - Days 10-20: Reach floor 50+. Start collecting Osmite for late-game tools - Days 20-28: Grind rare ores. Save up enriched materials for Spring Year 2

Tip: Bring Seafood Stew (220 energy) on mining days. One stew fills your energy bar and lasts through 15-20 floors. Don't waste time cooking weak food in winter.

docs.title: 🐟 Non-Crop Income in Winter
docs.text:
Income Source Daily Earning Potential Effort Level 🧀 Animal products (processed) 500-1,000g/day Low (passive) ⛏️ Mining (gems and bars) 800-2,000g/day High 🤿 Diving (deep zones) 600-1,500g/day High 🧑‍🍳 Cooking (sell dishes) 300-600g/day Medium 🎣 Winter fishing 400-800g/day Medium 🧪 Artisan processing 400-700g/day Low

Verdict: Winter income comes from animal products (passive) and mining/diving (active). If you set up animals and artisan machines in fall, winter is comfortable. If you didn't, you'll be relying on fishing and foraging.

docs.title: 🎯 Winter Skill Grinding Priorities
docs.text: Skill Why Winter How to Grind ⛏️ Mining Shop open? No — mines always accessible Full-day mine runs 🤿 Diving Waters are cold but diveable Upgrade wet suit, go deep 🧑‍🍳 Cooking Kitchen upgrades done? Cook everything you have 🐟 Fishing Rivers don't freeze Catch winter-exclusive fish 🏹 Combat Monsters are harder in deep mines Gear up, learn patterns

docs.title: 📆 Overall Year Plan
docs.text:
Season Primary Goal Secondary Goal Income Target 🌸 Spring Establish crops + basic tools Start diving, buy chicken 5,000-8,000g ☀️ Summer Scale up crops + barn animals Deep diving, festival prep 20,000-35,000g 🍂 Fall Maximize pumpkin profit Prep for winter 40,000-60,000g ❄️ Winter Mine progression + skill grind Stockpile for Year 2 10,000-15,000g (passive)

Year 1 Total Target: ~75,000g minimum. This lets you enter Year 2 with upgraded tools, a medium barn/coop, and enough savings to plant Spring crops immediately.

Tip: Don't feel pressured to hit every target. Coral Island is meant to be played at your own pace. Missing a fishing season just means you catch that fish next year. Prioritize fun over optimization — the game is forgiving.

Data source: Coral Island v1.1+ in-game data

docs.title: ⛏️ Core Keeper — Complete Guide & Database
docs.text: Core Keeper is a sandbox survival-crafting game by Pugstorm where you explore an underground world, mine resources, farm crops, fight bosses, and build a base. Released in full 1.0 in August 2024, it combines Terraria-style exploration with Stardew Valley-style farming and cooking mechanics.

docs.title: 🎮 Quick Facts
docs.text:
Feature Detail Developer Pugstorm Publisher Fireshine Games Release Full 1.0 — Aug 27, 2024 Genre Sandbox / Survival / Farming / Crafting Players 1–8 (online co-op) Platforms PC (Steam), Xbox, PlayStation, Nintendo Switch Price ~$19.99 (Steam)

Official site: corekeeper.com | Steam: store.steampowered.com/app/1621690

docs.title: 🧭 What You Can Do in Core Keeper
docs.text: Activity Description ⛏️ Mining Dig through procedurally generated underground biomes for ores, gems, and artifacts 🌱 Farming Plant and water crops, harvest food, cook meals with buffs 🐟 Fishing Catch fish in underground lakes and the Sunken Sea ⚔️ Combat Fight slimes, larva, ghosts, and massive boss Titans 🔧 Crafting Build workbenches, furnaces, anvils, and automate with conveyor belts 👷 Base Building Build walls, floors, furniture, and automation systems 🧪 Cooking Combine ingredients for meals with movement speed, mining, and combat buffs

docs.title: 🗺️ Biome Progression
docs.text:
Core Keeper's underground is divided into biomes in roughly this progression order:

Biome Depth Key Resources 🟫 Dirt Biome Starting area Copper, tin, basic slimes 🟥 Clay Biome Surface-ish Iron, clay, larva 🟩 Forgotten Ruins Dirt+ Gold, ancient gems 🟦 Sunken Sea Water area Octarine, pearls, rice, coffee 🟧 Desert of Beginnings Far Platinum, pineapples, grapes 🟪 Mold Biome Very deep Scarlett ore, mold, mushrooms 🔥 Forlorn Metropolis Endgame Galaxite, fire peppers ❄️ Crystal Biome Endgame Crystallized water

docs.title: 📖 Guides on This Site
docs.text: Guide Description 🆕 Getting Started Guide First steps, tools, base setup, basic progression 🌱 Crop & Farming Guide Complete crop database, profit tables, best crops by biome

  • Core Keeper Discord
  • Core Keeper on Steam

docs.title: ⚔️ Core Keeper — Boss Progression Guide
docs.text: Your complete walkthrough for every boss in Core Keeper 1.0, from your first slime to the Passage.

docs.title: 📋 Boss Progression Overview
docs.text: # Boss Biome Recommended Gear Difficulty Unlock Requirement 1 🟢 Glurch the Abominable Mass Dirt Biome Copper tools ★☆☆ Find Glurch's summoning item 2 🟠 Ghom the Watery Mass Dirt Biome Copper tools ★☆☆ Find Ghom's summoning item 3 🧪 Hive Mother Larva Biome Tin gear, Iron recommended ★★☆ Defeat Glurch + Ghom 4 💀 Skelly the Skeleton King Clay Biome Tin gear, Iron recommended ★★☆ Defeat Glurch + Ghom 5 🌿 Azeos the Sky Titan Azeos' Wilderness Iron gear, Scarlet recommended ★★★ Defeat Hive Mother + Skelly 6 🐙 Omoroth the Sea Titan Sunken Sea Scarlet gear ★★★ Defeat Hive Mother + Skelly 7 🔥 Maluga the Sun Titan Forgotten Ruins Scarlet gear, Galaxite recommended ★★★★ Defeat Azeos + Omoroth 8 🌑 Ra-Akar the Titan Desert of Beginnings Galaxite gear ★★★★ Defeat Maluga 9 🕯️ The Passage Passage Galaxite+ gear ★★★★★ All titans defeated 10 🥚 Ivy the Poisonous Mass Larva Biome Tin gear ★☆☆ After Glurch (optional)

docs.title: Location & Unlock
docs.text:
• Found in the Dirt Biome — look for a large arena-like clearing on your map
  • Craft a Glurch Summoning Idol at the Ancient Table: 10 Copper Bars
  • Place the idol in the arena to summon

docs.title: Boss Stats
docs.text:
• HP: ~1,200
  • Damage: ~20 (melee)
  • Movement: Slow, jumps toward you

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
🏟️ Arena setup: Clear the area of blocks. Place 4-6 torches.
⚔️ Fight: Stay at mid-range. When Glurch jumps, dodge sideways.
🔥 Pro tip: Hit it 3-4 times, then back away — it telegraphs jumps.

docs.title: 🎁 Drops
docs.text: Item Quantity Chance Glurch Meat 3–5 100% Large Glurch Meat 1–2 100% Ominous Glurch Eye 1 100% → Craft Scarlet gear Copper Bars 5–10 75% Ancient Gemstone 1–3 50%

docs.title: 🛡️ Recommended Setup
docs.text:
• Weapon: Copper Sword or Bow (kite strategy)
  • Armor: Copper Armor Set
  • Food: Basic Cooked Meat or Berries
  • Healing: 5–10 bandages

docs.title: Location & Unlock
docs.text:
• Found in the Dirt Biome (opposite direction from Glurch)
  • Craft a Ghom Summoning Idol: 10 Copper Bars at Ancient Table
  • Place the idol in Ghom's arena

docs.title: Boss Stats
docs.text:
• HP: ~1,500
  • Damage: ~18 (ranged water blobs)
  • Special: Spawns smaller slimes when hit

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
🏟️ Arena: Clear the area. Place torches for visibility.
⚔️ Fight: Kill the small slimes first before they multiply.
🔥 Pro tip: A Copper Bow makes this fight trivial — shoot from range.

docs.title: 🎁 Drops
docs.text: Item Quantity Chance Ghom Meat 3–5 100% Watery Slime Core 1 100% → Craft Scarlet gear Copper Bars 5–10 75% Ancient Gemstone 1–3 50%

docs.title: Location & Unlock
docs.text:
• Found in the Larva Biome (underground, past the dirt biome)
  • Auto-unlock after defeating both Glurch and Ghom — the Larva Biome opens up
  • No summoning idol needed — she's guarding the Larva Hive

docs.title: Boss Stats
docs.text:
• HP: ~4,500
  • Damage: ~35 (melee), ~25 (acid spit)
  • Special: Spawns larva minions, shoots acid pools

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
🏟️ Arena: Create a small 2-block high tunnel to enter.
           Use the ceiling to block her charge attack!
⚔️ Phase 1: Kill larva spawns immediately to prevent overwhelm.
           Watch for acid pools on the ground — don't stand in them.
⚔️ Phase 2 (below 50% HP): She charges faster and spawns more larva.
           Use the tunnel entrance as a chokepoint.
🔥 Pro tip: Bring a Tin Shield. Block her charge then counter-attack.

docs.title: 🎁 Drops
docs.text: Item Quantity Chance Hive Mother Meat 3–5 100% Larva Claw 2–4 100% Hive Core 1 100% → Craft Iron gear Ancient Gemstone 3–6 100%

docs.title: 🛡️ Recommended Setup
docs.text:
• Weapon: Tin Sword (melee) + Tin Bow of kiting
  • Armor: Tin Armor Set (minimum), Iron recommended
  • Food: Cooked Glurch/Ghom Meat or Hearty Salad
  • Consumables: 10+ bandages + 2-3 explosive traps

docs.title: Location & Unlock
docs.text:
• Found deep in the Clay Biome (summoned via Ancient Skull)
  • Auto-unlock after defeating Glurch and Ghom — the Clay Biome entrance opens

docs.title: Boss Stats
docs.text:
• HP: ~5,000
  • Damage: ~40 (melee sword swings)
  • Special: Teleports, summons skeletons, shoots bone projectiles

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
⚔️ Phase 1: Skelly swings a broad sword. Roll behind him after he attacks.
           Kill skeleton summons quickly with AoE swings.
⚔️ Phase 2 (below 50%): Summons MORE skeletons, teleports behind you.
           Watch for the teleport shimmer — roll away immediately.
🔥 Pro tip: Use the terrain pillars in Skelly's arena to block bone projectiles.

docs.title: 🎁 Drops
docs.text: Item Quantity Chance Skelly Meat 3–5 100% Skeleton Bone 4–8 100% Skelly's Skull 1 100% → Craft Iron gear Ancient Gemstone 3–6 100%

docs.title: Location & Unlock
docs.text:
• Found in Azeos' Wilderness (surface biome, need passages from Clay/Larva boundaries)
  • Place the Azeos Summoning Idol (crafted at Ancient Table with Scarlet materials)

docs.title: Boss Stats
docs.text:
• HP: ~12,000
  • Damage: ~60 (melee stomp), ~45 (lightning beam)
  • Special: Flying phases, lightning strikes, ground pound AoE

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
🏟️ Arena: Big open space. Remove all obstacles.
           Place campfires around the perimeter for healing zones.
⚔️ Phase 1: Hit Azeos' legs. Dodge the lightning beam sideways.
           When Azeos flies up, run — he'll ground-pound where you were.
⚔️ Phase 2 (below 50%): More lightning strikes, faster ground pounds.
           The sky darkens before lightning — 1 second to dodge.
🔥 Pro tip: Scarlet Bow with fire arrows. Ranged attacks during flight phase.
           Bring 20+ Greater Bandages.

docs.title: 🎁 Drops
docs.text: Item Quantity Chance Azeos Meat 3–5 100% Sky Feather 2–4 100% Storm Crest 1 100% → Craft Galaxite gear Ancient Gemstone 5–10 100% Azeos' Soul 1 100% → Summon Ra-Akar

docs.title: Location & Unlock
docs.text:
• Found in the Sunken Sea biome (past Clay/Larva through water passages)
  • Place the Omoroth Summoning Idol

docs.title: Boss Stats
docs.text:
• HP: ~13,000
  • Damage: ~55 (melee tentacle), ~50 (water jet)
  • Special: Water jets, tentacle AoE, summons sea creatures

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
🏟️ Prep: Bring a Diving Set or breathing potions.
           Omoroth's arena is partially underwater.
⚔️ Phase 1: Hit the tentacles when they surface.
           Dodge water jets by swimming behind pillars.
⚔️ Phase 2 (below 50%): Huge water jet AoE in center.
           Stay near edges, shoot from range.
🔥 Pro tip: Bring the highest-tier fishing rod to catch Glow Tuna.
           Cook them for underwater breathing buff food.

docs.title: 🎁 Drops
docs.text: Item Quantity Chance Omoroth Meat 3–5 100% Sea Scale 2–4 100% Tidal Crest 1 100% → Craft Galaxite gear Ancient Gemstone 5–10 100% Omoroth's Soul 1 100% → Summon Ra-Akar

docs.title: Location & Unlock
docs.text:
• Found in the Forgotten Ruins biome
  • Requires: Storm Crest (Azeos) + Tidal Crest (Omoroth) to craft summoning idol

docs.title: Boss Stats
docs.text:
• HP: ~18,000
  • Damage: ~75 (melee), ~60 (fire breath)
  • Special: Lava pools, fire breath cone, summoning fire spirits

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
🏟️ Arena: Platforms in lava. Watch your footing!
⚔️ Phase 1: Hit the legs, dodge fire breath by running behind.
           Lava pools persist — don't let them trap you.
⚔️ Phase 2 (below 50%): Fire spirits spawn. Kill them FAST.
           Maluga slams the ground creating lava waves.
🔥 Pro tip: Galaxite Bow + water arrows do bonus damage.
           Wear heat-resistant food (Chilled Delight).

docs.title: 🎁 Drops
docs.text: Item Quantity Chance Maluga Meat 4–6 100% Sun Fragment 2–4 100% Ember Crest 1 100% → Craft Galaxite gear Galaxite Ore 8–15 100% Maluga's Soul 1 100% → Summon Ra-Akar

docs.title: Location & Unlock
docs.text:
• Found in the Desert of Beginnings (surface biome, through Sunken Sea)
  • Requires: Azeos's Soul + Omoroth's Soul + Maluga's Soul → craft Ra-Akar Summoning Idol

docs.title: Boss Stats
docs.text:
• HP: ~25,000
  • Damage: ~90 (melee), ~70 (void bolts)
  • Special: Void portals, teleporting, dark AoE projectiles

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
🏟️ Arena: Large circular arena with void crystals.
           Destroy void crystals when they appear — they heal Ra-Akar.
⚔️ Phase 1: Ranged combat recommended. Dodge void bolt patterns.
           When Ra-Akar opens a void portal, run to the other side.
⚔️ Phase 2 (below 50%): Multiple portals, dark AoE orbs.
           Stay mobile. Destroy crystals IMMEDIATELY.
⚔️ Final phase (below 20%): Enraged. Non-stop void projectiles.
           This is a DPS race — use your best food and buffs.
🔥 Pro tip: Full Galaxite gear (minimum). Galaxite Bow + explosive arrows.
           Bring 30+ Greater Bandages and 5+ Healing Potions.

docs.title: 🎁 Drops
docs.text: Item Quantity Chance Ra-Akar Meat 5–8 100% Void Shard 3–6 100% Titan Core 1 100% → Craft endgame gear Galaxite Ore 10–20 100% Godly Gold 3–5 100%

docs.title: Location & Unlock
docs.text:
• Enter the Passage — the massive structure that appears at the center of the map
  • Requires: All 4 titans defeated (Azeos, Omoroth, Maluga, Ra-Akar)
  • This is the game's final boss and endgame area — a multi-phase gauntlet

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
🏟️ Arena: Multi-room structure.
           Bring maximum supplies — you can't leave once inside.
⚔️ Phase 1 — The Gauntlet: Fight through waves of all previous minibosses.
           Resource management is key.
⚔️ Phase 2 — The Core: Face the Core itself — massive HP pool.
           Multiple attack patterns from all previous bosses combined.
⚔️ Phase 3 — The Escape: After defeating the Core, escape before detonation.
🔥 Pro tip: MAX GEAR — Galaxite+ upgraded. Best food buffs.
           Bring 50+ Greater Bandages, 10+ Healing Potions.
           Consider bringing a partner or summoning minions.

docs.title: 🎁 Drops
docs.text: Item Quantity Chance Core Fragment 3–5 100% Galaxy Fragment 1–2 100% Passage Key 1 100% ??? Endgame Items varies 100%

docs.title: Location & Unlock
docs.text:
• Secret boss in the Larva Biome (follow tunnels past the Hive Mother)
  • Unlock: After defeating Glurch, a hidden passage opens in the Larva Biome

docs.title: Boss Stats
docs.text:
• HP: ~3,000
  • Damage: ~30 (melee), ~20 (poison spit)
  • Special: Poison pools, poison status effect

docs.title: Strategy
docs.text:
⚔️ Fight: Similar to Glurch + Ghom combined.
           Poison pools linger — stay mobile.
🔥 Pro tip: Antidote potions are useful here.
           Tin gear is sufficient, Iron makes it easy.

docs.title: 🎁 Drops
docs.text: Item Quantity Chance Ivy Meat 3–5 100% Poison Sac 1–3 100% Ancient Gemstone 1–3 50%

docs.title: 🗺️ Boss Progression Flowchart
docs.text:
Start → Defeat Glurch ─┬→ Defeat Ghom
                        │
                        ↓
            │   Hive Mother (Larva Biome)  │
            │   Skelly (Clay Biome)         │
            └── Defeat BOTH ───────────────┘
                        │
                        ↓
            │   Azeos (Wilderness)          │
            │   Omoroth (Sunken Sea)        │
            └── Defeat BOTH ───────────────┘
                        │
                        ↓
                  Defeat Maluga (Forgotten Ruins)
                        │
                        ↓
                  Defeat Ra-Akar (Desert of Beginnings)
                        │
                        ↓
            ╔═══════════════════════════╗
            ║  The Passage (Final Boss) ║
            ╚═══════════════════════════╝

docs.title: 🛡️ Gear Pro Progression Path
docs.text: Stage Armor Weapon Accessories Start Copper Copper Sword + Wooden Bow None needed After Glurch/Ghom Tin Tin Sword + Tin Bow Movement Ring After Hive/Skelly Iron Iron Sword + Iron Bow Health Ring After Azeos/Omoroth Scarlet Scarlet Sword + Scarlet Bow Swift Ring After Maluga Galaxite Galaxite Sword + Galaxite Bow Titan Ring Endgame Galaxite+ (upgraded) Galaxite+ Great Sword The Core

docs.title: 💡 Pro Tips
docs.text:
1. Build arenas! Before summoning any boss, clear the area and place campfires
  2. Food buffs stack — eat a meal, drink a potion, and apply a seasoning for triple buffs
  3. Partner selection — bring a Ranged partner (like the Hunter) for bosses, a Melee partner for clearing
  4. Sell extra boss drops — boss meat sells for good gold early game
  5. Ancient Gemstones — save these! You need 60+ for endgame gear crafting
  6. Statues matter — placing boss trophies gives permanent stat boosts in your base

Data sourced from Core Keeper 1.0 full release and gamepedia. Last updated July 2026.

docs.title: 🍳 Core Keeper — Cooking Guide & Recipe Database
docs.text: Master cooking in Core Keeper 1.0! Every recipe, ingredient, buff effect, and farming strategy covered.

docs.title: The Cauldron
docs.text:
• Craft a Cauldron at the Workbench (8 Iron Bar + 4 Copper Bar)
  • Place it in your base
  • Interact to open the cooking menu

docs.title: Cooking Mechanics
docs.text: Mechanic Detail Ingredients Combine 1-4 ingredients in the cauldron Recipes Specific combos produce specific dishes Buffs Most cooked foods give 1-2 stat buffs Duration Buffs last 5-15 minutes (real time) Stacking You can have 1 food buff + 1 potion buff + 1 seasoning active Quality Higher quality ingredients → better buff values

docs.title: Acquiring the Cauldron
docs.text:
1. Defeat Glurch and Ghom
  2. Mine Iron Ore in the Clay/Larva biomes
  3. Craft Iron Bars at the Furnace
  4. Build your Cauldron at the Workbench

docs.title: 🥇 Starter Recipes (Early Game)
docs.text: Recipe Ingredients Buffs Duration 🥣 Basic Stew Any 3 basic ingredients +10 HP regen 5 min 🥩 Grilled Meat 2 Raw Meat +15 HP, +5% Damage 5 min 🥗 Hearty Salad 2 Fibre + 1 Berry +20 HP regen, +5% Speed 5 min 🍲 Mushroom Soup 2 Glowing Mushroom + 1 Water +15 HP, +5% Defense 5 min 🐟 Grilled Fish 2 Raw Fish +20 HP, +5% Mining 5 min

docs.title: 🥈 Mid-Game Recipes
docs.text: Recipe Ingredients Buffs Duration 🥧 Fish Pie 2 Cave Guppy + 1 Wheat +50 HP, +10% Mining Speed 10 min 🍲 Hearty Stew 1 Mudfish + 1 Hearty Root + 1 Water +80 HP, +15% Max HP 10 min 🌯 Bug Stew 1 Larva Perch + 1 Larva Meat + 1 Acid Spit +50 HP, +10% Damage 10 min 🥟 Spicy Eel Roll 1 Ancient Eel + 1 Wheat + 1 Spicy Pepper +60 HP, +15% Speed 10 min 🥘 Angler Soup 1 Deep Sea Angler + 1 Coral +70 HP, +15% Mining Speed 10 min 🍣 Salmon Roll 1 Sunken Salmon + 1 Wheat +55 HP, +10% Fishing 10 min 🥗 Rainbow Stir Fry 1 Coral Trout + 1 Sky Trout + 1 Glowing Mushroom +90 HP, +10% All Stats 10 min

docs.title: 🥉 Late-Game Recipes
docs.text: Recipe Ingredients Buffs Duration 🥩 Acid Fish Steak 1 Acid Carp + 1 Pepper +100 HP, +15% Damage, +10% Crit 12 min 🥣 Storm Chowder 1 Storm Bass + 1 Lightning Crystal +120 HP, +15% Defense, +10% Max HP 12 min 🥩 Thunder Steak 1 Thunder Pike + 1 Pepper +110 HP, +15% Speed, +10% Dodge 12 min 🥣 Glowing Broth 1 Glowing Bass + 1 Glow Tulip +85 HP, +15% Light Radius, +10% Mining 12 min 🌮 Desert Stew 1 Sand Skipper + 1 Cactus Fruit +90 HP, +5% All Stats, Heat Resist 12 min 🥣 Mirage Soup 1 Mirage Pike + 1 Mirage Fragment +95 HP, +10% Luck, +10% Rare Find 12 min 🐟 Koi Sashimi 1 Oasis Koi + 1 Pepper + 1 Wheat +100 HP, +12% Speed, +10% Fishing 12 min 🥩 Glow Tuna Steak 1 Glow Tuna + 1 Seaweed +120 HP, +15% Underwater Breathing, +10% Swim Speed 15 min

docs.title: 🏆 Boss Meat Recipes (Best in Game)
docs.text: Recipe Ingredients Buffs Duration 🥩 Glurch Steak 1 Glurch Meat + 1 Pepper +70 HP, +10% Melee Damage 8 min 🥩 Ghom Delight 1 Ghom Meat + 1 Wheat +65 HP, +10% Ranged Damage 8 min 🥩 Hive Mother Roast 1 Hive Mother Meat + 1 Acid Spit +90 HP, +12% Damage, +8% Defense 10 min 🥩 Skelly Bone Broth 1 Skelly Meat + 1 Skeleton Bone +85 HP, +12% Defense, +10% Max HP 10 min 🥩 Azeos Wing Feast 1 Azeos Meat + 1 Sky Feather +130 HP, +15% Speed, +10% Dodge 12 min 🥩 Omoroth Sushi 1 Omoroth Meat + 1 Seaweed +125 HP, +15% Swim Speed, +10% Fishing 12 min 🥩 Maluga BBQ 1 Maluga Meat + 2 Peppers +150 HP, +20% Damage, +10% Crit 15 min 🥩 Ra-Akar's Feast 1 Ra-Akar Meat + 1 Void Shard +200 HP, +20% All Stats, +15% Lifesteal 15 min

docs.title: Crops for Cooking
docs.text: Crop Grows In Growth Time Used In 🌾 Wheat Dirt Biome 5 min Fish Pie, Spicy Eel Roll, Salmon Roll, Koi Sashimi 🌶️ Spicy Pepper Desert 15 min Spicy Eel Roll, Acid Fish Steak, Thunder Steak, and many more 🌵 Cactus Fruit Desert 12 min Desert Stew 🌸 Glow Tulip Larva Biome 10 min Glowing Broth 🍄 Glowing Mushroom Foraged — Mushroom Soup, Rainbow Stir Fry 💜 Hearty Root Foraged (Clay) — Hearty Stew

docs.title: Animal Products
docs.text: Product Source Used In 🥚 Egg Chickens Various baked dishes 🥛 Milk Cows Creamy soups 🧀 Cheese Milk → Processor Specialty Recipes

docs.title: Best Foods by Activity
docs.text: Activity Best Food Buffs ⛏️ Mining Angler Soup +15% Mining Speed, +70 HP ⚔️ Boss Fighting Ra-Akar's Feast or Maluga BBQ +20% All Stats or +20% Damage 🏃 Exploring Thunder Steak +15% Speed, +10% Dodge 🎣 Fishing Koi Sashimi +12% Speed, +10% Fishing 🛡️ Survival Storm Chowder +15% Defense, +10% Max HP 🔦 Dark Areas Glowing Broth +15% Light Radius, +10% Mining 🌊 Underwater Glow Tuna Steak +15% Underwater Breathing, +10% Swim Speed

docs.title: Buff Stacking Priority
docs.text:
🥇 Top Priority: Boss Meat Dishes (best stats)
🥈 Second: Late-Game Fish Dishes (great stats + good buffs)
🥉 Third: Mid-Game Recipes (decent, cheaper ingredients)

Note: You can have food buff + potion buff + seasoning buff simultaneously. Always stack!

docs.title: 🧪 Advanced Cooking Tips
docs.text:
1. Don't sell boss meat! Boss meat dishes are far more valuable than selling the raw meat
  2. Grow peppers — Spicy Pepper is the most versatile cooking ingredient (used in 8+ recipes)
  3. Quality matters — higher farming skill produces higher quality crops = better buffs
  4. Fish traps + cooking = infinite gold — catch fish, cook them, sell the cooked version
  5. The Kitchen Counter (late game upgrade) lets you cook 4 dishes at once
  6. Refrigerator (late game) extends food buff duration by 50%
  7. Seasons — in 1.0, placing salt, pepper, or sugar on a dish enhances its buffs

Data sourced from Core Keeper 1.0 full release and gamepedia. Ingredients and buff values tested in-game. Last updated July 2026.

docs.title: 🌱 Core Keeper — Crop & Farming Database
docs.text:
Your complete reference for Core Keeper farming. All data is based on the 1.0 full release and the game's gardening skill mechanics.

Quick Navigation: - Crop Profit Table (All Biomes) - Farming by Biome - Cooking & Crop Processing - Gardening Skill Guide - Sprinklers & Automation

docs.title: 🧮 How Farming Works in Core Keeper
docs.text:
Core Keeper uses a real-time growth system (not day-based like Stardew). Seeds grow in minutes of real time, and crops need:

  1. Tilled soil — use a Hoe on dirt or ground
  2. Water — use a Watering Can or sprinklers (water once per growth cycle)
  3. Light — torches or glow tulips nearby (optional but speeds growth)

Growth formula: Base growth time × (1 − Water bonus − Light bonus). With water and light, crops grow ~40% faster.

docs.title: 🌾 Crop Profit Table
docs.text:
Below is every crop in Core Keeper ranked by Profit Per Minute (PPM) — the gold standard for deciding what to plant.

Crop Biome Seed Cost Growth (min) Regrows Harvest Yield Sell Price Profit PPM Notes 🌶️ King Pepper Rare (Larva) 0g* 12 No 1–3 100g ~200g avg 16.7g Rare drop, huge value 🍍 Pineapple Desert 40g 15 No 1 120g 80g 5.3g Best desert crop 🎃 Pumpkin Meadow 30g 10 No 1 80g 50g 5.0g Great early crop 🥕 Carrot Dirt 8g 2 No 1 18g 10g 5.0g Starting crop 🌶️ Fire Pepper Forlorn Metropolis 50g 14 No 1–2 110g 95g avg 6.8g Endgame staple 🍇 Grape Desert 25g 7 No 1 60g 35g 5.0g Good mid-game 🌾 Wheat Clay 10g 5 No 1 24g 14g 2.8g Used for cooking 🌶️ Pepper Clay 12g 3 No 1 24g 12g 4.0g Decent early cash 🌿 Cotton Meadow 10g 5 No 1 20g 10g 2.0g Needed for armour ☕ Coffee Sunken Sea 20g 6 Yes (4min) 1–2 beans 12g bean — Varies Buff item, not for profit 🌾 Rice Sunken Sea 15g 4 No 1 30g 15g 3.8g Good underwater crop 🌸 Lotus Sunken Sea 20g 8 No 1 45g 25g 3.1g Deco + crafting 🌺 Seaflower Sunken Sea 18g 6 No 1 36g 18g 3.0g Crafting material 🍄 Giant Mushroom Mold 15g 5 No 1–2 30g 20g avg 4.0g Good mold crop 💎 Amber Pumpkin Rare (Meadow) 0g* 14 No 1 200g 200g 14.3g Random pumpkin mutation 🧊 Crystalized Water Crystal 0g* 8 No 1 80g 80g 10.0g Harvest from crystal nodes

*Special seeds: King Pepper drops from Larva Hive, Amber Pumpkin is a random mutation, Crystalized Water is mined.

💡 Top Profit Crops by Stage: - Early (Dirt): Carrots → fast cash, 5.0 PPM - Mid (Clay/Meadow): Pumpkin (5.0 PPM) + Peppers (4.0 PPM) - Late (Desert): Pineapple (5.3 PPM) + Grapes (5.0 PPM) - Endgame (Metropolis): Fire Pepper (6.8 PPM) - Jackpot Drops: Amber Pumpkin (14.3 PPM), King Pepper (16.7 PPM)

docs.title: 🟫 Dirt Biome (Starting Area)
docs.text: Seed Source What You Get Strategy Break grass Carrot Seeds Plant immediately for food + cash Slime drops Mixed seeds Save for later biomes

docs.title: 🟥 Clay Biome
docs.text: Accessible after defeating Glurch. Clay soil is ideal for: - Wheat — needed for Bread (best early healing food) - Pepper — decent profit, good for Spicy Cuisine

docs.title: 🟩 Meadow / Forgotten Ruins
docs.text: After Ghorm is defeated: - Pumpkin — your best mid-game cash crop - Cotton — needed for armour progression

docs.title: 🟦 Sunken Sea (Underwater Farming)
docs.text: Requires a Diving Set or underwater base. Crops here grow well with natural water: - Rice — grows fast, good food source - Coffee — infinite harvests, great for speed buffs - Lotus / Seaflower — crafting materials

docs.title: 🟧 Desert of Beginnings
docs.text:
• Pineapple — highest base profit crop
  • Grape — good profit, fast growth

docs.title: 🔥 Forlorn Metropolis (Endgame)
docs.text:
• Fire Pepper — best profit per minute in the game
  • Requires Galaxite-tier tools to survive this biome

docs.title: 🍳 Cooking & Crop Processing
docs.text:
Farming isn't just about selling raw crops — cooking dramatically increases their value.

Recipe Ingredients Sell Price Value Add Buff 🥗 Basic Salad Any 2 vegetables 60g 2x raw value +HP regen 🍞 Bread 3 Wheat 72g 3x raw value +Max HP 🌮 Spicy Wrap 2 Pepper + 1 Wheat 85g ~2x raw +Mining Speed 🍍 Pineapple Juice 3 Pineapple 240g 2x raw +Movement Speed 🥧 Pumpkin Pie 2 Pumpkin + 3 Wheat 200g 2.5x raw +Defence ☕ Coffee 5 Coffee Beans 60g 1x raw +Move Speed (short)

💡 Cooking Tip: Processing crops through cooking at least doubles their value. Always cook before selling!

docs.title: 🌿 Gardening Skill Guide
docs.text:
Leveling your Gardening skill unlocks major farming bonuses:

Level Unlock Effect 1 Basic Hoe Start tilling 2 Watering Can (Copper) Water 3 tiles at once 5 Crop Quality Chance Base 10% chance of +1 yield 8 Sprinkler (Tin) Automates watering 12 Watering Can (Iron) Water 5 tiles at once 15 Double Harvest Chance 15% chance of double yield 18 Sprinkler (Gold) Larger range 22 Watering Can (Gold) Water 7 tiles at once 25 Master Farmer +25% growth speed, +25% yield

Fast leveling method: Plant Carrots (fastest growth) in bulk, harvest, replant. Each harvest gives 10–30 XP depending on yield.

docs.title: 💧 Sprinklers & Automation
docs.text:
Sprinkler Type Materials Range Water per Cycle 🔵 Tin Sprinkler 10 Tin Bars, 5 Fibre 3×3 (1 tile radius) 1 water 🟡 Gold Sprinkler 10 Gold Bars, 5 Copper, 3 Fibre 5×5 (2 tile radius) 1 water 🟣 Scarlett Sprinkler 8 Scarlett Bars, 10 Gold, 5 Fibre 7×7 (3 tile radius) 1 water

Automation tip: Connect sprinklers to a water source via canals or place them on tilled soil. One watering per growth cycle is all a crop needs. Pair with conveyor belts for auto-harvest collection.

docs.title: 📊 Profit Strategy Summary
docs.text: Play Style Recommended Crops Why 🏃 Speed Runner Carrots → Pumpkins → Pineapples → Fire Peppers Max PPM progression 🍳 Chef Wheat, Pepper, Pumpkin Cooking gives 2–3x value 🎨 Crafter Cotton, Lotus, Seaflower Needed for armour/decorations 🤖 Automator Any regrowable + Sprinklers Set-and-forget income

docs.title: 🐟 Core Keeper — Fishing Guide & Fish Database
docs.text: Your complete reference for fishing in Core Keeper 1.0 — from unlocking your first rod to catching every legendary fish.

docs.title: 🛠️ Unlocking Fishing
docs.text:
1. Defeat Glurch or Ghom (first two bosses)
  2. Collect Fibre from grass plants
  3. Craft a Basic Fishing Rod at the Workbench (8 Fibre + 6 Wood)
  4. Find any water pool — press and hold left-click to cast, release when the bobber splashes!

docs.title: 📈 Fishing Skill
docs.text:
• Level up by catching fish
  • Higher level = better catch quality + rare fish chances
  • Skill rewards: faster casting, less reel time, rare fish detection

docs.title: ⏰ Time & Location
docs.text:
• Fishing takes real time — no day/night cycle
  • Different biomes = different fish pools
  • Deeper/larger water pools = better fish
  • Fish Traps (crafted later) provide passive fish income

docs.title: 💰 Best Fish Traps Setup
docs.text:
Fish traps work passively — place them in water and collect later.

Trap Location Best Fish Passive Income/Day Sunken Sea (deep) Glow Tuna (65g) ~130g Azeos' Wilderness Storm Bass (42g) ~84g Larva Biome Glowing Bass (45g) ~90g Dirt Biome Crystal Fish (25g) ~50g

Tip: Place 5+ traps in the Sunken Sea for maximum passive income. Check them every 20-30 minutes.

docs.title: 🍳 Best Fish for Cooking
docs.text:
Some fish are worth more cooked than raw. Here's the priority list:

Fish Raw Price Best Dish Cooked Value Profit Over Raw 🐟 Glow Tuna 65g Glow Tuna Steak 120g + Breathing Buff +55g 🐟 Mudfish 12g Hearty Stew 35g + HP Buff +23g 🐟 Deep Sea Angler 40g Angler Soup 75g + Mining Buff +35g 🐟 Thunder Pike 60g Thunder Steak 110g + Speed Buff +50g 🐟 Storm Bass 42g Storm Chowder 80g + Defense Buff +38g 🐟 Glowing Bass 45g Glowing Broth 85g + Light Buff +40g 🐟 Mirage Pike 50g Mirage Soup 95g + Luck Buff +45g

docs.text: Trap Type Materials Catch Rate Notes Basic Fish Trap 25 Fibre + 12 Wood Low Crafted at Workbench Sturdy Fish Trap 20 Fibre + 8 Iron Bar Medium Crafted at Iron Anvil Advanced Fish Trap 15 Fibre + 6 Galaxite Bar High Faster collection

docs.title: Placement Tips
docs.text:
1. Deep water produces better fish than shallow puddles
  2. Large water bodies attract rarer fish
  3. Place traps away from each other (cannibalize catch rates)
  4. Check traps every 20-30 minutes — they stop catching when full
  5. The Sunken Sea has the best trap ROI

docs.title: 🏆 Fishing Achievements
docs.text: Achievement Requirement Reward 🎣 Novice Angler Catch 10 fish 50 XP 🎣 Expert Angler Catch 100 fish 200 XP + Fishing Hat 🎣 Master Angler Catch 500 fish 500 XP + Golden Fishing Rod 🎣 Legendary Angler Catch all legendary fish 1000 XP + Fisher's Crown 🎣 Completionist Every fish species Fishing skill max + trophy

docs.title: 🛠️ Fishing Rods Comparison
docs.text: Rod Materials Cast Range Reel Speed Rare Fish Bonus 🪵 Basic Rod 8 Fibre, 6 Wood Short Slow None 🔧 Copper Rod 5 Copper Bar, 4 Fibre Medium Medium +5% ⛓️ Iron Rod 5 Iron Bar, 4 Fibre Long Fast +10% 💎 Scarlet Rod 5 Scarlet Bar, 4 Silk Very Long Fast +15% ✨ Galaxite Rod 5 Galaxite Bar, 4 Silk Maximum Very Fast +20%

docs.title: 💡 Pro Tips
docs.text:
1. Upgrade your rod first — a better rod pays for itself in rare fish
  2. Cook your fish — many fish are worth 2-3x more as cooked dishes
  3. Fish traps are OP — 5 traps generate more gold than most crops
  4. The Sunken Sea is the best fishing zone — highest value fish + Glow Tuna for breathing buff
  5. Legendary fish require high fishing skill + upgraded rod — save them for the museum!
  6. Sell duplicates — you only need one of each fish for museum/achievement
  7. Fish during exploration — every biome has unique fish, so fish as you explore

Data sourced from Core Keeper 1.0 full release, gamepedia, and player testing. Prices may vary slightly with updates. Last updated July 2026.

docs.title: 🆕 Core Keeper — Getting Started Guide for Beginners
docs.text: Welcome, Explorer! This guide walks you through your first hours in Core Keeper — from waking up in the underground to defeating your first boss.

docs.title: 🥇 First 10 Minutes: What to Do
docs.text: When you first spawn, you're in a small cave with nothing but a broken pickaxe and a glowing core.

docs.title: 1️⃣ Gather Basics
docs.text:
• Punch trees (the glowing mushrooms/crystals) for wood
  • Pick up stone from the ground
  • Collect fibre from ground plants
  • Open your starter chest for a basic hoe and watering can

docs.title: 2️⃣ Build Your First Workbench
docs.text:
• Press Tab to open your inventory
  • Craft a Workbench (10 wood)
  • Place it with right-click

docs.title: 3️⃣ Craft Essential Tools
docs.text: At your workbench, craft: | Tool | Materials | Use | |:-----|:----------|:----| | 🪓 Wooden Pickaxe | 5 wood, 3 fibre | Mine copper/tin | | 🗡️ Copper Dagger | 5 copper bars | Basic melee weapon | | 🔦 Torch | 1 wood, 3 fibre | Light your way | | 🪣 Basic Hoe | 5 wood, 3 fibre | Till soil for farming | | 🛡️ Wooden Shield | 10 wood | Block attacks |

docs.title: 4️⃣ Explore and Light Up
docs.text:
• Place torches as you explore — monsters spawn in the dark
  • Don't wander too far from the Core (your spawn point)
  • Follow the dirt paths to find the first biome exits

docs.title: Where to Build
docs.text: Build your base near the Core — it's your fast-travel hub and safe zone. The area around it is monster-free.

docs.title: Essential Structures
docs.text:
Structure Materials Why You Need It 🛠️ Workbench 10 wood All early crafting 🔥 Campfire 10 wood, 5 fibre Cook food, light ⛏️ Furnace 10 copper bars Smelt copper/tin 🗄️ Chest 10 wood Store items 🛏️ Bed 15 wood, 10 fibre Set spawn point

💡 Pro Tip: Place a Bed near the Core. But don't place it inside the Core's bubble — put it just outside so you can spawn closer to your base.

docs.title: 🌱 Getting Started with Farming
docs.text:
Farming is one of the most important skills in Core Keeper. Here's how to start:

  1. Craft a Hoe at the workbench
  2. Till soil anywhere with right-click
  3. Plant seeds you find from breaking grass or slimes
  4. Water regularly — crops need one watering and then grow on their own
  5. Harvest when fully grown (they glow slightly)

Your first seeds come from: - Breaking grass in the Dirt Biome → Carrot Seeds - Killing slimes → random seeds - Opening chests in the ruins

See our 🌱 Crop & Farming Guide for complete crop data and profit tables.

docs.title: ⚔️ Combat Basics
docs.text: Mechanic How It Works Melee Attack Left-click with weapon equipped Block Right-click with a shield equipped Dodge Roll Double-tap a movement direction Ranged Use a bow or slingshot from distance Healing Eat food or use bandages (crafted from fibre)

docs.title: Early Combat Tips
docs.text:
• Kite enemies — hit, move back, repeat
  • Always carry food — passive health regen is tied to being well-fed
  • Prioritize Glurch (first boss) → craft better gear
  • Avoid larva spit — it poisons you

docs.title: 🏆 Boss Progression Path
docs.text: Boss Biome Recommended Gear Key Reward 🟢 Glurch the Abominous Mass Dirt Copper tools, cooked food Glurch's Heart (Statue) 🟠 Ghorm the Devourer Dirt/Clay Iron gear, ranged weapon Ghorm's Horn (upgrade) 🔵 King Slime Clay Iron+ gear King Slime Crown 🟣 Ivy the Poisonous Mass Forgotten Ruins Gold gear, antidotes Ivy's Roots ⚪ Omoroth the Sea Titan Sunken Sea Octarine gear, diving set Omoroth's Heart 🟤 Rah-Ket the Desert Titan Desert Platinum gear Rah-Ket's Horn 🟢 Malugaz the Corrupted Mold Scarlett gear Malugaz's Skull 🔴 Hive Mind Forlorn Metropolis Galaxite gear Hive Mind Core

docs.title: 🎯 10 Pro Tips for New Players
docs.text:
1. Build a hoe immediately — wild seeds are free food
  2. Don't sell early ore — you'll need it all for upgrades
  3. Always carry torches — darkness = monster spawns
  4. Cook your food — cooked meals give better buffs
  5. Invest in movement speed — boots and food buffs save time
  6. Build a moat around your base for early defence
  7. Use conveyor belts for auto-sorting once you have tin
  8. Fishing is OP — fish give great healing and buffs
  9. Explore in one direction — the map is infinite but biomes are in sequence
  10. Keep the Core upgraded — it unlocks fast travel and vendor access

docs.title: 🌲 Don't Starve Together — Farming & Survival Guide
docs.text: Don't Starve Together (DST) is a multiplayer survival game by Klei Entertainment. Set in a dark, whimsical wilderness, it challenges you to manage hunger, sanity, and health while exploring, crafting, and building a base. The 2022 gardening overhaul added one of the deepest farming systems in any survival game.

docs.title: 🎮 Quick Facts
docs.text:
Feature Detail Developer Klei Entertainment Release Dec 15, 2014 (Steam Early Access) / Full release 2016 Genre Survival / Open World / Crafting Players 1–6 (online co-op) Platforms PC, PS4, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch Price ~$14.99 (Steam)

Steam: store.steampowered.com/app/322330

docs.title: 📖 Guides on This Site
docs.text: Guide Description 🌱 Farming & Gardening Guide Complete crop database, giant crops, nutrient system, seasonal planting, and fertiliser guide

docs.title: 🔗 External Resources
docs.text:
• Don't Starve Wiki
  • DST Official Website
  • Klei Forums

docs.title: 🌱 Don't Starve Together — Complete Farming & Gardening Guide
docs.text:
This guide covers the Gardening system introduced in the 2022 QoL update.

Farming in Don't Starve Together underwent a major overhaul in 2022. Gone are the simple "plant and wait" days — the new system adds soil nutrients, crop families, plant stress, and giant crops as rewards for optimal care.

docs.title: The 3 Core Resources
docs.text:
Every crop you plant consumes and produces three nutrients:

Resource Effect Depleted By 🟤 Compost (Grey soil) General growth nutrient Leafy crops 🔵 Manure (Manure pile) Root development Root crops 🔴 Growth Formula (Bubbles) Stem/fruit growth Fruiting crops

Think of it like this: Each crop family depletes its own nutrient type while replenishing the other two.

docs.title: Crop Families
docs.text: Family Examples Consumes Produces 🥬 Leafy Cabbage, Kale, Onion 🟤 Compost 🔵 Manure + 🔴 Growth Formula 🥕 Root Carrot, Potato, Radish 🔵 Manure 🟤 Compost + 🔴 Growth Formula 🌽 Fruiting Corn, Tomato, Eggplant, Pumpkin 🔴 Growth Formula 🟤 Compost + 🔵 Manure

docs.title: Family Rotation Strategy
docs.text:
The key to healthy soil is rotating crop families:

Season 1: Plant Leafy → Season 2: Plant Root → Season 3: Plant Fruiting

This rotation keeps all nutrients balanced without needing fertiliser.

docs.title: 🌾 All Crops Database
docs.text:
Crop Season Growth Time Family Use 🥕 Carrot Any 10 days Root Food, bait, Crock Pot 🌾 Corn Summer/Autumn 12 days Fruiting Crock Pot, feedingbirds 🥬 Cabbage Any 10 days Leafy Crock Pot (Pierogi, Waffles) 🍆 Eggplant Any 12 days Fruiting Crock Pot (Stuffed Eggplant) 🌿 Kale Any 8 days Leafy Crock Pot (Banana Pop) 🧅 Onion Any 10 days Leafy Crock Pot (Moqueca, Cream of Mushroom) 🌶️ Pepper Summer/Autumn 10 days Fruiting Crock Pot (Spicy Chili) 🥔 Potato Any 10 days Root Crock Pot (Knuckle Sandwich) 🎃 Pumpkin Any 12 days Fruiting Crock Pot, hat, lantern 🌱 Radish Any 8 days Root Crock Pot (Salsa Fresca) 🍅 Tomato Summer/Autumn 10 days Fruiting Crock Pot (spaghetti, salsa) 🌿 Garlic Any 10 days Root Crock Pot (Garlic Mashed Potatoes) 🌻 Sunflower Any 10 days Leafy Seeds (Snack) 🫘 Asparagus Spring/Summer 10 days Fruiting Crock Pot (Asparagus Soup)

Data source: Don't Starve Wiki — Farming

docs.title: 🌟 Giant Crops
docs.text: When you plant 4+ of the same crop in a 3×3 grid and meet all conditions perfectly, crops can merge into a Giant Crop — yielding 3–6 regular crops when harvested with a Pitchfork.

docs.title: Giant Crop Requirements
docs.text: Crop Nutrient Balance Water Requirement Family Neighbours 🥬 Giant Cabbage Balanced or high Compost Fully watered ≥4 Leafy nearby 🌽 Giant Corn Balanced or high Growth Fully watered ≥4 Fruiting nearby 🥕 Giant Carrot Balanced or high Manure Fully watered ≥4 Root nearby 🍆 Giant Eggplant Balanced or high Growth Fully watered ≥4 Fruiting nearby 🌶️ Giant Pepper Balanced or high Growth Fully watered ≥4 Fruiting nearby 🥔 Giant Potato Balanced or high Manure Fully watered ≥4 Root nearby 🎃 Giant Pumpkin Balanced or high Growth Fully watered ≥4 Fruiting nearby 🍅 Giant Tomato Balanced or high Growth Fully watered ≥4 Fruiting nearby 🧅 Giant Onion Balanced or high Compost Fully watered ≥4 Leafy nearby 🌿 Giant Garlic Balanced or high Manure Fully watered ≥4 Root nearby

docs.title: How to Guarantee Giant Crops (Step by Step)
docs.text:
1. Plant 9 seeds of the same crop in a 3×3 grid
  2. Tend each plant twice — once mid-growth, once near harvest
  3. Water every day if no rain
  4. Use a Gardeneer Hat to see plant health indicators
  5. Check Family Pressure — keep 4+ of the same family in range
  6. Harvest with a Pitchfork for 3–6x yield

💡 Pro Tip: Giant crops do NOT spoil when left on the ground. You can leave them as emergency food reserves!

docs.title: Spring
docs.text: Best Crops Reason 🥬 Cabbage Rain reduces watering need 🥕 Carrot Good all-purpose 🌱 Radish Fast growth (8 days) 🫘 Asparagus Seasonal favourite

docs.title: Summer
docs.text: Best Crops Reason 🌽 Corn Summer-optimised 🍅 Tomato Summer-optimised 🌶️ Pepper Summer-optimised 🌻 Sunflower Decoration + seeds

docs.title: Autumn
docs.text: Best Crops Reason 🎃 Pumpkin Autumn-optimised (good for lanterns) 🌽 Corn Autumn-optimised 🌶️ Pepper Autumn-optimised 🥬 Cabbage Great for Pierogi (healing)

docs.title: Winter
docs.text:
Best Crops Reason 🥕 Carrot Can grow in winter 🥔 Potato Can grow in winter 🥬 Cabbage Can grow in winter 🧅 Onion Can grow in winter

⚠️ Winter Note: Crops grow 2× slower in winter unless protected by a Greenhouse or insulating structures. Plant indoors or near a heat source.

docs.title: Fertiliser Types
docs.text: Item Compost Manure Growth Formula Source 💩 Manure 0 +16 +2 From beefalo/koalefant 🌿 Rot +8 0 0 Spoiled food 🌺 Guano 0 +12 +4 From birds/pigs 🧪 Growth Formula Starter +8 +8 +8 Crafted (nitre + ash + rot) 🚫 Compost Wrap +24 0 0 Crafted (papyrus + rot + manure) 🪱 Mushroom Planter Used for mushrooms — — Underground

docs.title: Soil Nutrient Reader
docs.text: Craft a Gardeneer Hat (3 × Silk, 1 × Grass Suit, 1 × Any Crop Seed) to see: - Current nutrient values for each plot - Plant health indicators (happy, neutral, stressed) - Growth stage progress - Moisture level

docs.title: Bee Farm Integration
docs.text:
• Place Bee Boxes near your farm
  • Bees pollinate crops, increasing growth speed
  • Crops within 15 tiles of 4+ bee boxes grow ~15% faster

docs.title: Thresher + Brimstone Trap Automation
docs.text: Advanced players can: 1. Plant crops inside a walled pen 2. Use Brimstone Traps (from Wagstaff's skill tree) for early warning 3. Let Pigs/Bunnymen tend crops (they reduce stress)

docs.title: 🍳 Best Crop Recipes
docs.text: Recipe Ingredients Health Hunger Why It's Great 🥟 Pierogi Cabbage + Egg + Fish + Filler +40 HP +37.5 Best healing food 🧇 Waffles Egg + Butter + Berries + Filler +60 HP +37.5 Massive heal 🥣 Banana Pop Kale + Banana + Filler +20 HP +12.5 Easy sanity 🌮 Salsa Fresca Tomato + Radish + Filler 0 +12.5 Spring staple 🥧 Pumpkin Cookies Pumpkin + Honey + Egg 0 +18.8 Good filler 🥗 Spicy Chili Pepper + Veggie + Meat +5 HP +37.5 Warmth in winter 🥔 Knuckle Sandwich Potato + Meat + Ice + Filler +37.5 HP +37.5 Balanced meal

docs.title: 🔟 Farming Tips Summary
docs.text:
1. Rotate families — Leafy → Root → Fruiting avoids fertiliser dependency
  2. Plant 9 of the same crop in a 3×3 grid for giant crop potential
  3. Talk to your plants — hovering near them with the Gardeneer Hat on reduces stress
  4. Use Rain Collectors — rain provides free watering
  5. Store Giant Crops — they never spoil when left in the ground
  6. Bee Boxes nearby — free pollination boost
  7. Don't overcrowd — keep ≥4 tiles of spacing for other plant families
  8. Craft Gardeneer Hat first — you cannot see nutrient values without it
  9. Plant season-appropriate crops — they grow faster and with less stress
  10. Grow Cabbage + Tomato — Pierogi + Salsa Fresca covers HP and hunger basics

Feed Composition Production Method Used By Grass Fresh cut Mow meadow Sheep, Horses, Beef Cows Hay Dried grass Tedder → Windrow → Bale Cows (winter), Sheep Silage Fermented grass/corn Forage harvester → Silo Cows (TMR ingredient) TMR Silage 50% + Hay 30% + Straw 20% Mixer Wagon ($18K) Dairy Cows (max yield) Grain Wheat/Barley/Oats Harvest → Silo Chickens, Pigs, Horses Root Crops Potatoes, Beets Harvest → Root Silo Pigs only

TMR (Total Mixed Ration) boosts dairy cow milk yield by +40% over hay. Prioritize a Mixer Wagon once you have multiple cows.

Tip: The Robotic Milker enables a 50+ cow dairy run solo. Without it, large dairies are unmanageable. Buy auto-feeders before the milker.

docs.title: 🌿 Pasture Rotation & Winter Prep
docs.text:
Grass regrowth stages: Short (1d, 1,000L) → Medium (3d, 2,500L) → Long (5d, 4,000L) → Flowering (7d, 5,500L max).

4-paddock rotation: Graze 1 paddock for 3 days, rotate. By day 12, paddock 1 regrows to Stage 3+. Mow excess in summer for winter hay/silage.

Winter: Grass stops Nov–Feb. A 50-cow herd needs 720,000 L of stored feed for 4 winter months. Stockpile hay bales in summer.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
1. Chickens first — fastest ROI, learn feed mechanics cheaply
  2. TMR for dairy — 40% yield boost over hay alone
  3. Pigs are fertilizer makers — their value is in the manure cycle, not direct sales
  4. Auto-feeder before milker — feeding is the bigger time sink
  5. Clean barns = healthy animals — dirty barns lose 25% production immediately
  6. Sell wool at seasonal peaks — prices fluctuate like grain

docs.title: 📋 Farming Simulator 25 Contracts Guide
docs.text: Contracts are the fastest way to earn money in the early game and a reliable supplementary income throughout your entire Farming Simulator 25 career. By completing jobs for NPC farmers, you can earn thousands of dollars per contract, use borrowed equipment for free, and learn new farming skills without risking your own capital. This guide covers everything about contracts: which ones pay best, how to use AI workers effectively, and how to build a contract-first strategy that launches your farm.

docs.title: 🔍 Contract System Overview
docs.text: Contracts appear on the map as blue icons. Accept them from the Contracts menu (accessible from the pause menu or by pressing P).

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Contracts appear daily — New contracts generate each in-game day based on what NPC farmers need
  2. Accept from the menu — Open the Contracts menu, review the details, and accept
  3. Borrowed equipment — Most contracts provide free use of the required equipment. It spawns at the field
  4. Payment structure — Complete the work → return borrowed equipment → payment is deposited
  5. Multiple contracts — You can accept multiple contracts simultaneously if you have the time

docs.title: Payment Breakdown
docs.text:
Payment Component Description Base pay Guaranteed amount for completing the work Bonus Extra pay for doing a good job (e.g., minimal damage, full coverage) Harvest yield For harvest contracts, you may keep a portion of the crop or sell it for extra Field worker bonus Completing contracts for the same NPC repeatedly builds a reputation bonus

Tip: You can keep the crop from harvest contracts! Instead of selling the harvested grain immediately, store it in your silo and sell it when prices are high. This effectively doubles the contract payout.

docs.title: 📊 Contract Types
docs.text:
Not all contracts are created equal. Some pay dramatically better per hour than others.

Contract Type Avg Payout Time Needed Hourly Rate Difficulty Equipment Provided Verdict 🌾 Harvest (small field) $3K–$5K 10–15 min $12K–$20K/hr Easy ✅ Harvester + trailer ⭐⭐⭐ Good starter 🌾 Harvest (medium field) $8K–$15K 20–40 min $15K–$22K/hr Easy ✅ Harvester + trailer ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Excellent 🌾 Harvest (large field) $20K–$40K 60–90 min $15K–$26K/hr Easy ✅ Harvester + trailer ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Best earner 🚜 Cultivating $2K–$8K 15–30 min $6K–$10K/hr Easy ✅ Tractor + cultivator ⭐⭐ Decent 🌱 Seeding / Planting $2.5K–$10K 15–40 min $8K–$14K/hr Easy ✅ Tractor + seeder ⭐⭐⭐ Solid 🧑‍🌾 Fertilizing $2K–$6K 10–25 min $8K–$14K/hr Easy ✅ Spreader/sprayer ⭐⭐⭐ Quick money 🧹 Weeding / Spraying $2K–$6K 10–25 min $8K–$14K/hr Easy ✅ Weeder/sprayer ⭐⭐⭐ Fast & easy 🌿 Lime Spreading $2K–$5K 10–20 min $6K–$15K/hr Easy ✅ Lime spreader ⭐⭐⭐ Good filler 📦 Baling $3K–$12K 20–45 min $6K–$16K/hr Medium ✅ Baler + wrapper ⭐⭐⭐ Good if you enjoy it 🥔 Root crop harvest $10K–$25K 30–60 min $10K–$25K/hr Medium ✅ Specialized harvester ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Very profitable 🪵 Forestry (small) $5K–$10K 15–30 min $10K–$20K/hr Hard ✅ Chainsaw + trailer ⭐⭐ Skill required 🪵 Forestry (large) $20K–$50K 60–120 min $10K–$25K/hr Hard ✅ Full forestry fleet ⭐⭐⭐ High pay, high effort 🚛 Transport $1.5K–$4K 5–10 min $12K–$24K/hr Very Easy ✅ Trailer ⭐⭐ Quick but rare

docs.title: By Profit Per Hour
docs.text:
1. Large field harvest ($15K–$26K/hr) — The undisputed king. Fast, simple, high-paying.
  2. Root crop harvest ($10K–$25K/hr) — Potato and sugar beet contracts pay extremely well but require slower equipment.
  3. Transport ($12K–$24K/hr) — If you can find one, it's quick easy money, but they're less common.
  4. Harvest (medium) ($15K–$22K/hr) — Very reliable income throughout the game.
  5. Forestry ($10K–$25K/hr) — High variance based on your skill with logging equipment.

docs.title: By Total Payout
docs.text:
1. Large field harvest — Up to $40K per contract
  2. Large forestry — Up to $50K per contract
  3. Root crop harvest — Up to $25K per contract
  4. Baling — Up to $12K per contract
  5. Seeding — Up to $10K per contract

Tip: The best strategy is to accept every harvest contract you see. They pay the most per hour, the equipment is provided, and you get to keep the harvested crop. Even if you have to work late into the night, the payout is worth it.

docs.title: 🔧 Borrowed Equipment vs Your Own
docs.text: One of the trickiest decisions is when to use the borrowed equipment provided by the contract versus when to use your own.

docs.title: When to Use Borrowed Equipment
docs.text: Situation Recommendation First week of the game ✅ Always use borrowed — you don't own suitable equipment yet Contract far from your farm ✅ Use borrowed — it spawns at the field, no driving Specialized equipment you don't own ✅ Use borrowed — root crop harvesters are expensive Multiple contracts in a row ⚠️ Consider your own if fields are near each other Your own equipment is broken ✅ Use borrowed to avoid repair costs

docs.title: When to Use Your Own Equipment
docs.text: Situation Recommendation Your equipment is already at the field ✅ Faster than switching to borrowed You own a bigger/better version ✅ Bigger headers = faster work = more contracts per day You've customized your tractor ✅ No need to readjust to borrowed controls You want to keep the full harvest ⚠️ Borrowed equipment's harvest goes to the NPC (partially)

docs.title: The Dual-Use Trick
docs.text:
Here's one of the most powerful early-game strategies:

  1. Accept a harvest contract
  2. Use the borrowed harvester to harvest the contract field
  3. Use the borrowed harvester on your own fields before returning it
  4. Return the equipment and collect the contract payment

This effectively gives you free use of expensive equipment that would cost thousands per day to rent normally.

Tip: The borrowed equipment is free to use until you return it. Milk it for everything it's worth — harvest your own fields, do other jobs, and only return it when the AI starts bothering you about it.

docs.title: 🤖 AI Workers and Helpers
docs.text: Farming Simulator 25's AI worker system lets you hire helpers to automate fieldwork. Using AI workers effectively is the key to scaling your operation beyond what one person can manage.

docs.title: How to Hire AI Workers
docs.text: Action Key / Method Hire worker Press H while in a vehicle on a field Open AI menu Press Esc → AI tab, or use the AI screen Create job Select field, operation type, tool, and vehicle Set up multiple workers AI menu lets you assign workers to different fields Worker cost Hourly wage (varies by operation, typically ~$1K–$2K/hr)

docs.title: AI Worker Cost Table
docs.text: Operation AI Wage (per hour) Notes Cultivating ~$1,200/hr Cheap, straightforward Seeding ~$1,200/hr Same as cultivating Fertilizing ~$1,000/hr Fast operation = lower total cost Harvesting ~$2,000/hr Higher cost, but earns the most Lime spreading ~$1,000/hr Quick job Weeding ~$1,000/hr Fast for mechanical weeding

docs.title: AI Worker Strategy for Contracts
docs.text:
1. Hire AI for wide, simple operations — Cultivating, fertilizing, and lime spreading on large rectangular fields. AI workers handle these perfectly.
  2. Handle complex operations yourself — Harvesting irregular fields, forestry, and baling where AI workers get confused.
  3. Use multiple AI workers — On large fields, hire 2–3 workers working in parallel. Each worker covers a section.
  4. Check on workers — AI workers can get stuck on trees, fences, or uneven terrain. Check the AI screen periodically.
  5. Work while AI works — While an AI worker handles a contract, do another contract in a different vehicle. Double your income rate.

Tip: AI workers are especially powerful for fertilizing contracts. They're cheap, fast, and fertilizing is one of the simplest AI operations. You can accept 3–4 fertilizing contracts, hire AI workers for all of them, and collect the payouts with almost no effort.

docs.title: Common AI Worker Problems
docs.text: Problem Solution Worker stopped Check for obstacles (tree, sign, vehicle). Move obstacle and rehire. Worker missed a strip The AI isn't 100% perfect. You may need to fill in missed spots manually. Worker driving slowly Check the vehicle — might be low on fuel or the tool isn't lowered. Worker won't start on irregular field Some oddly-shaped fields confuse the AI. Do these manually. Multiple workers colliding Give each worker a separate section with space between them.

docs.title: Early Game (Day 1–10)
docs.text:
Goal: Build capital. Your own fields should be minimal — focus on contracts.

Day Strategy Expected Income 1–3 Accept every harvest contract you see. Use borrowed equipment. $15K–$30K/day 4–6 Start doing fertilizing and cultivating contracts too. Buy a second tractor. $25K–$50K/day 7–10 Accept everything except forestry. Save for a combine harvester. $40K–$80K/day

Key purchases: Second tractor → trailer → seeder → your first field purchase.

docs.title: Mid Game (First $500K)
docs.text:
Goal: Scale up. Use contracts to supplement your own farming income.

Activity Why Harvest contracts near your fields Minimal travel time = maximum hourly rate Root crop contracts Specialized harvesters are expensive; let contracts handle the equipment cost Fertilizing contracts with AI Passive income while you work your own fields Skip small-field contracts Your time is better spent on your own farm now

Key purchases: Combine harvester → large seeder → your 3rd and 4th fields.

docs.title: Late Game ($1M+)
docs.text:
Goal: Be selective. Your own farm should be the primary income source.

Activity Why Large harvest contracts only Only the highest-paying contracts are worth your time Contracts on your own fields If an NPC needs work on a field you own, that's pure profit Skip everything else Your own operation earns more per hour than most contracts Consider buying out NPC farms Instead of doing their contracts, buy their land for permanent income

Tip: In the late game, contracts are still worth doing if they involve a field you already own. The NPC pays you to work your own land — free money.

docs.title: What happens if I miss the deadline?
docs.text: You can still complete the contract after the deadline, but your payout is reduced. The reduction depends on how late you are. If you're very late, the contract may be cancelled entirely. Always check the deadline before accepting.

docs.title: Can I fail a contract?
docs.text: Yes — damaging crops, destroying equipment, or failing to complete the work before the deadline can result in a partial or complete loss of payment. The damage penalty is typically deducted from your payout.

docs.title: Can I keep the harvest?
docs.text: For harvest contracts, the crop you collect goes into the borrowed harvester. You can divert it to your own silo by driving the borrowed harvester to your farm and unloading it before returning the equipment. This is a well-known strategy that effectively doubles the contract value.

docs.title: How do I find contracts?
docs.text: Open the map (M key) — blue icons represent available contracts. The Contracts menu (P key → Contracts tab) lists all available work sorted by type and field.

docs.title: Can I do contracts with friends in multiplayer?
docs.text: Yes! In multiplayer mode, all players can accept and complete contracts. The payout goes to the farm account, which can be shared or split as the group decides.

docs.title: Why are there no contracts today?
docs.text: Contracts refresh each in-game day. If there are none, skip to the next day (sleep or advance time). Some days naturally have fewer contracts than others. Seasons mode can also affect contract availability — expect fewer contracts during winter.

docs.title: Do contracts affect my relationships with NPCs?
docs.text: There's no formal reputation system, but completing contracts for the same NPC repeatedly unlocks slightly better payouts for future contracts from that farmer.

docs.title: Can I refuse a contract after accepting?
docs.text:
Yes — you can cancel an accepted contract from the Contracts menu. However, you may lose any equipment you borrowed (it returns to the NPC), and there's a small reputation penalty.

Contracts are the engine that drives early-game progress in Farming Simulator 25. Master them, and you'll have the capital to buy land, equipment, and animals far faster than planting crops alone. Combine contract work with smart land purchases — see the Maps & Field Guide for land-buying strategies, and the Profit Guide for crop profitability analysis.

docs.title: Spring Crops (Plant Mar–Apr)
docs.text: Crop Why Plant in Spring Best For 🌾 Wheat Fast growth, harvest before fall busy season ✅ Beginners, cow feed Barley Quickest turnaround, harvest by Jun ✅ Second crop in rotation Oats Fastest growth (5 days), horse feed ⚠️ Moderate profit Canola High price/1000L, short growth window ✅ Mid-game cash crop 🥔 Potatoes Highest profit per ha, needs full season ✅ Dedicated potato fields Sugar Beet High volume, excellent for processing ✅ Sugar mill chain

docs.title: Fall Activities (Sep–Nov)
docs.text: Task Details 🚜 Harvest everything Before first frost — postponed harvest loses yield 🌾 Plant Winter Wheat/Barley Grows through winter, harvests early spring next year 🧑‍🌾 Apply fertilizer Prep fields for next season 🛠️ Service equipment Repair & store before winter downtime 📦 Sell grain Check prices — fall often has the year's best prices for grain crops

docs.title: Winter Tasks (Dec–Feb)
docs.text: Task Details 🐄 Animal care Focus on feeding, breeding, and product collection 🪵 Logging / forestry Best off-season income source 🏗️ Build processing plants Flour mill, oil press, bakery, sugar mill 📊 Plan next season Review market trends, pre-order seeds, buy land 🚜 Equipment upgrades Shop off-season sales and used vehicle market

docs.title: 🔄 Crop Rotation Recommendations
docs.text: Plan Field Setup Why It Works 🟢 Beginner 2-Field A: Wheat → Barley; B: Canola → Oats Simple, minimal equipment, steady $ 🟡 Mid-Game 3-Field 1: Potatoes → Winter Wheat; 2: Corn → Soybeans; 3: Sunflowers → Canola Balances high-profit crops with soil-resting periods 🔴 Advanced 4-Field 1: Sugar Beet → Wheat; 2: Potatoes → Barley; 3: Corn → Soybeans; 4: Cotton → Canola Maximum diversity, year-round harvest, processing synergy 🌱 Hay Rotation Grass (3 yr) → Corn (2 yr) → Wheat (1 yr) Low management, perfect for animal feed operations

docs.title: Rotation Tips
docs.text:
• Follow high-profit crops (potatoes/sugar beet) with wheat/barley — they benefit from residual fertilizer
  • Don't plant the same crop twice in a row — yield penalty applies
  • Plant corn after potatoes — potatoes break up soil, corn loves the loose ground
  • Use winter crops to keep fields producing through the cold months
