docs.title: 🎪 Fields of Mistria — Festivals & Events Guide
docs.text: Mistria hosts 8 major festivals a year, each occurring only once. This guide covers winning strategies and reward priorities.

docs.title: 📅 Year-Round Festival Calendar
docs.text: Date Festival Core Reward Exclusive? Spring 15 🌸 Spring Harvest Festival Iridium Hoe Recipe ✅ Spring 22 🐣 Egg Hunt Egg Hat ✅ Summer 11 ☀️ BBQ Contest Magic Stove (15% double cooking) ✅ Summer 20 🐟 Fishing Contest Iridium Fishing Rod ✅ Summer 28 ✨ Starry Night Star Lantern — Fall 9 🏆 Fall Fair Star Fruit (+30 max energy) ✅ Fall 20 🎃 Spirit Festival Golden Pumpkin (2500g / decor) — Winter 13 ❄️ Winter Market Frost Sword (32–48 ATK + Freeze) ✅ Winter 25 🎁 Starfall Festival Random rare return gift —

docs.title: 🌸 Spring 15 — Spring Harvest Festival
docs.text:
Submit 5 Spring crops/foraged goods. Score = Σ(Quality Multiplier × Base Score) + (Category Count × 3). (Iridium=3×, Gold=2×, Silver=1.5×, Normal=1×)

Recommended Loadout Score Iridium Strawberry + Spring Flower + Mushroom + Fish + Ore ~50 (max) 5× Gold different categories (Year 1) ~30

Must-choose reward: Iridium Hoe Recipe — only available once a year, miss it and you wait 365 days. Hoe range +1, 40% farm efficiency boost.

docs.title: 🐣 Spring 22 — Egg Hunt
docs.text:
9 eggs drawn from a pool of 15 fixed spawn points. Speedrun route: Plaza flowerbed → Mayor's fence → General Store back → Well → Behind church → Under bridge → Blacksmith's roof (ladder) → Inn flowerpot. Practice twice for a full collection.

Tip: The Egg Hat is a one-of-a-kind cosmetic — miss it and it's gone forever.

docs.title: ☀️ Summer 11 — BBQ Contest
docs.text:
Submit 1 dish: Taste 30 + Seasonality 20 + Rarity 20 + Shareability 10 = 80 max. Score ≥65 wins.

Loadout Score Iridium Grilled Platter (summer ingredients) 70–75 Gold Veggie Soup (Year 1) 55–60

Reward → Magic Stove (15% double cooking chance, only source).

docs.title: 🐟 Summer 20 — Fishing Contest
docs.text:
5-minute timed contest ranked by total weight. Win condition: Hoard a 5kg+ legendary fish beforehand → submit it immediately. If you don't have one, catch Tiger Catfish (3–5kg) at the mine lake.

Reward → Iridium Fishing Rod (hook delay halved, +1 range). Miss it, wait a year.

docs.title: ✨ Summer 28 — Starry Night
docs.text: Purely scenic — receive a Star Lantern. Collect 3 years' worth → Starry Set (glowing floor + star-pattern wallpaper). Purely cosmetic, but completionists must have it.

docs.title: 🏆 Fall 9 — Fall Fair (Most Important)
docs.text:
Submit 9 items from different categories. Can bet on cockfighting / sheep racing for tokens.

Category Recommended Quality Category Recommended Quality Crop Iridium Pumpkin ≥Gold Animal Iridium Goat Milk ≥Gold Cuisine Harvest Cake ≥Gold Fish Legendary Fish Rare Ore Gold Bar / Gem Any Forage Fall Mushroom ≥Silver Artisan Wine ≥Gold Smithing Gold Bar Any Mystic Magic Mushroom Any

💰 Token Exchange Priority: Star Fruit (permanent +30 max energy) > Magic Seeds > Wall Decor. Cockfighting tip: bet on the heavier bird.

docs.title: 🎃 Fall 20 — Spirit Festival
docs.text:
3-floor maze → Golden Pumpkin at the end. Solutions: ① Push blocks: Left → Up → Right → Down ② Memory match: screenshot each card ③ Dark maze: follow the right wall.

Tip: The Golden Pumpkin is the only floor-glow decor item — collectible > sellable (2500g).

docs.title: ❄️ Winter 13 — Winter Market
docs.text:
Ice sculpting + Sled racing + Limited-time shop.

Activity Winning Strategy Reward Ice Sculpting Submit a Diamond / Ruby Frost Sword (freeze effect) Sled Racing Slow on turns, full speed on straights Tokens

Shop: Owl Statue (+1 night foraging) > Ice Crystal Fireplace > Hand Warmer Recipe.

docs.title: 🎁 Winter 25 — Starfall Festival
docs.text:
Return gift value = 60%–80% of what you give.

Gift Expected Return Iridium Diamond (750g) ~500g worth, possibly Ancient Seeds Legendary Fish (1000g+) ~800g rare item ❌ <200g Junk

Bottom line: Spring 15 / Summer 11 / Fall 9 / Winter 13 offer exclusive functional items — must attend and must win. The rest are low-stakes.

Data source: Fields of Mistria v0.8+ (Early Access)

docs.title: Fields of Mistria Fish Database
docs.text: Welcome to the complete fish database for Fields of Mistria. Whether you are a dedicated angler trying to complete the museum collection, a chef looking for the best ingredients, or a farmer seeking extra income, this guide covers every fish species currently available in the game. Use the tables below to find exactly where and when to catch each fish, along with their base sell prices.

docs.title: Understanding Fishing Mechanics
docs.text: Before diving into the species list, it helps to know the basics. Fishing in Fields of Mistria uses a timing-based minigame. Different fish appear in specific locations, seasons, times of day, and weather conditions. Your fishing level and the quality of your rod also affect what you can catch. Higher-quality fish (Silver, Gold, Iridium) sell for more and are worth more for cooking.

docs.title: Key Terms
docs.text:
• Location: The specific body of water or area where the fish is found.
  • Season: The in-game season(s) the fish is available.
  • Time: The in-game time window when the fish is active.
  • Weather: Some fish only appear in rain, sun, or storms.
  • Sell Price: The base price for a normal-quality fish. Prices increase with quality.

docs.title: Complete Fish List
docs.text: Below is the full database of fish organized by location. Each table includes all relevant catch conditions.

docs.title: Town River & Lake
docs.text:
The river running through the center of town and the small lake near the inn are the most accessible fishing spots for beginners.

Fish Name Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Minnow Spring, Summer, Fall Any Any 15g Carp Spring, Summer, Fall 6AM – 7PM Any 30g Perch Spring, Fall 6AM – 2PM Any 45g Sunfish Summer 9AM – 4PM Sunny 50g Catfish Summer, Fall 6PM – 2AM Rain 80g Rainbow Trout Spring 6AM – 12PM Any 65g Bass Spring, Summer, Fall Any Any 55g Pike Fall 6AM – 9AM, 4PM – 7PM Any 100g Eel Spring, Fall 6PM – 2AM Rain 70g Goldfish Spring, Summer 10AM – 4PM Sunny 120g

docs.title: Mistria Beach & Ocean
docs.text:
The ocean to the south of town offers saltwater species. You will need the Fishing Rod Upgrade from the blacksmith to cast far enough to reach deep water fish.

Fish Name Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Sardine Spring, Summer, Fall 6AM – 6PM Any 40g Herring Spring, Winter 6AM – 2PM Any 50g Flounder Spring, Summer 6AM – 7PM Any 75g Tuna Summer, Fall 10AM – 4PM Sunny 110g Red Snapper Summer, Fall 6AM – 7PM Rain 130g Halibut Spring, Fall 6AM – 11AM, 4PM – 7PM Any 150g Lobster Summer, Fall Any Any 200g Crab Spring, Summer 6AM – 12PM Any 180g Octopus Summer 6PM – 2AM Any 250g Shark Summer 6AM – 9AM, 4PM – 7PM Sunny 350g Sea Turtle Summer, Fall 10AM – 4PM Sunny 400g

docs.title: Mountain Lake
docs.text:
The lake in the northern mountain area is home to cold-water and rare species. Access requires clearing the path north of the farm.

Fish Name Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Largemouth Bass Spring, Summer, Fall 6AM – 7PM Any 60g Walleye Fall, Winter 12PM – 2AM Rain 105g Sturgeon Summer, Winter 6AM – 7PM Any 180g Salmon Fall 6AM – 7PM Any 120g Trout Spring, Summer 6AM – 12PM Any 70g Bluegill Spring, Summer 9AM – 4PM Sunny 45g Lake Catfish Summer, Fall 6PM – 2AM Any 90g Ghost Fish Winter 6PM – 2AM Any 220g Ice Carp Winter 6AM – 7PM Snow 160g Legendary Fish (The Ancient One) Spring 6AM – 9AM Rain 1,000g

docs.title: Secret Fishing Spots
docs.text:
There are a few hidden fishing locations that require specific quests or tools to access. These spots contain unique fish.

Fish Name Location Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Lava Eel Volcanic Cave (floor 10+) Any Any Any 450g Crystal Carp Crystal Caverns Any Any Any 300g Moon Jellyfish Mistria Lake (during Festival of Stars) Fall 28 8PM – 12AM Clear 500g Void Fish Sewers (unlocked after museum donation milestone) Any Any Any 600g

docs.title: Fish Uses
docs.text:
Fish are not just for selling. They have several important uses in Fields of Mistria:

  • Cooking: Many recipes require specific fish. Cooked meals provide energy, health, and temporary buffs.
  • Museum Donations: The museum curator accepts one of each species. Donating all fish unlocks special rewards.
  • Gifting: Some villagers love certain fish. For example, the fisherman NPC loves Catfish and Pike.
  • Fish Ponds: You can raise fish in ponds on your farm. They produce roe, which can be aged and sold for profit.
  • Quests: The town bulletin board occasionally requests specific fish for rewards.

docs.title: Best Fish for Profit
docs.text:
If your goal is to make gold, focus on these high-value fish:

  1. Legendary Fish (The Ancient One) – 1,000g (Spring, Rain, Mountain Lake)
  2. Moon Jellyfish – 500g (Fall 28 only)
  3. Lava Eel – 450g (Volcanic Cave)
  4. Sea Turtle – 400g (Summer/Fall, Beach)
  5. Shark – 350g (Summer, Beach)

For consistent daily income, Catfish (80g) and Red Snapper (130g) are reliable options that are easier to catch.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Upgrade your rod early: Better rods let you cast farther and catch rarer fish.
  • Use bait: Craft bait from bug meat to reduce bite time and increase catch rate.
  • Check the weather: Rain and snow unlock exclusive species. Always fish during bad weather.
  • Fish at different times: Many fish are only active at dawn, dusk, or night. Set reminders to check each time window.
  • Complete the museum: The rewards for donating all fish include a permanent fishing buff and a unique decoration.
  • Cook before selling: Some fish sell for more when cooked into dishes. Check the recipe book before selling raw fish.
  • Build a fish pond: Once you have a surplus of high-value fish like Sturgeon or Lava Eel, place them in a pond for passive roe income.

docs.title: 🌱 Fields of Mistria — Complete Beginner's Guide
Your First Day in Mistria
docs.text: When you arrive at your overgrown farm in the charming town of Mistria, you will have basic tools — a watering can, hoe, axe, and pickaxe — plus a small patch of arable land covered in weeds and debris. Your first day sets the tone for the entire season, so let's make every minute count.

docs.title: Day 1 Checklist
docs.text: Step Task Details ✅ Clear 6-8 farm tiles Remove small weeds and debris — save large stumps for later ✅ Visit general store Buy starter seeds (turnips or spring greens) ✅ Plant crops 6-8 plants is the sweet spot for day 1 stamina ✅ Water everything Use the basic watering can — each tile consumes stamina ✅ Meet 3+ villagers Talk to NPCs you encounter at the store and town square ✅ Check notice board Town quests provide early gold and reputation

docs.title: Stamina Management
docs.text:
Your stamina bar is small and depletes fast on day 1. Prioritize:

  1. Clearing enough tiles to plant your starter seeds
  2. Actually planting and watering them
  3. Talking to villagers (costs no stamina)

Avoid: chopping large stumps, mining, or clearing the entire field on day 1. Those tasks are better saved for after your first harvest when you have food to restore energy.

Tip: Forage every berry bush and flower you pass. Even if you don't sell them, they serve as early gifts and quest items. Wild berries (restore 15 energy each) are especially valuable for keeping you working longer on day 1-3.

docs.title: 🌾 First Season Strategy — Spring
docs.text: Spring is your foundation season. Every decision you make here affects your summer capabilities.

docs.title: Best Early Crops
docs.text:
Crop Seed Cost Grow Time Sell Price Profit/Day Notes Turnip 15g 4 days 60g 11.3g/day Fastest turnaround — ideal for rapid reinvestment Spring Green 25g 5 days 90g 13.0g/day Best profit-per-day of basic crops Tomato 30g 7 days 120g 12.9g/day Regrows after harvest — plant 3-4 for passive income Potato 20g 6 days 75g 9.2g/day Decent all-rounder

Recommended planting strategy: - Day 1: Plant 4 turnips + 4 spring greens (uses ~1,200g of starter gold) - Day 5: Harvest turnips → buy 6 more spring greens - Day 10: Harvest everything → reinvest in tomatoes + more spring greens - Day 15+: You should have 12-15 tiles producing consistently

docs.title: Money-Making Outside Crops
docs.text: Method Daily Income Requirements Notes 🌿 Foraging 200-400g None Berries, flowers, seasonal herbs 🎣 Fishing 300-700g Fishing rod (800g from general store) Requires practice but pays well ⛏️ Mining 400-900g Pickaxe (free starter tool) Ores and gems from mines 📋 Town Quests 150-500g per quest Check notice board daily Easy reputation + money 🗑️ Trash 50-150g None Recycled items from town bins

docs.title: 🧙 Unlocking Magic (Days 3-7)
docs.text: Magic is Fields of Mistria's unique feature — the ability to cast spells that help with farming, combat, and exploration.

docs.title: How to Unlock
docs.text: Step Action Requirement 1 Complete "Clearing the Path" Remove 10 large debris items from your farm 2 Talk to Valen (town elder) Visit her house east of the town square after step 1 3 Complete "Spell Font" quest Collect 5 mana crystals from the mine (floors 1-5) 4 Reward: Water Spell Water a 3×3 area in one cast — massive stamina saver

docs.title: First Magic Tips
docs.text:
• The Water Spell is your biggest quality-of-life unlock in Spring — it reduces watering time by 60%
  • Check the Mana Shrine in the forest west of town weekly — it refills your mana
  • Mana crystals glow faintly in mine walls; break every suspicious rock
  • Always carry 20+ energy worth of food when mining for mana crystals

Tip: Rush the Spell Font quest. The Water Spell turns a 15-minute watering session into a 4-minute one. That saved time can go directly into mining or fishing, boosting your income by 40-50% in the second half of Spring.

docs.title: ⛏️ Mining in Your First Season
docs.text: The Mistria mines are unlockable around day 4-5. Enter through the cave in the northwest ridge of town.

docs.title: Floor Progression Goals
docs.text: Floors Ores Found Goal Unlock 1-5 Copper, Stone Get 10 copper + 5 mana crystals First tool upgrade + Water Spell 6-10 Copper, Iron Collect 15 iron for pickaxe upgrade Break mid-tier rocks on farm 11-15 Iron, Silver Deep mine access Better weapons and tools

docs.title: Combat Basics
docs.text:
The mines contain slimes, bats, and cave spiders. Your starter sword is weak but enough for floors 1-5:

  1. Kite enemies — hit, dodge back, hit again
  2. Eat food between fights — don't let HP drop below 50%
  3. Save monster drops — slime goo and bat wings are used in early crafting recipes
  4. Use the elevator — every 5 floors has a save point

docs.title: 🏠 First Season Milestones
docs.text: Timeframe Goal Why It Matters Day 1-5 Plant 8+ tiles, visit town, start mining Foundation for all income Day 5-10 Copper pickaxe upgrade, unlock magic Double mining speed, save stamina Day 10-15 15+ crop tiles, visit mine floor 10 Consistent daily income Day 15-20 Iron tools, mining floor 15 Unlock mid-game content Day 20-28 Save 5,000g for house upgrade Unlock cooking and kitchen

docs.title: Common Beginner Mistakes
docs.text:
Mistake Why It Hurts Fix Ignoring the notice board Misses easy gold and reputation Check it every single day Selling all monster drops Blocks crafting progression Keep 5-10 of each type before selling Over-clearing farm day 1 Waste stamina you need for planting Clear only 6-8 tiles initially Not saving for house upgrade Cooked meals are 3x more stamina-efficient Set aside 200g/day starting week 2

Final tip: Fields of Mistria rewards steady, balanced progress over sprinting. Focus on getting magic unlocked by day 7, a copper pickaxe by day 10, and 15+ crop tiles before summer. If you hit those three milestones, you'll enter Summer with 3,000-4,000g in savings and iron-tier tools — a fantastic position for season two.

docs.title: Fields of Mistria — NPC Gift Guide & Friendship
docs.text:
Build relationships with the residents of Mistria! This guide covers every NPC's gift preferences, organized by Loved, Liked, Neutral, Disliked, and Hated items.

Friendship Tip: Gifting on a character's birthday gives 3× the usual friendship points. Loved gifts on birthdays are the fastest path to max hearts.

docs.title: Gift Quality Multiplier
docs.text: Quality Friendship Multiplier Normal 1.0× Silver 1.1× Gold 1.25× Iridium 1.5×

docs.title: Adeline
docs.text: Category Items ❤️ Loved Diamond, Pearl, Golden Relic, Ancient Coin, Ember of Strength 👍 Liked Amethyst, Topaz, Emerald, Coffee, Tea, Crocus, Pink Cake 😐 Neutral Milk, Egg, Wool, Most Fruits, Most Vegetables 👎 Disliked Seaweed, Trash, Bug Meat, Slime, Rotten Food 💀 Hated Void Essence, Radioactive Ore, Sap

docs.title: 🏠 Fields of Mistria — Housing Upgrades & Decor Complete Guide
docs.text: From a rundown cabin to a dream estate, Fields of Mistria's housing system offers 4 upgrade tiers, each unlocking new rooms, features, and decor slots. With the full furniture and decoration system, you can build a home that's both functional and beautiful.

docs.title: 📐 House Upgrade Tiers
docs.text:
Tier Cost Materials Needed New Rooms Total Rooms 1️⃣ Starting — — Living Room + Bedroom + Kitchen 3 2️⃣ Upgrade 2500g Wood×50 + Stone×30 Bathroom + Storage Room 5 3️⃣ Expansion 8000g Hardwood×40 + Copper Bar×20 Study + Guest Room 7 4️⃣ Mansion 25000g Iron Bar×30 + Marble×15 Basement + Sunroom + Attic 10

💡 Tip: Before upgrading to Tier 2, prioritize tool upgrades and backpack space. The Storage Room unlocks at Tier 2 — it's your first real bulk storage solution. Getting it early drastically reduces your need for outdoor chests.

docs.title: Functional Furniture
docs.text: Item Function How to Get 🛏️ Bed Sleep recovery (higher tier = more recovery) Workbench crafting 📦 Chest Item storage (24 base slots) Starting recipe 🔥 Furnace Ore smelting (indoor version) Unlock via upgrade 🍳 Stove Cooking (unlocks more recipes) Comes with kitchen 🪡 Sewing Machine Cloth / clothing crafting Quest reward 📚 Bookshelf Skill XP bonus Town shop

docs.title: Decorative Furniture
docs.text:
Decor doesn't affect stats, but it does affect your House Score. The scoring system awards points based on furniture quantity, rarity, and color consistency across rooms. Reaching thresholds unlocks special rewards.

Score Threshold Reward Description 100 🌟 Vitality Aura +10 energy on waking up 250 🌟🌟 Luck Aura +5% fishing / mining crit chance 500 🌟🌟🌟 Harvest Blessing +10% crop yield 1000 🏆 Master Badge Exclusive wall decor + achievement

docs.title: Chest Layout Design
docs.text:
┌───────── Kitchen / Stove Area ──────────┐
│  [Ingredient Chest] → [Finished Chest] → [Stove]  │
│  Veggies | Fruits | Meat | Seasonings             │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
┌───────── Workshop / Workbench Area ─────┐
│  [Ore Chest] → [Fuel Chest] → [Furnace]          │
│  [Wood Chest] → [Stone Chest] → [Workbench]      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
┌───────── Entryway / Hallway ─────────────┐
│  [Tool Chest]  [Seed Chest]  [Loot Chest]         │
│  Grab-and-go in the morning, dump on return       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

💡 Tip: Use different-colored chests to categorize — Green=ingredients, Gray=ores, Brown=wood, Blue=fishing haul. Naming chests (right-click the chest name) lets you identify them at a glance when opened.

docs.title: Storage Priority
docs.text: Sort by item type: - High-turnover items (ingredients, ore, wood) → near the corresponding workstation - Collectibles (fish, artifacts) → sell after donating; store deep in the storage room - Seasonal items → move to the storage room when the season changes — don't clog main chests

docs.title: Fence Type Comparison
docs.text: Type Materials Durability Aesthetics Best For 🪵 Wood Fence Wood×2 Low ⭐⭐ Early temp enclosures 🪨 Stone Wall Stone×3 Medium ⭐⭐⭐ Main farm border fence 🏛️ Brick Wall Clay×3 + Stone×2 High ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Residential area fence ✨ Magic Fence Magic Dust×3 + Iron Bar×2 ∞ ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Ultimate decor

docs.title: Path Paving
docs.text:
The path system affects movement speed and aesthetics:

Path Speed Bonus Materials Unlock Dirt Path 0% Free (hoe) Starting Gravel Path +10% Stone×2 Early Stone Path +15% Stone×5 Mid-game Magic Brick Path +25% Magic Brick×3 Late-game

Recommended layout: Build a Stone Path main road from your front door → mailbox → farm gate. Use Gravel Path for branches leading to barns and the greenhouse.

docs.title: Lighting & Greenery
docs.text:
• Lanterns / Torches: Place one every 6 tiles to prevent nighttime monster spawns on your farm
  • Tree Planning: Fruit trees need at least 3×3 space; decorative trees only need 2×2
  • Flower Beds: Plant flowers within 8 tiles of beehives → doubles honey quality

docs.title: 🔑 Rare Furniture Farming
docs.text:
Furniture Source Rarity Notes 🖼️ Star Hanging Mine floors 40–60 chests 🌟🌟🌟 +25 House Score 🕯️ Magic Chandelier Fall festival prize 🌟🌟🌟🌟 +40 House Score, lights entire room 🛋️ Velvet Sofa Town shop (Saturday refresh) 🌟🌟🌟 2000g, +20 House Score 🪴 Glowing Potted Plant Mystic Forest exploration 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟 +50 House Score, night glow effect 🐟 Large Fish Tank Complete 50% of fishing collection 🌟🌟🌟🌟 Extra score with rare fish inside

💡 Tip: The shop refreshes daily — check it every day. Rare furniture often appears in Saturday sales, festival stalls, and mine chests. Decor doesn't take up backpack space, so buy it when you see it.

docs.title: Efficient Utility Layout (80 Score / 5 Rooms)
docs.text:
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│   🛏️ Bedroom        🛁 Bathroom     │
│   [Bed] [Wardrobe]    [Tub] [Basin]  │
├──────────── ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ────┤
│   🍳 Kitchen        📚 Study       │
│   [Stove][Fridge]   [Desk][Bookshelf]│
│   [Ingredient Chest] [Skill Bonus]   │
├──────────── ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ────┤
│   🛠️ Storage Room   🛋️ Living Room  │
│   [Furnace][Workbench] [Sofa][Coffee Table]│
│   [8× Chest Wall]   [Fireplace][TV] │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

Design Principles: 1. Shortest path: Stove ↔ Ingredient Chest ↔ Fridge — triangle layout 2. Group categories: All chests on one wall in the storage room — no hunting across the house 3. Cluster decor: At least 3 decor items per room to trigger score bonuses

docs.title: Aesthetic Layout (500+ Score / 7 Rooms)
docs.text:
Split the Tier 4 Mansion's 10 rooms into: Bedroom (starry theme), Bathroom (nature style), Kitchen (countryside), Study (antique), Living Room (luxury), Storage Room (purely functional — don't waste score here), Sunroom (potted plants + fish tank), Basement (wine + pickling processing area).

Data source: Fields of Mistria Wiki 1.0, game version Early Access 0.8+

docs.title: ⛏️ Fields of Mistria — Mining & Combat Deep Dive
docs.text: Mining in Fields of Mistria is more than an ore run — it's the game's primary combat arena, gear progression pipeline, and mid-game money engine. The mines are gated by story progression and hold 20 floors of increasing danger, each with unique enemies, ores, and loot tables.

docs.title: 🧱 Mine Structure & Progression
docs.text:
Aspect Details Total Floors 20 (unlocked in 5-floor tiers via story milestones) Tier 1 Floors 1–5 (after completing Spring quest "Earthquake Aftermath") Tier 2 Floors 6–10 (after reaching Town Rank B) Tier 3 Floors 11–15 (after restoring the mine cart) Tier 4 Floors 16–20 (endgame, requires Town Rank S) Shafts Random ladder + guaranteed exit at every floor

Tip: Unlike Stardew Valley, there is no elevator in Fields of Mistria. You must clear each floor's threat room to unlock the ladder. Once cleared, the floor stays cleared — you can revisit without fighting again.

docs.title: Floor Composition Table
docs.text: Floors Theme Primary Ores Common Enemies Boss 1–5 Dirt Cavern Copper, Tin Green Slimes, Cave Bats King Slime (F5) 6–10 Stone Depths Iron, Coal Blue Slimes, Stone Golems, Crystal Bats Golem Guardian (F10) 11–15 Magma Core Gold, Rubies Red Slimes, Lava Bats, Magma Golems Magma Warden (F15) 16–20 Abyssal Vault Mythril, Gems Purple Slimes, Shadow Bats, Abyss Golems Void Construct (F20)

docs.title: 👾 Enemy Encyclopedia
docs.text:
Each enemy type has distinct resistances and drop tables. Knowing their weaknesses doubles your clear speed.

Enemy HP Weakness Resists Key Drops 🟢 Green Slime 25 Fire magic None Slime Gel, Healing Vial (10%) 🔵 Blue Slime 45 Earth magic Fire (−20%) Slime Gel, Iron Ore (15%) 🔴 Red Slime 70 Water magic Fire (−40%) Slime Gel, Gold Ore (12%) 🟣 Purple Slime 110 Earth magic All magic (−30%) except Earth Slime Gel, Mythril Ore (8%) 🦇 Cave Bat 20 None None Bat Wing, Copper Ore 💎 Crystal Bat 40 Pickaxe swing All magic (−50%) Bat Wing, Gems (rare) 🌋 Lava Bat 55 Water magic Fire (immune) Bat Wing, Gold Ore 👻 Shadow Bat 75 Light magic Shadow (immune) Bat Wing, Mythril Shard 🪨 Stone Golem 80 Pickaxe (3 hits) Swords (−40%) Stone Blocks, Iron Ore 🔥 Magma Golem 120 Water magic Fire (immune) Stone Blocks, Gold Ore, Coal ⚫ Abyss Golem 180 Earth magic All physical (−25%) Dark Stone, Mythril Bar

Tip: Crystal Bats and Shadow Bats are generally not worth fighting in bulk — their magic resistance makes them take 2× longer to kill. Skip them unless they block the ladder.

docs.title: ⚔️ Gear Progression
docs.text: Your output in the mines is entirely gear-dependent. Don't fight floor 10 with floor 1 equipment.

docs.title: Pickaxe Upgrades
docs.text: Level Cost Effect Unlock Requirement Copper — Starting pick None Iron 500g + 5 Iron Bars 1.5× damage vs rocks Floor 5 clear Gold 1500g + 8 Gold Bars 2× damage, mining speed +3 Floor 10 clear Mythril 5000g + 6 Mythril Bars 3× damage, can break dark stone Floor 15 clear

docs.title: Weapon Progression
docs.text: Weapon Damage How to Get Best For Rusty Sword 4–8 Starting weapon Floors 1–2 only Copper Blade 8–14 Forge (3 Copper Bars) Floors 1–5 Iron Broadsword 16–25 Forge (6 Iron Bars) Floors 5–10 Gold Saber 28–40 Forge (8 Gold Bars + 2 Rubies) Floors 10–15 Mythril Greatsword 45–65 Forge (10 Mythril Bars + Gem Shard) Floors 15–20

docs.title: Armor Tiers
docs.text: Set Defense Set Bonus Required Copper 5 None 10 Copper Bars Iron 12 +15 HP 15 Iron Bars Gold 22 +25 HP, +2 defense 20 Gold Bars Mythril 35 +40 HP, +5 defense, fire resist 25 Mythril Bars

docs.title: Accessory Slot
docs.text:
Unlocked at floor 10. One accessory at a time:

Accessory Effect Drop Source Ruby Ring +15% melee damage Golem Guardian (30%) Sapphire Ring +20% water magic Magma Warden (25%) Emerald Ring +20% earth magic King Slime (30%) Amethyst Ring +15% magic regen Void Construct (20%)

docs.title: 🪄 Magic Combat
docs.text:
Magic is Fields of Mistria's unique combat mechanic — it pulls from a shared mana pool (recharges slowly, or instantly via mana potions).

Spell Cost Effect Best Against 🔥 Fireball 15 MP 30–50 damage, burn DoT 3s Slimes (all colors), Golems 💧 Water Jet 12 MP 20–35 damage, slows enemy Red Slimes, Lava Bats 🌍 Earth Spike 18 MP 40–60 damage, stun 1.5s Blue Slimes, Abyss Golem ✨ Light Burst 25 MP 50–70 damage, AoE Shadow Bats, Purples

Recommended Loadout: - General floors: Fireball (cheap, effective against most enemies) - Floor 6–10: Earth Spike (Golems are the main threat) - Floor 11–15: Water Jet (Lava enemies resist fire) - Floor 16–20: Light Burst (necessary for Shadow enemies)

Tip: Don't spam magic. Use 2–3 Fireballs to soften a group, then mop up with melee. Mana potions cost 250g each at the Adventurer's Guild — use them only on boss floors.

docs.title: King Slime (Floor 5)
docs.text: Stat Value HP 250 Phase 1 Jumps toward you, leaving slime puddles (damage zone) Phase 2 (< 50% HP) Splits into 3 small slimes (each 40 HP) — kill all to win Recommended Gear Copper Armor + Copper Blade Strategy Keep moving in a circle. Fireball from range. When it splits, kill the small slimes ASAP — they have no attack but extend the fight

docs.title: Golem Guardian (Floor 10)
docs.text: Stat Value HP 450 Attacks Ground slam (AoE, slow), Charge (linear), Rock throw (ranged) Weak Spot Back — takes 50% more damage from behind Recommended Gear Iron Gear + Iron Broadsword Strategy Dodge roll through its charge attack to get behind it. Earth Spike stuns it for 3 hits. Never stand still — the slam AoE covers 3 tiles

docs.title: Magma Warden (Floor 15)
docs.text: Stat Value HP 700 Attacks Fire breath (cone, 80 damage), Magma puddle (DoT zone), Tail sweep (180°) Immune Fire magic (0 damage) Recommended Gear Gold Armor + Gold Saber + Sapphire Ring Strategy This fight is a DPS check. Use Water Jet exclusively (4× damage). When it breathes fire, dodge behind it. The magma puddles last 20 seconds — don't trap yourself in a corner

docs.title: Void Construct (Floor 20)
docs.text: Stat Value HP 1200 Attacks Shadow beam (linear, 100 damage), Void orbs (homing, 40 damage each), Summon adds (2 Shadow Bats) Immune Physical (−75%), Shadow (immune) Recommended Gear Full Mythril Gear + Amethyst Ring Strategy The hardest fight in the mines. Use Light Burst exclusively. Kill summoned Shadow Bats instantly (they heal the boss on contact). When Void orbs appear, kite them into walls — they despawn after hitting terrain

docs.title: 💰 Profit Analysis: What to Sell vs. Keep
docs.text:
Item Sell Price Keep? Reason Copper Ore 3g 🗑️ Sell after floor 5 You need ~60 total for upgrades, excess is vendor trash Iron Ore 6g 🔧 Keep Needed for mid-game crafts and all building structures Gold Ore 15g 🔧 Keep Scarce — every bar is needed for tools and gear Mythril Ore 40g 🔧 Keep Extremely rare, never sell Coal 5g 🔧 Keep Always in demand for smelting Ruby 250g 🗑️ Sell No crafting use, pure money Emerald 300g 🗑️ Sell No crafting use Slime Gel 8g 🔧 Keep Used in healing potion crafting Bat Wing 5g 🗑️ Sell Only used in one late-game recipe Golem Core 120g 🔧 Keep Required for accessory upgrades Dark Stone 35g 🔧 Keep Floor 16–20 only, used for endgame gear Treasure Chest 500–2000g 🗑️ Sell Open first (may contain gear), then sell

Best money-makers by floor tier: - Floor 1–5: Mine Copper Ore but don't bother selling — save for upgrades. Money comes from gem nodes (Ruby, Emerald). - Floor 6–10: Gold Ore nodes spawn here at low rate. Sell excess Coal (over 50). - Floor 11–15: Treasure chests appear on every 3rd floor. Each averages 800g. - Floor 16–20: Mythril Ore is the jackpot — but keep every piece. Money here comes from Abyss Golem Golem Core drops (120g each, 30% drop rate).

docs.title: 🚀 Deep Mining Tips: Reaching Floor 20 Efficiently
docs.text:
1. Pre-mine prep: Bring 10 Meals (any cooked dish that restores 30+ energy) + 3 Mana Potions + Pickaxe + Best Weapon
  2. Clear path first, loot later: Each floor has one threat room. Kill the enemies, take the ladder, then backtrack for ore. Enemies don't respawn after the ladder is unlocked.
  3. Skip non-threatening fights: Cave Bats and Blue Slimes on later floors waste time. If they're not between you and the ladder, ignore them.
  4. Use pickaxe on golems: Stone Golems take 3 pickaxe hits to stun, during which you can mine freely. Don't waste weapon durability on them.
  5. Stamina management: Mining costs 4 stamina per swing, fighting costs 2. Keep a mental budget — if you're under 20 stamina on floor 14, eat a meal before the boss. Magma Warden will oneshot you if you can't dodge.
  6. Best time to mine: Rainy days. Crops don't need watering, giving you 2 extra hours in the mines.
  7. Floor reset trick: If a floor spawns too many Crystal Bats (magic resistant), exit to the mine entrance and re-enter — enemy composition reshuffles. This costs 1 in-game hour, so only do it on floors 13+.

Tip: The Mine Cart system (unlocked at floor 10) costs 20 Iron Bars to repair but is worth every ingot. It lets you warp directly to floor 10, 15, or 20 from the entrance, saving 15+ minutes of walking per run.

docs.title: 🏛️ Fields of Mistria — Museum Donation & Collection Complete Guide
docs.text: Donate your discoveries to the Mistria Museum to restore the ancient building, unlock new areas and features, and earn the game's richest milestone rewards. This guide covers all donation categories, rarity tiers, farming strategies, and a complete collection checklist.

docs.title: 🔓 Unlocking the Museum
docs.text:
Trigger: Around day 5, the mayor sends you a letter announcing the museum is open. Head to the museum on the right side of town center and speak with curator Katherine to begin donating.

💡 Tip: From day 1, save every "donatable" item you find — don't rush to sell them. Many early items that seem valuable (like Golden Gears or Ancient Coins) are only worth a few hundred gold, but the rewards they unlock are worth far more.

docs.title: 📂 Donation Categories Overview
docs.text: Category Total Items Sample Items Difficulty 🧱 Artifacts 35 Ancient tools, pottery shards, jewelry ⭐⭐⭐ 🦴 Fossils 25 Dinosaur bones, ancient shells, amber ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 💎 Gems 20 Ruby, Emerald, Amethyst ⭐⭐⭐ 🐟 Fish 30 River fish, sea fish, cave fish ⭐⭐ 🌿 Flora 20 Rare flowers, magical plants ⭐⭐ 🥘 Cuisine 25 Seasonal specialty dishes ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 🎭 Relics 15 Rune stones, ancient scrolls, artifacts ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Total 170 — —

docs.title: 🎲 Rarity System
docs.text:
Every item has 4 rarity tiers, directly affecting donation score and reward quality:

Tier Color Base Score Common Sources Price Range ⚪ Common White 5 Ground pickup, shallow digging 10–50g 🟢 Uncommon Green 15 Mine floors 1–20, fishing 50–150g 🔵 Rare Blue 40 Mine floors 20–60, special events 150–400g 🟣 Epic Purple 100 Mine floors 60+, Boss drops 400–1000g

Rarity Weight: One Epic item in a category is worth roughly 20 Common items for score. Prioritize high-rarity items to hit milestone thresholds faster.

docs.title: 🏆 Milestone Rewards
docs.text:
Donation Score Reward Unlocks 50 📜 Archaeology Guide Dig site markers revealed 100 🧪 Identification Tool Can identify unidentified items 200 🗺️ Treasure Map Shows hidden collectible locations 350 🎣 Rare Bait Recipe×5 Fish collection accelerator 500 💧 Water Magic Scroll New magic skill unlocked 750 🪴 Greenhouse Blueprint Can build a greenhouse 1000 🚂 Train Station Repair Unlocks new area — new goods daily 1500 ⚡ Thunder Magic Scroll New magic skill 2000 🏅 Museum Master Trophy Full collector achievement progress

💡 Tip: 500 points for the Greenhouse is the game's most important mid-game milestone. Prioritize Gems and Fish donations — these categories have lots of items with steady availability, getting you to 500 fastest. Fossils and Epic artifacts give high scores but are too RNG-dependent.

docs.title: Artifacts
docs.text: Item Best Location Recommended Tool Pottery Shard Southern beach dig sites Shovel (Copper+) Ancient Tool Mine floors 10–25 Pickaxe Jewelry Mine floors 40–50 chests Keys Rune Tablet Forest ruins (higher spawn rate on rainy days) Shovel

docs.title: Fossils
docs.text: Item Best Location Time Small Bone Mine floors 5–15 (ground pickup) Any Large Bone Mine floors 55–70 Any Amber Fossil Forest dig sites (near tree stumps) Chance doubles after rain Ancient Shell Beach area Low tide

docs.title: Gems
docs.text: Gem Floors Mining Tips Amethyst 10–30 Look for purple crystal clusters Ruby 30–50 Reflects torchlight — easy to spot Emerald 50–70 Green against dark rock background Diamond 70+ Use bombs on large rock formations Star Shard 80+ Special cracked walls only

docs.title: Artifacts (35/35)
docs.text:
• [ ] Pottery Shard (1/6) — Common, Beach
  • [ ] Ancient Coin (1/3) — Uncommon, Floors 5–20
  • [ ] Copper Tool Head (1/2) — Common, Farm digging
  • [ ] Iron Tool Head (1/2) — Uncommon, Floors 30–45
  • [ ] Ancient Key — Rare, Floors 50+ chests
  • [ ] Broken Statue (1/4) — Various pieces on different floors
  • [ ] Rune Stone (1/5) — Epic, Floors 60+
  • [ ] ... (see in-game collection for full list)

docs.title: Fish (30/30)
docs.text:
• [ ] Bass — River, any time
  • [ ] Catfish — River, rainy weather
  • [ ] Goldfish — Mine underground lake
  • [ ] Lantern Fish — Mine floors 40+ water areas
  • [ ] Seahorse — Beach, early morning
  • [ ] Clownfish — Beach, afternoon
  • [ ] Ancient Fish — Mine floors 70+ (Epic)
  • [ ] ... (see fishing collection for full list)

docs.title: 🔄 Duplicate Item Strategy
docs.text:
Category Dupes → Recommended Why Common Artifacts ✅ Sell 10–50g each — already got the donation score Uncommon Artifacts ⚖️ Keep 1–2 Some used in crafting or quests Rare Artifacts ❌ Keep Potential hidden uses or future updates Common Fish ✅ Cook / Sell You'll catch plenty Rare Fish 🐟 Put in fish tank Fish tank decor gives high score Common Gems ✅ Sell Not worth hoarding Rare Gems ❌ Keep Needed for high-end accessory crafting Fossils ✅ Sell No use beyond donation

💡 Tip: Donation score ≠ item sell price. An Epic artifact might sell for 800g at the store, but it gives 100 donation points — the same score you'd need 20 Common items (worth ~200g total) to match. So: donate first, sell later — donate one of each, sell the rest.

Data source: Fields of Mistria Wiki 1.0, game version Early Access 0.8+

docs.title: 🌱 Fields of Mistria — Seasonal Crop Strategy Guide
docs.text: Maximizing profit in Fields of Mistria requires more than planting the most expensive seed. You must factor in growth time, regrowth cycles, energy cost, artisan multipliers, and magic enhancement bonuses. This guide breaks down every crop by true profit per day.

docs.title: 📊 Spring Crop Tier List
docs.text:
Rank Crop Seed Cost Growth Regrow Sell Price Profit/Day Energy 1 Strawberry 🥇 60 7d 4d 85 10.7 2 2 Cauliflower 🥈 50 9d No 135 9.4 3 3 Broccoli 🥉 40 7d No 100 8.6 2 4 Cabbage 30 6d No 75 7.5 2 5 Green Bean 25 5d 3d 45 6.7 1 6 Carrot 20 4d No 46 6.5 1 7 Potato 20 5d No 50 6.0 2 8 Bamboo 10 3d No 22 4.0 1

Tip: Strawberry's regrowth every 4 days makes it the spring king. With magic water enhancement, its profit/day jumps to ~14.9 — plant at least 20 on Spring 1.

docs.title: ☀️ Summer Crop Tier List
docs.text:
Rank Crop Seed Cost Growth Regrow Sell Price Profit/Day Energy 1 Watermelon 🥇 60 10d No 160 10.0 4 2 Melon 🥈 55 9d No 140 9.4 3 3 Corn 🥉 35 7d 3d 70 8.8 2 4 Blueberry 40 8d 4d 75 7.9 2 5 Tomato 30 6d 4d 60 7.5 2 6 Pepper 25 5d 3d 50 7.5 1

Strategy: Watermelon has the highest single-harvest profit but a 10-day wait. Invest in Corn (3-day regrowth) for steady mid-season income, then pivot to Watermelon for the late-season lump sum.

docs.title: 🍂 Fall Crop Tier List
docs.text:
Rank Crop Seed Cost Growth Regrow Sell Price Profit/Day Energy 1 Pumpkin 🥇 50 9d No 150 11.1 3 2 Cranberry 🥈 40 7d 5d 85 9.0 2 3 Artichoke 🥉 45 8d No 110 8.1 2 4 Sweet Potato 25 6d 4d 55 7.5 2 5 Rice 35 8d No 90 6.9 3

Fall dominates all seasons — Pumpkin at 11.1 profit/day is the game's best crop. Plant the maximum your energy budget allows.

docs.title: 🏭 Artisan Processing — Best Multipliers
docs.text:
Crop Base 🫙 Jam 🥒 Pickle 🍷 Wine Best Route Watermelon 160 340 290 420 Wine (+260) Pumpkin 150 320 280 400 Wine (+250) Cauliflower 135 290 250 350 Wine (+215) Strawberry 85 200 170 240 Wine (+155) Cranberry 85 210 175 240 Jam (+125) Corn 70 175 150 195 Wine (+125)

Build 3 Preserve Jars per 10 regrowth crops and 2 Wine Presses per 10 high-value crops. Rotate harvests to keep equipment loaded continuously.

docs.title: ✨ Magic-Enhanced Crops
docs.text:
Enhancement Element Effect Best Applied To 🌊 Water Boost Water +25% growth speed Pumpkin, Watermelon 🌍 Earth Enrich Earth +40% sell price Strawberry, Cranberry 🌪️ Air Pollinate Air +50% yield Corn, Tomato 🔥 Sun Empower Fire Double quality chance Any artisan crop

Stacking example: Earth Enrich on Strawberry (85 → 119g) + Jam processing (119g × 235% = 280g/jar) = 18.6 profit/day per tile — nearly double standard.

docs.title: ♻️ Greenhouse & Field Layout
docs.text:
Greenhouse priority: 60% Pumpkin (wine) → 25% Strawberry (jam) → 15% Cranberry (jam). Keeps artisan equipment running at full capacity year-round.

Layout Crops Daily Energy Season Profit Cheap & Fast 20 Carrot + 10 Bamboo 30 ~800g Max Profit 20 Pumpkin + 10 Cranberry 60 ~4000g Artisan Focus 12 Watermelon + 8 Corn + 10 Strawberry 55 ~5500g processed

Energy budget: Base 200 energy/day. Deduct 30 for tools. You have ~170 for watering (1 energy/tile, 2 for magic). Max manageable tiles without spells: ~85. With Rain Dance (3×3 area): ~200 tiles.

Ultimate endgame: Full greenhouse Pumpkins → Wine Press → Earth Enchantment on all tiles = ~44,000g per 28-day cycle from 80 tiles with 4 wine presses.

Data source: Fields of Mistria Wiki 1.0, game version Early Access 0.8+

docs.title: 🏞️ Stardew Valley Beginner's Guide: Day 1 Complete Walkthrough
docs.text: Reference: Stardew Valley Wiki - Getting Started · r/StardewValley Beginner FAQ

docs.title: 📋 What This Guide Covers
docs.text:
• Pre-game setup: Character creation & farm type selection
  • Morning routine: Wake up, watch TV, and check your mail
  • Afternoon exploration: Tour your farm and visit Pelican Town
  • Evening fishing: Your first money-making session
  • Newbie pitfalls to avoid
  • Optional: Rushing the mines on day 1

docs.title: 🎮 1. Critical Choices Before You Start
docs.text: When you first open the game, the main menu has three key decisions:

docs.title: Character Creation
docs.text: Your appearance choices affect nothing mechanically — pick whatever you like. The only meaningful decision is pet preference: choose between a cat or a dog. This affects which pet bowl item you interact with but has no gameplay impact beyond aesthetics.

docs.title: Farm Type Selection — The Most Important Decision
docs.text:
Farm Type Focus Difficulty Best For 🌱 Standard Farm All-around Easy First-time players — largest tillable area 🌲 Forest Farm Foraging + berry bushes Easy Forage-heavy playstyles 🪨 Hill-top Farm Mining Medium Players who want more mining access 🏝️ Wilderness Farm Combat Hard Monsters spawn at night 🪴 Four Corners Farm Multiplayer Easy Co-op players, organized layouts 🪷 Beach Farm Fishing Hard Limited sprinklers — advanced only 🏜️ Meadowlands Farm Animals Medium 1.6 new farm type — starter animals + grass

For your first playthrough: Choose Standard Farm. It has the most space and no gimmicks. The Forest Farm is also beginner-friendly if you like foraging.

Tip: Once you pick a farm type, you cannot change it without starting a new save. The Meadowlands Farm (added in 1.6) gives you two starter chickens and a coop already built — a great choice if you want to jump straight into animals.

docs.title: Wake Up (6:00 AM)
docs.text: You arrive in Stardew Valley via bus, greeted by Robin and Lewis. Follow the brief tutorial cutscene, then you are free to explore.

docs.title: Watch TV (Essential!)
docs.text:
Channel What You Get Priority 🎤 Fortune Teller Daily luck forecast Must-watch before mining 🌤️ Weather Report Tomorrow's weather Important for planning 🍳 Queen of Sauce Cooking recipes (Sunday reruns on Wed) Collect every new recipe 🏋️ Livin' Off The Land Farming tips Useful for brand new players

Day 1 Specific: Check the weather — if it's going to rain tomorrow, you can skip watering and spend the day mining or fishing instead.

docs.title: Check Your Mail
docs.text: You receive several letters on day 1: - Introduction to the Valley — from Lewis - The Adventurer's Guild invitation — from Marlon - A package from your grandfather (contains 15 parsnip seeds and 15g)

docs.title: Clearing the Farm
docs.text:
You start with an overgrown farm full of: - Weeds → Mixed Seeds (save these), Fiber - Twigs & branches → Wood - Small stones → Stone - Large stumps & boulders → need an upgraded axe/pickaxe (leave for later)

Day 1 clearing priorities: 1. Clear enough space for 15 parsnip seeds (about 20 tiles) 2. Clear a path to your front door 3. Chop enough wood for a chest (50 wood) and fence (optional) 4. Leave large stumps and boulders — you can't break them day 1

docs.title: Planting Parsnips
docs.text:
Plant all 15 parsnip seeds. Here's why:

Factor Parsnip Other Crops Notes Grow Time 4 days 6-12 days Harvest before Spring 5 Seed Cost 20g 50-150g Affordable for starting capital Sell Price 35g 60-250g Quick reinvestment cycle Water Daily Daily Same requirements

Planting strategy: 1. Hoe 15 tiles of soil (right-click with hoe equipped) 2. Apply fertilizer if you have any (optional day 1) 3. Plant seeds (right-click with seeds selected) 4. Water each tile (right-click with watering can equipped)

Tip: Leave at least 2 empty tiles for mixed seeds from weeds. You will also want room for Spring Onion foraging patches along the river south of your farm — those respawn daily and sell for 8-24g each.

docs.title: 🎣 4. Evening: Fishing For Starter Gold
docs.text: After planting and watering, head to Pelican Town via the bus stop path (south of your farm).

docs.title: Get Your Fishing Rod
docs.text:
1. Walk to Beach (east from town square, then south)
  2. Talk to Willy at the fish shop (south pier)
  3. He will give you the Bamboo Pole for free after a short tutorial
  4. Buy Bait (5g each from Willy's shop) — 20-30 pieces is enough for day 1

docs.title: Where to Fish on Day 1
docs.text:
Location Common Fish Difficulty Best For 🌊 Ocean (Beach) Sardine, Anchovy, Herring Medium Best prices 🏞️ River (Town) Chub, Sunfish, Perch Easy Beginners 🌲 Mountain Lake Carp, Bullhead Medium Good variety

Beginner Recommendation: Start at the river near Leah's Cottage (south of town). The fish are easier to catch, and chub sell for a reliable 50g each.

docs.title: Fishing Mini-Game Tips
docs.text: Problem Solution 🎯 Fish bounces too fast Tap, don't hold — short bursts keep the bar steady 🐟 Fish moves to top/bottom Let the bar float down naturally, then burst back ⚡ Small fish icon Harder to catch — skip these, focus on medium fish 🤚 Green bar too small Level up fishing skill = bigger bar

docs.title: ⛏️ 5. Optional: Rush the Mines Day 1
docs.text:
If you prefer combat over fishing, you can reach the mines on day 1 with a focused sprint:

  1. Skip fishing — go straight to the Adventurer's Guild (east of town, north of the mines entrance)
  2. Talk to Marlon to register
  3. Enter the mines (requires Rusty Sword from Marlon)
  4. Clear floors 1-5 for copper ore and gems

Rewards for rushing: - Copper ore for tool upgrades days earlier - Gemstones that sell for 100-300g each - Experience toward mining skill - Bug Meat drops — usable in 1.6 recipes

Tip: Don't overdo it. If your health drops below 50%, eat or leave. Passing out in the mines costs you 10% of your gold and you might lose items. Day 1 stamina is limited — mine until you have 10-15 copper ore, then head home.

docs.title: ❌ Common Newbie Pitfalls
docs.text: Mistake Consequence Solution ❌ Buying too many seeds Can't afford fishing rod or food Stick to the 15 free parsnips + 2-3 extra ❌ Cutting all grass No hay for animals later Leave at least 50 grass tiles uncut ❌ Selling all resources Can't build chests/fences day 2 Keep 50 wood + 50 stone minimum ❌ Ignoring the TV Miss weather forecast Watch TV every morning, no exceptions ❌ Overworking stamina Pass out at 2:00 AM, lose gold Stop farming by 10 PM, save energy for last-minute fishing ❌ Foraging but not saving Need Spring Onions for community bundle Store 1 of each foragable before selling ❌ Not talking to NPCs Miss early relationship opportunities Talk to everyone you pass — costs no stamina ❌ Throwing away artifacts/donations Delays museum completion Check the museum before selling anything marked "artifact"

docs.title: ✅ Day 1 Summary Checklist
docs.text:
✅ Task Status ✅ Complete prologue cutscene ✅ Check TV (weather + fortune) ✅ Read all mail ✅ Clear 20+ farm tiles ✅ Plant and water 15 parsnips ✅ Craft at least 1 chest ✅ Visit Pelican Town ✅ Talk to Willy → get fishing rod ✅ Catch 3-5 fish ✅ Save 50 wood + 50 stone ✅ Sleep before 12:00 AM (to avoid low energy next day)

Final tip: Day 1 in Stardew Valley is about foundation, not perfection. You don't need to min-max — just plant your parsnips, catch a few fish, and enjoy the atmosphere. The game rewards consistent daily progress far more than perfect day 1 execution. Save your min-max energy for Spring 5+, when you have a feel for the game and some gold in your pocket.

docs.title: 🌾 星露谷物语 自动抚摸机
docs.text:
本攻略由 B站 UP主「王道長」视频教程整理生成 最后更新: 2026-07-08

自动抚摸机（Auto-Petter）是星露谷物语中极为稀有且实用的物品，能每天自动抚摸畜棚或鸡舍内的所有动物。本攻略详细讲解自动抚摸机的获取方法、使用技巧和刷取策略。

docs.title: 📋 本篇涵盖
docs.text:
• ✅ 什么是自动抚摸机及其作用
  • ✅ 获取方法一：骷髅洞穴宝箱层（14%概率）
  • ✅ 获取方法二：挑战神殿激活后怪物掉落（2%概率）
  • ✅ 自动抚摸机的放置与使用
  • ✅ 刷取效率技巧与避坑

docs.title: 🎮 1. 什么是自动抚摸机？
docs.text:
自动抚摸机是一种可放置的建筑设备，将其放入畜棚（Barn）或鸡舍（Coop）后，它会每天自动抚摸里面的所有动物，无需玩家手动操作。

自动抚摸机在农场中自动抚摸动物的效果演示

docs.title: 自动抚摸机的主要作用
docs.text:
作用 说明 自动提升动物好感度 每天自动抚摸，动物好感度稳步提升 节省大量时间 动物多时每天手动抚摸需5-10分钟，自动抚摸省去此步骤 提高产出品质 高好感度动物产出更高质量的蛋、奶等产品 多人游戏利器 多人存档中动物数量翻倍，自动抚摸优势更大

💡 重要提示：自动抚摸效果与手动抚摸一样，不会降低好感度提升速度。

docs.title: 🏆 2. 获取方法一：骷髅洞穴宝箱层
docs.text:
在原版游戏中，自动抚摸机最主要的获取途径是骷髅洞穴（Skull Cavern）的宝箱层（Treasure Floors）。

在骷髅洞穴中获得自动抚摸机——这是最直接的获取方式

docs.title: 宝箱层机制
docs.text: 项目 说明 出现位置 骷髅洞穴（Skull Cavern）任何层数 宝箱层概率 每层约有 2~4% 概率为宝箱层（非固定） 自动抚摸机概率 宝箱层中约 14% 概率出现 建议层数 越深层越容易遇到宝箱，100层以后效率更高

docs.title: 刷骷髅洞穴必备准备
docs.text:
• 大量楼梯（Staircase）：用99个石头制作，快速下层寻找宝箱层
  • 沙拉（Salad）或奶酪（Cheese）：保持血量
  • 咖啡（Coffee）+ 三倍浓缩咖啡（Triple Shot Espresso）：提升移动速度
  • 南瓜汤（Pumpkin Soup）：增加运气和防御
  • 幸运午餐（Lucky Lunch）或魔法糖冰棍（Magic Rock Candy）：提高运气
  • 超级炸弹（Mega Bomb）：快速清场

💡 运气值越高，遇到宝箱层的概率越大。选择运气最好的日子（占卜显示" spirits are very happy"）前往。

docs.title: ⚔️ 3. 获取方法二：挑战神殿激活后怪物掉落（2%概率）
docs.text:
第二个获取方式来自于挑战神殿（Shrine of Challenge）机制。

在挑战神殿激活后的骷髅洞穴中探索，寻找自动抚摸机掉落

docs.title: 挑战神殿是什么？
docs.text: 挑战神殿位于姜岛（Ginger Island）的核桃房（Walnut Room）内。激活后，骷髅洞穴的怪物会被替换为更强大的变种，但也会掉落特殊物品。

docs.title: 激活挑战神殿后的掉落表
docs.text:
当挑战神殿激活时，任何楼层的怪物都有几率从以下特殊掉落表中掉落物品：

物品 概率 铱针（Iridium Needle） 14% 铱带（Iridium Band） 14% 强化剂（Enricher） 14% 压力喷嘴（Pressure Nozzle） 14% 放射性棒（Radioactive Bar） 14% 气宝石（Qi Gem） 14% 自动抚摸机（Auto-Petter） 2% 宝箱层随机物品 14%

挑战神殿活跃状态下，自动抚摸机的掉落概率为2%，需要注意这是概率最低的掉落项

docs.title: 刷挑战神殿策略
docs.text:
策略 说明 怪物种类 所有怪物均可掉落，但大群怪物群更有效率 效率核心 使用炸弹清场 + 快速下层刷新怪物 楼梯必备 快速到达100+层，怪物密度更高 推荐装备 吸血武器（如银河剑）、铱环、幸运戒指

💡 两种方法对比：宝箱层（14%但层数随机）适合深度刷楼梯打法；挑战神殿（2%但每层都刷）适合清场打法，推荐结合使用。

docs.title: 🏠 4. 放置与使用
docs.text: 在畜棚内整理资源，准备放置自动抚摸机至畜棚或鸡舍

docs.title: 放置步骤
docs.text:
1. 获得自动抚摸机后，进入背包选中它
  2. 走进你想放置的畜棚（Barn）或鸡舍（Coop）内部
  3. 在空地上右键放置
  4. 无需电力或维护，放进去就自动工作

docs.title: 使用注意事项
docs.text: 要点 说明 动物范围 对同一建筑内的所有动物生效 每天触发 每天早上动物醒来后自动触发 好感度上限 5心（满好感），与手动抚摸进度一致 多个同时使用 同一建筑放多个不会叠加效果 与手动抚摸关系 自动 + 手动不会叠加，自动后无需再手动 能否拆除 可以用斧头/镐子敲掉回收

docs.title: ✅ 目标清单
docs.text:
• [x] 了解自动抚摸机的功能和价值
  • [x] 获得方法一：骷髅洞穴宝箱层刷取（14%概率）
  • [x] 获得方法二：挑战神殿怪物掉落（2%概率）
  • [x] 正确放置到畜棚或鸡舍
  • [x] 享受每天自动抚摸带来的便利

docs.title: 🚫 新手避坑清单
docs.text: ❌ 不要做 ✅ 正确做法 以为自动抚摸机会降低效率 自动抚摸与手动抚摸效果完全一样 只在矿井1-20层刷宝箱 骷髅洞穴越深层宝箱层概率越高 不打挑战神殿 挑战神殿激活后多一条获取途径 放完不检查动物状态 少量动物仍可以手动抚摸来加速好感 同一个建筑放多个自动抚摸机 一个建筑只需一个即可覆盖全部动物 忘记带楼梯下骷髅洞穴 至少50个楼梯，否则效率极低

docs.title: 💡 小贴士
docs.text:
• 最大效率刷法：准备200+楼梯 → 选运气最好的一天 → 吃幸运午餐 → 直奔200层 → 看到宝箱层就搜刮
  • 中后期推荐：解锁姜岛并激活挑战神殿后，日常刷矿时顺便刷自动抚摸机
  • 多人游戏：每个玩家都需要自己的自动抚摸机，建议一起刷骷髅洞穴

docs.title: Game Overview
docs.text:
• Developer: Singularity 6
  • Platform: PC / NS
  • Genre: Farming MMO (Free-to-Play)
  • Features: Multiplayer online farming + adventure

docs.title: Fast Leveling 1-10
docs.text:
1. Complete the tutorial → Unlock basic tools
  2. Plant wheat (3-day maturity, cooking staple)
  3. Fish → Fastest early-game money maker
  4. Mine → Important materials for tool upgrades

docs.title: Fastest Money-Making Methods
docs.text: Method Hourly Rate Requirements Fishing (hotspots) 3K-5K Fishing skill 4+ Rare bug catching 2K-4K Advanced bug net Cooking quests 1K-2K Kitchen upgrade Growing cotton 1K-3K Seeds unlocked

docs.title: Best Leveling Path
docs.text: Skill Priority Recommended Method Fishing ⭐⭐⭐ Daily hotspots Foraging ⭐⭐⭐ Pick as you go Cooking ⭐⭐ Fulfill orders for money Gardening ⭐⭐⭐ Plant every day Bug Catching ⭐ Level up when you have time Mining ⭐⭐ Go when ore respawns

  • Housing System — Customizable decoration
  • Festival Events — Limited-time quests

1. ❌ Don't rush to expand your house — Upgrade tools first

docs.title: 🐾 Palia Animals & Pets Guide
docs.text:
Palia's world is full of wildlife. From the graceful sernuk grazing in Kilima Valley to the skittish chapaa darting through Bahari Bay, the creatures of Palia are more than just scenery — they're a source of food, crafting materials, and gold. This guide covers every animal you'll encounter, how hunting works, and what companion pets can do for you.

Note: Unlike some farming games, Palia doesn't have domesticatable barn animals you can buy and raise. Animal husbandry here is about hunting wildlife for materials and collecting companion pets for cosmetic and gameplay benefits.

docs.title: 🦌 Wildlife Overview
docs.text:
Palia's creatures fall into a few broad categories. Each behaves differently, drops unique materials, and requires different tools to interact with.

Category Examples Tool Required Primary Drops 🦌 Hoofed Sernuk, Elder Sernuk Hunting Bow Meat, Hide, Antler 🐰 Small Mammals Chapaa, Muujin, Proudhorn Hunting Bow / Sneak Meat, Fur, Tail 🐛 Bugs Paper Lantern Bug, Scrap Bug, Garden Ladybug Bug Net Chitin, Silk, Parts 🐦 Birds Phoenixfire, Azurite Hopper Bug Net Feathers, Carapace 🐟 Aquatic All fish species Fishing Rod Fish, Roe

docs.title: Sernuk
docs.text:
The most common large herbivore in Palia. Sernuks graze in open fields across both Kilima Valley and Bahari Bay. They're alert and will flee if they sense you approaching. An arrow to the body will bring one down, but a headshot yields star-quality materials that sell for more.

  • Locations: Kilima Valley meadows, Bahari Bay grasslands
  • Drops: Raw Meat, Sernuk Hide, Sernuk Antler
  • Star Quality: Headshot kills produce star-quality drops (+50% sell price)
  • Best Time: Any time of day

Tip: Sneak upwind of sernuk by crouching (Left Ctrl on PC) and approach from behind. Getting within 15 meters before drawing your bow guarantees a clean headshot.

docs.title: Chapaa
docs.text:
These fluffy, squirrel-like creatures are fast and small, making them tricky targets. Chapaas are most active during the day and can be found in wooded areas. They're also the stars of the Chapaa Chase event during Majii Market.

  • Locations: Kilima Valley forests, Bahari Bay brush
  • Drops: Raw Meat, Chapaa Fur, Chapaa Tail
  • Star Quality: Headshot bonus applies
  • Behavior: Runs in zigzag patterns when spooked

docs.title: Muujin
docs.text:
The Muujin is a deer-like creature with a distinctive cry. They're slightly larger than sernuk and drop higher-quality materials. The Proudhorn is a rare Muujin variant with impressive antlers that only spawns in specific conditions.

  • Locations: Bahari Bay (ruins areas)
  • Drops: Raw Meat, Muujin Hide, Muujin Antler
  • Rare Variant: Proudhorn — drops Proudhorn Antler (used in high-end recipes)

docs.title: Elder Sernuk
docs.text:
A larger, paler variant of the standard sernuk. Elder Sernuks are slower but much tougher, requiring multiple arrows to bring down. They spawn less frequently but drop better materials.

  • Locations: Bahari Bay (rare spawn)
  • Drops: Raw Meat, Elder Sernuk Hide, Sernuk Antler
  • HP: Roughly 3× that of a normal sernuk
  • Reward: Elder Sernuk Hide sells for 3× the price of regular hide

docs.title: Tools & Upgrades
docs.text: Tool Skill Required Damage Range Notes Makeshift Bow Hunting Lv. 1 Low Short Starting bow Standard Bow Hunting Lv. 3 Medium Medium First upgrade Fine Bow Hunting Lv. 7 High Long Good damage, good range Exquisite Bow Hunting Lv. 10 Very High Very Long Best in slot Dispel Bow Quest Unlock High + Special Long Dispel magic effects

docs.title: Hunting Tips
docs.text:
1. Always aim for the head. A headshot is an instant kill on most standard creatures and guarantees star-quality drops. Practice your aim on stationary targets in Kilima before hitting the field.

  2. Use slowdown arrows. Crafted using Sapwood and Stone, these arrows reduce the target's movement speed, making follow-up shots easier. Essential for Muujin and Elder Sernuk.

  3. Hunt at dawn and dusk. Creatures are most active during the transition hours (05:00-07:00 and 18:00-20:00). You'll find more spawns and larger groups.

  4. Don't sell raw meat too fast. You'll need bulk meat for cooking recipes, especially for high-value dishes like Sernuk Stew and Muujin Bahari. Only sell excess once you've stockpiled 50+ units.

  5. The Exquisite Bow is worth the grind. The damage jump from Fine to Exquisite is dramatic. You'll one-shot standard sernuk from much farther away, making your hunting trips twice as efficient.

docs.title: 🐕 Companion Pets
docs.text: Companion pets in Palia are cosmetic follow-alongs that trot beside you as you explore. Most are obtained through the Premium Store, though some are event rewards or Twitch drop bonuses.

docs.title: How to Get a Pet
docs.text:
• Premium Store: The primary source. Pets cost anywhere from 100-800 Palia Coins depending on rarity and animation complexity.
  • Majii Market: Limited-time pet rewards during event rotations.
  • Twitch Drops: Occasionally offered during live events and game anniversaries.
  • Referral Program: Some pets were available during the game's referral bonus events.

docs.title: Available Pets
docs.text:
Pet Name Type Source Special Animation 🦊 Fennec Fox Fox-like companion Premium Store Digs, jumps on rocks 🐱 Striped Cat Cat companion Premium Store Chases butterflies 🐶 Fluffy Dog Dog companion Early Access Bundle Fetches sticks 🦜 Palcat Cat-bird hybrid Majii Market exclusive Glides short distances 🐰 Glowbunny Glowing rabbit Seasonal event reward Leaves light trail 🦉 Scrafox Fox with scarf Twitch Drop Poses for screenshots

Tip: Pets are purely cosmetic — they don't help with gathering, combat, or farming. Buy the one whose animations you like most. The Fennec Fox has the most interactive animations of the standard store pets.

docs.title: Pet Care
docs.text:
Pets in Palia require zero maintenance. No feeding, no cleaning, no petting required. Once you've acquired a companion pet, you can:

  • Toggle visibility from your inventory or housing menu
  • Switch between owned pets freely — they're account-bound
  • Name your pet — each pet can be given a custom name
  • Pet interaction — press the interact key near your pet to trigger its special animation

Note: There are no pet-affection mechanics, no breeding, and no pet-based resource generation in Palia. If you're coming from Stardew Valley or Coral Island, treat Palia's pets as stylish flair rather than productive farmhands.

docs.title: 🏠 Housing Plot Wildlife
docs.text:
Your housing plot attracts wild creatures based on what you've built and planted. While you can't pen or domesticate them, having wildlife visit your plot adds life and atmosphere.

Attraction What Shows Up How to Attract Flower Garden Butterflies, Garden Ladybugs Plant 5+ flower crops Pond/Fountain Kilima Frog, Water Strider Bugs Build a water feature Fruit Trees Birds, Muujin (rare) Plant 3+ fruit trees Compost Bin Scrap Bugs, Dung Beetles Build compost bins Tall Grass Chapaas (occasional) Leave grass unmowed

docs.title: Wildlife Photography
docs.text:
Palia has a photography feature (Camera item) that lets you take pictures of wildlife. This is required for certain quests and is a fun side activity. Some creatures are trophy-photo targets for the "Palia Photographer" achievement.

Tips for wildlife photography: - Crouch before approaching — sudden movements scare subjects away - Use the zoom function to frame shots from a distance - The golden hour (in-game dawn/dusk) gives the best lighting for photos - Target star-quality creatures for the best photo scores

docs.title: 📊 Material Value Reference
docs.text:
Knowing what each animal drop is worth helps you decide what to sell vs. save for crafting.

Material Source Base Sell Price Used In Raw Meat Sernuk, Chapaa, Muujin 15 Cooking (many recipes) Sernuk Hide Sernuk 25 Furniture, Armor Sernuk Antler Sernuk (headshot) 60 Decorative furniture Chapaa Fur Chapaa 30 Soft furniture, Rugs Chapaa Tail Chapaa (rare drop) 80 Exotic furniture Muujin Hide Muujin 40 Durable furniture Muujin Antler Muujin (headshot) 90 Wall mounts, Decor Elder Sernuk Hide Elder Sernuk 75 High-end furniture Proudhorn Antler Proudhorn (rare) 200 Special recipes, Quests

Quick tip: Hide all your regular leather and meat. Crafting furniture for your housing plot almost always beats selling raw materials. The furniture you build can then raise your plot's Appeal score, which unlocks more housing upgrades.

docs.title: 🏭 Palia Artisan Goods Guide
docs.text: Processing your raw crops into artisan goods is the single biggest jump in income you'll make in Palia. A tomato that sells for 30 gold raw becomes pickled for 80+ gold. A handful of blueberries turns into jam worth triple the raw price. This guide covers every processing method, which crops to prioritize, and how to build a profit machine on your housing plot.

docs.title: ⚙️ Processing Equipment Overview
docs.text:
Palia has four main processing stations. Each converts raw inputs into higher-value outputs.

Machine Unlock Build Materials Processing Time What It Does 🫙 Preserves Jar Gardening Lv. 3 10 Copper Bar, 15 Sapwood, 5 Stone 45 min real-time Turns crops into pickled goods or jam 🌱 Seed Collector Gardening Lv. 4 5 Copper Bar, 10 Sapwood, 3 Ceramic 30 min real-time Harvested crop → 3 seeds + bonus crop 💩 Compost Bin Gardening Lv. 2 10 Stone, 5 Sapwood Continuous Spoils/crops → Quality Fertilizer 🍳 Cooking Station Starting recipe 3 Stone, 2 Sapwood Instant Crops + meat → Cooked dishes

Note: Palia's processing system is simpler than games like Stardew Valley or Coral Island. You won't find kegs, cheese presses, or mayo machines here. The Preserves Jar is your primary value-adding machine, and it's incredibly effective.

docs.title: 🫙 Preserves Jar — Pickling & Jam
docs.text: The Preserves Jar is your best friend for turning crops into gold. Stick a crop in, wait 45 minutes real-time, and collect a pickled version worth 2.5-3× the base price.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
• Insert any vegetable or fruit crop (single unit)
  • Processing takes 45 minutes real-time (not in-game time)
  • Output: Pickled Vegetable or Fruit Jam for that specific crop
  • Output sell price = roughly 2.5× the raw crop's base sell price
  • The jar is single-input — one crop at a time per jar

Tip: The Preserves Jar processes in real-world time, not in-game time. A 45-minute cycle means during a typical gaming session you can run 2-3 rounds. Start processing before you go mining, and come back to a finished batch.

docs.title: Profit Table — All Preservable Crops
docs.text:
Sorted by profit per processing cycle.

Input Crop Raw Price Preserved Price Profit Gain Multiplier 🍅 Tomato 30 85 +55 ×2.8 🫐 Blueberry 100 260 +160 ×2.6 🍇 Grape 130 340 +210 ×2.6 🌾 Rice 75 195 +120 ×2.6 🌽 Corn 50 130 +80 ×2.6 🫘 Potato 25 70 +45 ×2.8 🥕 Carrot 15 45 +30 ×3.0 🧅 Onion 35 90 +55 ×2.6 🌶️ Pepper 60 155 +95 ×2.6 🍎 Apple 120 310 +190 ×2.6 🍑 Peach 150 380 +230 ×2.5 🍒 Cherry 180 460 +280 ×2.6

docs.title: Best Preserves Jar Crops
docs.text:
By raw profit per cycle: 1. 🍈 Cherry — 280 gold profit per jar cycle 2. 🍑 Peach — 230 gold profit 3. 🍇 Grape — 210 gold profit 4. 🍎 Apple — 190 gold profit

By efficiency (profit ÷ grow time): 1. 🍅 Tomato — high turnover (12h grow, multi-harvest) 2. 🥕 Carrot — fastest grow time (6h) 3. 🌽 Corn — good balance (14h grow) 4. 🫐 Blueberry — set-and-forget (24h grow, multi-harvest)

Tip: Build at least 3 Preserves Jars as soon as you can. Having multiple jars running in parallel lets you process a full garden harvest before the next one comes in. 5 jars is the mid-game sweet spot; 10+ for late-game optimization.

docs.title: 🌱 Seed Collector
docs.text: The Seed Collector lets you turn one harvested crop into three seeds plus a chance at an extra bonus crop. This is how you become self-sufficient and stop buying seeds from the vendor.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
• Input: 1 harvested crop (any type)
  • Output: 3 seeds of that crop + chance for a bonus crop
  • Processing time: 30 minutes real-time
  • The type of seed produced matches the input crop

docs.title: When to Use the Seed Collector
docs.text: Situation Should You Seed It? Early game (need gold) ❌ Sell raw instead Mid game (steady gold) ✅ Seed high-value crops Late game (optimizing) ✅ Seed everything, never buy Event-limited seeds ✅ ALWAYS seed rare seeds Star-quality crops ✅⚠️ See below

docs.title: Seed or Sell — Quick Decision
docs.text:
Crop Raw Price Seeds Produced Seed Vendor Cost Save per Cycle Carrot (15g) 15 3 seeds 10 each → 30 +15 gold saved Tomato (30g) 30 3 seeds 25 each → 75 +45 gold saved Blueberry (100g) 100 3 seeds 50 each → 150 +50 gold saved Grape (130g) 130 3 seeds 90 each → 270 +140 gold saved

The math is simple: If you plan to plant that crop again, seed it. The seed value produced always exceeds the raw sell price for mid-to-high-value crops.

Tip: Always seed starfruit and event seeds. You can't reliably buy them from vendors, and losing a seed cycle means waiting for the next event or rare drop. One starfruit turned into 3 seeds is 3 future harvests you don't have to gamble for.

docs.title: 💩 Quality Fertilizer & Star Quality
docs.text: Fertilizer boosts your crop quality, and star-quality crops sell for roughly 1.5× the base price. Getting into the fertilizer loop early pays off massively.

docs.title: Fertilizer Types
docs.text: Fertilizer Effect How to Get 🟢 Quality Fertilizer +25% chance for star-quality harvest Compost Bin (spoiled crops), Vendor 🟡 Rich Fertilizer +1 bonus harvest yield Crafting recipe (Gardening Lv. 6) 🟣 Weaved Fertilizer Combines Quality + Rich effects Special recipe (Gardening Lv. 9) 🔵 Harvest Boost Fertilizer -20% grow time Recipe, Premium Store (rare)

docs.title: The Fertilizer Loop
docs.text:
1. Grow crops → harvest → keep the good ones
  2. Spoiled crops or low-quality harvests go in the Compost Bin
  3. Compost Bin produces Quality Fertilizer over time
  4. Apply Quality Fertilizer to your next batch of seeds
  5. Higher star-quality harvest → more gold or better seeds
  6. Repeat

This loop is especially powerful with multi-harvest crops like Tomatoes, Blueberries, and Grapes. One application of Quality Fertilizer benefits every regrowth cycle.

docs.title: Star Quality Price Boost
docs.text: Crop Base Price Star Quality Price Bonus Tomato 30 45 +15 Blueberry 100 150 +50 Grape 130 195 +65 Apple 120 180 +60 Peach 150 225 +75 Cherry 180 270 +90 Starfruit 500 750 +250

docs.title: 🏆 Artisan Profit Comparison Table
docs.text:
Which processing method gives the best return on each crop?

Crop Raw Sell Preserves Jar Cooked (Best Dish) Best Method 🥕 Carrot 15 45 60 (Sernuk Stew) ⭐ Cook (bulk) 🍅 Tomato 30 85 70 (Tomato Soup) ⭐ Preserves 🫐 Blueberry 100 260 200 (Blueberry Tart) ⭐ Preserves 🍇 Grape 130 340 280 (Grape Juice) ⭐ Preserves 🍎 Apple 120 310 250 (Baked Apple) ⭐ Preserves 🍑 Peach 150 380 310 (Peach Cobbler) ⭐ Preserves 🍒 Cherry 180 460 380 (Cherry Pie) ⭐ Preserves 🌾 Rice 75 195 160 (Rice Cake) ⭐ Preserves

Verdict: The Preserves Jar beats cooking for almost every crop. Cooking is better for meat-based dishes where you combine hunted materials with a few crops.

docs.title: Early Game (Gardening Lv. 3-5)
docs.text: Machine Quantity Purpose 🫙 Preserves Jar 1-2 Process best crops 🌱 Seed Collector 1 Start seed multiplication 💩 Compost Bin 1 Make fertilizer

docs.title: Mid Game (Gardening Lv. 6-8)
docs.text: Machine Quantity Purpose 🫙 Preserves Jar 3-5 Primary income 🌱 Seed Collector 2 Full seed self-sufficiency 💩 Compost Bin 2 Steady fertilizer supply 🍳 Cooking Station (Lv. 2) 1 Quest dishes & buff food

docs.title: Late Game (Gardening Lv. 9-10)
docs.text: Machine Quantity Purpose 🫙 Preserves Jar 8-12 Process entire harvest 🌱 Seed Collector 3 Never buy seeds again 💩 Compost Bin 2-3 Fertilizer for all plots 🍳 Cooking Station (Lv. 3) 1 High-end dishes

docs.title: 💡 Artisan Tips Summary
docs.text:
1. Start with one Preserves Jar. Build it as soon as you hit Gardening Lv. 3. Processing one tomato into pickled tomato pays for the jar's materials in 2 cycles.

  2. Don't process everything. Low-value crops (Carrots, Potatoes) are better sold raw or used in cooking. Focus your jars on Blueberries, Tomatoes, Grapes, and tree fruits.

  3. Seed your own high-value crops. Never buy Grape or Starfruit seeds from the vendor once you have a Seed Collector. Self-sufficiency is how you scale.

  4. Quality Fertilizer on multi-harvest crops. One application of Quality Fertilizer on a Tomato plant boosts every harvest for that plant's entire life. Insane value.

  5. Run jars in batches. With 3+ jars, start them all at the same time. 45 minutes later, collect them all at once. Batch processing is more efficient than staggered single-jar runs.

  6. Star-quality preserves sell for more. Base preserves are already 2.5× raw value. Star-quality preserves from star-quality inputs sell for ~1.5× on top of that. Always fertilize your preserves-jar crops.

Final word: The Preserves Jar is the single best investment in Palia. It costs a few copper bars and sapwood to build, and it turns your garden into a gold-printing machine. Build one early, build more as you grow, and never sell raw crops again.

docs.title: Palia Quick Tips for Beginners
docs.text: New to Kilima Valley? These six tips will save you time, gold, and frustration as you settle into Palia.

docs.title: 1. Keep Everything (At First)
docs.text: It's tempting to sell items for quick gold, but nearly every resource has a use — whether for crafting, gifting, or completing quest bundles. Stash everything in your storage chest and only sell when you genuinely need the cash.

docs.title: 2. Talk to Everyone Daily
docs.text: Each villager resets their daily conversation and gift limit, and chatting costs nothing. Building friendship unlocks quests, recipes, and even free tools, so make the rounds whenever you log in.

docs.title: 3. Upgrade Your Axe and Pick First
docs.text: Better tools mine rarer ores and chop larger trees, which gates nearly every crafting progression. Prioritize copper upgrades for your axe and pick before spending resources on a better hoe or watering can.

docs.title: 4. Join a Community
docs.text: Palia is kinder with friends. Calling out rare resources like flow trees or palium in chat lets everyone strike it for shared loot — and asking for help with cooking or hunting makes those activities far more efficient.

docs.title: 5. Watch Your Focus
docs.text: Focus acts as an XP multiplier for every skill action. Cook meals at your campfire and eat before gathering, mining, or hunting to level up noticeably faster. Renown can be spent at the Phoenix Shrine to increase your focus pool.

docs.title: 6. Unlock Fast Travel Early
docs.text: The stable board in town sells fast-travel points for a modest gold fee. Unlocking the major zones early saves you endless running and makes daily errand loops much more pleasant.

docs.title: 🍳 Palia Cooking & Recipes Complete Guide
docs.text: Cooking is the most strategically deep skill in Palia. Food provides Focus, which directly determines your experience gain multiplier. This guide covers everything from unlocking stations to endgame profit strategies.

docs.title: 🔧 Cooking Station Unlocks
docs.text:
Station Unlock Method Use Campfire Learning the Ropes quest reward Basic cooking (grilled meat, grilled fish, tea) Prep Station Reth's Guild Store — 1,500 gold Chopping, kneading, prepping ingredients Standard Stove Reth's Guild Store — 2,500 gold Stews, stir-fries, soups Standard Oven Reth's Guild Store — 2,000 gold Baking (pies, bread, lasagna) Mixing Station Reth's Guild Store — 1,800 gold Juices, smoothies, desserts

Tip: Prioritize the Prep Station + Standard Stove — these two cover the most high-profit recipes. The Campfire is fine for basic leveling up to Lv 5.

docs.title: 📊 Best Recipe Guide
docs.text: Recipe Rarity Main Ingredients Focus Value Sell Price Key Effect Grilled Meat Common Raw Meat×2 25 24 Basic satiety Fish Stew Common Any Fish + Spices + Garlic 50 55 🎣 +Fishing aim speed Meaty Stir Fry Rare Meat + Veg + Rice + Oil 150 180 ⛏️ +Mining efficiency Celebration Cake Epic Blueberry + Butter + Egg + Flour + Fruit 375 420 ❤️ Best gifting Muujin Bahari Epic Meat + Spices + Veg + Oil + Egg 500 650 💪 All-stat boost Chapaa Masala Epic Meat + Milk + Spices + Tomato 350 410 🌿 +Foraging range Bouillabaisse Rare Bass + Oyster + Potato + Onion 200 240 🎣 +Fishing quality Blueberry Pie Rare Butter + Flour + Blueberry×2 220 260 🎁 NPC friendship Sushi Variable Any Fish + Rice + Seaweed 100 135 ❤️ Multiple NPCs' loved gift

docs.title: 🔵 Focus System
docs.text:
Focus is Palia's unique experience multiplier system:

Focus State XP Multiplier Notes No Focus 1× No bonus XP Low Focus (<50%) 1.25× Small bonus Medium Focus (50-80%) 1.5× Decent bonus High Focus (>80%) 2× Maximum bonus Focus Cap — Increases with skill level

Core Mechanics: - Eating cooked food gives Focus points - The higher your Focus, the higher the XP multiplier for all skills - Focus naturally drains over time (any skill action consumes it) - Keeping Focus >80% = leveling speed doubled

Tip: Before grinding a skill, eat a high-Focus food (like Celebration Cake at +375 Focus), then dive into skill actions to maximize XP per minute.

docs.title: 🎯 Best Food Per Activity
docs.text: Activity Recommended Food Reason ⛏️ Mining Meaty Stir Fry (+Mining efficiency) Faster mining speed 🌿 Foraging Chapaa Masala (+Foraging range) Gather more per swing 🎣 Fishing Fisherman's Brew / Fish Stew Better aim speed and catch zone 🏹 Hunting Grilled Meat (low cost, high Focus) Mass-producible, keeps Focus up 🌱 Gardening Any Fruit Pie Balances Focus and gift utility ❤️ Gifting Celebration Cake / Blueberry Pie Almost universally loved

docs.title: ⭐ Quality System (Ingredient Quality)
docs.text:
Ingredient Quality Effect on Dish How to Obtain Normal Ingredients Base dish quality Standard gathering/fishing/hunting ⭐ Starred Ingredients Raises chance of starred dish High skill, perfect execution, Honey Lure Mixed Quality Depends on average quality One starred + three normal = moderate chance

Key Rules: - Using starred ingredients gives your finished dish a higher chance of being starred too - Starred dishes sell for 1.5× and give bigger NPC friendship boosts - To consistently make starred dishes, use all starred ingredients

Tip: When batch cooking, count your starred ingredients first. If you don't have enough for a full set, consider selling starred ingredients individually (1.5×) rather than mixing them into normal dishes.

docs.title: 🎭 Story Cooking Quests
docs.text: Quest Name Required Dish Rewards Cooking 101 Grilled Fish / Grilled Meat Cooking XP, basic recipes Half Boiled Specific cooked food Rare ingredients The Great Human Bake-Off Macaron Quest chain + decorations Lost in the Wind Akwinduu Chapaa Rare recipe + XP Various NPC Cooking Requests Multiple dishes Friendship + Gold + Exclusive recipes

docs.title: 💰 Profit Analysis
docs.text:
Dish Ingredient Cost Sell Price Net Profit Profit Margin Grilled Meat Meat×2 (~30) 24 -6 ❌ Loss Sushi Fish+Rice (~50) 135 85 170% ✅ Blueberry Pie Blueberry×2+Flour+Butter (~160) 260 100 62% ✅ Celebration Cake Many ingredients (~200-250) 420 170-220 68-85% ✅ Muujin Bahari Meat+Veg+Spices (~300) 650 350 116% ✅ Fish Stew Fish+Spices+Garlic (~80) 55 -25 ❌ Loss

Tip: Celebration Cake is the best value dish — not too hard to make, sells well, and is a universal loved gift for all NPCs. Muujin Bahari is the best late-game gold-making dish. Note that Fish Stew and Grilled Meat cost more than their sell price — use them for leveling and Focus, not for profit.

docs.title: Gold-Making Cooking Flow
docs.text:
1. Lv 1-5: Mass-produce Grilled Meat / Grilled Fish → fast leveling, don't worry about profit
  2. Lv 5-8: Make Sushi / Blueberry Pie → start turning a profit
  3. Lv 8-10+: Mass-produce Celebration Cake → great for self-gifting and selling
  4. Lv 10+ Group Cooking: Organize Cake Parties — divide labor to produce 50-100 cakes per session

docs.title: Palia — Crop Profit Guide
docs.text:
Optimize your garden layout and crop selection in Palia with this complete profit guide. Unlike most farming games, Palia uses a garden-plot grid system where crops can be planted individually or in rows, and quality (star value) affects sell prices.

Note: All prices shown are base (non-star) quality. Star-quality crops sell for roughly 1.5× base price. Gardening skill level and fertilizer can also boost yield and quality.

docs.title: Crop Profit Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Price (Gold) Growth (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price Profit/Plot Carrot 10 24h 1 18 8 Potato 15 48h 1 33 18 Tomato 20 48h 2 16 each 12 Onion 12 36h 1 25 13 Corn 25 60h 2 20 each 15 Rice 15 36h 1 28 13 Wheat 10 24h 1 20 10 Blueberry 30 72h 3 15 each 15 Cotton 20 48h 2 18 each 16 Pepper 18 48h 2 15 each 12 Lettuce 8 18h 1 14 6 Cabbage 22 48h 1 45 23 Bok Choy 14 30h 1 28 14 Spinach 12 24h 1 24 12 Ginger 35 72h 1 65 30 Mushroom 30 72h 2 28 each 26

docs.text: Crop Growth Profit/Plot Profit/Hour Onion 36h 13 0.36 Bok Choy 30h 14 0.47 Rice 36h 13 0.36 Potato 48h 18 0.38 Tomato 48h 12 0.25 Cabbage 48h 23 0.48 Cotton 48h 16 0.33 Pepper 48h 12 0.25 Corn 60h 15 0.25

docs.title: Star Quality Value Multiplier
docs.text:
Quality Price Multiplier Example: Carrot ⭐ Base (No Star) 1.0× 18 Gold ⭐⭐ Star Quality ~1.5× 27 Gold ⭐⭐⭐ Rare Star ~2.0× 36 Gold

Star quality is random but can be increased with: - Quality Up Fertilizer — Boosts star quality chance by 25% - Gardening Skill Level — Each level slightly increases base quality chance - Special Soil Plots — Some home plot layouts have natural quality bonuses

docs.title: Gardening Level Rewards
docs.text: Level Unlock 1 Basic Soil Plots (3×3), Carrot Seeds 2 Potato Seeds, Watering Can Upgrade 3 Onion Seeds, Cotton Seeds 4 Extra Soil Plot (unlock 4th row) 5 Tomato Seeds, Quality Up Fertilizer 6 Rice Seeds, Bok Choy Seeds 7 Extra Soil Plot (unlock 5th row) 8 Corn Seeds, Pepper Seeds 9 Blueberry Seeds, Mushroom Seeds 10 Ginger Seeds, Deluxe Fertilizer

docs.title: Best Crops for Each Play Style
Early Game (Levels 1–4)
docs.text: Priority Crop Why 1st Carrot Fastest turnaround, cheap seeds 2nd Wheat Quick cash + cooking ingredient 3rd Onion Decent profit, 36h growth 4th Potato Best early profit-per-plot

docs.title: Mid Game (Levels 5–8)
docs.text: Priority Crop Why 1st Cabbage Best profit-per-plot at 48h 2nd Bok Choy Fast medium profit 3rd Cotton High yield (2 per harvest) 4th Corn Good multi-harvest crop

docs.title: Late Game (Levels 9–10)
docs.text: Priority Crop Why 1st Ginger Highest profit-per-plot (30 Gold) 2nd Mushroom Second best at 26 Gold/plot 3rd Blueberry Multi-harvest, good for gifts 4th Cabbage Still top-tier for 48h cycle

docs.title: Gardening Tips
docs.text:
• Watering: Crops only grow when watered. Unwatered crops pause growth but don't die.
  • Weeds: Weeds spawn randomly on unplanted soil plots. Remove them before they spread.
  • Fertilizer: Quality Up Fertilizer is best for high-base-price crops (Ginger, Cabbage, Mushroom).
  • Multi-harvest: Tomato, Corn, Cotton, Pepper, and Blueberry produce multiple items per harvest — great for cooking ingredients.
  • Gifting: Star-quality Blueberries are a liked gift for many NPCs; Star-quality Sweet Leaf is a weekly want for some.
  • Plot expansion: Invest in extra soil plots as soon as you level up — more plots = more gold.

docs.text:
