docs.title: Palia Crops Database
docs.text: Gardening is one of the most rewarding and profitable skills in Palia. Whether you are a new player looking to make your first gold or a seasoned farmer optimizing your plot for maximum efficiency, understanding each crop’s grow time, seed cost, and profit per harvest is essential. This database covers every crop currently available in Palia, organized by category, with detailed tables and practical tips to help you plan your farm.

docs.title: Crop Categories Overview
docs.text: Palia crops are divided into three main categories: Vegetables, Fruits, and Special Crops. Each category has unique growth mechanics, seed costs, and harvest yields. Vegetables are the most common and form the backbone of any farm. Fruits require more time but often yield higher profits per harvest. Special crops include rare or event-specific plants that can be used for cooking, gifting, or selling.

docs.title: Vegetable Crops
docs.text:
Vegetables are the foundation of Palia gardening. They grow quickly, have lower seed costs, and are excellent for generating consistent income. Below is the complete list of vegetable crops with their key stats.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Carrot 10 6 2 15 20 Potato 15 8 2 25 35 Onion 20 10 2 35 50 Tomato 25 12 3 30 65 Corn 30 14 2 50 70 Pepper 35 16 2 60 85 Rice 40 18 2 75 110 Wheat 20 8 3 20 40

Notes on Vegetables: - Carrots and Potatoes are ideal for early-game gold due to their short grow times. - Tomatoes and Corn offer the best profit per hour when planted in bulk. - Wheat is unique because it yields 3 units per harvest, making it excellent for cooking recipes.

docs.title: Fruit Crops
docs.text:
Fruits take longer to grow but produce higher-value items. Many fruits are also used in advanced cooking recipes or as gifts for villagers. Fruit crops often require trellises or specific garden layouts.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Blueberry 50 24 2 100 150 Apple 60 30 2 120 180 Peach 70 36 2 150 230 Cherry 80 40 2 180 280 Grape 90 48 3 130 300

Notes on Fruits: - Grapes are the most profitable fruit per harvest, but they require the longest grow time. - Blueberries are a great mid-game crop with a reasonable 24-hour grow time. - Fruits are often used in juice-making or jam recipes, which can double their value.

docs.title: Special Crops
docs.text:
Special crops include rare seeds obtained from events, quests, or high-level gardening. These crops often have unique properties, such as being used for special recipes or having a chance to produce multiple harvests.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Starfruit 150 72 1 500 350 Mushroom 100 48 2 200 300 Spice Sprout 80 36 2 160 240 Cotton 40 20 3 50 110

Notes on Special Crops: - Starfruit is the highest-value single crop but has a long grow time and high seed cost. It is best used for gifting or cooking. - Mushrooms are a reliable source of income for mid-to-late game players. - Cotton is essential for crafting fabric and furniture, making it valuable beyond just selling.

docs.title: Profit Analysis and Best Crops by Strategy
Best Crops for Early Game (First 20 Hours)
docs.text:
If you are just starting out, focus on crops with low seed costs and fast grow times. Carrots and Potatoes are your best friends. Plant as many as your energy allows, and reinvest your profits into more seeds.

  • Carrot: 6-hour grow time, 20 gold profit per harvest. You can plant and harvest 4 times in a single day.
  • Potato: 8-hour grow time, 35 gold profit per harvest. Slightly better profit per hour than carrots.

docs.title: Best Crops for Mid Game (20-50 Hours)
docs.text:
Once you have a steady gold flow, switch to higher-profit crops like Tomatoes, Corn, and Blueberries. These crops offer better returns per garden plot.

  • Tomato: 12-hour grow time, 65 gold profit per harvest. Great for cooking and selling.
  • Blueberry: 24-hour grow time, 150 gold profit per harvest. Perfect for daily harvest cycles.

docs.title: Best Crops for Late Game (50+ Hours)
docs.text:
Late-game players should focus on Grapes, Starfruit, and Mushrooms. These crops require patience but yield the highest profits and are essential for end-game recipes.

  • Grape: 48-hour grow time, 300 gold profit per harvest. Best profit per plot.
  • Starfruit: 72-hour grow time, 350 gold profit per harvest. Ideal for special dishes.

docs.title: Watering and Fertilizer
docs.text: All crops require regular watering. You can use a watering can or build sprinklers to automate the process. Fertilizer can be applied to reduce grow time or increase yield. Quality fertilizer reduces grow time by 20%, while Rich Fertilizer increases harvest yield by 1 unit.

docs.title: Crop Rotation and Soil Quality
docs.text: Soil quality degrades over time. Planting the same crop repeatedly reduces yield. Rotate your crops to maintain soil health. Using fertilizer also helps replenish soil nutrients.

docs.title: Multi-Harvest Crops
docs.text: Some crops, like Tomatoes and Grapes, can be harvested multiple times. After the initial harvest, the plant will regrow in half the original grow time. This makes them extremely efficient for long-term farming.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Start small: Focus on Carrots and Potatoes until you have at least 500 gold.
  • Automate early: Build sprinklers as soon as possible to save time and energy.
  • Use fertilizer: Quality Fertilizer on high-value crops like Grapes can significantly boost your daily profit.
  • Sell raw or cook: Cooking ingredients into meals often doubles or triples their value. Check recipes before selling. | - Gift wisely: Some villagers love specific crops. Gifting a Starfruit to a villager can increase friendship faster than any other gift. | - Plan your layout: Leave space for trellises if you plan to grow fruits like Grapes. A well-organized garden saves time and maximizes space. | - Check the market: Crop prices fluctuate daily. Sell when prices are high for maximum profit.

docs.title: Additional Crops (Cross-Reference)
docs.text:
The following crops are available in Palia but may not appear in every seed vendor's inventory. They are unlocked through Gardening level progression or special sources.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Lettuce 8 18 1 14 6 Cabbage 22 48 1 45 23 Bok Choy 14 30 1 28 14 Spinach 12 24 1 24 12 Ginger 35 72 1 65 30

Notes on Additional Crops: - Lettuce is the fastest-growing crop at just 18 hours — ideal for quick turnaround plots. - Cabbage offers the best profit-per-hour among 48-hour crops. - Ginger ties with Starfruit as the highest profit-per-plot crop at 30 Gold. - Bok Choy and Spinach are solid mid-game options with moderate grow times and decent margins.

Data note: There are minor differences between community sources for some crop stats. The tables above represent the most commonly accepted values. Star-quality crops sell for ~1.5x base price.

docs.title: Early Game Combo (Gardening Lv 1-4)
docs.text:
Plot Slots Crop Mix Total Cost Est. Daily Profit Why 9 (3×3) 5 Carrot + 4 Wheat 130 ~180 Fast turnaround, cooking staples 12 (3×4) 6 Carrot + 3 Wheat + 3 Potato 165 ~260 Balanced profit and cooking use

Strategy: Plant in the evening before logging off. Carrots (6h) are ready by morning, Wheat (8h) by midday. Harvest, sell, reinvest.

docs.title: Mid Game Combo (Gardening Lv 5-8)
docs.text:
Plot Slots Crop Mix Total Cost Est. Daily Profit Why 20 (4×5) 8 Tomato + 6 Corn + 6 Cabbage 582 ~500 Multi-harvest + high profit 20 (4×5) 10 Cotton + 5 Bok Choy + 5 Rice 660 ~550 Cotton for crafting + food

Strategy: Tomatoes and Corn give you multi-harvest — plant once, harvest repeatedly. Cabbage gives the best single-harvest profit. Cotton is essential for furniture crafting.

docs.title: Late Game Combo (Gardening Lv 9-10)
docs.text:
Plot Slots Crop Mix Total Cost Est. Daily Profit Why 20 (4×5) 6 Ginger + 6 Mushroom + 8 Blueberry 810 ~1,100 Highest profit per plot 20 (4×5) 8 Grape + 6 Starfruit + 6 Spice Sprout 1,720 ~1,600 End-game max efficiency

Strategy: Ginger + Mushroom + Blueberry is your daily gold machine. Grape + Starfruit is for long-term investors (72h cycles). Use Quality Fertilizer on Ginger for star-quality bonuses.

docs.title: The "Set It and Forget It" Combo
docs.text:
For players who can only log in once per day:

Crop Why Blueberry (24h) Harvest once daily, multi-harvest Cabbage (48h) Harvest every other day, best profit/plot Tomato (12h) Can stretch to 24h without losing crop Rice (18h) Flexible timing, decent profit

Plant a 50/50 mix of Blueberries and Cabbages for ~800 Gold/day with minimal effort.

docs.title: Star Quality Optimization
docs.text:
Maximizing star-quality crops significantly boosts income:

  1. Quality Up Fertilizer on high-base-price crops (Ginger, Cabbage, Mushroom)
  2. Gardening skill levels passively increase star quality chance
  3. Special soil plots — home plot layouts with natural bonuses
  4. Sell star-quality crops individually — don't cook them unless a recipe specifically needs star ingredients

Estimated income uplift: A full plot of Ginger with Quality Fertilizer yields ~3,000+ Gold/week vs. ~1,500 without.

docs.title: 🎪 Palia Festivals & Events Guide
docs.text: Palia's calendar is full of celebrations that bring the community together. From the rotating Majii Market to special holiday events, there's almost always something happening. This guide breaks down every event, its schedule, and exactly how to make the most of it.

docs.title: 📅 Event System Overview
docs.text:
Palia runs on a live-service event model. Most events run for a fixed duration (7-21 days) and rotate throughout the year. Events fall into three main categories:

Event Type Frequency Duration Examples 🌙 Majii Market Monthly rotation 7 days Majii Market (different themes each month) 🎉 Seasonal Festivals Quarterly 14-21 days Spring Fling, Summer Festival, Fall Feast, Winterlights ⭐ Special Events Occasional 7-14 days Twitch Drops, Anniversary Events, New Content Celebrations 🎁 Login Events Monthly Varies Daily login rewards, Check-in bonuses

Tip: Events are on real-world time, not in-game time. If an event says "June 15-21," it means real dates. Set reminders in your phone so you don't miss limited-time rewards.

docs.title: 🗓️ Event Calendar
docs.text:
This calendar is based on Palia's recurring event schedule. Dates may shift slightly year to year, but the general pattern stays consistent.

Month Event Name Duration Type Key Reward 🎊 January Winterlights Celebration Jan 1-21 Seasonal Winterlights Decor Set, Snowman Plush ❄️ February Lunar New Year Festival ~14 days Seasonal Zodiac Pet, Lantern Decor 🌸 March Spring Fling March 15-April 5 Seasonal Spring Blossom Furniture, Bunny Ears 🐰 April Egg Hunt April 7-21 Seasonal Decorated Eggs, Spring Pet 🌺 May Majii Market: Bloom 7 days Majii Bloom-exclusive furniture ☀️ June Summer Festival June 10-30 Seasonal Beach Furniture, Swimsuit Outfits 🌻 July Majii Market: Solstice 7 days Majii Solstice-exclusive decor 🎂 August Palia Anniversary ~7-14 days Special Anniversary Hat, Exclusive Pet 🍂 September Harvest Festival Sept 10-30 Seasonal Harvest Decor, Autumn Outfits 🎃 October Spooky Event Oct 15-31 Seasonal Halloween Decor, Costume Items 🦃 November Fall Feast Nov 11-25 Seasonal Feast Recipes, Cozy Furniture ❄️ December Winterlights + New Year Dec 15-Jan 5 Seasonal Full Winterlights Set, Fireworks

docs.title: How Events Are Announced
docs.text: Events are announced through: - In-game mail — Check your mailbox in Kilima Village - Social media — Palia's official Twitter/X and Discord - Patch notes — Major updates typically come with new events - Game launcher — News section on login screen

docs.title: 🌙 Majii Market — The Rotating Event
docs.text: The Majii Market is Palia's signature event. It's a traveling magical market that sets up near Kilima Village for one week each month. Each rotation has a unique theme with exclusive items.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
• The market is open 24/7 during its active week
  • Located on the outskirts of Kilima Village (follow the glowing lights at night)
  • Each rotation has a different theme with unique furniture and decor
  • Some vendors are permanent; others rotate with the theme
  • You can participate as much as you want — no daily limits on most activities

docs.title: Market Activities
docs.text: Activity Description Reward Type 🏃 Chapaa Chase Catch as many chapaas as you can in the pen Lucky Envelopes 🎯 Dart Game Hit targets with throwing darts Prize Tickets 🎣 Fishing Pond Fish in the market's special pond Lucky Envelopes 🎪 Show Tent Watch performances (interactive emote) Minor rewards 🛍️ Vendor Shopping Buy theme-exclusive items Gold, Event Currency

docs.title: Lucky Envelopes
docs.text:
The main currency of Majii Market. You earn them from activities and can redeem them at the Prize Booth for exclusive items.

How to earn Lucky Envelopes: - Chapaa Chase: 3-5 per round depending on performance - Market Fishing: 1-3 per catch - Daily login during Majii week: 2-5

Tip: Chapaa Chase is by far the fastest way to farm Lucky Envelopes. Learn the chapaas' paths and position yourself at a corner where they bunch up. A good run nets 5 envelopes in under 2 minutes.

docs.title: Majii Market Themes
docs.text:
Each month has a different theme. Themed items are exclusive to that rotation and may not return for months.

Theme Atmosphere Example Items 🌸 Bloom Cherry blossoms, pink lighting Bloom Gazebo, Pink Lantern Tree ☀️ Solstice Golden hour, sun motifs Sun Wheel, Golden Rays Rug 🍂 Harvest Autumn leaves, warm tones Harvest Arch, Cornucopia Centerpiece ❄️ Frost Ice crystals, blue lights Frost Chandelier, Ice Sculpture Bench

docs.title: Event-Only Items Worth Prioritizing
docs.text:
Not all event items are created equal. Here's what's actually worth your time:

S-Tier (must-get): - Winterlights Wreath — Large wall decoration, fits many themes - Majii Market Gazebo — Excellent outdoor centerpiece - Seasonal Pets — Limited-time pets are account-bound and never return to the store - Exclusive Outfits — Event outfit sets are often the best-looking cosmetics in the game

A-Tier (great to have): - Festival Recipes — Some event-exclusive recipes make great gifts or sell well - Holiday Decor Sets — Good for theme-specific housing builds - Emote Items — Fun for social screenshots

B-Tier (nice but skippable): - Banners and Flags — Niche use - Minor Decor — Only if you're building a specific theme - Consumables — Most are worse than what you can craft normally

docs.title: Prize Booth Tier List
docs.text: Prize Tier Cost (Envelopes) Typical Items Grab Priority 🏆 Grand Prize 50-80 Large furniture item, Pet ⭐⭐⭐ 🥇 Upper Tier 25-40 Medium decor, Outfit piece ⭐⭐ 🥈 Mid Tier 10-20 Small decor, Recipe ⭐ 🥉 Lower Tier 3-8 Wallpaper, Flooring 🟢 (if you have spare)

docs.title: Before the Event
docs.text:
1. Clear your quest log. Some events add time-sensitive quests. Having a full log means you might miss them.
  2. Stock up on gold. Many events have vendors that accept gold for event-specific items. Having 5,000-10,000 gold ready ensures you can buy everything.
  3. Free up housing storage. Event furniture can eat up slots fast. Clear out old items you don't need.
  4. Check the official announcement. Events sometimes have pre-event activities or login streaks that start before the main event begins.

docs.title: During the Event
docs.text:
1. Log in every day. Most events have a daily login reward track. Missing a day means missing a reward.
  2. Prioritize currency farming. Learn which activity gives the best event currency per minute and focus on it.
  3. Don't hoard envelopes/tickets. Unlike gold, event currencies usually expire or convert to useless items after the event ends. Spend them before the last day.
  4. Check the Prize Booth daily. Some events rotate their prize pool or add new items mid-event.

docs.title: After the Event
docs.text:
1. Unspent currency warning. Most event currencies convert to gold at a poor exchange rate (or just disappear). Spend everything before the event ends.
  2. Organize your new items. Place event furniture, move outfits to your wardrobe, and sort your inventory.
  3. Screenshot your setup. Events are temporary — capture your decorated housing plot while the items are fresh.

docs.title: Common Mistakes
docs.text:
• ❌ Waiting until the last day. Prize booths can sell out of the best items early in some events. Buy your most-wanted item first.
  • ❌ Skipping the tutorial quest. Each event has a quick intro quest that often gives bonus currency. Never skip it.
  • ❌ Spending gold on cosmetic consumables. Fireworks and temporary decorations are fun but vanish. Spend gold on permanent items first.
  • ✅ Trading with friends. Some event items are giftable. Coordinate with friends who play different events to cover each other's wishlists.

Tip: The best time to start Palia and catch an event is during a Majii Market week paired with a Seasonal Festival overlap. These double-event periods happen 2-3 times a year and offer the most dense reward schedule. The Majii Market + Winterlights overlap in December is unmissable.

docs.title: Palia — Fishing Guide
docs.text:
Complete fishing guide for Palia covering every fish species, including location, time of day, quality levels, and sell prices. Fishing is a great way to earn gold, complete bundles, and build relationships through gifting.

Note: Fishing levels unlock new rods, bait types, and zones. Higher fishing skill also increases the chance of catching rare and star-quality fish.

docs.title: Fishing Mechanics
docs.text: Mechanic Detail Rod Levels Makeshift Rod → Standard Rod → Fine Rod → Exquisite Rod Bait Types Worm (common), Glow Worm (uncommon), Honey Lure (rare) Bobbler Types Standard, Radon, Survey (each affects fish behavior) Fishing Zones Rivers, Lakes, Ponds, Coast/Ocean, Underground Time of Day Some fish only appear at Day (6AM–6PM) or Night (6PM–6AM) Weather Some fish are more common during rain

docs.title: Star Quality Prices
docs.text:
All fish can be caught in star quality (⭐), which sells for significantly more:

Star quality is determined by: - Fishing Level — Higher level = better star chance - Bait Quality — Honey Lures increase star quality chance significantly - Perfect Catch — Reeling in without the fish escaping gives a quality bonus - Bobbler — Survey Bobbler improves star quality odds

docs.title: Palia Fish Database — Complete Fish Catalog
docs.text: Welcome to the most comprehensive Palia fish database. Whether you’re a seasoned angler looking to complete your collection or a new player trying to finish a bundle, this guide covers every fish in the game. We’ve organized everything by location, bait type, and bundle relevance so you can spend less time guessing and more time reeling in the big ones.

docs.title: How Fishing Works in Palia
docs.text:
Fishing in Palia is a core skill that rewards you with food ingredients, quest items, and valuable resources. Each body of water has its own unique fish pool. Your success depends on three main factors:

  • Location: Rivers, lakes, ponds, and coastal waters each host different species.
  • Bait: Standard worms work for most fish, but glow worms and special bait are required for rare and epic catches.
  • Time of Day: Some fish only appear during the day (6:00–18:00), others at night (18:00–6:00), and a few are active 24/7.

You can also use fishing hooks and buffs from food to increase your catch rate, but the tables below assume no modifiers.

docs.title: Bundle Information
docs.text: Bundles are a major endgame goal in Palia. Completing them rewards you with unique furniture, recipes, and progression items. Here’s exactly which fish you need for each bundle.

docs.title: Fisherman’s Bundle
docs.text:
• Catfish (Kilima River, Night, Worm)
  • Longnose Gar (Bahari River, Night, Glow Worm)
  • 1 additional uncommon fish of your choice

Reward: Fishing Rod Stand (decorative item)

docs.title: Oceanic Bundle
docs.text:
• Giant Stingray (Kilima Coast, Any, Glow Worm)
  • Coral Trout (Bahari Coast, Day, Glow Worm)
  • 1 additional ocean fish of uncommon or higher rarity

Reward: Seashell Chime (wall decoration)

docs.title: Night Sky Bundle
docs.text:
• Void Ray (Kilima River, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Midnight Pike (Bahari River, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Glowing Koi (Underground Lake, Night, Glow Worm)

Reward: Starry Night Lantern (light source)

docs.title: Master Angler Bundle
docs.text:
• Kilima Sturgeon (Kilima River, Any, Glow Worm)
  • Ancient Fish (Kilima Coast, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Mythic Fish (Bahari Coast, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Crystal Carp (Underground Lake, Any, Glow Worm)

Reward: Master Angler Trophy (display item) + 5,000 Gold

docs.title: Bait Guide
docs.text:
Knowing which bait to use saves you time and resources.

  • Worm Bait: Standard bait. Catches all common and uncommon fish. Can be dug from the ground or purchased from the General Store for 10 Gold each.
  • Glow Worm Bait: Required for all rare and epic fish. Crafted using 1 Worm Bait + 1 Brightshroom. Brightshrooms are found in caves and near water at night.
  • Special Bait: Some limited-time events introduce unique bait, but glow worms are your primary tool for high-tier fishing.

Pro Tip: Always carry at least 50 glow worms when heading to Bahari Bay or the Underground. Rare fish appear more frequently in these zones, and you don’t want to waste a trip.

docs.title: Fishing Skill Progression
docs.text:
Your fishing level increases as you catch fish. Higher levels unlock new recipes, better rods, and the ability to catch rarer fish without as much struggle.

Level Unlock 1 Basic Rod, Worm Bait 3 Standard Rod (longer cast) 5 Glow Worm Bait recipe 7 Fine Rod (better reel speed) 10 Exquisite Rod (best stats) 15 Fishing Hook upgrades available

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
1. Always fish at night for epics. Every epic fish in Palia (except Crystal Carp) is only available between 18:00 and 6:00. Plan your fishing trips accordingly.
  2. Use glow worms for bundles. If you’re working on bundles, skip worm bait entirely. Glow worms are the only way to catch the rare and epic fish required.
  3. Fish in the Underground for variety. The Underground Lake is the only place to catch Glowing Koi and Crystal Carp, both needed for bundles. It’s also a great spot for leveling up.
  4. Don’t sell rare fish. Before selling any fish, check if it’s needed for a bundle. You’ll save yourself hours of frustration later.
  5. Eat fishing buff food. Dishes like Fisherman’s Brew (+2 Fishing) and Spicy Fish Stew (+3 Fishing) increase your catch rate and reduce the time fish take to bite. Always cook these before a serious fishing session.
  6. Upgrade your rod first. Spend your early gold on the Standard Rod, then the Fine Rod. The longer cast and faster reel speed make a huge difference when chasing rare fish.

Happy fishing, and may your line always be tight! For more Palia guides, check out our crop database and crafting recipes.

docs.title: Complete Friendship & Romance Guide for Palia
docs.text: Building relationships with the villagers of Palia is a core part of the game. Not only does it unlock heartwarming cutscenes and lore, but it also grants you essential recipes, furniture, and even the ability to romance multiple characters. This guide covers everything you need to know: from NPC gift preferences and weekly wants to romance levels and the fastest path to maxing out every relationship. For other Palia resources, check our Crops Guide, Fish Database, or Crop Profit Calculator.

docs.title: Beginner Tips for Friendship & Romance
docs.text:
Before diving into the specifics, here are the golden rules for efficient relationship building in Palia:

  1. Talk to Everyone Daily: Speaking to an NPC once per day gives a small friendship boost. It’s free and adds up over time.
  2. Gift Weekly Wants First: Every NPC has a "Weekly Want" that changes every Monday. Gifting this item gives a massive friendship bonus (usually 3x a normal liked gift). Prioritize these.
  3. Know Their Loves vs. Likes: "Love" gifts give the most points, "Like" gifts give moderate points, and "Dislike" gifts give nothing or negative points. Never give a disliked item.
  4. Use Cake Parties for High-Value Gifts: The best universal "Love" gifts are often high-tier cooked meals. Join a "Cake Party" (a player-organized event on the Palia Discord or in-game chat) to mass-produce cakes like Celebration Cake or Bundled Blueberry Pie with other players sharing ingredients and labor.
  5. Romance is Separate from Friendship: You can max friendship (Level 4) with everyone. Romance (Level 1-4) is an additional track that requires you to first reach friendship level 3 and then give a "Heartdrop Lily" to initiate.
  6. You Can Romance Everyone: Palia allows polyamory. You can romance every eligible NPC simultaneously without jealousy penalties.
  7. Focus on One NPC at a Time: For fastest results, focus your high-value gifts on one NPC per day until they are maxed, then move to the next.

docs.title: Friendship Levels (1-4)
docs.text:
• Level 1: Unlock basic dialogue and the ability to give gifts.
  • Level 2: Unlock the NPC's first friendship quest and a small reward (e.g., recipe, furniture).
  • Level 3: Unlock the ability to initiate romance (requires a Heartdrop Lily). Also unlocks a second friendship quest.
  • Level 4: Max friendship. Unlocks the final friendship quest and a significant reward (e.g., a rare recipe, a plushie, or a writ).

docs.title: Romance Levels (1-4)
docs.text:
• Level 1: Unlock the "Flirt" dialogue option and a pin.
  • Level 2: Unlock a romance-specific quest and a unique reward (e.g., a wall decoration).
  • Level 3: Unlock a second romance quest and a more personal reward (e.g., a portrait).
  • Level 4: Max romance. Unlocks the final romance quest and the ultimate reward (e.g., a unique outfit, a special emote, or a furniture set).

docs.title: Step 1: Stockpile High-Value Gifts
docs.text:
The single fastest way to gain friendship is to always give a "Love" gift. The best universal loves are:

  • Celebration Cake: Loved by almost everyone (Delaila, Kenyatta, Reth, Badruu, etc.).
  • Any Sushi: Loved by Ashura, Hassian, Hodari, Nai'o, Sifuu, Tau.
  • Any Gem (e.g., Emerald, Garnet): Loved by Eshe, Hekla, Jel, Jina, Najuma, Zeki.
  • Heartdrop Lily: Loved by Chayne and required to initiate romance.

Strategy: Focus on cooking high-tier meals. Join a Cake Party to get 50+ Celebration Cakes in one session. Alternatively, check the Palia Fish Database for rare fish locations to make sushi.

docs.title: Step 2: Prioritize Weekly Wants
docs.text:
Every Monday, check each NPC's weekly want. Gifting this item gives 3x the friendship points of a normal liked gift. If the weekly want is also a "Love" item, you get an even bigger boost.

Example: If Delaila's weekly want is "Any Pie" and you give her a Celebration Cake (which is a pie), you get a massive bonus.

docs.title: Step 3: Daily Routine
docs.text:
1. Talk to everyone (takes 5 minutes).
  2. Gift the weekly want to 2-3 NPCs per day (use your best items).
  3. Gift a "Love" item to the remaining NPCs.
  4. Complete quests as they appear. Quests give huge friendship boosts.

docs.title: Step 4: Romance Initiation
docs.text:
Once you reach friendship level 3 with an eligible NPC, you need a Heartdrop Lily to start the romance track. Heartdrop Lilies are rare flowers found in Bahari Bay (especially near the flooded steps) or purchased from the Underground Market (Zeki's secret shop) for 500 Gold each.

Tip: Farm Heartdrop Lilies by planting them in your garden. They take 5 days to grow but can be harvested repeatedly.

docs.title: Advanced Strategy: The "Cake Party" Method
docs.text:
This is the most efficient way to max all relationships.

  1. Gather Ingredients: You need Sweet Leaf, Blueberry, Wheat, Egg, Milk, and Butter.
  2. Join a Cake Party: Use the Palia Discord or in-game chat to find a group. Usually, one person provides the ingredients, others provide the fuel (coal) and cooking skills.
  3. Mass Produce: You can make 50-100 Celebration Cakes in one session.
  4. Distribute: Give one Celebration Cake to every NPC who loves it (most of them). This single gift will often jump them from Level 1 to Level 2 instantly.

Pro Tip: Save your Celebration Cakes for NPCs whose weekly want is a "Cooked Dessert" for an even bigger boost.

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text:
Q: Can I lose friendship or romance progress? A: No. Once gained, friendship and romance points never decay. You cannot lose levels.

Q: How many gifts can I give per day? A: You can give one gift per NPC per day (resets at 6:00 AM Palia time).

Q: Do I need to complete quests to progress romance? A: Yes. Each romance level requires completing a specific quest given by the NPC. These quests often involve gathering rare items or cooking specific meals.

Q: Can I date multiple people at once? A: Yes! Palia has no jealousy system. You can romance every eligible NPC simultaneously.

Q: What is the fastest way to get Heartdrop Lilies? A: Plant them in your garden. They are a perennial crop, meaning they regrow after harvest. Also check Zeki's Underground Market daily.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
Maxing out friendships and romances in Palia is a marathon, not a sprint, but with the right strategy, you can significantly speed up the process. Focus on weekly wants, stockpile high-value cooked meals, and talk to everyone daily. The rewards—from unique outfits to heartwarming cutscenes—are well worth the effort.

Now go out there, make some friends (and lovers), and enjoy all that Palia has to offer!

docs.title: 🏠 Cohabitation & Proudhorn Ceremony (Marriage Equivalent)
docs.text: Once you reach Romance Level 4 with an NPC, you can propose a Proudhorn Ceremony — Palia's equivalent of marriage or civil partnership.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: Requirement Detail Romance Level Level 4 with your chosen NPC Proudhorn Antler Crafted from 1 Proudhorn Antler (dropped by Proudhorn sernuks in Bahari Bay) Ceremony Location The gazebo near Kilima Lake Planning Time ~3 in-game days to prepare the ceremony

docs.title: What Cohabitation Unlocks
docs.text: Benefit Detail 🏡 Shared Housing Your partner moves into your home plot (decorates their own room) 🎁 Daily Gifts Your partner leaves gifts for you each morning 💬 Special Dialogue New romantic dialogue and interactions 👨‍👩‍👧 New Quests Cohabitation-specific quest chain 🪑 Unique Furniture Set Exclusive Proudhorn-themed furniture recipe

docs.title: Can You Cohabitate with Multiple NPCs?
docs.text: Palia allows one active cohabitation at a time. However, you can max romance with everyone — the Proudhorn Ceremony is a separate commitment step beyond Romance Level 4. You may break up and choose a different partner later.

docs.title: Breaking Up / Divorce
docs.text:
• No penalty for ending a cohabitation — friendship and romance levels remain intact.
  • Talk to your partner and choose the breakup dialogue option.
  • They move out of your house but remain friendly.

docs.title: ❌ Disliked Gifts (What NOT to Give)
docs.text:
Giving a disliked gift gives zero friendship points and wastes your daily gift opportunity. Avoid these for each NPC:

NPC Disliked Items Auni Gatherables (common ones), low-value items Ashura Sweet foods, desserts Badruu Fish dishes, bug-related items Caleri Messy items, bug parts Chayne Meat dishes, violent items Delaila Spicy food, meat Einar Man-made items, furniture Elouisa Boring/common items (no bugs/gatherables) Eshe Common items, low-value gatherables Hassian Sweet food, desserts, flowers Hekla Fabric, clothing items Hodari Decor, furniture (too fancy for a miner) Jel Rough/ugly items, mining resources Jina Common fish, plain food Kenyatta Formal items, books Nai'o Sweet food, delicate items Najuma Fancy decorations, formal wear Reth Raw ingredients (he prefers cooked meals) Sifuu Delicate items, flowers Tamala Common food, boring items Tau Vegetables, non-meat food Tish Ugly items, common resources Zeki Common items, low-value gatherables

docs.title: 📆 Weekly Want System — Deep Dive
docs.text: Weekly wants update every Monday at 6:00 AM Palia time. Here's how to exploit them:

docs.title: Weekly Want Mechanics
docs.text: Factor Detail Reset Time Monday 6:00 AM (in-game time) Friendship Boost 3x normal "Like" gift points Stacking If the weekly want is also a "Love" item, the bonus multiplies further Tracking Check the "Villager" tab in the menu to see current weekly wants Notification A mail icon appears next to NPCs whose weekly want you haven't fulfilled

docs.title: Auto-Complete Checklist
docs.text:
Print this checklist and run through it every Monday:

  • [ ] Check all 23 NPCs' weekly wants in the menu
  • [ ] Check your inventory for items matching any weekly want
  • [ ] For "Cooked Meal" wants — check what ingredients you have
  • [ ] For "Fish" wants — visit the relevant fishing spot
  • [ ] For "Gatherable" wants — check Bahari Bay and Kilima
  • [ ] Prioritize NPCs whose weekly want matches their "Love" category
  • [ ] Give the weekly want gift BEFORE any other gift (it's the most efficient use)

docs.text: Each skill levels independently, max Lv10. Each level unlocks new recipes, tools, and quests.

docs.title: Fish Locations

docs.title: 🏠 Palia — Complete Housing & Decorating Guide
docs.text: Your home is the central hub for every Palia player — not just a place to sleep, but a workshop cluster, gardening center, collection gallery, and social space. Smart housing layout dramatically improves your gameplay efficiency.

docs.title: Step 1: Get Your First Plot
docs.text:
Step Details 📋 Trigger Quest Complete the basic tutorial «Welcome to Palia» series, reach Kilima Village and talk to Jina 🏠 Get Your Tent Quest reward: Builder's Tent — free, comes with a basic storage chest 🗺️ Location Default in the Kilima Valley residential area, fast-travel home from the map 📏 Initial Size A standard ~30×40 tile plot

Tip: First thing after getting your tent — place a Workbench next to it to unlock basic furniture crafting. Otherwise you'll have to buy even a bed from Tish.

docs.title: Tent → Manor Complete Upgrade Table
docs.text: Tier Name Cost 💰 Materials Rooms Storage Slots Unlocks 🏕️ T0 Builder's Tent Free — 1 20 Initial shelter 🪟 T1 Harvest House 1,000g Wood×30, Stone×20 4 40 Room additions, wallpapers/floors 🪨 T2 Stone House 8,000g Wood×80, Stone×60, Copper Bar×15 7 60 Second floor, outdoor platforms, double-height windows 🏛️ T3 Manor 25,000g Wood×200, Stone×150, Iron Bar×50, Glass×30 10 80 Luxury exterior, spired roof, large windows, courtyard lampposts

docs.title: Upgrade Tips
docs.text: Tip: Don't rush T3! T1 → T2 at 8,000g gives the best value — 7 rooms is enough for all your workshops, a bedroom, and a living room. T3 at 25,000g is mostly a visual upgrade. Prioritize T2, then use extra gold to add more rooms. Save T3 for after gardening and cooking are paying off.

docs.title: How to Add Rooms
docs.text:
Once you upgrade to T1 Harvest House, you can manually add rooms:

Action Method Cost ➕ Add Standard Room Choose «Add Room» on a house edge — auto-generates a new doorway 1,000g ➕ Add Window Room Same, choose «Window Room» 1,250g 🚪 Add Porch Exterior connecting hallway 500g 🪜 Add Stairs Unlocked at T2, leads to second floor 1,500g 🏗️ Remove Room Demolish unwanted rooms (materials not refunded) Free, but material loss

docs.title: Room Layout Suggestions
docs.text:
+------+------+------+------+
| Pantry| Kitchen |Dining | Deck  |
+------+------+------+------+
| Bedroom| Hall | Living | Study |
+------+------+------+------+
       | Stairs |
       | (to 2F)|
       +-------+

Tip: Keep the kitchen (cooking stations) and workshop (crafting stations) on the same floor near the entrance — collect your harvest, head inside to process, all without extra running. Leave the second floor for a bedroom and collection room.

docs.title: By Source
docs.text: Furniture Type How to Get Examples 🏅 Level Rewards Auto-unlocked as Furniture Making skill levels up Ranch House, Makeshift, Log Cabin 🛍️ Zeki's Underground Shop Zeki's Underground Wondrous (black market), weekly refresh, needs Zeki Coins Dragon Tales, Lunar, Exquisite 💕 Tish Friendship Raising friendship with Tish (Lv.2/3/4 unlocks recipes) Bellflower, Emberborn, Florist 📦 Bundle Rewards Completing Vault bundles Vault, Ancient, Phoenix 🎨 Crafted Workbench with recipe and materials Basic: Coarse Wood, Homestead, Stone 🏪 Furniture Store Tish's furniture shop, daily refresh, gold purchase Random limited/seasonal decor 🛡️ Prize Tickets Redeem Prize Tickets at Zeki's entrance Prize Wheel series

docs.title: By Function
docs.text:
Category Includes Effect 🛏️ Bed Single bed, double bed, tent bedroll Sleep restores Focus, core Coziness item 🪑 Seating Chairs, sofas, benches, floor cushions Increases Coziness 🗄️ Storage Chests, cabinets, shelves Store items (see storage section) 💡 Lighting Chandeliers, lamps, wall sconces, candles Increases Harmony, improves room rating 🌿 Plants Potted plants, flowerpots, wall vines High Coziness-to-cost ratio 🖼️ Walls Wallpapers, paintings, tapestries, mirrors Wall decor, affects room rating 🧶 Floor Rugs, floor tiles Big Coziness boost 🎭 Special Fish tanks, sculptures, music boxes, trophies Collection display + high rating bonus

Tip: The fastest early-game Coziness boost is a rug + potted plant combo. Rugs add 30-50 Coziness to a room, potted plants add 20-30. A few quality pieces beat scattering cheap items everywhere.

docs.title: ⚖️ Room Rating System: Coziness vs Harmony
Two-Part Room Rating
docs.text: Attribute What It Means How to Boost Main Sources Affects 🛋️ Coziness The room's warmth and comfort Rugs, sofas, beds, potted plants, fireplace Fabric/wooden furniture Focus recovery rate 🕊️ Harmony The room's aesthetic balance and cohesion Lighting, windows, matching sets, color coordination Lamps/matching furniture sets Bonus XP multiplier

docs.title: Top Coziness Furniture
docs.text: Rank Item Type Coziness Value Difficulty 🥇 Grand King Bed Bed 120 ⭐⭐⭐ (Tish Lv.4 blueprint) 🥈 Large Stone Fireplace Decor 100 ⭐⭐ (Crafted) 🥉 Grand Rug Rug 85 ⭐⭐ (Zeki Shop / Crafted) 4 Sectional Sofa Seating 75 ⭐⭐ (Crafted) 5 Large Potted Plant Plant 60 ⭐ (Tish Shop)

docs.title: Top Harmony Furniture
docs.text: Rank Item Type Harmony Value Difficulty 🥇 Crystal Chandelier Light 110 ⭐⭐⭐ (Zeki Shop) 🥈 Moonshelf Shelf 85 ⭐⭐ (Crafted) 🥉 Full Dragon Tales Set Set Bonus Set piece count ×15 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (Zeki Black Market) 4 Arched Window Window 60 ⭐ (House Building) 5 Full-length Mirror Wall 50 ⭐⭐ (Crafted)

docs.title: Rating Bonus Mechanism
docs.text:
Room Stars Total Rating Required Bonus Effects ⭐ 0-200 Basic Focus recovery ⭐⭐ 201-500 Focus recovery +10% ⭐⭐⭐ 501-1,000 Focus recovery +25%, XP gain +10% ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1,001-2,000 Focus recovery +50%, XP gain +25% ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2,001+ Focus recovery +100%, XP gain +50%

Tip: Same-set furniture gives Harmony bonuses! Placing furniture from the same set (e.g., all Bellflower series, all Dragon Tales series) triggers a set bonus — each additional matching piece adds increasing Harmony. This is the shortcut to high ratings. Mixing sets won't hurt Coziness much, but Harmony will suffer.

docs.title: Storage Organizing Golden Rules
docs.text: Tip: Place 1 general material chest (wood/stone/fiber/copper/iron) near the workshop area → 1 ingredient chest next to cooking stations → 1 collection chest (gems/fish/bugs) in the bedroom. Sorting by type is 3× faster than one mega-chest — Palia's chest search iterates chests in order.

docs.title: Crafting Station List
docs.text: Station Unlock Level Use 🪚 Workbench Lv.1 Furniture crafting 🔩 Forge Lv.2 Metalworking 🧵 Sewing Table Lv.3 Fabric / Clothing ⚗️ Alchemy Table Lv.4 Potions / Dyes 🎣 Fishing Node Lv.5 Fishing rod upgrades 🎯 Hunting Node Lv.5 Arrow / Bow upgrades

docs.title: Seed Types & Growth
docs.text: Seed Buy Price Growth Days Harvests/Season Base Price ★ Starred Price 🥕 Carrot 10g 3 days 1 18g 24g 🥬 Cabbage 15g 4 days 1 24g 33g 🫘 Green Bean 20g 4 days Every 3 days 20g 28g 🍅 Tomato 25g 5 days Every 4 days 32g 44g 🫑 Pepper 30g 5 days 1 40g 55g 🌽 Corn 20g 6 days 1 28g 38g 🍎 Apple 50g 7 days Every 5 days 60g 82g 🫐 Blueberry 40g 6 days Every 4 days 52g 72g 🌾 Wheat 10g 4 days 1 15g 20g

docs.title: Quality System
docs.text:
Quality Level Price Multiplier How to Get ⚪ Normal 1× Basic planting 🟢 Good Quality 1.2× Use Quality Fertilizer 🔵 Star Quality 1.5× Consistent watering + Quality Fertilizer 🟣 Excellent 2.0× Use Super Fertilizer + max gardening skill

Tip: Pepper is Palia's per-season profit king — 5-day maturity, sells for 40g, seeds cost only 30g. With Quality Fertilizer getting it to blue star (60g), that's a 100% profit margin. Tomato and Blueberry's regrowth mechanics are great for mid-game steady income.

docs.title: Basic Principles
docs.text:
Palia flowers can be cross-bred between different colors to produce new color varieties.

Step Action 1️⃣ Get at least two colors of the same flower type (e.g., Red + White Tulips) 2️⃣ Plant them adjacent to each other (within a 3×3 grid, neighboring tiles) 3️⃣ Water both until they bloom 4️⃣ Check daily: new colored buds may sprout on empty ground next to flowers 5️⃣ New buds are the cross-bred variety

docs.title: Flower Color Cross-Breeding Table
docs.text:
Parent 1 Parent 2 Possible Offspring Colors 🔴 Red ⚪ White 🟣 Pink, 🟠 Salmon 🔴 Red 💛 Yellow 🟠 Orange, 🔴 Deep Red 💛 Yellow ⚪ White 🟡 Pale Yellow, 🟣 Light Pink 🔴 Red 🔵 Blue 🟣 Purple, 🟠 Coral 💛 Yellow 🔵 Blue 🟢 Light Green, 🟣 Violet

Tip: Prioritize cross-breeding Tulips — they have the most color varieties (12 types) and sell for the most. Crossing Red + White produces Pink, and Pink Tulips sell for 200g each — 5× the price of a regular flower.

docs.title: Paths
docs.text: Type How to Get Per-Tile Cost Notes 🪵 Wood Path Workbench crafted Wood×2 Basic, natural style 🪨 Stone Path Workbench crafted (Lv.2) Stone×3 Durable, classic 🧱 Brick Path Workbench crafted (Lv.3) Clay×3 Refined, attractive 💎 Crystal Path Alchemy Table (Lv.4) Glass×2 + Crystal×1 Glowing effect

docs.title: Fences
docs.text: Type How to Get Per-Section Cost Notes 🪵 Wood Fence Initial recipe Wood×2 1 tile high 🪨 Stone Wall Workbench Lv.2 Stone×3 2 tiles high, opaque 💐 Flower Fence Tish Friendship Lv.3 Wood×2 + Flower×1 Decorative 🏗️ Wrought Iron Fence Forge Lv.3 Iron Bar×2 Premium look

docs.title: Lighting
docs.text:
Type How to Get Light Range Recommendation 🪔 Small Torch Initial recipe 3 tiles ⭐ Essential starter light 🏮 Lantern Workbench Lv.2 5 tiles ⭐⭐⭐ Best value 🔦 Street Lamp Workbench Lv.3 6 tiles ⭐⭐ Needs iron bars 💡 Magic Lantern Zeki Shop 8 tiles ⭐⭐⭐ Beautiful, wide range

Tip: Outdoor decor directly affects your visitor rating system. Lantern path + Flower fence is the recommended combo — lanterns light up both sides of the stone path, flower fences border the garden beds. Costs under 10,000g for a premium courtyard look.

docs.title: 📋 Quick Checkup Checklist
docs.text: Stage Goal Budget 🆕 Beginner T1 Harvest House + 2 extra rooms ~5,000g 🌱 Growing T2 Stone House + all crafting stations placed ~15,000g 🏆 Mid-Late T3 Manor + all plots unlocked ~50,000g 🌟 Decorating 5-star room rating + full outdoor decor ~100,000g+

docs.title: Daily Must-Do's
docs.text:
• [ ] 🪣 Water flowers — cross-breeding progress resets if they go a day without water
  • [ ] 🌾 Harvest crops — don't forget regrow plants (Tomato/Blueberry/Apple)
  • [ ] 🪚 Collect station outputs — full backlog stops production
  • [ ] 🎁 Visit Tish's shop — daily refresh, might have high-Harmony furniture
  • [ ] 🏷️ Check Zeki's billboard — possible discounted furniture blueprints

🏠 Closing thoughts: Palia's housing system is a «function first, aesthetics later» progression. Upgrade to T2 first to unlock workshop space, then use gardening and cooking profits to slowly decorate. A good house layout = saving tons of daily running time. Smart room placement, workshop clustering, and garden layout all translate into saved time — and saved time is gold.

docs.title: 🏹 Palia Hunting & Bug Catching Complete Guide
docs.text: Hunting and Bug Catching are the core skills for obtaining leather, meat, insect materials, and gold in Palia. This guide covers everything from unlocking your first bow to the best gold-farming routes.

docs.title: 🎯 Hunting Unlock
docs.text:
Complete the main quest Learning the Ropes (given by Hassian) to receive your first Makeshift Bow and basic arrows. Afterward, talk to Hassian to buy advanced bow and arrow recipes.

Item Details Unlock Condition Complete the Learning the Ropes quest Guild Representative Hassian Currency Hunting Medals Skill Cap Lv 10 (further levels give achievement rewards)

docs.title: 🦌 Huntable Creatures
docs.text:
Creature Variant Zone HP Drops Base Sell Price Sernuk (Deer) Common All maps 1 arrow (Standard) Raw Meat×2, Leather Meat 15 Elder All maps 3 arrows (Fine) Raw Meat×3, Quality Leather Meat 25 ⭐ Proudhorn Bahari 4 arrows (Dispel) Magic Leather, Horn Horn 500 Chapaa (Fluffball) Spotted All maps 1 arrow (Standard) Meat×1, Fur Meat 10 Striped Bahari 2 arrows (Fine) Meat×2, Quality Fur Meat 18 ⭐ Azure Kilima 3 arrows (Slowdown) Magic Fur Fur 150 Muujin (Wolf) Common Bahari 1 arrow (Fine) Meat×2, Hide Meat 22 Banded Bahari 2 arrows (Fine) Meat×3, Thick Hide Meat 30 ⭐ Bluebristle Bahari Elderwood 3 arrows (Dispel) Magic Hide Hide 200

Tip: Proudhorn Sernuk and Azure Chapaa are magic creatures. Use Dispel Arrows to cancel their special abilities and drastically improve hunting efficiency.

docs.title: 🏹 Bow Upgrade Path
docs.text: Bow Level Required Materials Notes Makeshift Bow Lv 1 Starting gift Short range, low damage Standard Bow Lv 3 20 Sapwood Plank + 5 Copper Bar + 200g Improved range Fine Bow Lv 6 10 Heartwood Plank + 5 Iron Bar + 1,500g Longer range, better accuracy Exquisite Bow Lv 9 5 Flow-Infused Plank + 5 Palium Bar + 3,000g Best range, fastest draw

docs.title: Arrow Types
docs.text:
Arrow Effect Best For Standard Arrow Base damage Normal Sernuk / Spotted Chapaa Fine Arrow High damage Mid-tier creatures and above Slowdown Arrow Slows target Azure Chapaa / Bluebristle Muujin Dispel Arrow Cancels magic effects Proudhorn / Azure / Bluebristle

Tip: Carry both Fine Arrows and Dispel Arrows in your inventory and switch with right-click. Against magic creatures, hit them with one Dispel arrow first, then swap to Fine arrows for damage.

docs.title: Smoke Bomb Types
docs.text: Type Unlock Level Effect Standard Smoke Bomb Lv 1 Basic catch, scares nearby bugs Sneaky Smoke Bomb Lv 3 Doesn't scare nearby bugs — great for dense areas Buzzy Jar Lv 6 Shows nearby rare/epic bug locations on the minimap Honey Lure Lv 8 Spawns a swarm of bugs (including rares) at the landing site Smoke Candle Consumable Approach bugs without triggering escape while sneaking

docs.title: Best Bug-Catching Routes
docs.text:
1. Kilima Night Route: Fountain → Library Garden → Farm → Riverside Grass (night moths, fireflies)
  2. Bahari Day Route: Pulsewater Plains → Seaside → Waterfall Area (rare butterflies, jewel beetles)
  3. Honey Lure Method: Pick an open spot in Bahari → use Honey Lure → chain-catch with Sneaky Bombs

Tip: Set your game's Foliage graphics setting to Low — ground-crawling bugs become much easier to spot. Buzzy Jar + Smoke Candle is the golden combo for catching epic bugs.

docs.text: Quality Marker Price Multiplier Special Uses Normal None 1× Cooking, selling ⭐ Starred Golden glow 1.5× Displayable, NPC loved gift ✨ Magic (Magic Creatures) Blue/Purple glow Special drops Crafting magic gear, furniture

docs.title: 💰 Best Gold-Farming Routes
docs.text: Strategy Gold per Hour Requirements Execution Normal Sernuk Loop (Kilima) 1,500-2,500g Lv 1+ Circle the river, one arrow each Elder Sernuk + Leather 2,500-4,000g Lv 6+, Fine Bow Focus on deer in Bahari Highlands Magic Creature Chase 4,000-6,000g Lv 9+, Exquisite Bow Dispel Arrows + hunt Proudhorn Honey Lure Bug Catching 3,000-5,000g Lv 8+ Honey Lure + sell rare bugs Buzzy Jar Epic Bugs 5,000-8,000g Lv 6+, Sneaky Bomb Track down epic bugs

docs.title: 📅 Weekly Want Keep List
docs.text:
NPC Weekly Wants may ask for the following hunting/bug items. Keep these instead of selling:

  • Sernuk Meat / Chapaa Meat — Cooking ingredient, NPCs often want cooked meat
  • Any starred bugs — Multiple NPCs love rare bugs (Elouisa, Auni)
  • Magic Leather — Crafting high-end gear and furniture
  • Quality Leather / Thick Hide — Essential for equipment upgrades
  • Raw Meat — Keep a stock of 30+

Tip: When the Weekly Wants refresh on Monday, check if any hunting-related items are requested. Prioritize completing NPC tasks before selling excess materials.

docs.title: 🗺️ Palia Maps & Zones Guide
docs.text:
Palia's world spans two main zones — Kilima Valley and Bahari Bay — connected by a network of trails, stable fast-travel routes, and vertical shortcuts using your glider. This guide covers every zone, key landmark, navigation trick, and how to unlock areas as you progress through the game.

Tip: Unlike traditional open-world MMOs, Palia's maps are compact and handcrafted. Everything is within a few minutes' walk once you know the routes. The key is learning the vertical shortcuts.

docs.title: 🌍 Map Overview
docs.text:
Palia currently features two main explorable zones, with hints at future expansions. Each zone has its own distinct biome, resources, and activities.

Zone Approx. Size Unlock Requirement Key Features Kilima Valley ~0.5 km² Starter zone (immediately available) Town, farm, NPC homes, central lake, beginner resources Bahari Bay ~1.0 km² Complete "The Void" quest (Early Main Story) Mining caves, high-tier resources, epic fishing, Proudhorn Forest ??? TBA Not yet released Teased as a third zone in development

Connecting the zones: A single overland route runs between Kilima Valley and Bahari Bay through a canyon pass near the southeast corner of Kilima. The stable system also connects the two.

docs.title: 🏘️ Kilima Valley — The Starting Zone
docs.text: Kilima Valley is your home base. It's a peaceful valley with a central village, scattered NPC homesteads, and gentle terrain perfect for learning the basics. You'll spend your first 20-30 hours here before naturally venturing to Bahari Bay.

docs.title: Key Locations

Tip: Place your housing plot marker on the map to always find your way home. Use the compass points — the giant Kilima Lighthouse is your North reference point.

docs.title: 🌊 Bahari Bay — The Mid-Game Zone
docs.text: Bahari Bay is larger, more dangerous, and packed with high-value resources. You unlock it by progressing the main story quest "The Void" — roughly 8-12 hours into the game depending on your pace.

docs.title: How to Unlock Bahari Bay
docs.text:
1. Complete the main quest "The Dragon's Legacy" (learn about the Phoenix)
  2. Complete "The Void" quest — Jina and Hekla will guide you to the Bahari Bay pass
  3. Walk through the canyon path (southeast of Kilima) or take the stable from Kilima Town

Tip: Don't rush to Bahari Bay before you have at least a Fine Bow and an Iron Pickaxe. The resources are tougher, the animals hit harder, and you'll struggle without upgrades.

docs.title: 🏃 Fast Travel System
docs.text: Palia's fast travel uses Stable Signs — wooden signposts with horse icons found throughout both zones. You unlock the ability to use them early in the main quest.

docs.title: How Stables Work
docs.text:
1. Walk up to any Stable Sign and interact with it
  2. A menu shows available destinations with gold costs
  3. Pay the fee to instantly teleport to the chosen sign
  4. The cost increases with distance

docs.title: Fast Travel Cost Table
docs.text:
Route Cost Travel Time (walking) Kilima Town ↔ Library 50g ~2 min Kilima Town ↔ Badruu's Farm 50g ~1.5 min Kilima Town ↔ Remembrance Beach 50g ~3 min Kilima Town ↔ Bahari Pass 80g ~4 min Kilima Town ↔ Pulsewater Plains 150g ~8 min Kilima Town ↔ Pavel Mines 150g ~10 min Kilima Town ↔ Bahari Coast 150g ~9 min Kilima Town ↔ Hideaway Bluffs 150g ~11 min Pulsewater Plains ↔ Pavel Mines 80g ~3 min Pulsewater Plains ↔ Bahari Coast 80g ~2.5 min Pulsewater Plains ↔ Hideaway Bluffs 100g ~3.5 min

Tip: Fast travel costs are trivial once you have a steady income (under 200g per hop). Use stables liberally — the time saved is worth far more than 150g. Never walk across the entire map when you can teleport.

docs.title: 🧭 Navigation Tips & Shortcuts
docs.text: Mastering movement in Palia is about understanding the terrain and using your Glider effectively.

docs.title: Vertical Shortcuts with the Glider
docs.text:
The Glider is your most important navigation tool. You unlock it through the main story, and it changes how you traverse both zones.

Trick How To Best Use Cliff Launch Run off a high ledge, deploy glider Skip entire climbing routes Updraft Canyon Launch from high cliffs toward Bahari Fastest way between zones Tree Launch Climb a tall tree, jump + glide Reach elevated ore deposits Building Leap Jump from NPC roof spans Town navigation shortcuts Coast Glide Use wind currents along Bahari coast Rapid east-west travel

Key glider routes: - Kilima Library → Town: Jump off Library cliff, glide directly south to town center — saves 2 minutes of walking - Bahari Bluffs → Coast: Leap from Hideaway Bluffs, glide west to Bahari Coast pier — saves 3+ minutes - Pavel Mines → Flooded Steps: Exit Pavel Mines upper entrance, glide east across the valley

docs.title: Wall Jumping
docs.text:
Palia has a hidden mobility secret — wall jumping. If you jump against a wall and immediately jump again while pressing into the wall, you can climb surfaces that aren't marked as climbable.

Surface Type Wall Jumpable? Notes Stone Cliffs Yes Best surface, consistent Wood Walls Sometimes Building exteriors Fences No Too low Trees No Climb marked trees instead Cave Walls Yes Pavel Mines shortcuts

Tip: Wall jumping is essential for reaching hidden ore deposits in Pavel Mines. The Palium nodes on the upper ledges are only reachable via wall jumps or careful gliding from higher ground.

docs.title: Terrain Navigation Guide
docs.text: Terrain Type Movement Best Strategy Flat ground (Kilima town, Pulsewater Plains) Sprint freely Use speed boost (shift) Grassy hills (Most of Kilima) Slight slowdown Follow paths, avoid steep angles Wooded areas (Proudhorn Forest, Elderwood) Moderate slowdown Stick to animal trails Rocky cliffs (Bahari highlands) Climbing only Wall jump + glider combo Water Swimming (very slow) Avoid — use bridges or glide over Caves (Pavel Mines) Walking only Mark your path, caves loop around

docs.title: Quick Travel Routes Between Zones
docs.text: From To Fast Travel Recommended Walk Route Time Kilima Town Bahari Bay Stable (150g) Southeast path → canyon → Pulsewater 8 min Kilima Town Pavel Mines Two stables (230g) Town → Bahari Pass → Pavel Mines 10 min Bahari Coast Proudhorn Forest Walk only Coast → Pulsewater → Northeast 5 min Anywhere Housing Plot Map screen Use "Return to Home" button Instant

docs.title: 🔮 Future / Possible New Zones
docs.text:
The Palia development team has hinted at additional zones coming in future updates. Here's what we know (or can speculate based on in-game evidence):

Speculative Zone Evidence Expected Content The Void Main story quest name, incomplete map Possibly a corrupted/danger zone The Elderwoods Referenced in lore books Deeper forest zone, new creatures A New Island Far off-map islands visible from Bahari Coast Ocean content, sailing, new fish Underground City Zeki's hints, cave system beyond Pavel Massive dungeon-like zone Airborne / Sky Zone Dragon lore mentions sky temples Glider-focused content, sky creatures

Tip: When new zones drop, the Devs typically add new stable signs to the network immediately. Check your map after major updates for new fast travel points appearing.

docs.title: Tips for New Zone Preparation
docs.text:
• Bank resources — Keep 50+ of each refined material (Planks, Bars, Fabric)
  • Save gold — Aim for at least 20,000g emergency fund before new zones
  • Max your tools — Having Exquisite-tier tools makes new content smoother
  • Keep reputation — Don't neglect your friendship with NPCs; new zones often have reputation gates

docs.title: 📍 Quick Reference: Zone Comparison
docs.text:
Feature Kilima Valley Bahari Bay Unlock Immediate Complete "The Void" quest Difficulty Beginner (Lv 1-10 skills) Intermediate (Lv 5-25 skills) Resources Copper, Iron, Sapwood, Heartwood Palium, Flow Wood, Magic Materials Creatures Common Sernuk, Spotted Chapaa Elder Sernuk, Striped Chapaa, Muujin Magic Creatures Azure Chapaa Proudhorn Sernuk, Bluebristle Muujin Fish Lake fish, basic river fish Saltwater fish, cave fish, epic fish Fast Travel Points 5 signs 4 signs Best For Daily quests, gardening, social Resource farming, hunting, profit Night Experience Peaceful, fireflies More aggressive creatures, rare spawns

Tip: If you're feeling stuck in Kilima, take the stable to Bahari Bay just to explore. Even if you can't farm the top resources yet, learning the layout early will pay off when you're ready to graduate from the starting zone.

docs.title: 💰 Palia Money Making Guide — Best Ways to Earn Gold
docs.text:
Gold is the lifeblood of Palia. Unlike traditional farming sims where you sell crops at a bin, Palia's economy runs on selling to NPCs, weekly rewards, and crafting. There's no daily shipping bin — everything goes through NPC shops, Zeki's Underground, or the Weekly Wants system. This guide breaks down every money-making method from your first 1,000 gold to end-game 50k+ weeks.

Tip: Palia has a soft gold earning cap — you can only make so much before you run out of energy, bag space, or sellable items. Plan your sessions around one or two focused activities rather than trying to do everything.

docs.title: 🪙 Gold System Overview
docs.text:
Palia's economy is simpler than it first appears. Here's how it works:

Mechanic Details Earning Sell items to NPC vendors (Badruu for crops, Hassian for leather, etc.) or at Zeki's General Store Weekly Wants NPCs request specific items each week — grants bonus gold + reputation Zeki's Underground Weekly auction every Sunday — rare items and high-value flipping Gold Cap No hard cap, but inventory space and energy limit how much you can earn per session Sell Price Base price is fixed per item; star-quality items sell for ~1.5× base price

docs.title: The Gold Scale
docs.text: Stage Weekly Income Playtime Suggestion Fresh Start (Lv 1-5) 1,000 - 5,000g Casual play, 1-2 hours Early Game (Lv 5-10) 5,000 - 20,000g Daily 1-hour sessions Mid Game (Lv 10-25) 20,000 - 50,000g Efficient routing, 2-3 hours Late Game (Lv 25+) 50,000 - 100,000+g Optimized loops, cake parties

docs.title: 🌱 Beginner Money ($1,000 - $20,000/week)
docs.text: When you first arrive in Kilima Village, your goal is simple: build up a small gold reserve so you can buy seeds, upgrade tools, and expand your backpack.

docs.title: Foraging & Scavenging
docs.text:
The fastest starter income. Every bush, tree, and rock in Kilima Valley has something valuable.

Activity Items Est. Gold/Hour Notes Berry Picking Sweet Leaf, Berry 200-400g Spawn in bushes, high density Ore Collecting Copper, Iron 300-500g Craft into bars first Sapwood Gathering Sapwood, Heartwood 150-300g Can craft into planks for profit Mining Stone, Flint 100-250g Good while learning combat

Tip: Sweet Leaf sells for 10g each and respawns quickly. Spend your first 30 minutes running a loop around Kilima's outskirts collecting Sweet Leaf, Copper Ore, and Sapwood. You'll have 500-800g by the time you finish.

docs.title: Basic Crops
docs.text:
Grab a handful of seeds from Badruu and plant the plot you get from the tutorial.

Crop Grow Time Seed Cost Harvest Profit/Plot/Day Carrot 6h 10g 2 × 15g = 30g 80g Potato 8h 15g 2 × 25g = 50g 105g Wheat 8h 20g 3 × 20g = 60g 120g Onion 10h 20g 2 × 35g = 70g 120g

docs.title: Beginner Bug Catching
docs.text:
Bugs are undervalued by new players but are excellent early gold. Use the Standard Smoke Bombs you get from Auni's intro quest.

Bug Location Sell Price Notes Common Butterfly Kilima Meadows 25g Very common, ignore after first few Firefly Kilima at night 45g Easy to spot — glowing Garden Lanternbug Kilima Gardens 60g Dusk/night only Fairy Bee Library Garden 80g Good starter gold Striped Stag Beetle Kilima Trees 90g On tree trunks

Beginner Strategy: Run a loop: your housing plot → Kilima Village gardens → Library → Fountain area. Catch everything you see, sell to Auni. This loop earns ~500-800g per 15-minute circuit.

docs.title: 🏹 Mid-Game Money ($20,000 - $50,000/week)
docs.text: Once you have unlocked Bahari Bay and reached skill levels around 5-8, the real money opens up.

docs.title: Hunting Routes
docs.text:
Hunting is one of the best mid-game earners. With a Standard or Fine Bow, you can clear routes efficiently.

Route Location Est. Gold/Hour Requirements Kilima Sernuk Loop Around Kilima Lake 1,500-2,500g Standard Bow Bahari Chapaa Run Pulsewater Plains 2,000-3,000g Fine Bow Bahari Highlands Pavel Mines area 2,500-4,000g Fine Bow, Slowdown Arrows Muujin Edge Trails Elderwood Road 2,000-3,500g Fine Bow

Optimal hunting method: Equip Fine Arrows. Headshot Sernuk and Chapaa for one-shot kills. Sell Raw Meat to the Hunting Guild (15g each) and Leather to the crafting vendor. Keep Quality Leather for upgrades or Weekly Wants.

docs.title: Fishing Hotspots
docs.text:
Fishing in Palia is not just relaxing — it's profitable.

Fishing Spot Fish Est. Gold/Hour Best Bait Kilima Lake Catfish, Trout 1,000-1,500g Regular Worm Bahari Coast Saltwater fish 1,500-2,500g Glow Worm Bahari River (Pulsewater) Epic fish 2,000-4,000g Premium Worm Pavel Mines Pond Cave fish 2,500-5,000g Glow Worm

Tip: The Bahari Coast pier facing the sunset is a consistent spot for Uncommon and Rare fish. Fishing with Glow Worms increases your chance of higher-value catches.

docs.title: Preserve Jar Profit
docs.text:
Turning crops into pickled goods or preserves is a massive profit multiplier. Unlock the Preserve Jar from the Gardening Guild after reaching Lv 6 Gardening.

Crop Base Price Preserve Jar Value Profit After Seeds Tomato 30g ~90g +55g Corn 50g ~140g +85g Cabbage 45g ~130g +85g Blueberry 100g ~250g +185g

Mid-game strategy: Dedicate 4-5 garden plots to Cabbage or Tomatoes. Put them in Preserve Jars. The passive income from jars while you hunt or fish adds up to 3,000-5,000g/day.

docs.title: 💎 Advanced Money ($50,000+/week)
docs.text: For end-game players with Exquisite tools, high skill levels, and a well-organized routine.

docs.title: Cooking Parties (The Cake Meta)
docs.text:
Cooking parties are the best gold/hour activity in Palia bar none. Players gather, split ingredient costs, craft high-value dishes, and share profits equally.

Dish Ingredients Profit/Unit Party (8 people) Est. Gold/Hour Celebration Cake Sweet Leaf ×3, Butter ×3, Flour ×3, Egg ×3, Sugar ×3 1,500-2,500g 10,000-15,000g Cream of Mushroom Soup Mushroom ×2, Milk ×1, Butter ×1 800-1,200g 6,000-10,000g Muujin Bahari Muujin Meat ×2, Heat Root ×2, Spice Sprout ×1 1,200-2,000g 8,000-12,000g Meat Stir Fry Meat ×3, Sweet Leaf ×2, Pepper ×1 600-1,000g 5,000-8,000g

How to join: Check the official Palia Discord (#looking-for-group channel) or the in-game chat for "cake party" or "cooking party" announcements. Hosts typically post ingredient requirements — bring your share of materials and follow the cooking rotation.

Tip: For solo cooking, focus on Meat Stir Fry (simple ingredients, good profit) or Cream of Mushroom Soup (if you grow Mushrooms). You'll earn ~3,000-5,000g per hour solo versus 10k+ in a full party.

docs.title: Proudhorn Sernuk Farming
docs.text:
The Proudhorn Sernuk is a magic creature that spawns in Bahari Bay and drops the valuable Proudhorn Horn (500g base) and Magic Leather (used for high-end crafting).

Item Sell Price Spawn Mechanics Proudhorn Horn 500g Guaranteed drop when killed Magic Leather ~300g Random drop Raw Meat ×3 45g Standard creature drop

Proudhorn Spawn Locations: Pavel Mines entrance, Flooded Steps, Proudhorn Forest, Ancient Underbrush.

Farming strategy: 1. Equip Exquisite Bow with Dispel Arrows 2. Server-hop by fast-traveling between Kilima and Bahari 3. Hit Proudhorn with 1 Dispel Arrow first (cancels its magic shield) 4. Switch to Fine Arrows and finish it off 5. Repeat every ~30 minutes (respawn time)

A skilled hunter can farm 3-5 Proudhorn horns per hour, earning 2,500g+ just from horns.

docs.title: Zeki's Underground Flipping
docs.text:
Zeki's Underground is a weekly auction that runs from Sunday to Tuesday. It features rare and exclusive items that can be flipped for massive profit.

Item Buy Price (Auction) Estimated Flip Value Best For Plushies (rare) 5,000-10,000g 15,000-30,000g Long-term hold Exotic Furniture 1,000-5,000g 5,000-15,000g Flip to collectors Limited Recipes 2,000-8,000g N/A (learn) Keep for cooking profit Decor Items 500-3,000g 2,000-8,000g Flip or decorate

Flipping strategy: Buy items you know are in high demand (plushies, limited furniture). Hold for 1-2 weeks, then sell in the next Underground auction or through in-game trading. The market fluctuates — rare plushies can 3× in value.

docs.title: 🌽 Best Gardening Crops for Gold
docs.text:
With the 6-day growth cycle (real-time, not seasonal), Palia gardening is about optimizing daily input vs. output.

Crop Grow Time Seed Cost Base Profit With Quality Fertilizer Multi-Harvest? Best For Carrot 6h 10g 20g 30g No Early game Potato 8h 15g 35g 53g No Early game Tomato 12h 25g 65g (3× harvest) 98g Yes Mid game Corn 14h 30g 70g 105g No Mid game Blueberry 24h 50g 150g 225g Yes Daily farmer Cabbage 48h 22g 23g 35g No Preserve jars Pepper 16h 35g 85g 128g No Cooking ingredient Rice 18h 40g 110g 165g No Good profit/plot Grape 48h 90g 300g (3× harvest) 450g Yes Late game Mushroom 48h 100g 300g (2× harvest) 450g Yes Cooking + profit Ginger 72h 35g 30g 45g No Best w/ Quality Up

Tip: Apply Quality Up Fertilizer to high-value single-harvest crops like Ginger and Cabbage. Apply Yield Boost Fertilizer to multi-harvest crops like Tomatoes and Blueberries for even more per cycle.

docs.title: 📋 Weekly Wants System
docs.text: The Weekly Wants system is one of the most overlooked money-makers in Palia. Every Monday at reset, each NPC requests up to 3 items. Gifting them these items gives bonus gold and reputation.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Open your map and look at each NPC's profile
  2. Weekly Wants are listed with a special icon
  3. Gift the exact requested item — you get bonus gold on top of regular friendship
  4. You can fulfill each NPC's want once per week
  5. The gold bonus scales with the item's base value

docs.title: High-Priority NPCs for Gold
docs.text:
NPC Typical Wants Bonus Gold Why Focus Badruu Crops, Cooked meals 100-500g Easy to fulfill with garden Kenyatta Exotic items, gems 150-600g High bonus Hassian Leather, hunted items 100-400g Hunting mats are free Ashura Cooked fish, meat 100-400g Fishing yields double value Caleri Foraged items, bugs 100-350g Free from daily runs Elouisa Rare bugs, mushrooms 200-600g Highest single bonus

Weekly Wants Strategy: Check all NPCs on Monday morning. If they want items you can produce cheaply (foraged greens, common bugs, basic crops), fulfill those immediately. Save hard-to-get items for the weekend. You can easily earn 3,000-5,000g/week from Weekly Wants alone.

docs.title: ⚡ Quick Reference: Gold/Hour Comparison
docs.text:
Method Est. Gold/Hour Difficulty Consistency Best Stage Foraging (Sweet Leaf run) 200-400g ★☆☆☆☆ High Beginner Basic Crop Farming 300-800g ★★☆☆☆ High Beginner Bug Catching (Kilima) 500-1,000g ★☆☆☆☆ Medium Beginner Kilima Hunting Loop 1,500-2,500g ★★☆☆☆ High Early Basic Fishing 1,000-1,500g ★★☆☆☆ Medium Early Preserve Jar Passive 2,000-4,000g/day ★★★☆☆ Very High Mid Bahari Hunting Route 2,500-4,000g ★★★☆☆ High Mid Epic Fishing (Bahari) 2,000-5,000g ★★★☆☆ Low Mid Honey Lure Bug Catching 3,000-5,000g ★★★☆☆ Medium Mid Solo Cooking (Stir Fry) 3,000-5,000g ★★★☆☆ High Mid-Late Advanced Crop Farm 5,000-8,000g/day ★★★☆☆ Very High Late Cake Party (Group) 10,000-15,000g ★★★★★ Variable Late Zeki's Flipping Variable (up to 30k/week) ★★★★★ Low Late Proudhorn Farming 2,500-5,000g ★★★★☆ Medium Late

Tip: For a balanced approach, combine 2-3 methods. For example: manage your garden (passive income) + 30 minutes of hunting + check Weekly Wants. This hybrid approach yields the most consistent weekly income without burnout.

docs.title: Sample Daily Routine for 50k/Week
docs.text: Time Block Activity Est. Gold Morning (5 min) Harvest Garden, replant, refill Preserve Jars 1,500-3,000g (passive) Active Session (30 min) Bahari Hunting Route or Cake Party 5,000-10,000g Evening (15 min) Fish at Bahari Coast (Glow Worms) 2,000-4,000g Weekly (Mon reset) Fulfill Weekly Wants 3,000-5,000g Total Weekly ~5.5h active play 50,000-80,000g

docs.title: 🎯 Final Tips
docs.text:
• Never sell raw materials you'll need for upgrades (Copper, Iron, Flow Glands, Magic Leather, etc.) — always check your crafting stations first
  • Bank 10,000g before spending on cosmetics or housing — you need working capital for seeds and tools
  • Join a cake party at least once — it's the single best gold/hour activity in the game
  • Sell star-quality items individually rather than cooking them unless a recipe specifically needs star ingredients
  • Use the Buzzy Jar for bug catching — it pays for itself quickly by revealing rare bug locations
  • Don't sleep on Furniture Making — premium furniture can sell for hundreds of gold to other players through the Underground or trade channels

docs.title: 🎁 Palia Redeem Codes (Active & Expired)
docs.text: Last Updated: July 5, 2026 — This page is updated as new codes are released. Bookmark this page and check back regularly for the latest Palia codes!

docs.title: How to Redeem Codes in Palia
docs.text:
1. Launch Palia and enter the game world
  2. Press ESC to open the main menu
  3. Click the "Redeem Code" button (or navigate to Settings → Redeem Code)
  4. Enter the code exactly as shown (codes are case-sensitive)
  5. Click Redeem — rewards will appear in your inventory

💡 Tip: Codes usually expire within 7–14 days, so redeem them as soon as you see them!

docs.title: ✅ Active Codes
docs.text:
Code Reward Status Added — No active codes right now ⏳ Waiting for new codes —

Palia releases codes through Twitch Drops, Discord events, and social media giveaways. Check back often!

docs.title: 📋 How to Get New Codes
docs.text:
• Twitch Drops — Watch Palia streams with Drops enabled to earn exclusive codes
  • Official Discord — Join the Palia Discord for announcements
  • Twitter / X — Follow @PaliaDev for code drops
  • In-Game Events — Seasonal events often include redeemable rewards
  • Newsletter — Sign up on the official Palia website
