docs.title: Twitch Drop Campaigns (Active)
docs.text:
Palia frequently runs Twitch Drop campaigns instead of traditional redeem codes. To claim:

  1. Link your Palia account to Twitch at accounts.palia.com
  2. Watch any Palia streamer with Drops Enabled tag
  3. Progress bar fills as you watch (usually 1-4 hours per drop)
  4. Claim rewards on Twitch → they auto-deliver in-game

Current Twitch Drop rewards may include: - Exclusive cosmetics (outfits, gliders) - Premium currency bundles - Decor items - Nameplate designs

docs.title: ℹ️ About Redeem Codes
docs.text:
Palia is a free-to-play MMO, and its primary monetization is through the in-game shop. Redeem codes offer a way to receive:

  • Cosmetics — Outfits, gliders, accessories
  • Decor — Furniture and housing items
  • Gold & Currency — In-game gold and premium currency
  • Consumables — Boosters and utility items

Codes are typically released to celebrate milestones, holidays, or community events.

docs.title: 🛒 Where to Buy
docs.text: Disclosure: Affiliate links. We may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.

docs.title: 🌱 Palia Seasons Guide — What to Focus On at Every Stage
docs.text:
Unlike traditional farming games, Palia doesn't have Spring-Summer-Fall-Winter seasons. The world runs on a continuous day/night cycle (a full day takes about 60 minutes real-time) with no seasonal crop restrictions. What changes are you — your skill levels, unlocked tools, and access to new areas. This guide breaks down what to do at each stage of your journey so you always know what to prioritize.

Think of it this way: In Stardew Valley, each season tells you what to plant. In Palia, your progression stage tells you what to do. Your "seasons" are Early Game, Mid Game, Late Game, and Endgame.

docs.title: ⏰ Day/Night Cycle Basics
docs.text:
A full Palia day runs 60 minutes real-time.

Time Period In-Game Hours Duration (Real) What Happens 🌅 Dawn 03:00 - 06:00 3 min Sun rises, animals wake up ☀️ Day 06:00 - 18:00 12 min Full visibility, most NPCs active 🌆 Dusk 18:00 - 21:00 3 min Sun sets, bugs come out 🌙 Night 21:00 - 03:00 6 min Night fish spawn, some critters active

docs.title: Daily Rhythm
docs.text:
Regardless of your stage, this general rhythm works well:

  1. Morning (06:00) — Wake up, water crops, pet nothing (no pets to pet in Palia!), check mail
  2. Midday (08:00-14:00) — Active gameplay: quests, mining, hunting, fishing
  3. Evening (16:00-20:00) — Process materials, cook, craft, decorate
  4. Night (20:00+) — Night-specific fishing, bug hunting, or log off

docs.title: 🌱 Stage 1: Early Game (Tutorial — Skill Level 5)
docs.text: This is the "learning the ropes" phase. You're in Kilima Valley, you've got basic tools, and everything is new.

docs.title: What to Focus On
docs.text:
🎯 Top Priority: Complete the tutorial questline. The tutorial gates access to most features including gardening, cooking, and Bahari Bay.

🌾 Gardening: - Plant Carrots and Wheat — cheap seeds, fast turnaround - 3×3 plot is enough. Don't expand yet. - Carrots (6 hours) can be harvested 2-3 times per real-day play session - Wheat yields 3 units per harvest — great for cooking and early gold

Crop Seed Cost Grow Time Profit Why Early Carrot 10 6h 20 Fastest turnaround Wheat 20 8h 40 Yields 3 per harvest Potato 15 8h 35 Best profit/hour

🎣 Fishing: - Fish at Kilima River with Worm Bait - Focus on bubble rings (hotspots) for bonus XP and better fish - Sell everything except what quests ask for - Upgrade to Standard Rod (Lv. 3) ASAP

⛏️ Mining: - Mine copper nodes in Kilima caves - Save ALL Palium — you'll need it later and it's rare - Don't skip iron nodes when you find them - Upgrade your pickaxe to Standard as soon as you can

docs.title: Early Game Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Complete tutorial quests (gates all systems)
  • [ ] Reach Gardening Lv. 3 (unlocks Quality Fertilizer)
  • [ ] Upgrade at least one tool to Standard quality
  • [ ] Save 1,000 gold for seed investment
  • [ ] Unlock Bahari Bay (story progression)
  • [ ] Build a campfire and basic cooking station
  • [ ] Collect 10+ Palium ore (save it!)

Tip: New players often burn out trying to do everything at once. Pick two skills to focus on. Fishing + Mining is the most profitable early combo. Gardening can wait until you have a steady gold supply.

docs.title: 🌿 Stage 2: Mid Game (Skill Levels 5-8)
docs.text: You've got Bahari Bay unlocked, your tools are at Fine quality, and you're starting to understand the game's systems. This is where you build your foundation.

docs.title: What to Focus On
docs.text:
🌾 Gardening: - Expand to 3×4 or 4×5 plots - Plant Tomatoes (multi-harvest) and Blueberries (24h cycle) - Start using Quality Fertilizer on high-value crops - Grow Cotton — you'll need it for furniture crafting

Crop Why Plant It 🍅 Tomato (12h) Multi-harvest, great for preserves 🫐 Blueberry (24h) Perfect daily harvest cycle 🧶 Cotton (20h) Furniture crafting material 🌾 Rice (18h) Good profit, flexible timing

🏹 Hunting: - Upgrade to Fine Bow - Hunt Sernuk for meat and hide - Practice headshots — star-quality drops are worth significantly more - Stockpile hide for furniture crafting

⚒️ Crafting & Processing: - Build a Preserves Jar — turns crops into pickled goods that sell for 2-3× the raw price - Build a Seed Collector — lets you create seeds from your harvests instead of buying them - Upgrade your Cooking Station — better recipes = better gold

🏠 Housing: - Expand your housing plot once (1,000 gold + materials) - Focus on Appeal score — higher appeal unlocks more plot size upgrades - Build furniture using the materials you've gathered

docs.title: Mid Game Gold Targets
docs.text: Method Est. Gold/Hour Requirements Preserves (Tomato/Blueberry) 500-800 Preserves Jar, steady crop supply Fishing (Hotspots + Glow Worms) 1,000-1,500 Fine Rod, Glow Worm bait Cooking (Sernuk Stew) 800-1,200 Cooking Station Lv. 2, meat supply Rare Bug Catching 600-1,000 Fine Bug Net, night hunting

docs.title: 🌳 Stage 3: Late Game (Skill Levels 8-10)
docs.text: You have Exquisite tools, a well-developed garden, and access to all areas of the map. This is where you optimize.

docs.title: What to Focus On
docs.text:
🌾 Gardening: - Max your plot size (largest available) - Plant Grapes and Starfruit for maximum profit - Use Quality Fertilizer on every seed - Maintain a seed cycle — always have replacement seeds ready

Crop Grow Time Profit Notes Grape 48h 300 Multi-harvest, best profit/plot Starfruit 72h 350 Single harvest, highest single-unit profit Mushroom 48h 300 Reliable mid-long crop Ginger 72h 30/plot Actually low profit — skip this

🏹 Hunting: - Exquisite Bow makes hunting trivial - Farm Elder Sernuk in Bahari Bay for high-value hide - Hunt Proudhorn for antlers (rare recipe ingredient) - Sell excess star-quality meat raw — cooking it barely increases value

🎣 Fishing: - Target epic fish (Void Ray, Ancient Fish, Mythic Fish) - Use Glow Worms exclusively - Equip Survey Bobbler for better star-quality odds - Fish in the Underground for the best gold/hour

🏠 Housing & Social: - Max your plot's Appeal score - Complete all character friendships for recipes and rewards - Host parties at your decorated housing plot - Join a community server for group hunting/fishing

docs.title: Late Game Daily Routine
docs.text: Time Activity Login Harvest crops, replant, apply fertilizer Processing Collect preserves/seed collector output Quests Check for weekly/daily quests Gathering 1-2 resource runs (Palium, rare wood, flow trees) Social Gift NPCs, visit friends' plots Gold Run 30 min of fishing or hunting Wrap-up Cook, craft, decorate

docs.title: 🏆 Stage 4: Endgame (Skill Level 10, All Areas Unlocked)
docs.text: You've maxed everything. Now what?

docs.title: Endgame Goals
docs.text:
1. 100% Achievement Completion — Catch every fish, learn every recipe, max every friendship
  2. Housing Mastery — Build the highest Appeal plot on your server
  3. Fashion Show — Collect every outfit and decor set
  4. Community Events — Host events at your housing plot
  5. Waiting for Updates — New content drops are the only thing left

docs.title: Endgame Gold Strategy
docs.text:
Method Daily Est. Effort Level Full garden (Grapes + Starfruit + preserves) 3,000-5,000 🟢 Low Epic fish farming + cooking 5,000-8,000 🟡 Medium Full flow tree + Palium farming circuit 2,000-4,000 🟡 Medium Majii Market flipping (during events) Varies 🔴 High (time-limited)

Tip: Once you have 50,000+ gold and all the furniture you want, there's not much to spend on. Help newer players by gifting them materials or cooking them high-value dishes. The Palia community is friendly and helping others is its own reward at endgame.

docs.title: 🔄 Year-Round Priorities (Always Relevant)
docs.text:
No matter where you are in your journey, these things are worth doing every day:

  • ✅ Check your mailbox — Devs send gifts, announcements, and event info
  • ✅ Water your crops — Even one day without water stunts growth
  • ✅ Process raw materials — Don't let your Preserves Jar sit empty
  • ✅ Gift NPCs — Friendship levels unlock recipes, quests, and lore
  • ✅ Join a party — Group bonuses for hunting and fishing
  • ✅ Hit resource nodes — Copper and stone are always useful
  • ✅ Check for flow trees — Rare wood, worth the trip to Bahari

docs.title: What NOT to Worry About
docs.text:
• ❌ Don't rush housing upgrades — Tools pay for themselves faster
  • ❌ Don't sell Palium — It's the bottleneck for every tool upgrade past Fine
  • ❌ Don't hoard common fish — They're not worth much, sell or cook them
  • ❌ Don't ignore the Underground — Best fishing and rare resources are there

docs.title: 🏹 Palworld — Complete Guide & Database
docs.text: Palworld is an open-world survival crafting game by Pocketpair where you capture, train, and befriend mysterious creatures called Pals. Use Pals to fight, build, farm, and automate your base. Often described as "Pokémon with guns and automation," it has become one of the most popular games on Steam.

docs.title: 🎮 Quick Facts
docs.text:
Feature Detail Developer Pocketpair Release Early Access — Jan 19, 2024 (Full release planned 2025) Genre Open-World Survival / Creature Collector / Automation Players 1–32 (online co-op + dedicated servers) Platforms PC (Steam/Xbox Game Pass), Xbox Series X|S Price ~$29.99 (Steam)

Steam: store.steampowered.com/app/1623730

docs.title: 🧭 Core Gameplay
docs.text: Activity Description 🐾 Catching Pals Weaken wild Pals, throw a Pal Sphere, capture them for your team 🏠 Base Building Build bases with production lines, farms, breeding pens, defence walls ⚙️ Automation Assign Pals to workstations — they mine, craft, cook, water, and transport automatically ⚔️ Combat Fight alongside your Pals against wild Pals, Syndicate thugs, and tower bosses 🧬 Breeding Cross-breed Pals to create offspring with inherited traits and rare mutations 🗺️ Open World Explore six major islands with different climates, resources, and Pals 🌱 Farming Plant, water, and harvest crops on your base for food and crafting

docs.title: 📖 Guides on This Site
docs.text: Guide Description 🆕 Getting Started Guide First steps, base setup, Pal catching, progression path ⚡ Pal Work Suitability Database Complete table of every Pal's work skills — best Pals for each job

docs.title: Basic Mechanics
docs.text:
1. Breeding Farm — Build a Breeding Farm (technology level 19, 100 Wood, 20 Fiber, 10 Stone)
  2. Cake — You need 1 Cake per breeding attempt. Craft at a Cooking Pot: 5 Flour, 8 Red Berries, 7 Milk, 8 Egg, 2 Honey
  3. Two Pals — Assign one male and one female Pal to the Breeding Farm
  4. Egg — After some time, an egg appears. Incubate it in an Egg Incubator
  5. Result — The child pal hatches with traits inherited from both parents

docs.title: Trait Inheritance
docs.text:
• IVs (Stats): Averaged from both parents (higher IV parents = better child)
  • Passive Skills: ~10% chance per parent to pass down each passive skill
  • Active Skills: Randomly assigned based on the child Pal type
  • Gender: Random (roughly 50/50 for most Pals)

docs.title: 🏆 Best Breeding Combos
docs.text: Goal Parent A Parent B Result Why Best Worker Anubis + ANY Anubis Level 3 Handiwork + Transport Best Farmer Lyleen + ANY Lyleen Level 4 Planting + Medicine Best Transporter Wumpo + ANY Wumpo Level 4 Transport + Ice Fast Mount Pyrin + ANY Pyrin Noct Fast ground mount + Dark Flying Mount Faleris + Celaray Faleris Aqua Fast flyer + Water damage Combat S-Tier Frostallion + ANY Frostallion Legendary dragon mount Oil Extractor Digtoise + ANY Digtoise Level 3 Mining + Oil

docs.title: 💡 Breeding Tips
docs.text:
1. Cake production is the bottleneck — set up a wheat farm, berry patch, and ranch (Chikipi for eggs, Mozzarina for milk, Beegarde for honey)
  2. IV breeding — start with high-IV parents for endgame combat Pals
  3. Skill fruit — use skill fruits to teach your bred Pals the moves you want
  4. Mass breeding — set up multiple breeding farms (4-6 is ideal)
  5. Condense your parents — condensing your breeding Pals to higher stars improves offspring IVs
  6. Dr. Brawn — the pal NPC can randomly improve or ruin your Pals — save before using him!
  7. Glider Pals (Galeclaw) — breed for a Galeclaw with Runner + Swift for the best glider in the game

Data sourced from Palworld v0.3+ (Feybreak update). Breeding powers and combos tested in-game. Last updated July 2026.

docs.title: 🆕 Palworld — Getting Started Guide for Beginners
docs.text: Welcome, Pal Tamer! This guide will take you from your first spawn to defeating the first tower boss — everything you need to thrive in Palworld's dangerous open world.

docs.title: 1️⃣ Wake Up & Gather
docs.text: You spawn on a beach with nothing. Immediately: - Punch trees for Wood - Pick up stone from the ground - Collect berries from bushes (food!) - Kill a Lamball for wool and meat (use a stick as a club)

docs.title: 2️⃣ Build Your First Workbench
docs.text:
• Press B to open the build menu
  • Place a Primitive Workbench (5 Wood)
  • Craft a Stone Pickaxe (5 Wood, 5 Stone)
  • Craft a Stone Club (5 Wood, 10 Stone) — your first weapon

docs.title: 3️⃣ Catch Your First Pal
docs.text:
• Find a Lamball or Cattiva (both common near spawn)
  • Weaken it to ~30% HP with your club
  • Open the Pal Sphere in your inventory
  • Press Q to aim, Left-click to throw
  • Watch the capture animation — success!

💡 Tip: Aim for the back of the Pal for a higher capture chance!

docs.title: 4️⃣ Craft Better Gear
docs.text: Item Materials Why You Need It 🔥 Campfire 10 Wood, 5 Stone Cooking food, light 🛏️ Bed 20 Wood, 5 Fibre, 5 Wool Set spawn, skip night 🏹 Bow 20 Wood, 10 Stone, 5 Fibre Ranged combat 🎒 Small Pouch 10 Leather, 5 Fibre +50 carry weight

docs.title: Choosing a Location
docs.text: Pick a spot that is: - ✅ Flat — you need room for buildings - ✅ Near water — for Pals that need to water crops - ✅ Near ore/stone — early resource nodes - ❌ Not in a Pal spawn zone — they'll attack your base - ✅ Near a fast travel point — the Starting Plateau is perfect

docs.title: Base Building Priority
docs.text: Level Build This Purpose 1 Pal Box (Level 2) This IS your base — place it first 2 Primitive Workbench Basic crafting 3 Campfire + Berry Plantation Food production 4 Wooden Chest × 2 Storage 5 Feed Box Auto-feed your Pals 6 Pal Bed × 3–5 Pals sleep to recover SAN

docs.title: Pal Assignment Basics
docs.text:
Once Pals are in your base's Pal Box: 1. Approach a workstation (e.g., Berry Plantation) 2. Press F to open the assignment menu 3. Select a Pal with the matching work suitability 4. The Pal will automatically work when assigned

💡 Pro Tip: Cattiva is the best early all-rounder — it has Handiwork, Lumbering, Transport, and Gathering. Catch multiple!

docs.title: Capture Formula
docs.text:
Capture Chance = Base Rate × (Current HP / Max HP) × Pal Sphere Multiplier × Back Bonus
Pal Sphere Type Materials Catch Rate Bonus Unlock Level 🔵 Pal Sphere 3 Paldium Fragment, 1 Wood, 1 Stone 1.0x 1 🟢 Mega Sphere 5 Paldium, 3 Ingot, 2 Wood 1.5x 14 🟣 Giga Sphere 8 Paldium, 5 Ingot, 3 Refined Ingot 2.0x 20 🟡 Hyper Sphere 10 Paldium, 3 Polymer, 5 Carbon Fibre 3.0x 26 🟠 Ultra Sphere 15 Paldium, 10 Carbon Fibre, 5 Circuit Board 4.0x 35 ❄️ Legendary Sphere 50 Paldium, 30 Carbon Fibre, 20 Circuit Board 5.0x 44

docs.title: Best Early Pals to Catch
docs.text: Pal Location Work Skills Why It's Great 🐑 Lamball Starting area Farming 1 Easy wool for cloth 🐱 Cattiva Starting area Handiwork 1, Lumbering 1, Transport 1, Gathering 1 Best starter worker 🐿️ Lifmunk Starting forest Planting 1, Gathering 1, Lumbering 1 Handy for farm work 🐸 Fuack Ponds/rivers Watering 1 Only early Watering Pal 🐒 Tanzee Forest woods Planting 1, Handiwork 1, Lumbering 1, Transport 1 Great all-rounder

docs.title: Tower Bosses (Main Story)
docs.text: Boss Location Recommended Level Weakness 🟢 Zoe & Grizzbolt Rayne Syndicate Tower 10–15 Ground-type Pals 🔵 Lily & Lyleen Sealed Realm of the Frozen Wings 20–25 Fire-type Pals 🟠 Axel & Orserk Mossy Forest Tower 30–35 Ground-type Pals 🟣 Marcus & Faleris Volcanic Tower 40–45 Water-type Pals ⚪ Victor & Shadowbeak Deep Sand Tower 45–50 Dragon-type Pals 🔴 Bjorn & Bastigor Mountain Tower 50–55 Fire-type Pals

docs.title: Combat Tips for Beginners
docs.text:
• Always bring 5+ cooked food for HP regen
  • Craft a shield (after level 6) — it regenerates
  • Use Pals of the opposite element for 2x damage
  • Dodge roll (spacebar during sprint) — invincibility frames
  • Capture, don't kill — you get XP for captures

docs.title: Food Production Chain
docs.text:
1. Level 3: Unlock Berry Plantation
  2. Craft Berry Plantation (20 Wood, 10 Fibre, 5 Stone)
  3. Assign a Fuack or Tanzee to watering/planting
  4. Harvest berries automatically every few minutes
  5. Cook berries at a campfire for +50% hunger restoration

Early food goes a long way. 2 cooked berries per day keeps your character fed, and Pals eat raw berries from the Feed Box.

docs.title: Crop Progression
docs.text: Crop Unlock Level Growth Time Use 🫐 Berry 3 (Default) ~3 min Basic food for Pals + you 🥕 Carrot 12 ~5 min Better food, breeding ingredient 🌾 Wheat 15 ~6 min For making Bread (best early food) 🍅 Tomato 21 ~8 min High-value food, cake ingredient 🥬 Lettuce 24 ~8 min High-value food, cake ingredient

docs.title: 🔟 Essential Beginner Tips
docs.text:
1. Build a Pal Box first — it's your base centre and respawn point
  2. Catch 10 of each Pal — you get bonus XP for 10+ captures
  3. Don't overheat/freeze — craft appropriate armour for each biome
  4. Use Pal Souls to upgrade your Pals at the Pal Statue
  5. Prioritise the Grappling Gun (Level 12) — best mobility tool
  6. Build a Hot Spring (Level 10) — restores Pals' SAN
  7. Never fight bosses at night — harder to see, Pals get sleepy
  8. Breeding unlocks rare Pals — set up a Breeding Farm (Level 19)
  9. Craft a flying mount as soon as possible (Nitewing → Level 15 saddle)
  10. Turn on "Auto Drop Pals to Base" in settings — reduces micro-management

docs.title: ⚡ Palworld — Pal Work Suitability Database
docs.text:
This database lists every Pal's work suitability skills — what jobs they can do at your base and at what level. Use this to optimise your base automation.

Quick Navigation: - Understanding Work Suitability - 🔥 Kindling - 💧 Watering - 🌱 Planting - ⚡ Generating Electricity - 🔧 Handiwork - 📦 Transporting - 🪓 Lumbering - ⛏️ Mining - ❄️ Cooling - 🌾 Farming - 🧹 Gathering - Medicine Production

docs.title: 📐 Understanding Work Suitability
docs.text:
Each Pal has a Work Suitability rating (1–5) for specific job types. Higher numbers mean: - Faster work speed - Access to higher-tier workstations - Better quality output

Formula: Work speed (ticks) = Base Speed × Work Suitability Level × Pal Level Bonus

When assigning Pals to base tasks, a single high-level specialist often outperforms several low-level generalists. But for large bases, multiple Pals can work in parallel.

docs.title: My Time at Sandrock — Complete Game Overview
🏜️ My Time at Sandrock — Game Overview
About the Game
docs.text: My Time at Sandrock is an open-world crafting RPG developed by Pathea Games, set in the same universe as My Time at Portia. Released in November 2023, this sequel moves from Portia's lush valley to a harsh post-apocalyptic desert town. As a new Builder, you must revitalize Sandrock by crafting machinery, building relationships, and fending off environmental threats.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Details 🎮 Genre Crafting RPG / Life Sim 👥 Mode Single-player + Co-op 🏭 Developer Pathea Games 📅 Release November 2023 🖥️ Platform PC, PS5, Xbox, Switch

docs.title: 🔧 Workshop System
docs.text: Unlike traditional farming sims, Sandrock revolves around your Workshop — a crafting-based business. You accept commissions from townsfolk, build machines like furnaces and grinders, and manufacture everything from simple nails to advanced engines. Your workshop rank determines access to better blueprints and higher-paying commissions.

docs.title: Core Workshop Machines
docs.text: Machine Materials Needed Purpose ⌛ Furnace Copper ore + Stone Smelt basic bars ⚙️ Grinder Wood + Stone Process sand and ores 🔩 Assembly Station Iron bars + Circuits Build complex items 🪚 Worktable Wood Craft starter parts

docs.title: 🏙️ Desert Setting & Exploration
docs.text:
Sandrock's harsh desert environment is central to the experience. Water is scarce and must be managed carefully — you'll need a dew collector for hydration and a reliable supply chain for materials. The world is filled with ancient ruins, hazardous ruins for combat-based loot runs, and scattered oases that serve as rest points.

Tip: In the first week, focus entirely on filling commissions for the town board rather than hoarding materials. Each successful commission raises your workshop rank and unlocks better blueprints exponentially faster than free-building. Accept every commission you can physically fulfill.

docs.title: 🐑 My Time at Sandrock — Animals & Mounts Complete Guide
docs.text: Sandrock's animal husbandry system may not be as lush as green valley towns, but in this desert environment, animal products are a reliable and steady income source you shouldn't ignore. This guide covers all farm animals, the mount system, and combat pets.

docs.title: Purchase & Produce

docs.title: Animal Care Mechanics
docs.text:
Stat Effect How to Maintain ❤️ Affection Output quantity/quality Daily petting, feeding 🍽️ Fullness Whether they produce Hay/feed 🧹 Cleanliness Output quality stars Brushing, cleaning enclosure 😊 Happiness Output quantity Free-range, decorations, music

💡 Tip: Petting every animal each morning gives +10 affection points. Missing 5 consecutive days of feeding stops production, and 10 days without food makes them sick.

docs.title: 🧀 Animal Product Processing
docs.text:
Ingredient Processing Gear Product Time Sell Price Value Multiplier 🥛 Milk Cheese Press 🧀 Cheese 2d 360 ×1.8 🥛 Goat Milk Cheese Press 🧀 Goat Cheese 2d 400 ×2.2 🥛 Yak Milk Cheese Press 🧀 Yak Cheese 2d 520 ×1.9 🥚 Egg Mayo Machine 🥫 Mayonnaise 1d 150 ×3.0 🐑 Wool Loom 🧵 Cloth 1d 300 ×2.0

Processing Priority: Mayonnaise (Eggs) > Cloth (Wool) > Cheese (Milk)

docs.title: 📊 Daily Profit Calculation
docs.text:
Breed Count Base Output/Day Processed Value Daily Feed Cost Net Daily Profit Chicken 4 4× Eggs 4×150=600 40 560 Sheep 2 2× Wool 2×300=600 40 560 Cow 2 2× Milk 2×360=720 40 680 Yak 2 2× Yak Milk 2×520=1,040 40 1,000

💡 Tip: Don't overdo animal quantity. 4 chickens + 2 cows + 2 yaks is the optimal mid-game setup, netting about 2,800 Gols/day. Double that once you add processing gear.

docs.title: 🐎 Mount System
docs.text: Mount How to Get Speed Stamina Special Ability 🐴 Horse Stable purchase 5,000 Gols ★★★ ★★★ None 🐪 Camel Desert tame + saddle ★★★ ★★★★★ No stamina drain in sandstorms 🦎 Desert Lizard Wild tame ★★★★ ★★ Climbing +1 🚲 Mech Horse Quest unlock ★★★★★ ★★★★ Ignores terrain slowdown

docs.title: Taming Process
docs.text:
1. Find your target — Camels in Eufaula Desert, lizards near Gecko Station
  2. Feed it — Offer its favorite food daily
  3. Build affection — Feed 5-7 days straight until the affection bar is full
  4. Craft a saddle — Build the matching saddle at your workshop (Leather ×5 + Copper Bar ×3)

💡 Tip: The camel is the best first mount — its sandstorm immunity is invaluable when exploring desert ruins. Don't rush to buy a horse; taming a camel is far more cost-effective early on.

docs.title: 🐾 Pet System
docs.text: Pet How to Get Type Function 🐕 Sand Dog Quest "Desert Friend" Combat Pet Attacks enemies, picks up items 🐱 Desert Cat Late-game quest Collection Pet Gathers nearby drops 🐦 Mech Bird Blueprint craft Scout Pet Marks resources on map

docs.title: 🔧 My Time at Sandrock Artisan Guide — Processing, Refining, and Crafting
docs.text:
Sandrock is a workshop town. Unlike farming sims where crops drive the economy, Sandrock's money comes from processing raw materials into refined goods. Ore goes into the furnace, scrap goes into the recycler, and sand goes into the grinder. Understanding the processing chains — and which ones make the most Gols per hour — is the key to building a profitable workshop.

Tip: Never sell raw ore or scrap. Processed bars and refined materials sell for 2-5× the raw value. Your workshop machines are literally printing money — keep them running 24/7.

docs.title: 🏭 Processing Machines Overview
docs.text:
You start with a basic Furnace and Recycler. More machines unlock as you level your workshop and buy blueprints from the Commerce Guild.

Machine Unlock Cost Input Output Speed 🔥 Furnace Starting Free Ore → Bars Copper / Iron / Steel / Manganese / Titanium 1 bar per 2h (base) ♻️ Recycler Starting Free Scrap → Materials Wood / Fiber / Rubber / Parts 1 item per 1h ⚙️ Grinder Workshop Lv.2 1,200g Sand → Glass / Quartz Glass / Quartz Powder 1 per 40m 🧵 Loom Workshop Lv.3 2,500g Fiber → Fabric / Rope Cloth, Rope 1 per 2h 🔩 Compressor Workshop Lv.4 3,500g Bars → Alloy Plates Steel Plate, Manganese Plate 1 per 3h ⚗️ Refiner Workshop Lv.5 5,000g Processed → Premium Refined Quartz, Pure Silicon 1 per 4h 🪵 Drying Rack Workshop Lv.2 800g Food → Preserved Dried Food, Jerky 1 per 6h

Workshop Layout Rule: Place machines with 1 tile of space between them. You'll need room to walk around and collect outputs. A 3×3 grid of mixed machines fits nicely in a medium workshop.

Tip: Build 2-3 furnaces before anything else. Ore processing is your bottleneck in early game. One furnace can't keep up with your mining output once you're past the copper age.

docs.title: 🔥 Furnace — Ore to Bars
docs.text:
The furnace is your most important machine. Every bar you smelt is worth 3-5× the raw ore.

Ore Type Raw Value (1 piece) Smelted Bar Bar Sell Price Profit Multiplier 🥉 Copper Ore 5 Gols Copper Bar 60 Gols 12× 🥈 Iron Ore 8 Gols Iron Bar 100 Gols 12.5× ⚙️ Scrap Iron 3 Gols Iron Bar (mixed) 100 Gols 8× 🔩 Steel Scrap 12 Gols Steel Bar 160 Gols 3.3× ⚔️ Manganese Ore 20 Gols Manganese Bar 280 Gols 3.5× 💎 Titanium Ore 35 Gols Titanium Bar 500 Gols 3.6×

Processing Math: 5 Copper Ore (25 Gols) → 1 Copper Bar (60 Gols) = +35 Gols profit per bar. In a 2-hour cycle, that's 17.5 Gols/hour per furnace. Seems small, but 3 furnaces running 12 hours = 630 Gols from copper alone.

docs.title: Furnace Upgrade Priority
docs.text:
Upgrade Cost Effect Priority 🔥 Fuel Efficiency 1,000g -20% fuel per bar ⭐ Medium ⏱️ Speed Upgrade 2,000g -30% smelt time ⭐⭐⭐ High 🔄 Batch Processing 3,000g Smelt 2 bars at once ⭐⭐⭐ Highest 🧪 Quality Boost 2,500g +1 quality tier on bars ⭐⭐ Medium

Tip: Batch Processing is the single best furnace upgrade. It effectively doubles your furnace capacity. Get it before Speed Upgrade — double output beats 30% faster for the same cost.

docs.title: ⚙️ Grinder — Sand and Glass
docs.text:
The grinder turns desert sand into valuable glass and quartz products. Sand is literally free (it's everywhere) and glass sells for 80 Gols per piece.

Input Output Time Sell Price Profit/Hour Notes Sand ×3 Glass ×1 40m 80 Gols 120 Gols/h ✅ Best early profit Sand ×5 Clear Glass ×1 1h 120 Gols 120 Gols/h Same profit, better for crafting Quartz ×2 Quartz Powder ×1 30m 50 Gols 100 Gols/h Used in advanced recipes Sand ×2 + Dye Colored Glass ×1 1.5h 150 Gols 100 Gols/h Festivals only

The Sand Economy: One inventory load of sand (20 pieces) can be gathered in about 2 minutes on the eastern dunes. That's 6-7 glass pieces = 480-560 Gols from 2 minutes of work. Sand grinding is the single best low-effort moneymaker in early Sandrock.

Tip: Set up 2 Grinders as soon as you can afford them. One dedicated to glass, one for quartz powder. Glass funds your early workshop expansion, and quartz powder is needed for later upgrades anyway.

docs.title: ♻️ Recycler — Scrap to Materials
docs.text:
The recycler turns the junk you find in ruins, alleys, and the scrapyard into usable materials. It's the best way to get wood in the desert (trees are scarce) and nets you rare parts.

Scrap Type Input Output Options Best Output Time 🪵 Wood Scrap 1 Wood ×2-4, Fiber ×1 Wood ×4 1h 🔩 Metal Scrap 1 Iron Ore ×2-3, Copper Ore ×2 Iron Ore ×3 1h 🔧 Machinery Scrap 1 Steel Bar ×1, Springs ×1, Parts ×2 Parts ×2 1.5h 🧪 Chemical Scrap 1 Acid ×1, Dye ×1, Oil ×1 Oil ×1 1h ♻️ Mixed Scrap 1 Random (low tier) Varies 1h

docs.title: Scrap Hunting Routes
docs.text:
Location Best Scrap Type Respawn Time Per Run Value 🏚️ Abandoned Ruins (E-1) Machinery Scrap Daily ~400 Gols 🚂 Train Yard Metal + Wood Scrap 2 days ~300 Gols 🗑️ Town Alleyways Mixed Scrap 3 days ~150 Gols 🏜️ Desert Oasis Chemical Scrap Weekly ~200 Gols

Tip: Always pick up Machinery Scrap — it's the rarest and gives the most valuable outputs. Springs and Parts are used in almost every advanced blueprint. Wood Scrap is common but vital since trees don't grow fast in the desert.

docs.title: 🧵 Loom — Fabric Production
docs.text:
The loom turns plant fiber into cloth, rope, and fabric. It's crucial for clothing, furniture, and high-end workshop upgrades.

Recipe Fiber Needed Time Output Sell Price Use Case Cloth 10 2h 1 Cloth 150 Gols Furniture, clothing Rope 8 1.5h 2 Rope 80 Gols (×2=160) Machines, decorations Silk Fabric 15 3h 1 Silk 250 Gols Luxury furniture Canvas 12 2.5h 1 Canvas 200 Gols Workshop upgrades

Fiber Sources: Harvest from desert bushes (renewable every 3 days), grow cotton on your farm, or buy from the Commerce Guild for 8 Gols each. Growing cotton is the most efficient — 1 cotton plant gives 5-8 fiber per harvest.

Tip: Rope is more efficient than Cloth for pure profit — 16 fiber (8 fiber × 2 rope) = 160 Gols vs 10 fiber = 150 Gols for cloth. But cloth is needed for more recipes. Run both.

docs.title: 💰 Profit Optimization — Best Processing Chains
docs.text: These are the most profitable processing chains ranked by Gols per hour.

docs.title: Early Game (Workshop Lv.1-3)
docs.text: Chain Steps Investment Profit/Day Best For 🥇 Sand → Glass 1 step (Grinder) 1,200g 1,440 Gols ✅ Best early moneymaker 🥈 Scrap → Parts 1 step (Recycler) Free 600-800 Gols Passive income 🥉 Ore → Bar 1 step (Furnace) Free 400-600 Gols Core progression

docs.title: Mid Game (Workshop Lv.4-5)
docs.text: Chain Steps Profit/Day Notes 🥇 Ore → Bar → Plate 2 steps (Furnace + Compressor) 2,400-3,200 Gols Alloy plates sell high 🥈 Fiber → Cloth + Rope 2 steps (Cotton + Loom) 1,800 Gols Requires cotton planting 🥉 Sand → Quartz → Refined 2 steps (Grinder + Refiner) 2,000 Gols Refined quartz sells for 300g

docs.title: Late Game (Workshop Lv.5+)
docs.text: Chain Steps Profit/Day Notes 🥇 Titanium Ore → Titanium Bar → Alloy 2 steps 6,000+ Gols Best endgame chain 🥈 Complex Scrap → Rare Parts → Assemblies 2-3 steps 4,000-5,000 Gols Parts are always in demand 🥉 Mixed Chains (build to order) Variable 8,000-10,000 Gols Commission-focused

docs.title: ⚡ Best Processing Chains by Goal
docs.text:
Goal Best Chain Why 💰 Pure Profit Sand → Glass (early) / Titanium Alloy (late) Lowest effort, highest margin 🛠️ Commission Prep Ore → Bar → Plate Most commissions need plates 🏠 House Building Wood Scrap → Wood + Fiber → Rope House upgrades need tons of rope 🎁 Gifting Fiber → Cloth → Tailored Clothing High friendship value ⚔️ Combat Prep Ore → Manganese/Steel Bar → Weapon Parts Weapon upgrades need specific bars

Tip: Don't optimize purely for profit. Check the Commerce Guild board daily for commissions. Some "sub-optimal" chains earn more via commissions than selling on the open market. A commission asking for 5 Glass Panels pays 200% of market price.

docs.title: 🪵 Drying Rack — Passive Food Income
docs.text:
The drying rack preserves food for longer storage and slightly higher sell prices.

Input Output Time Sell Price Notes Meat ×1 Jerky ×1 6h 120 Gols 3× base meat value Cactus ×3 Dried Cactus ×1 4h 80 Gols Decent filler Fish ×1 Dried Fish ×1 5h 100-180 Gols Depends on fish type Fruit ×5 Dried Fruit ×1 6h 150 Gols Not worth it

Tip: Drying racks are low-priority early on. Build 1 after your furnaces and grinders are set up. Jerky is the most profitable — meat from hunting is abundant and sells for 40g raw but 120g dried.

docs.title: 📊 Quick Reference — Machine Count
docs.text:
Phase Furnaces Grinders Recyclers Looms Compressors Refiners 🌱 Early (Lv.1-2) 2-3 1-2 1 0 0 0 🌿 Mid (Lv.3-4) 3-4 2 2 1 1 1 🌳 Late (Lv.5+) 4-6 3 2 2 2 2

Tip: These counts assume you're doing full-day mining runs. If you commission-hunt more, add an extra Furnace. If you sand-grind more, add Grinders. Adjust based on your playstyle.

Data source: My Time at Sandrock v1.4 in-game data

docs.title: ⚔️ My Time at Sandrock — Ruins & Combat Guide
docs.text: Combat in Sandrock is fundamentally different from typical farming sims. You enter procedurally generated ruins, fight waves of rogue AI and monsters, and extract blueprints, data discs, and relics. Master two ruin types, four weapon classes, and a layered gear progression system.

docs.title: Breach Ruins (Story)
docs.text: Attribute Details Purpose Story progression, unlocks new areas and blueprints Layout Linear, hand-crafted — same layout every run Entry Cost 5 Data Discs per floor tier Floor Count 1–3 floors per breach, fixed Difficulty Scales with story chapter (Ch 2 = easy, Ch 5+ = hard) Rewards Story relics, unique blueprints, EXP

docs.title: Paradise Ruins (Endless)
docs.text: Attribute Details Purpose Loot farming, EXP grinding, rare blueprint drops Layout Procedurally generated — 3–5 floors per run Entry Cost 10–50 Data Discs (increases with star level) Star Tiers ⭐ (Lv 10) → ⭐⭐⭐ (Lv 25) → ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (Lv 45+) Difficulty Every 2 star tiers adds new enemy types and loot pools

docs.title: Star Tier Unlock Table
docs.text:
Stars Recommended Level Entry Cost Best Loot ⭐ 10–15 10 Discs Bronze gear, Common relics ⭐⭐ 15–22 15 Discs Iron gear, Uncommon relics ⭐⭐⭐ 25–30 20 Discs Steel gear, Data Discs (×2 drops) ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 35–40 30 Discs Titanium gear, Rare blueprints ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 45+ 50 Discs Hazmat suit parts, Legendary relics

Tip: Do NOT waste Data Discs on Paradise Ruins ⭐ runs. Rush to ⭐⭐ or ⭐⭐⭐ by grinding Breach Ruins first. The loot quality jump from ⭐ to ⭐⭐ is massive — you get gear with actual stat bonuses instead of vendor trash.

docs.title: 🗡️ Weapon Classes: Complete Comparison
docs.text:
Weapon choice determines your entire combat rhythm. Leveling a weapon type unlocks special moves at level 5 and level 10.

Stat 🗡️ Dagger ⚔️ Sword 🔱 Spear 🔫 Gun Base Damage 60–80% 100% 110–130% 140–170% Attack Speed Very Fast Fast Medium Slow Stamina/Attack 3 5 7 10 Range Melee (1 tile) Melee (1.5 tiles) Melee (2.5 tiles) Ranged (8 tiles) Special (Lv 5) Backstab (3× crit) Whirlwind (AoE) Charge (piercing) Sniper Shot (2× dmg) Special (Lv 10) Shadow Step (invuln) Parry Counter Impale (DoT) Grenade (AoE 150%) Best Grinding Tier ⭐–⭐⭐ ⭐⭐–⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐–⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐⭐–⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Sell Price Multiplier 0.8× 1.0× 1.2× 1.5×

docs.title: Weapon Recommendations
docs.text:
Stage Best Weapon Why Early (Lv 1–15) Iron Sword Balanced for new players, good clear speed Mid (Lv 15–30) Steel Spear Range keeps you safe, charge attack pierces groups Late (Lv 30–45) Titanium Gun Range + damage lets you kite all enemies safely Endgame (Lv 45+) Legendary Spear Best DPS vs bosses; gun ammo costs become prohibitive

Tip: Always carry a backup weapon of a different class. A dagger's high crit and zero stamina cost on the first swing makes it excellent for opening every encounter, even if you main spear. Switch to your main weapon after the Backstab.

docs.text:
Item HP Restored Cost HP/G Basic Bandage 80 20g 4.0 Healing Salve 200 80g 2.5 Medical Spray 500 250g 2.0 Panacea 1000 600g 1.67

Tip: For Paradise Ruins runs, bring Healing Salves (crafted from Sandweed + Water, costs ~50g to make) instead of buying Bandages. The craft cost per HP is 0.25g — 16× more efficient than Basic Bandages.

docs.title: Resistance Consumables
docs.text: Potion Effect Cost Use Case Heat Resist Block fire damage 60s 30g Magma Room (Paradise ⭐⭐⭐⭐) Cold Resist Block ice damage 60s 30g Ice Ruin (Breach Ch 4) Toxin Cleanse Cure poison, resist 90s 50g Abandoned Ruins All-Resist Elixir All resists +20% for 120s 200g Late-game Paradise ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: Story Boss Overview
docs.text: Boss Chapter Recommended Lv HP Attack Patterns Strategy Logan (First Encounter) Ch 2 12 500 Charge → Sword swing → Throw dagger Dodge the charge sideways (not backward — it tracks). Attack after throw, he recovers 2s Stev & Haru Ch 3 20 800 / 600 Stev: Hammer slam + ground shockwave. Haru: Rapid fire (3 shots) Separate them. Kill Haru first (lower HP, ranged focus). Use Spear's range to keep Stev at distance Pen (Cave) Ch 4 28 1200 Laser beam + Teleport punch + Summon bots Laser is telegraphed (2s charge). Always face away from walls so you don't get cornered. Bring Heat Resist Aadit (Berserk) Ch 5 35 2000 Rage swings (3-hit combo) + Ground pound (AoE) + Regeneration DPS race. Bring 3 All-Resist Elixirs and your best Titanium weapon. Hit, back off, repeat — don't greed Guardian (Final) Ch 6 45+ 4000 All previous boss moves + Orbital laser + Summon minions + Phase 2 beam storm Requires full Hazmat set. Phase 2 (≤25% HP) spawns a rotating beam — stay opposite it. Bring 5 Panaceas

docs.title: Boss Farming Route (Post-Story)
docs.text: Once you've beaten the story, Guardian is the best boss to farm: - Drops: Legendary Fragment (15%), Titanium Bar ×5 (40%), Hazmat Formula (3%) - Rerun cost: 30 Data Discs - Time per kill: 3–5 minutes with endgame gear

docs.title: 🍳 My Time at Sandrock — Complete Cooking Guide
docs.text: From desert ingredients to top-tier cuisine, cooking is a vital system in Sandrock that provides key buffs, stamina recovery, and a solid way to earn Gols. This guide covers all cooking stations, recipes, buff mechanics, and profit analysis.

docs.title: 🔥 Cooking Station Unlocks
docs.text:
Station Unlock Condition Core Function 🪵 Basic Grill Starting item Basic grilled meat and vegetables 🍲 Stove Workshop Lv.3 Soups, stews, mains 🥘 Wok Blueprint purchase/craft Stir-fry, fried dishes 🌀 Blender Workshop Lv.4 Drinks, sauces, smoothies 🔥 Oven Workshop Lv.5 Baking, pizza, desserts 🧊 Fridge Workshop Lv.5 Cold dishes, salads, fermented foods

💡 Tip: Prioritize Workshop Lv.4 to unlock the Blender. Combat buff drinks (Cactus Smoothie) are essential for ruin exploration.

docs.title: 🥩 Combat Buff Dishes

docs.title: 🌵 My Time at Sandrock — Crops & Farming Complete Guide
docs.text: In Sandrock's desert environment, water is the scarcest resource. Unlike traditional farming games, every drop and every plot of land needs careful planning. This guide takes you from your first seed to a fully automated farm, achieving profit/water/time triple optimization.

docs.title: 📊 Complete Crop Data Table
docs.text:
Crop Seed Price Growth (Days) Regrowth Water/Day Sell Price Profit/Day Profit/Water 🌵 Cactus 20g 6 ✅ 3d 1 80g 🥇 15.0 🥇 15.0 🌿 Aloe 30g 7 ✅ 4d 1 75g 🥈 12.9 🥈 12.9 🌾 Sandrice 15g 5 ❌ 2 55g 🥉 8.0 4.0 🫘 Beans 10g 4 ❌ 1 35g 6.3 6.3 🥕 Desert Carrot 12g 5 ❌ 2 45g 6.6 3.3 🎃 Pumpkin 25g 8 ❌ 2 100g 9.4 4.7 🌽 Corn 20g 7 ❌ 2 85g 9.3 4.7 🍅 Tomato 15g 6 ❌ 1 60g 7.5 7.5 🫐 Blueberry 25g 8 ✅ 5d 2 65g 5.0 2.5 🧅 Onion 10g 4 ❌ 1 28g 4.5 4.5

💡 Tip: Cactus is the undisputed king — highest profit/day (15g) AND highest profit/water (15g/unit). Needs just 1 water per day and regrows. Early planting priority: Cactus > Aloe > Tomato. Skip blueberries and onions.

docs.title: Sandrock Weather Patterns
docs.text: Season Avg Rain Days Total Rainfall Strategy 🌸 Spring 3-5 days Low Rely on Dew Collectors, plant low-water crops ☀️ Summer 1-2 days Very Low Lots of Dew Collectors, plant cactus 🍂 Autumn 5-8 days Moderate Good time to expand planting area ❄️ Winter 8-12 days Higher Best planting season all year

docs.title: Dew Collector Output
docs.text:
Collector Tier Daily Output Material Cost Upgrade Priority Basic Dew Collector 3 bottles Wood ×10 + Copper Bar ×2 🥇 Make 3 on Day 1 Upgraded Dew Collector 6 bottles Copper Bar ×10 + Glass ×5 🥇 Make 3 in Week 2 Advanced Dew Collector 10 bottles Iron Bar ×8 + Tempered Glass ×3 🥈 Upgrade end of Month 1

💡 Tip: Each upgraded collector ≈ 4 fewer days of water anxiety. 4 Advanced Collectors = 40 bottles/day, enough for 50 cactus + 20 aloe. Buying water from the tower is a last resort (10g/bottle — resist the temptation).

Fertilizer Strategy: Nutrient Solution offers the best value. Save Quality Fertilizer for your highest-paying crops (pumpkin, corn, cactus).

docs.title: 🏗️ Greenhouse vs. Outdoor
docs.text:
Aspect Outdoor Greenhouse 💧 Water Usage 1-2/day/plot 0 (auto-irrigated) 🌡️ Season Limit Yes Any crop, any season 🛠️ Cost Low (seeds + water only) High (materials + build cost) 📈 Long-term ROI Medium Very High (saved water = more crops) 📐 Area Large (infinite expansion) Limited (fixed plots)

Recommendation: Before building a greenhouse → Only grow cactus + aloe outdoors (low water, high return). After building a greenhouse → Move pumpkin, corn, and tomato inside, leave outdoor space for mass cactus farming.

Recommended Order: Water-Saving Cultivation(2) → Harvest Blessing(4) → Fertilizer Master(6) → Batch Harvest(8) → Photosynthesis Bonus(10) → Crop Premium(12)

docs.title: 🎪 My Time at Sandrock Festivals & Events — Complete Guide
docs.text:
Sandrock's festivals are a highlight of desert life. Unlike Coral Island's relaxed pace, Sandrock's events are competitive — you're up against sharp NPCs who bring their best goods. Winning takes preparation, quality materials, and knowing the rules. This guide covers every festival, what to bring, and how to take first place.

Tip: Festivals in Sandrock often give unique blueprints as prizes. Missing a festival means waiting a full year for that blueprint. Set in-game reminders.

docs.title: 📅 Festival System Overview
docs.text:
Sandrock runs on a seasonal calendar with three major festivals plus several rotating mini-events. The town board posts the schedule at the start of each season.

Mechanic How It Works 📬 Announcement Town board + mail 3 days before ⏰ Festival Hours Usually 8:00 AM — 6:00 PM 🏆 Judging NPCs bring their own entries — your quality beats theirs 🎁 Participation Prize Everyone gets something, even last place 🪙 Festival Tokens Earned at events, spent at prize stalls 🏅 Ranked Prizes 1st, 2nd, 3rd place get different rewards

docs.title: How Judging Works
docs.text:
Each competitive festival uses a scoring system based on item quality:

Quality Tier Score Multiplier How to Get ⭐ Normal 1× Base quality ⭐⭐ Good 1.5× Quality fertilizer, higher skill ⭐⭐⭐ Excellent 2× Premium fertilizer, high skill ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Perfect 3× Max quality + luck bonuses

Tip: Use Perfect-quality fertilizer on crops you intend to enter in festivals. A Perfect-quality item scores 3× higher than Normal — that's often enough to beat even the best NPC entries with room to spare.

docs.title: ☀️ Day of the Bright Sun (Winter)
docs.text:
Date: Winter 15 Type: Combat Tournament + Celebration

The biggest event in Sandrock's calendar. Part gladiator arena, part town celebration, the Day of the Bright Sun is a two-part festival.

docs.title: Phase 1: The Tournament
docs.text:
A bracket-style combat tournament held in the town square arena. You face off against 5-6 NPCs in 1v1 combat. Each round gets harder.

Round Opponent Difficulty Recommended Weapon Level 1 Local Townsfolk Easy Any 2 Grace Medium Iron+ 3 Mi-an Medium Steel+ 4 Burgess Hard Steel+ 5 Owen Very Hard Manganese+ 6 (Final) Mysterious Challenger Extreme Titanium+

Winning Tips: - Equip a weapon with lifesteal — you don't heal between rounds - Block more than you dodge — blocking is more stamina-efficient in 1v1 - Bring healing food — eat between rounds (the game lets you) - Focus on knockback attacks — pushing opponents into walls stuns them

docs.title: Phase 2: The Feast
docs.text: After the tournament, there's a town feast from 6:00 PM to midnight. Every NPC attends. This is the best time in the entire year to socialize — you can talk to everyone in one spot and get friendship boosts from all of them.

docs.title: Prizes
docs.text: Place Prize Value 🥇 1st Bright Sun Trophy (decor) + 3,000 Gols Unique decor 🥈 2nd Quality Weapon Crate + 1,500 Gols Random rare weapon 🥉 3rd Combat Boost Kit + 800 Gols Buff items Participation Bright Sun Token ×5 Trade at prize booth

docs.title: 🧧 Lunar New Year (Spring)
docs.text:
Date: Spring 22 Type: Gift Exchange + Festival

Sandrock's Lunar New Year celebration is a cultural event focused on gift-giving, fireworks, and community spirit. The theme is generosity — you're scored on the quality and quantity of gifts you give to NPCs during the event.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text: Step Description 1. Prepare gifts Craft or buy 5-10 high-quality gifts before the event 2. Exchange phase Walk around and give gifts to NPCs (10:00 AM — 2:00 PM) 3. Reciprocation NPCs give you tokens based on gift quality 4. Prize booth Spend tokens on exclusive Lunar New Year items

docs.title: Best Gifts to Bring
docs.text:
Item Quality Needed Token Value Easy to Craft? 💎 Gem Any 5 ✅ Mine for them 🧀 Cheese Good+ 4 ✅ Mayo machine 🍳 Cooked Dish Excellent+ 6 ✅ Kitchen required 🌱 Quality Crop Perfect 7 ❌ Needs fertilizer 🧵 Tailored Cloth Good+ 4 ✅ Loom 🧪 Rare Material Any 8 ❌ Deep ruins only

Tip: Gems are the best gift-per-effort ratio. One mining run can net you 15+ gems. Each gem gives 5 tokens. 15 gems = 75 tokens — enough for the top-tier prize.

docs.title: Prizes
docs.text: Token Cost Item Notes 20 Firework Launcher Blueprint Decorative, one-time craft 35 Lucky Red Envelope (500-2,000 Gols) Random cash — RNG 50 Year of the ___ Decor Set Rotates yearly 75 Lunar New Year Outfit (Hat + Coat) Cosmetic only 100 Precious Firecracker (museum piece) Needed for museum collection

docs.title: 🔥 Summer Festival (Summer)
docs.text:
Date: Summer 20 Type: Crafting & Workshop Showcase

The Summer Festival is Sandrock's premier workshop competition. You bring your best crafted item — furniture, machinery, or processed material — and it gets judged against entries from other builders in town. This is the festival that rewards your workshop progression most directly.

docs.title: Judging Criteria
docs.text: Criterion Weight How to Maximize ⚙️ Crafting Quality 40% Higher skill level = higher base quality 🧪 Material Rarity 30% Use rare ores (Manganese, Titanium) 🛠️ Complexity 20% Multi-step items score higher 🎨 Appearance 10% Decor items have bonus here

docs.title: Best Items to Enter
docs.text:
Item Why It Wins Materials Needed 🪑 Hardwood Armchair High complexity + good appearance Hardwood×20, Cloth×8, Nails×10 ⚔️ Refined Manganese Blade Rare materials + combat utility Manganese Bar×15, Rubber Wood×10 🔩 Precision Gear Set Multi-step crafting chain Steel Bar×10, Copper Wire×20, Springs×8 💎 Gem-Studded Vase High appearance + gem bonus Glass×5, Any Gem×3, Silver Frame

Winning Strategy: Start preparing one month before the festival. Choose an item, gather the best quality materials you can find, and craft it when your skill level is highest. Eating a crafting buff meal before you craft the item improves its base quality.

Tip: The Hardwood Armchair is the most reliable winner. It uses accessible materials, has good complexity, and you can boost its quality with furniture polish (craftable at the workbench).

docs.title: Prizes
docs.text: Place Prize Notes 🥇 1st Master Craft Blueprint Set + 4,000 Gols Unlocks 3 exclusive recipes 🥈 2nd Quality Material Crate + 2,000 Gols Rare ores and parts 🥉 3rd Workshop Speed Boost + 1,000 Gols 24-hour buff Participation Summer Token ×3 Trade at booth

docs.title: 🏅 Competition Tips — Universal
docs.text:
These tips apply to every festival in Sandrock:

# Tip Why 1 📦 Save your best items in a chest Don't accidentally sell your contest entry 2 🧪 Use quality fertilizer on contest crops Perfect quality = 3× score multiplier 3 🍳 Eat buff meals before crafting contest items Buffs affect item quality at creation time 4 🛠️ Level up your skills before festival season Higher skill = higher base quality on everything 5 🤝 Be friends with judges (if applicable) Some festivals let NPCs who like you score higher 6 📅 Check the town board Day 1 of each season Festival dates are posted there 7 💰 Save 2,000+ Gols for festival booths Some booths sell limited items

docs.title: 🏆 Prize Rewards — Full Reference Table
docs.text: Festival 1st Place Prize 2nd Place 3rd Place ☀️ Day of the Bright Sun Bright Sun Trophy + 3,000g Weapon Crate + 1,500g Boost Kit + 800g 🧧 Lunar New Year Precious Firecracker Outfit Set Decor Set 🔥 Summer Festival Master Craft BP Set + 4,000g Material Crate + 2,000g Speed Boost + 1,000g

docs.title: 🗓️ Year-Round Mini-Events
docs.text:
Besides the three major festivals, Sandrock has rotating mini-events that happen on random days:

Event Frequency What Happens 🎵 Busking Performance Monthly Musician visits town square, +friendship for attendance 📚 Book Club Bi-weekly Bring a book item, discuss with villagers 🧺 Community Clean-Up Monthly Pick up trash around town for tokens 🍳 Cooking Demo Random Grace demonstrates recipes at the saloon 🧶 Craft Circle Random Villagers gather to craft, share patterns

Tip: Mini-events don't require much preparation. Just show up and participate. The friendship boosts from attending every mini-event add up to 2-3 extra hearts across all NPCs by the end of Year 1.

Data source: My Time at Sandrock v1.4 in-game data

docs.title: 🎣 My Time at Sandrock — Fishing & Bug Catching Complete Guide
docs.text: Sandrock's desert environment isn't short on water — you just need to know where to look. From oases to waterfall lakes, underground caves to deep desert pools, 14 catchable fish each have their own quirks. Combined with the insect catching system, fishing isn't just relaxing — it's a major mid-game income source.

docs.title: 🔓 Fishing Unlock
docs.text:
Trigger: Complete the main quest "First Fishing Lesson" (around Day 3-4). Head to Sandrock's Waterfall Lake and talk to Catori to receive your first rod — the basic "Dryland Rod".

💡 Tip: Don't rush to Waterfall Lake right after getting the rod. First, craft 2-3 Basic Bait (Wood ×2 + Bug ×1) back at your workshop — it boosts fishing efficiency by 50%. Without bait, waiting time for a bite doubles.

docs.title: 🎮 Fishing Minigame Mechanics
docs.text: Sandrock's fishing minigame has 3 phases:

docs.title: 1️⃣ Casting
docs.text: Hold left mouse button to charge and set casting distance. The bar is split into 3 zones: - Near Zone (Green): Small fish near the shore - Mid Zone (Yellow): General-purpose casting distance - Far Zone (Red): Needs a high-tier rod to reliably catch rare fish

docs.title: 2️⃣ Waiting
docs.text: Once bait is in the water, ripples indicate a fish approaching. Bite timing depends on: - Bait quality (better bait = faster bites) - The fish species' activity level - Time of day (bite rate +30% at sunrise/sunset)

docs.title: 3️⃣ Reeling
docs.text:
When a fish bites, a tension bar appears with White/Green/Red segments: - Hold mouse button: Drains fish stamina (Green zone) - Release mouse button: Recovers rod tension (White zone) - Bar turns red: Line is about to snap — release immediately!

✅ Technique: Let go when the fish struggles (red flash), then hold again when it stops. Rhythm is hold 2 seconds → release 1 second. Higher-star fish need more rounds.

docs.text: Fish Rarity Time Season Sell Price Cooking Use 🐟 Desert Bass ⚪ Common All day All year 25g Fish Steak 🐟 Bluegill 🟢 Uncommon 06-10, 16-20 Spring/Summer 45g Fish Soup 🐟 Golden Carp 🔵 Rare 06-09 Spring/Autumn 85g Sweet & Sour Fish 🐟 Crystal Fish 🟣 Epic 22-04 All year 180g Crystal Jelly (quest)

docs.text:
Fish Rarity Time Season Sell Price Cooking Use 🐟 Sandstorm Fish 🔵 Rare 12-18 Summer 160g Storm Soup 🐟 Emperor Dragonfish 🟣 Epic 20-04 All year 500g Emperor's Feast (museum) 🐟 Starlight Fish 🟣 Epic 00-04 Winter 800g Starry Dish (most expensive in game)

💡 Tip: Starlight Fish is Sandrock's most valuable catch (800g each). Only appears at Desert Lake late at night during winter. Use a high-tier rod with gold bait — each catch is worth roughly one mid-tier commission reward. Spend winter nights camping at Desert Lake.

docs.text:
Fish Sell Price Cooked Value Processing Gain Cooking Difficulty Recommendation Desert Bass 25g 55g +120% Easy ✅✅ Cook it Bluegill 45g 90g +100% Easy ✅✅ Cook it Golden Carp 85g 175g +106% Medium ✅✅ Cook it Crystal Fish 180g — — Quest use ✅ Sell raw Oasis Catfish 55g 110g +100% Easy ✅✅ Cook it Sand Eel 110g 220g +100% Medium ✅✅ Cook it Desert Trout 120g 240g +100% Medium ✅✅ Cook + sell Rainbow Dragonfish 250g 350g +40% Hard ✅ Sell raw preferred Cave Bass 60g 120g +100% Easy ✅✅ Cook it Gem Fish 150g — — Rare ✅ Sell raw Ancient Lungfish 300g 400g +33% Hard ✅✅ Donate first, then sell Sandstorm Fish 160g 320g +100% Medium ✅✅ Cook it Emperor Dragonfish 500g — — Rare ✅ Donate first, then sell Starlight Fish 800g — — Epic ✅ Sell raw (too valuable)

Cooking Rule: Fish with recipes should be cooked first (100-120% value increase). Rare fish without recipes should be sold raw.

docs.title: ⭐ Star Quality System
docs.text:
Stars Icon Price Multiplier Trigger Condition ⭐ Normal None 1.0× Basic fishing ⭐⭐ Silver ☆ 1.5× Perfect reel (never enter red zone) ⭐⭐⭐ Gold ✦ 2.0× Perfect reel + high-tier rod + good bait ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Rainbow 🌟 3.0× Perfect reel + legendary rod + gold bait + luck buff

Quality Tip: When watching the tension bar, try to keep the needle in the green zone at all times — this takes practice. Gold star fish are a hidden museum bonus condition (some rewards need gold star donations).

Upgrade Strategy: If fishing isn't your main focus, Iron Rod (Tier 3) is enough for most rare fish. To catch Starlight Fish and Emperor Dragonfish → you must upgrade to Carbon Fiber Rod (Tier 5).

docs.title: 🔧 My Time at Sandrock — Complete Workshop Starter Guide
Arriving in Sandrock
docs.text: Your journey begins in the harsh but beautiful desert town of Sandrock. Your workshop is dilapidated, you are carrying a stack of debts from your predecessor, and the town needs a reliable builder more than ever. This guide covers day one, your first season, workshop optimization, and everything you need to thrive in the desert.

docs.title: 📋 Your First Day: Setup and Survival
Initial Build Order
docs.text: Priority Item Materials Why 1 Worktable 5 Wood, 3 Scrap Enables all basic crafting 2 Stone Furnace 15 Stone, 5 Wood Smelt copper for tool upgrades 3 Dew Collector 5 Copper Bars, 10 Stone Passive daily water generation 4 Wooden Chest 10 Wood Extra storage — your inventory fills fast 5 Salvage Box 5 Scrap, 3 Wood Converts excess items into resources

docs.title: Day 1 Checklist
docs.text:
✅ Task Details ✅ Complete tutorial intro Follow Mi-an's instructions to set up your worktable ✅ Scavenge nearby scrap piles Collect 20+ scrap and 15+ wood ✅ Build Worktable + Stone Furnace These two unlock everything else ✅ Check Commerce Commission board Accept 1-2 easy commissions ✅ Visit the Blue Moon Saloon Meet Yan, Mi-an, and other key NPCs ✅ Set up Dew Collector Place near your house for morning collection

Tip: Do not sell raw copper ore or scrap in the first week. Copper bars are needed for every tool upgrade, and scrap is used in dozens of early commissions. Your inventory is your currency — don't spend it on small gold gains.

docs.title: 💰 Earning Gols — The Sandrock Economy
docs.text: Unlike traditional farming games, Sandrock's primary economy is commission-based. You earn most of your gols by fulfilling building requests from townsfolk.

docs.title: Income Sources Ranked
docs.text: Method Daily Gols Effort Best For 📋 Town Commissions 200-1,500 Medium Steady income + reputation ⛏️ Mining + Ore 300-800 High Quick cash when commissions are slow 🏚️ Ruins Salvage 400-1,200 High Best risk/reward — combat + resources 🌵 Foraging 100-300 Low Supplemental, daily free resources 🏭 Crafted Items 500-2,000 Medium Once you unlock advanced refiners 🗑️ Scrap Recycling 150-400 Low Passive income from recyclers

docs.title: Commission Strategy
docs.text:
The Commerce Commission board is your primary income driver. Key tips:

  1. Check every morning — new commissions appear daily
  2. Accept commissions that use materials you already have — don't commit to items requiring rare ores
  3. Prioritize time-sensitive quests — some pay 50% bonus for same-day delivery
  4. Keep a "commission chest" — store 5 of every common material (copper bars, basic mechanisms, wooden planks)
  5. Build reputation — higher reputation unlocks better-paying commissions

docs.title: Commission Priority Matrix
docs.text: Commission Type Gols Reputation Time Pressure Take It? Basic repair (copper bars, planks) 200-400 Low 2-3 days ✅ Always — uses common mats Gift requests (rare items) 500-800 Medium 1-2 days ✅ If you have the item Building construction 800-1,500 High 4-7 days ✅ Best payout per commission Custom furniture 400-600 Medium 2-3 days ✅ Good mid-game income Delivery (fetch quests) 150-300 Low 1 day ⚠️ Only if on the way

docs.title: Upgrade Priority
docs.text: Priority Tool Copper Cost Effect ⭐ 1 Pickaxe 20 Copper Bars 2× mining speed, break desert rocks ⭐ 2 Axe 15 Copper Bars 2× wood chopping, cut cactus ⭐ 3 Jetpack (Quest reward) Huge exploration advantage ⭐ 4 Hoe 10 Copper Bars Till larger areas for planting 5 Watering Can 10 Copper Bars Water 3 plants at once

docs.title: Mining for Materials
docs.text:
The Breach (eastern side of town) is your main mining area. Enter through the door at the tall mesa.

Mine Depth Ores Available Key Resources Floors 1-5 Copper, Stone, Dirt Starter materials Floors 5-15 Iron, Copper, Crystals Mid-tier metals Floors 15-25 Iron, Manganese, Gems Advanced crafting Floors 25+ All ores, Rare relics End-game materials

Mining tips: - Bring food for stamina — you'll spend 4-6 hours per trip - Use elevators every 5 floors as save points - Do not sell raw ore — every ore goes into tool upgrades or commissions - Collect data discs — they unlock new crafting recipes from the Research Center

docs.title: Week 1: Survival Mode
docs.text: Day Focus Checkpoint 1 Worktable + Furnace + Dew Collector Basic workshop running 2-3 Copper tools, first commissions 5 Copper Bars produced 4-5 Unlock ruin access, first mining trip 10+ floor depth 6-7 Second furnace, 2-3 commissions completed 2,000+ gols earned

docs.title: Week 2: Building Momentum
docs.text: Goal How Milestone Copper pickaxe upgrade Mine 20 copper ore + smelt 2× mining speed Iron tools progress Reach floor 10+ in ruins Access iron veins 5+ commissions completed Daily board checks 2-star reputation Horse stable Buy from Magic Shop (8,000g) 2× travel speed

docs.title: Week 3-4: Mid-Season Goals
docs.text:
• Jetpack — complete the quest line to unlock this game-changer
  • Water tank upgrade — more daily water = more crops and crafting
  • Factory expansion — build a second worktable and recycler
  • House upgrade — more room for processing machines
  • 8,000 gols saved — for horse and stable

docs.title: Beginner Mistakes to Avoid
docs.text:
Mistake Why It Hurts Better Approach Selling copper ore Delays tool upgrades by 3-5 days Only sell processed bars after upgrades done Accepting every commission Rare material commissions drain resources Check requirements before accepting Ignoring the Research Center Misses advanced recipes Donate data discs every 2-3 mining trips No Dew Collectors Water becomes a bottleneck early game Build 2-3 in week 1 Selling monster drops Used in dozens of crafting recipes Store 10+ of each before selling

Final tip: Sandrock's economy rewards preparation over speed. Keep a well-stocked material chest, check the commission board before every mining trip (so you know what ores to prioritize), and never turn down a chance to upgrade your tools. If you enter month 2 with iron-tier tools, 5,000 gols saved, and the jetpack, you are in the top 10% of new players.

docs.title: Gift Giving Tips
docs.text:
• Loved gifts give the highest relationship boost — always check an NPC's Loved items before visiting.
  • Birthday gifts provide a massive 3x multiplier on relationship points.
  • Item quality matters — higher-quality versions of Loved items grant additional points.
  • Weekly limits apply: you can give each NPC up to 2 gifts per week (1 for spouses).
  • Commission rewards sometimes include exclusive Loved gifts for specific NPCs.

Data source: My Time at Sandrock Wiki 1.0

docs.title: 🏠 My Time at Sandrock — Housing & Decor Complete Guide
docs.text: Having a comfortable home in the desert directly affects your stamina recovery, machine efficiency, and NPC relationships. This guide covers everything from house upgrades to yard layout planning.

docs.title: 🏗️ House Upgrade Path
docs.text:
Stage Cost Core Materials Rooms Unlocked Features 🌵 Starter Shack Free None 1 Basic rest 🏚️ Small House 5,000 Wood ×50, Copper Bar ×20 2 Kitchen 🏠 Medium House 15,000 Hardwood ×80, Iron Bar ×30, Glass ×10 3 Bedroom (+Energy) 🏡 Large House 35,000 Rubber Wood ×60, Manganese Steel Bar ×40, Glass ×20 4 Basement 🏰 Luxury Mansion 80,000 Titanium Alloy ×50, Magnesium Bar ×30, Silicon Wafer ×15 6 All features

💡 Tip: Recommended pace: Medium (before Workshop Lv.4) → Large (before Workshop Lv.6) → Luxury (post-story). Keep some cash aside for machine upgrades at each stage. Don't rush to max it out all at once.

docs.title: 🛏️ Room Functions in Detail
docs.text:
Room Unlock Stage Core Function Best Setup 🛌 Bedroom Medium House Stamina recovery +25% to +75% Bed + wardrobe + rug 🍳 Kitchen Small House Cooking buff duration +50% Stove + fridge 🔧 Workshop Large House Crafting speed +10% to +30% Workbench + tool rack + lighting 🧪 Lab Large House Research speed +20% Analyzer + bookshelf 🪴 Greenhouse Luxury Mansion Indoor planting Flower pots + irrigation 🎮 Game Room Luxury Mansion Daily passive affection boost Arcade machine + decorations

Stamina Recovery Formula: Base 30 STA × (1 + Bedroom Bonus%). Luxury bed (75%) → 30 × 1.75 = 52.5 STA/night, which is roughly 2-3 extra ruin floors per day.

docs.title: 📐 Blueprint Acquisition & Decor Mechanics
docs.text:
Blueprint Source Examples Decor Items Stat Effects 🛒 Furniture Shop (Tue-Sat) Basic bed, chairs 🖼️ Wall paintings Charm +1~3 🏚️ Ruin Data Discs Advanced workbench, decorative lights 🏮 Carpets Stamina recovery +2%~5% 🎁 Quest Rewards Exclusive limited furniture 💡 Lighting Crafting speed +1%~3% 🤝 NPC Friendship (5/10 hearts) Mi-an's Sandy Style Set 🌿 Potted plants Mood +2/pot

💡 Tip: Every 10 Charm points in your home = +1 affection per gift given to NPCs. At 100+ Charm you can host parties inviting multiple NPCs at once, dramatically speeding up friendship grinding.

docs.title: 🔧 Workshop Layout Principles
docs.text:
Principle Explanation 🔄 Raw → Process → Finished Arrange by workflow to minimize walking 📦 Categorized Storage Separate chests for ores, plants, finished goods 🚶 1-tile aisles Leave walking space between machines 💡 Full lighting coverage Place a light next to each machine for efficiency

Recommended 4-Zone Layout: Furnace Zone (3 units against wall) → Processing Zone (Blender + Grinder + Compressor) → Woodworking Zone (Workbench + Tailoring Table + Assembly Table) → Storage Zone (4-8 chests)

docs.title: 🌿 Yard Expansion
docs.text:
Area Unlock Cost Recommended Use Front Yard Default Free Mailbox + decoration Side Yard Small House 2,000 Animal pen + planting Back Yard Medium House 5,000 Large machines + drying racks Extension Large House 15,000 Greenhouse + orchard

Desert Theme Idea: Entry cactus pots ×2 + sandstone path → left zone small pond + palm tree → right zone succulent garden + clay pot → back yard furnace area under a shade canopy.

💡 Tip: Purely decorative items can be packed densely without blocking machine space. Dark wood furniture + cactus greenery combo uses the fewest materials while keeping that Sandrock feel.

docs.title: 📊 ROI Quick Reference
docs.text:
Project Investment Return Payback Period Medium Bedroom 15,000 +15 STA/day ≈100 days Luxury Bed 8,000 +7.5 STA/day ≈80 days Kitchen 5,000 Cooking Buff +50% 2-3 ruin runs Charm 100 ≈20,000 2× affection 20 gift exchanges

Data source: My Time at Sandrock v1.4 in-game data

docs.title: My Time at Sandrock — Resource & Material Guide
docs.text: Surviving and thriving in the harsh desert of Sandrock depends on knowing where to find every resource, how to gather it efficiently, and what it's used for. This comprehensive guide covers all major materials organized by type.

docs.text: Resource Where to Find Purpose Rarity Blue Data Disk Ruins, Salvage Research, Unlock Recipes Common Yellow Data Disk Deeper Ruins, Gecko Station Advanced Research Uncommon Red Data Disk Paradise Lost, Depths Late-game Technology Rare Relic Scrap Salvage, Ruins Reconstructing Relics Common Ancient Relic Piece Ruins (specific spots) Museum, Commission Rewards Varies Prototype Parts Abandoned Ruins Advanced Machines Rare Old Parts Breach, Salvage Early Repair Quests Common

docs.title: Water & Fuel
docs.text:
Water is the most critical resource in Sandrock. It is used for farming, crafting, and hydration.

Resource Source Primary Use Dew Drops Grass, Early Morning Harvest Minimal Water, Early Farming Water Bottle Water Tower, Purchase Farming, Cooking Large Water Tank Water Tower (upgraded) Bulk Water Storage Portable Water Tank Purchase from Water World Water on the Go Wood Chopping Trees Furnace Fuel Hardwood Chopping Large Trees Efficient Furnace Fuel Coal Mining (Gecko Station+), Purchase High-Efficiency Fuel Power Stones Mining, Monster Drops Machine Power Source

docs.title: Gathering Efficiency Tips
docs.text:
1. Upgrade your pickaxe and axe as soon as possible — higher tiers unlock access to rarer ores and woods.
  2. Scavenge the Breach daily for early-game scrap and data disks — it refreshes each in-game day.
  3. Plant Sandrice and Cactus on your farm for a renewable source of plant materials.
  4. Save Titanium and Magnesium ores for alloy production rather than selling raw — refined materials are worth significantly more.
  5. Check the train that passes through Sandrock — sometimes it drops rare materials on the tracks.
  6. Commission board rewards often include rare materials that are difficult to mine — prioritize these commissions.
  7. Geode processing at the Grinder station yields gems and rare crafting components — always crack geodes open rather than selling them.

Data source: My Time at Sandrock Wiki 1.0

docs.title: 📅 My Time at Sandrock — Seasonal & Monthly Guide
docs.text: Every day in the desert counts. Sandrock has 12 months per year, 28 days each. This guide breaks down the best Year 1 rhythm month by month.

docs.title: 🗓️ Climate System Overview
docs.text: Quarter Months Characteristics Effects ☀️ Dry Season 1-6 Hot and dry Water consumption +50% 🌧️ Rainy Season 7-12 Occasional rain Water consumption -30%, mushroom growth 🌪️ Sandstorm Period 3, 6, 9 Low visibility Outdoor efficiency -50%, recommend ruins

docs.title: Month 1: Rebuilding (D1-28)
docs.text:
★★★★★ Complete tutorial → Build stone furnace + workbench (Day 1) ★★★★ Sweep the map for scrap, copper ore → Build Dew Collector ★★★★ Accept commissions daily, prioritize high-reward ones

💡 Tip: Don't farm in Month 1! Water is extremely scarce. Forage cactus and grill it for food.

docs.title: Month 2: Getting Established (D29-56)
docs.text:
★★★★★ Upgrade to copper pickaxe + copper axe ★★★★ Explore Breach ruins for yellow data discs ★★★ Buy your first chicken + plant sandrice ×10

🎉 Founding Day (M2 D15): Building contest. Prepare Copper Bars ×10 + Iron Bars ×5 + Hardwood ×20. Winner gets "Desert Lamp" blueprint + 3,000 Gols.

docs.title: Month 3: Sandstorm (D57-84)
docs.text: ★★★★★ Spend sandstorm days in Breach second floor farming discs ★★★★ Upgrade Workshop to Lv.4, unlock Blender/Oven blueprints ★★★ Build cheese press + buy a goat

docs.title: Month 4: Expansion (D85-112)
docs.text: ★★★★★ Upgrade house to Medium → Bedroom energy +25% ★★★★ Buy chili/tomato seeds from the store ★★★ Tame a mount + build a loom

docs.title: Months 5-6: Sprint (D113-168)
docs.text:
★★★★★ Unlock Gecko Station ruins + upgrade to iron pickaxe ★★★★ Cooking Contest (M5 D15) — stock up on 3⭐ ingredients early ★★★ Buy a cow + upgrade house to Large

🎉 Harvest Festival (M6 D15): Crop quality competition. Bring 3⭐+ rare crops → Golden Watering Can + fertilizer blueprints.

docs.title: Months 7-8: Rainy Season (D169-224)
docs.text: ★★★★★ Explore Paradise Lost, upgrade to manganese steel tools ★★★★ Collect rainy season mushrooms + use rainfall to expand farm ★★★ Tame a camel + research silicon wafers in the lab

docs.title: Months 9-12: Late Game (D225-336)
docs.text: ★★★★★ Luxury house + Sandrock Depths for titanium ore ★★★★★ Main story completion push ★★★ NPC 10-heart sprint + titanium alloy tools

docs.title: 🔧 My Time at Sandrock — Workshop Optimization Guide
docs.text: Efficiency is everything in Sandrock. The difference between scraping by and dominating the workshop rankings lies in production chain optimization, commission selection, and machine layout. This guide walks you through every optimization lever from day 1 to endgame.

docs.title: 🏗️ Early Game Machine Layout
docs.text:
Priority Machine Qty Input → Output Why 1 Stone Furnace 2 2 Copper Ore + 1 Wood → Copper Bar First gol-maker 2 Stone Furnace 1 1 Tin + 1 Copper Ore → Bronze Bar Unlock advanced machines 3 Worktable 1 — All intermediate crafting 4 Dew Collector 3 → 3 Water Bottles/day Passive water income

Furnace ratio: Maintain 3 furnaces per 1 grinder for the first 30 days. Dedicate one to glass production — it's required for 40% of early commissions.

docs.title: 📦 Production Chain Profit Analysis
docs.text:
Product Materials Cost Sell Margin Time Gol/Hour Copper Bar 8g 18g +10g 30min 20 Bronze Bar 12g 32g +20g 45min 27 Glass 🥇 6g 22g +16g 20min 48 Tempered Glass 10g 55g +45g 1.5hr 30

Best early margin: Glass at 48 gol/hour beats everything. Keep 10 in stock for commissions. Best mid-game: Tempered Glass — manganese investment pays off fast.

docs.title: 📋 Commission Strategy
docs.text:
Tier Gol Range Avg Time Gol/Min Priority 💎 Premium (red) 800-2000 5-10 min 80-400 Always accept ⭐ High (blue) 400-800 5-10 min 40-160 Next priority 📋 Standard (white) 100-400 3-5 min 20-133 Fill gaps

Rules: Only accept same-day deliveries — missed deadlines cost reputation. Stockpile Copper Bar (×5), Glass (×10), Bronze Bar (×5). Skip premium Titanium/Opal commissions early-game. Board refreshes at 8 AM and 3 PM.

docs.title: 🏠 Workshop Expansion Priority
docs.text:
Phase Room Cost Benefit When 1 Assembly Room 5000g + 20 Wood +2 assembly stations Week 2 2 Furnace Room 8000g + 30 Hardwood +4 furnace slots Week 3-4 3 Grinder Ext. 12000g + 10 Tempered Glass +2 grinder slots Month 2 4 Automation Hub 50000g + 20 Data Disks Conveyor system Month 4+

Do not expand until you have 10,000g liquid — you need operating capital for seeds and fuel.

docs.title: 🤖 Automation Milestones
docs.text:
Rank Unlock Impact 5 Conveyor Belts Connect furnaces→grinders — eliminates 70% walking 7 Auto-feed Hoppers Machines pull from chests automatically 9 Smart Sorters Route copper to chest A, bronze to chest B

Manual flow: Ore chest → furnace → bar chest → grinder → refined chest → assembly → output. With conveyors, zero movement time.

docs.title: 🏛️ Relic Restoration — Worth It?
docs.text:
Relic Pieces Time Sell Price Worth? Ancient Vase 3 2hr 1500g ✅ Quick Dinosaur Skeleton 8 8hr 5000g ✅ Museum prize Old Computer 5 4hr 3000g ✅ Story needed Broken Watch 4 3hr 1200g ❌ Low gol/hr

Rule: Restore relics paying ≥400g per restoration hour. Sell raw pieces for everything else.

docs.title: 💧 Water Management
docs.text:
Source Daily Output Cost Best Use Dew Collector (basic) 3 bottles Free First 3 months Dew Collector (upgraded) 6 bottles 10 Copper Bars Month 2+ Water Tower Unlimited 50g/5 bottles Emergency only

Water efficiency: Cactus (16 gol/water) → Aloe Vera (15) → Sandrice (12). Plant Cactus — half the water of Sandrice. Four upgraded Dew Collectors = 24 bottles/day, enough for 30 cactus tiles with surplus.

docs.title: 📈 Workshop Rank Fast Track
docs.text:
Rank Rep Unlock Fastest Path 0→2 150 Grinder Do 5 commissions 2→4 400 Assembly Station Blue commissions only 4→6 1500 Conveyor Belts Premium + relic quests 6→8 5000 Tier 4 machines Museum + story commissions 8→9 8000 Automation Hub Endgame multi-stage commissions

Focus exclusively on commissions the first 15 days to reach Rank 4 by day 15.

docs.title: 🏁 Endgame Max Gol/Hour
docs.text:
Final setup: 6 auto-fed Furnaces + 3 Grinders + 3 Assembly Stations + 12 Conveyor Belts + 4 Smart Sorters + 40 Cactus planters. Loop: Ore → furnace → bar → grinder → refined → assembly → output, zero movement.

Max output: ~16,400 gol/day passive + active (950 gol/hr from Tempered Glass + Titanium Alloy, 2000 gol/day from crops, 3000 gol/day from relics).

Data source: My Time at Sandrock Wiki 1.0, game version 1.4+
