docs.title: 🏛️ Stardew Valley — Ancient Fruit Empire Setup Guide (1.6)
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6.15 (current) | Sources: Stardew Valley Wiki | Last verified: 2026-07-06

Ancient Fruit wine is the undisputed king of endgame money in Stardew Valley. A single Ancient Seed can grow into an empire producing 277,200g/week from the greenhouse alone — and twice that with the Ginger Island farm added.

But getting from one Ancient Seed → full greenhouse → island farm → endless wine requires careful planning. This guide breaks down every step with exact numbers, seed maker math, player-tier timelines, and the optimal path for each playstyle.

docs.title: 📊 Why Ancient Fruit?
docs.text:
Let's start with the hard numbers that make Ancient Fruit the best crop in the game:

Metric Ancient Fruit Starfruit Blueberry (Summer only) Base sell price 550g 750g 50g×3 berries = 150g Growth time 28 days 13 days 13 days Regrowth Every 7 days No (replant) Every 4 days Annual harvests (greenhouse) 52 28 36 (3 seasons) Wine price (Artisan) 2,310g 3,150g 2,100g (uses 3 berries) Wine GPD per tile (Artisan) 330g/day 242g/day 300g/day Replant cost Zero 400g/seed Zero Seed Maker input needed One fruit = 2 seeds avg One fruit = 1 seed Not needed (buy seeds) Total annual value per tile (Artisan wine) 171,600g 88,830g 108,000g

🏆 Ancient Fruit wins on every measure except single-bottle peak price. Starfruit wine sells for more per bottle (3,150g vs 2,310g), but Ancient Fruit produces every 7 days with zero replant cost, giving it a 36% higher gold-per-day-per-tile in the greenhouse.

docs.title: The Real Advantage: Passive Income
docs.text:
Once your Ancient Fruit empire is established, your weekly routine is: 1. Harvest on Sunday (takes 2 minutes) 2. Walk to the keg shed (30 seconds) 3. Process all fruit into wine (uses existing kegs) 4. Collect finished wine from previous batch (10 seconds per keg) 5. Free to do anything else for the remaining 6 days, 23 hours

No replanting. No re-watering. No buying seeds. Pure passive income.

docs.title: 🌱 Phase 1: Acquiring Your First Ancient Seed
docs.text: Before any empire, you need one Ancient Seed. Here are all acquisition methods, ranked by reliability:

docs.title: Method 1: Seed Maker from the Dye Bundle (Most Reliable)
docs.text:
Step Action When 1 Complete the Dye Bundle (Bulletin Board) Spring-Fall Year 1 2 Receive 1 Seed Maker as reward Instant 3 Place any non-forage crop in the Seed Maker Anytime 4 0.5% chance to produce Ancient Seeds Per input crop

The Dye Bundle requires: - Red: Red Mushroom, Hot Pepper, or Cranberry - Orange: Pumpkin, Orange, or Summer Spangle - Yellow: Corn, Dandelion, or Sunflower - Green: Leek, Emerald, or Fiddlehead Fern - Blue: Blueberry, Blue Jazz, or Aquamarine - Purple: Amaranth, Eggplant, or Amethyst

💡 Zero-Spend Tip: The Dye Bundle is the only guaranteed way to get a Seed Maker without reaching Farming Level 9 (which requires massive XP). Prioritize completing this bundle.

docs.title: Method 2: Artifact Spot (Most Common First Seed)
docs.text:
Ancient Seeds can be found as an artifact from:

Source Chance Notes Artifact Spots (Mountain/River/Forest) ~0.7% Dig with hoe, any season Fishing Treasure Chests ~0.7% Any fishing location Cinder Shard Nodes (Volcano Dungeon) ~0.8% Floor 5+ Traveling Cart ~0.9% (when available) 600-1,000g, Fri/Sun Bug kills (Floors 5-19 of Mines) Very rare Rare drop from bugs

Seed Maker Conversion (Artifact → Seed): 1. Donate the Ancient Seed artifact to the Museum (Gunther gives you the crafting recipe) 2. Craft with: Ancient Seed artifact + Starfruit = 1 packet of plantable Ancient Seeds 3. Plant the seeds

docs.title: Method 3: Seed Maker Spam (Fastest for Speedrunners)
docs.text:
Once you have one Seed Maker and a steady crop stream:

Input Probability of Ancient Seeds Expected crops needed Blueberry (3 berries = 3 inputs) 0.5% per input ~67 inputs (or 23 blueberry plants once) Cranberry (2 berries = 2 inputs) 0.5% per input ~100 inputs Corn (1 ear) 0.5% per input ~200 inputs Any single fruit/veg 0.5% per input 200 inputs avg

The Seed Maker math for 1 Ancient Seed: - Expected inputs: 1 ÷ 0.005 = 200 inputs - At 2 seeds per input avg: You sacrifice ~100 crops worth of seeds to get 1 Ancient Seed - Best crop to sacrifice: Blueberries (fastest to mass-produce, 3 berries per harvest = 3x the Seed Maker chances)

⚠️ 1.6 Change: The 1.6 update changed game randomization, which removed the old Seed Maker output exploits (where you could reload a save until you got Ancient Seeds). It's now purely random per input.

docs.title: 🌿 Phase 2: Seed Multiplication (One → Full Greenhouse)
docs.text: This is the most critical phase. You need to turn 1 Ancient Seed into 120+ seeds to fill the greenhouse.

docs.title: The Multiplication Loop
docs.text:
Plant 1 Ancient Seed (grows in 28 days)
         ↓
First harvest: 1 Ancient Fruit (after 7 more days)
         ↓
Put in Seed Maker → avg 2 Ancient Seeds (97.5% chance)
         ↓
Plant both seeds → wait 28 days
         ↓
2 fruits → Seed Maker → 4 seeds
         ↓
4 seeds → plant → 4 fruits → 8 seeds
         ↓
...exponential growth...

docs.title: Elite Growth Timeline
docs.text:
Cycle Seeds Planted Days Elapsed Total Fruits Seeds Produced 1 1 0 (plant) 0 0 2 1 28 (first harvest) 1 → 2 seeds 2 3 2 56 2 → 4 seeds 4 4 4 84 4 → 8 seeds 8 5 8 112 8 → 16 seeds 16 6 16 140 16 → 32 seeds 32 7 32 168 32 → 64 seeds 64 8 64 196 64 → 128 seeds 128 ✅

Total time: ~196 days (7 seasons) from 1 seed to 128 seeds.

⚠️ This is too slow for most players. You need to accelerate this by finding more Ancient Seeds naturally. Every additional Ancient Seed you find (artifact spot, Traveling Cart, bug drop) shortens the timeline dramatically.

docs.title: Accelerated Path (Finding 3-5 Seeds Year 1)
docs.text:
If you find 3 Ancient Seeds by Summer Year 1:

Cycle Seeds Planted Days Seeds Produced 1 (3 seeds) 3 28 3 fruits → 6 seeds 2 6 56 6 fruits → 12 seeds 3 12 84 12 → 24 seeds 4 24 112 24 → 48 seeds 5 48 140 48 → 96 seeds 6 96 168 96 → 192 seeds

Total time: ~168 days (6 seasons) → enough for greenhouse + surplus.

docs.title: Speedrun Path (Greenhouse by Summer Year 2)
docs.text:
If you find 8+ Ancient Seeds by Winter Year 1 (via Traveling Cart, artifact hunting, Seed Maker spam):

Cycle Seeds Planted Days Seeds Produced 1 (8 seeds) 8 28 8 → 16 seeds 2 16 56 16 → 32 seeds 3 32 84 32 → 64 seeds 4 64 112 64 → 128 seeds

Total time: ~112 days (4 seasons) → full greenhouse by Summer Year 2.

docs.title: 🏡 Phase 3: Greenhouse Setup
docs.text: The greenhouse has 120 plantable tiles (12×10 grid). Here's the optimal layout:

docs.title: Sprinkler Requirements
docs.text:
Sprinkler Type Count Needed Cost Coverage Quality Sprinkler 6 60,000g Covers 114 tiles Iridium Sprinkler 4 400,000g Covers 120 tiles Iridium (w/ pressure nozzle) 2 400,000g + 50,000g Covers 120 tiles

💡 Zero-Spend Option: Use Quality Sprinklers (crafted, no gold cost besides bars). 6 Quality Sprinklers require 6 Gold Bars + 6 Iron Bars + 3 Refined Quartz — all mineable for free.

docs.title: The Deluxe Retaining Soil Alternative
docs.text:
In 1.6, Deluxe Retaining Soil (crafted from stone ×5 + fiber ×3 + clay ×2 per unit) guarantees 100% water retention. This means:

  • No sprinklers needed — every tile is plantable
  • Total crops: 120 tiles (vs 114-120 with sprinklers)
  • Cost: ~600 fiber + 1,000 stone + 400 clay for the full greenhouse

🏆 This is the best option for Ginger Island (where you can't use sprinklers without the island farm obelisk). Apply Deluxe Retaining Soil once and never water again.

docs.title: Greenhouse Income Projection
docs.text:
Setup Weekly Harvest Weekly Wine (Artisan) Seasonal Income 120 Ancient Fruit, full Kegs 120 fruit 277,200g 1,108,800g 120 Ancient Fruit, 60 Kegs (phased) 120 fruit ~138,600g (half processed) ~831,600g 120 Ancient Fruit, no Kegs (raw) 120 fruit — 66,000g (raw, no processing)

⚠️ Critical: Without kegs, greenhouse Ancient Fruit is worth only 550g each (66,000g/week). With Artisan Wine, it's 2,310g each (277,200g/week). Kegs are 4.2× more important than the crop itself.

docs.title: 🏝️ Phase 4: Ginger Island Expansion
docs.text: Once the greenhouse is sorted, the next step is the Island Farm — which has 788 arable tiles.

docs.title: Island Farm Ancient Fruit Setup
docs.text:
Zone Tiles Setup Cost Notes Main field 300 300 seeds (worth ~165k in sacrificed fruit) Use Deluxe Retaining Soil Left section 200 200 seeds Expand gradually Right section 200 200 seeds Add once main field is stable

Total island tiles: 788 (theoretical max) Practical tiles for Ancient Fruit: 500-600 (leave room for pathways, huts, keg sheds)

docs.title: Island Farm Layout Principles
docs.text:
1. Deluxe Retaining Soil on every tile (no sprinklers needed on island)
  2. Keg sheds near the farmhouse (minimize walking)
  3. One Junimo Hut per 2 fields (auto-harvest, saves hours)
  4. Fiber Seeds on unused tiles (prevent debris from spawning)

docs.title: Combined Income (Greenhouse + Island)
docs.text:
Location Tiles Harvests/Season Fruit/Season Wine Value (Artisan) Greenhouse 120 13 (once per week) 1,560 3,603,600g Island Farm 500 13 6,500 15,015,000g Combined 620 13 8,060 18,618,600g/season

💰 That's 18.6 million gold per season — enough to buy the Return Scepter (2M), Gold Clock (10M), and still have 6M left over. In one season.

docs.title: 🍷 Phase 5: Keg Infrastructure
docs.text: Ancient Fruit are worthless without kegs. You need enough kegs to process every single fruit within its 7-day regrowth cycle.

docs.title: Keg Requirements
docs.text: Setup Fruit/Week Kegs Needed Keg Material Cost Greenhouse only 120 120 3,600 Wood + 120 Copper + 120 Iron + 120 Oak Resin Greenhouse + 300 Island tiles 420 420 12,600 Wood + 420 Copper + 420 Iron + 420 Oak Resin Full empire (620 tiles) 8,060/season 620+ 18,600 Wood + 620 Copper + 620 Iron + 620 Oak Resin

docs.title: Oak Resin Bottleneck
docs.text:
This is the real bottleneck in scaling your empire. Each keg needs 1 Oak Resin.

Oak Resin Source Production Rate Time to 120 Kegs Time to 420 Kegs Tapper on Oak Tree 1 resin/7 days per tree 28 trees, 7 days 28 trees, 105 days 20 Oak Trees (realistic Year 1) 20 resin/7 days 42 days 147 days Heavy Tapper (1.6) 1 resin/3.75 days per tree 17 trees, 30 days 17 trees, 92 days

💡 Strategy: Plant 20-30 Oak Trees on your farm in Spring Year 1 (save all acorns!). Tapper them by Fall. By the time you have enough Ancient Fruit for the greenhouse, you'll have Oak Resin stockpiled. Heavy Tappers (unlocked at Foraging Level 8) cut the time in half.

docs.title: Keg Rotation Schedule
docs.text:
Day Activity Sunday Harvest Ancient Fruit → All into Kegs Sunday night Fill last keg, start timer Sunday+7 All 120 kegs finish simultaneously → Collect wine → Refill

With 120 kegs and 120 fruit per week, the timing is perfect. Every Sunday you harvest, refill, and collect the previous batch. One visit to the greenhouse + keg shed per week.

docs.title: Plan A: Zero-Spend / Casual (Year 3+ Goal)
docs.text:
Goal: Fill the greenhouse with Ancient Fruit by Year 3. Don't rush.

Year Focus Milestone Year 1 Complete Dye Bundle, find 1-2 Ancient Seeds Seed Maker acquired, first fruit harvested Year 2 Plant first seeds, multiply slowly ~8-16 seeds by Winter Year 2 Year 3 Fill greenhouse gradually 120 seeds by Summer Year 3

Keg plan: Craft 20-30 kegs per year. Use existing oak trees.

docs.title: Plan B: Optimized (Greenhouse by Year 2 Summer)
docs.text:
Goal: Fill greenhouse by Summer Year 2, add island by Year 3.

Year Focus Milestone Year 1 Spring Plant 20 Oak Trees (save ALL acorns) ~5 Ancient Seeds found Year 1 Summer Seed Maker spam with blueberries 10-15 Ancient Seeds by Fall Year 1 Fall Plant seeds in greenhouse (once unlocked) 15 seeds growing Year 1 Winter Harvest first crop, Seed Maker loop 30 seeds Year 2 Spring Plant 30 seeds in greenhouse 60 seeds by Summer Year 2 Summer Full greenhouse 120 Ancient Fruit

Keg plan: Tapper oak trees from Fall Year 1. Build 60 kegs by Winter Year 1, another 60 by Spring Year 2.

docs.title: Plan C: Speedrun (Greenhouse by Year 1 Winter)
docs.text:
Goal: Full greenhouse Ancient Fruit by Winter Year 1. Requires luck + intense optimization.

Week Action Spring 1-14 Dig every artifact spot. Complete Dye Bundle. Find 3+ Ancient Seeds. Plant 30 oak trees. Spring 15-28 Complete Community Center. Unlock greenhouse. Plant 3 Ancient Seeds. Summer 1-14 Blueberry farming. Seed Maker spam (put every 3rd blueberry batch through Seed Maker). Summer 15-28 Collect first Ancient Fruit harvests. Full Seed Maker push. Aim for 12+ seeds. Fall 1-14 Plant 12 seeds in greenhouse. Continue Seed Maker loop. Fall 15-28 Harvest, Seed Maker, expand. Aim for 30+ seeds. Winter 1-14 Full Seed Maker loop. Harvest every 7 days. Winter 15-28 120 seeds in greenhouse. 🏆

Keg plan: Heavy Tappers on 30 oak trees from Summer. Build kegs in batches of 20 as materials allow.

docs.title: 1. Don't Sell the First Fruit
docs.text: Never sell your first Ancient Fruit harvest. It's worth 550g raw. Put it in a Seed Maker and it becomes 2 seeds worth 2,310g each in wine form. The Seed Maker is 4.2× more valuable than selling the fruit.

docs.title: 2. Casks Are Secondary
docs.text:
Casks (in the cellar) age wine from Silver → Gold → Iridium quality over 2 seasons. An Iridium Ancient Fruit Wine sells for 4,620g (vs 2,310g base with Artisan).

Quality Time Price (Artisan) GPD Base 0 2,310g 330g/day Silver 14 days 2,886g 206g/day Gold 28 days 3,465g 124g/day Iridium 56 days 4,620g 83g/day

⚠️ Casks are a trap for early-mid game. Your base wine GPD is 330g/day. Casking drops it to 83g/day (because the wine spends 56 days not selling). Only cask after you have more wine than kegs can process. Fill the cellar with 125 casks and age Iridium Starfruit Wine instead — the longer base time makes the 56-day wait less painful.

docs.title: 3. Ginger Island Priority
docs.text:
Don't rush to plant Ancient Fruit on the island. The Volcano Dungeon should come first: - Get the Infinite Watering Can upgrade (enchantment) - Unlock the Island Obelisk (cheaper than farm obelisk) - Farm Cinder Shards for Heavy Tappers

Once those are done, convert the island farm to Ancient Fruit.

docs.title: 4. The 1.6 Dried Fruit Alternative
docs.text:
The Dehydrator (purchased from Pierre for 10,000g in 1.6) creates Dried Fruit from 5 fruits:

Input (5 Ancient Fruit) Dried Fruit Price Per Fruit Value 5 × 550g = 2,750g 4,150g (7.5×550 + 25) 830g each

At 830g per fruit, Dried Ancient Fruit beats raw (550g) but loses to wine (2,310g). The Dehydrator is a fallback option for when you have excess fruit and not enough kegs. It processes in 1 day (vs 7 for wine), making it useful for overflow.

Method Per Fruit Value Time Best For Artisan Wine 2,310g 7 days Main income Dried Fruit 830g 1 day Overflow / backup Raw Sell 550g Instant Never do this

docs.title: Income Projection (Artisan Profession)
docs.text:
Stage Tiles Fruit/Week Wine/Week Wine/Season 🌱 Greenhouse Start 30 30 69,300g 277,200g 🌿 Greenhouse Full 120 120 277,200g 1,108,800g 🏝️ + Island (300) 420 420 970,200g 3,880,800g 🏛️ Full Empire (620) 620 620 1,432,200g 5,728,800g

🏆 Full empire: 1.4 million gold per week. 5.7 million per season. This is Stardew Valley's ultimate passive income machine.

docs.title: What to Buy with Your Empire Money
docs.text: Item Cost Why Return Scepter 2,000,000g Instant teleport home — saves hours Gold Clock 10,000,000g No more fences, debris, or decay Obelisks (all 4) 5,000,000g Fast travel everywhere House Upgrades 350,000g Cellar for casks Island Obelisk 1,000,000g Quick island access for harvesting Total 18,350,000g One season of full empire income

docs.title: 💡 Ranching Tips
docs.text:
1. Pigs in Fall are unbeatable — Place them near trees, let them roam, collect truffles daily. One pig can earn 725-1,250g/day.
  2. Goat cheese > Cow cheese — The 1.7x markup on goat milk beats the 1.2x on cow milk. Prioritize goats once you can afford them.
  3. Dinosaur mayonnaise is the best daily artisan product — 800g/day with no inputs after purchase. ROI in less than 1 day.
  4. Auto-petter is worth it for large ranches — At 8+ animals, the time saved petting adds up to hours per week.
  5. Bee hives near flowers — Place hives next to fairy roses, poppies, or spring flowers for maximum honey output.
  6. Rabbits are niche — Only keep for Lucky Rabbit's Foot quests. Poor ROI compared to chickens.
  7. Sheep are the worst animal — 4-day shearing cycle + low cloth markup. Skip unless you need wool.
  8. Gold Watering Can for animals — Increases chance of gold-quality products. Essential for max profits.

All prices and values are based on Stardew Valley 1.6.9. Animal products may vary slightly with professions (Coopmaster gives +20% chicken products, Rancher gives +25% animal products).

docs.title: 👢 Complete Boots & Footwear Guide
docs.text:
Boots are an essential equipment slot that provides defense (reduces damage taken) and immunity (reduces debuff duration). This guide covers every pair of boots available in Stardew Valley 1.6, from the humble Sneakers to the endgame Cinderclown Shoes.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 📊 Boots Comparison Table
docs.text: Boots Defense Immunity How to Get Sell Price Rating Sneakers +1 −1 Starting equipment 50g ⭐⭐ Rubber Boots +1 −1 Mines F10 reward (chest) 50g ⭐⭐ Leather Boots +1 −1 Adventurer's Guild (500g) 100g ⭐⭐ Work Boots +1 −1 Adventurer's Guild (500g) 100g ⭐⭐ Combat Boots +3 −1 Adventurer's Guild (1,500g) 250g ⭐⭐⭐ Tundra Boots +2 −1 Adventurer's Guild (750g) 150g ⭐⭐ Thermal Boots +1 +1 Adventurer's Guild (1,000g) 125g ⭐⭐⭐ Dark Boots +2 −1 Adventurer's Guild (2,000g) 300g ⭐⭐⭐ Firewalker Boots +3 +3 Adventurer's Guild (4,000g) 500g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Genie Shoes +1 −1 Adventurer's Guild (2,500g) 250g ⭐⭐ Cowboy Boots +3 −1 Adventurer's Guild (5,000g) 450g ⭐⭐⭐ Darker Boots +4 −1 Adventurer's Guild (6,000g) 600g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Crystal Shoes +3 +5 Adventurer's Guild (10,000g) 750g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Mermaid Boots +5 +5 Adventurer's Guild (20,000g) 1,200g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Dragonscale Boots +7 +7 Adventurer's Guild (25,000g) 1,500g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Space Boots +4 +4 Adventurer's Guild (15,000g) 750g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Leprechaun Shoes +1 +1 Adventurer's Guild (3,000g) 200g ⭐⭐ Cinderclown Shoes +6 +6 Combat Mastery (1.6) 1,600g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: Early Game (Years 1–2)
docs.text: Boots Why Get It Strategy Leather Boots First upgrade from Sneakers Buy at Adventurer's Guild for 500g — cheap defense boost Combat Boots +3 defense for the mid-Mines Best value upgrade at 1,500g — carry to floor 80+ Thermal Boots +1 immunity against slime debuffs Good for Slime Hutch and Quarry Mine runs

docs.title: Mid Game (Year 2–3)
docs.text: Boots Why Get It Strategy Firewalker Boots +3 defense & immunity Excellent all-rounder for Skull Cavern early runs Crystal Shoes +3 defense, +5 immunity Best mid-game boots — immunity to most debuffs Space Boots +4 defense & immunity Solid stats, reasonable price (15,000g)

docs.title: Late Game (Year 3+)
docs.text: Boots Why Get It Strategy Dragonscale Boots +7 defense & immunity Second-best in game — 25,000g Cinderclown Shoes +6 defense & immunity Free (Combat Mastery) — requires Mastery Cave in Cindersap Mermaid Boots +5 defense & immunity Budget alternative at 20,000g

docs.title: 📊 Boots Stat Evolution
docs.text:
This chart shows how defense and immunity improve as you progress:

Defense:  Sneakers (1) → Combat (3) → Firewalker (3) → Crystal (3) → Space (4) → Cinderclown (6) → Dragonscale (7)
Immunity: Sneakers (−1) → Thermal (+1) → Firewalker (+3) → Space (+4) → Crystal (+5) → Cinderclown (+6) → Dragonscale (+7)

Key insight: Immunity is more valuable than defense in the late game. Debuffs from serpents, slimes, and ghosts are the main cause of death — not raw damage.

docs.title: 🏆 Boot Progression Roadmap
docs.text:
1. Day 1: Sneakers (+1/−1) — all you need for the first mines run
  2. Floor 10: Check for Rubber Boots in the chest — free upgrade
  3. Guild unlock (Slime kills x10): Buy Combat Boots (1,500g) — best early investment
  4. After reaching floor 80: Save for Firewalker Boots (4,000g)
  5. Deeper Mines clear: Save 15,000g for Space Boots or 20,000g for Mermaid Boots
  6. Mastery Cave: Claim free Cinderclown Shoes
  7. Endgame: Dragonscale Boots (25,000g) — final pair, only if you have gold to spare

docs.title: 💡 Tips
docs.text:
• Boots never break — buy the best you can afford, they last forever
  • Cinderclown Shoes are the only boots that don't cost money (Combat Mastery reward)
  • Immunity reduces the duration of: Slime debuff (slow), Ghost debuff (dark), Bat debuff (speed down), and Serpent debuff (defense down)
  • Defense reduces damage by roughly 1 HP per point, so +7 defense = 7 less damage per hit
  • You can find rare boot drops in Volcano Dungeon treasure rooms — always check them

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6. All boot stats, prices, and locations verified for version 1.6.9.

docs.title: 💡 Crafting Tips
docs.text:
1. Keg is king — Always prioritize kegs over preserves jars. Wine profit is 2-3x jelly.
  2. Seed Maker for Ancient Fruit — The single best investment after your first ancient seed. Turns one seed into an infinite income stream.
  3. Beer has the highest multiplier — Wheat grows in 4 days, costs 10g, and beer sells for 200g. That's a 20x return.
  4. Mayonnaise is the best early-game income — Chickens are cheap, eggs are free, and mayo sells for 190g.
  5. Crystalarium for gems — Dump duplicate diamonds/emeralds into a crystalarium for passive gem income.
  6. Super Bomb for mining — 5x faster than a pickaxe for clearing floors. Essential for Skull Cavern.
  7. Gold Watering Can — Buy from the Wizard after donating the first prismatic shard. Guarantees iridium-quality crops.
  8. Repair Kits — Always carry basic repair kits when going deep into mines. A broken pickaxe = game over.

All recipes and costs are based on Stardew Valley 1.6.9. Crafting recipes unlock at Farming Level 6. Some items have different costs or requirements in multiplayer.

docs.title: Top 5 Keg / Preserves Profit
docs.text:
Crop Base Price Product Product Price Multiplier Starfruit ⭐ 750g Starfruit Wine 🍷 2250g 3x Melon 🍈 250g Melon Wine 🍷 750g 3x Pumpkin 🎃 320g Pumpkin Juice 🧃 720g 2.25x Hops 🌿 25g Pale Ale 🍺 300g 12x Wheat 🌾 10g Beer 🍺 200g 20x

Hops are the Keg efficiency king! Though the base price is low, they're harvested daily, take 11 days to mature, then produce every day — a year-round Keg assembly line.

Note: Fruits go into Kegs to make Wine (3x base price). Vegetables go into Kegs to make Juice (2.25x base price). Pumpkin is a vegetable, so it produces Pumpkin Juice, not wine. Blueberries (fruit) produce Blueberry Wine at 150g (50g × 3).

docs.text:

💡 Beginner Tip: In the first year, Spring is mainly about making money by fishing. Only grow enough parsnips to complete the bundle, save your gold for Strawberries (buy at Egg Festival on Spring 13).

docs.title: ⚙️ Equipment & Tool Upgrade Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6 | Complete upgrade paths, costs, materials, and comparisons

Every tool in Stardew Valley can be upgraded to improve its performance. This guide covers all 5 tools (Pickaxe, Axe, Sword, Watering Can, Hoe), the upgrade costs, materials required, and performance comparisons.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 🪓 Pickaxe Upgrades
docs.text:
The pickaxe determines how many tiles you can break at once and how quickly you can mine.

Upgrade Material Required Cost Effect Rating Copper Copper Bar ×5 2,000g Breaks 1 tile, slightly faster swing ⭐⭐ Iron Iron Bar ×5 5,000g Breaks 1 tile, faster swing, wider range ⭐⭐⭐ Gold Gold Bar ×5 10,000g Breaks 1 tile, even faster, slight range increase ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Iridium Iridium Bar ×5 25,000g Breaks 1 tile, fastest swing, best durability ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Upgrade Cost Breakdown: - Copper → Iron: 2,000g + 5 Copper Bars (500g ore) = 2,500g total - Iron → Gold: 5,000g + 5 Gold Bars (1,250g ore) = 6,250g total - Gold → Iridium: 25,000g + 5 Iridium Bars (750g ore) = 25,750g total - Grand Total (Copper → Iridium): ~34,500g + all ores

Performance Comparison:

Upgrade Swing Speed Durability Special Copper 1.0x 50 hits Base tool Iron 1.2x 100 hits Slightly faster Gold 1.4x 200 hits Noticeably faster Iridium 1.6x 999 hits Near-infinite

docs.title: 🪓 Axe Upgrades
docs.text:
The axe determines chopping speed and whether you can chop larger trees.

Upgrade Material Required Cost Effect Rating Copper Copper Bar ×5 2,000g Chops regular trees (green stump) ⭐⭐ Iron Iron Bar ×5 5,000g Chops regular + large trees (brown stump) ⭐⭐⭐ Gold Gold Bar ×5 10,000g Faster chop, chops hardwood trees (purple stump) ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Iridium Iridium Bar ×5 25,000g Fastest chop, chops all tree types ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Key Milestone: Iron axe is the minimum needed to chop large trees (brown stumps), which yield 2 hardwood. Gold axe adds hardwood trees (purple stumps).

Chop Speed Comparison:

Upgrade Chops per Tree (Regular) Chops per Tree (Large) Chops per Tree (Hardwood) Copper 4 Cannot chop Cannot chop Iron 3 4 Cannot chop Gold 2 3 4 Iridium 1 2 3

docs.title: ⚔️ Sword Upgrades
docs.text:
Swords are upgraded at the Adventurer's Guild (not Clint's). They determine combat damage.

docs.title: 💧 Watering Can Upgrades
docs.text:
Watering can upgrades determine how many tiles you water at once.

Upgrade Material Required Cost Tiles Watered Rating Copper Copper Bar ×5 2,000g 1 tile (cross) ⭐⭐ Iron Iron Bar ×5 5,000g 4 tiles (2x2) ⭐⭐⭐ Gold Gold Bar ×5 10,000g 9 tiles (3x3) ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Iridium Iridium Bar ×5 25,000g 25 tiles (5x5) ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Time Savings Analysis (per 120-tile farm):

Upgrade Waterings Needed Time Saved vs Copper Copper 120 waterings — — Iron 30 waterings 75% ~90 min/day Gold 13–14 waterings 89% ~105 min/day Iridium 5 waterings 96% ~115 min/day

Recommendation: Gold watering can is the sweet spot for most players. The jump from 4 tiles to 9 tiles cuts waterings by more than half. Iridium is nice but the marginal gain from 9 to 25 tiles is smaller.

docs.title: 📊 Upgrade Priority Guide
docs.text: Priority What to Upgrade When P0 Watering Can → Gold Year 1, Spring Biggest time savings early P0 Axe → Iron Year 1, immediately Required for large trees P1 Pickaxe → Iron Year 1, Spring Better mining speed P1 Sword → Cutlass Year 1, after reaching Mine F40 Survive deeper mines P2 Watering Can → Iridium Year 2 Maximum convenience P2 Hoe → Gold Year 2 Large farm efficiency P3 Pickaxe → Gold Year 2 Faster mining P3 Axe → Gold Year 2 Chop hardwood trees P4 Pickaxe → Iridium Year 3+ Max mining speed P4 Axe → Iridium Year 3+ Max chopping speed P4 Hoe → Iridium Year 3+ Max tilling speed P4 Sword → Lava Katana Year 3+ Best pre-Galaxy sword

docs.title: 💡 Pro Tips
docs.text:
1. Upgrade the watering can first — it saves the most time daily. Gold (9 tiles) is the sweet spot.
  2. Iron axe ASAP — you need it to chop large trees for hardwood, essential for barns/coops.
  3. Don't rush Iridium tools — Gold tools are sufficient for 90% of gameplay. Save iridium for weapons.
  4. Keep a backup Copper pickaxe — if yours breaks (rare), you can still mine.
  5. Upgrade at the Blacksmith — Clint handles all tool upgrades. Talk to him, select your tool.
  6. The Sword upgrade path goes through the Adventurer's Guild — not Clint's shop. Talk to Willy's replacement.
  7. Iridium tool durability is effectively infinite — 999 hits means you'll never need to upgrade again.

This guide covers all standard tool and building upgrades in Stardew Valley 1.6. Prices and costs reflect base values without any discounts from professions or events.

docs.title: 🍁 Complete Fall Guide
docs.text: Fall is the most profitable season in Stardew Valley! Cranberries provide continuous yield + Pumpkins sell at a high price, combined with keg profits to double your income.

New farm suggestion: Cranberries×80 + Pumpkins×40

  3. Unlock the greenhouse (if you haven't yet)

docs.title: 🏛️ Stardew Valley Fair — 1000 Star Tokens Strategy
docs.text:
The Summer 16 Stardew Valley Fair is your chance to get rare items. Here's how to get 1000 tokens easily:

Best Approach: Fishing mini-game - Takes ~5 minutes at the fishing booth - Easy to score 100+ tokens per attempt with practice - No need to save items all year

Alternative (Item-Based): Bring high-quality items for the grange display: - 9 Items × iridium quality + varied categories = max points - Diamond, Wine, Gold Bar, Iridium-quality crops

Best Purchase: Stardrop (2,000 tokens) — permanent energy boost. Save your tokens for this.

docs.title: 💰 Fall Profit Maximization
docs.text:
Activity Daily Gold Notes Cranberries (200 plants) ~3,000g Continuous harvest, low effort Pumpkin (100) + Keg ~2,500g High profit per plant Ancient Fruit + Keg ~4,000g If unlocked from greenhouse Preserves Jars (Cranberries) +40% value Continuous input Kegs (Hops saved from Summer) ~500g/day Already grown, just process

Bottom Line: Cranberries for steady passive income + Pumpkins (kegged) for big batches = optimal Fall economy. Don't forget to plant corn on Summer 28! It survives into Fall for a head start.

docs.title: 🏠 Farm Layout Planning
docs.text: A good layout = time-saving + profitable + beautiful. This guide provides layout plans for every stage from beginner to endgame.

docs.title: Golden Rules of Layout
docs.text: Rule Description 1. Centralized Irrigation Concentrate farming in one area for maximum sprinkler coverage 2. Optimal Pathing Daily route: House → Mailbox → Farmhouse → Greenhouse → Barn, in a straight line 3. Modular Design Four main zones: Planting Zone + Animal Zone + Processing Zone + Decoration Zone 4. Leave Room Buildings can be moved, but plan roads and tree positions in advance

docs.title: 🌱 Beginner Layout (First Year Spring)
docs.text:
Goal: Maximize planting area with minimal money

│        │                         │

│     │  Furnace+Anvil │           │

First Spring Focus: Don't worry about layout — earn money and upgrade tools first. Save enough by the end of spring to buy Copper/Iron Sprinklers.

Beginner recommendation: Standard Farm or Four Corners Farm.

docs.title: ❓ Layout FAQ
docs.text:
Q: Do I need to plan the layout all at once? A: No. Buildings can be moved by Robin (free!), but roads and trees can be planned in advance.

Q: When should I start planning the final layout? A: First year winter — by then all the land is cleared and you have some money to spare.

Q: When can I get lots of Iridium Sprinklers? A: After reaching floor 80+ in the mines, open geodes and kill monsters for iridium ore, then craft iridium sprinklers.

docs.title: 🎪 Festival Events Calendar
docs.text:
Stardew Valley has 12 major festivals each year, plus the 3-day Night Market. Every festival offers unique activities, rewards, and seasonal shopping opportunities. This guide lists every festival with complete details.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.text:

🛒 Festival Shop (Must-Buy Items)

💰 Strawberry Seed Investment Guide

Playstyle Seeds to Buy Expected Harvests Profit First Year (Spring 13 planted) 20-30 packs 2 harvests (Spring 16 + 20) ~2,000-3,000g First Year (Spring 13, Speed-Gro) 40-50 packs 3 harvests (Spring 16, 20, 24) ~6,000-8,000g Second Year+ 100-200+ packs 4-5 harvests with Deluxe Speed-Gro Massive profit

docs.title: Spring 24 — Flower Dance
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM Location Cindersap Forest (south of the Wizard's Tower) Start Location Enter the forest — automatically starts Main Activity Dance with an NPC (requires ≥4 hearts) Reward None (cosmetic only) Secondary Activities Shop for decorations + flowers

💞 How to Dance with an NPC

  1. Give 2 quality gifts per week (Loved gifts preferred)
  2. Talk to them daily
  3. By Spring 24, you need 4 hearts
  4. Easiest NPCs to dance with Year 1:
  5. Haley — Loves Daffodils (foraged) and Sunflowers
  6. Elliott — Loves Duck Feathers or Pomegranate (harder Year 1)
  7. Leah — Loves Salad (buy at Saloon for 220g)
  8. Harvey — Loves Coffee (buy at Saloon for 300g)

Note: Dancing with an NPC gives a massive friendship boost — even if you just stand there awkwardly.

docs.title: Winter 8 — Festival of Ice (Fishing Tournament)
docs.text:

Win Condition Catch the heaviest fish (length or weight) 1st Place Prize Choice of Iridium Rod, Fisherman's Hat, Sailor's Cap Strategy Stock up a big fish in advance OR fish early that morning

🎣 How to Win (Guaranteed)

Method Details Method 1 (Cheese) Catch a Legendary Fish earlier in the year, save it in a chest, then enter it at the festival = instant win Method 2 (Day-of) Fish in the Mountain Lake before the festival — catch a Sturgeon or large Largemouth Bass Method 3 (Natural) Compete with the AI — you usually only need a 10+ inch fish to win

docs.title: 🐟 Fish Pond Guide — Complete Roe & Pond Products
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6.15 | All fish pond species with roe values, aged roe, pond products, and profit analysis

Fish ponds are a passive income powerhouse in Stardew Valley. Unlike crops or kegs that require daily attention, a well-stocked fish pond produces roe and special items automatically. This guide covers the economics of every pond fish so you know which ones pay off.

Data sourced from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 🏗️ Fish Pond Basics
docs.text: Item Cost Materials Unlocks At Fish Pond 5,000g 200 Stone + 5 Seaweed + 5 Green Algae Farming Level 3

docs.title: How Fish Ponds Work
docs.text:
1. Population starts at 1 fish. As it reproduces, population caps increase at 3, 5, 7, and 10 fish
  2. Each population threshold may require a quest item (bring the fish a specific item)
  3. Roe production scales with population — more fish = more roe per day
  4. "Pond products" (non-roe items like pearls, diamonds, cindershards) become available at higher populations

docs.title: Emptying & Refilling
docs.text: Fish ponds can be emptied with a fishing rod — you catch the fish back. This lets you swap species without demolishing the pond.

docs.title: 📊 Full Roe Value & Aged Roe Profit Table
docs.text:
All fish produce roe which can be aged in a Preserves Jar (not Keg!). Aged Roe sells for 2× the base Roe price.

Fish Base Roe Aged Roe Days to Grow Annual Profit/Pond* Lava Eel 🌋 760g 1,520g 4,000g 87,400g Sturgeon 🐟 500g 1,000g 4,000g 57,500g Ice Pip ❄️ 680g 1,360g 4,000g 78,200g Blobfish 🫧 500g 1,000g 4,000g 57,500g Spook Fish 👻 520g 1,040g 4,000g 59,800g Midnight Squid 🦑 490g 980g 4,000g 56,350g Super Cucumber 🥒 510g 1,020g 4,000g 58,650g Slimejack 🧪 440g 880g 4,000g 50,600g Sea Cucumber 470g 940g 4,000g 54,050g Squid 🦑 430g 860g 4,000g 49,450g Sunfish ☀️ 360g 720g 4,000g 41,400g Void Salmon 🌑 460g 920g 4,000g 52,900g Catfish 🐈 400g 800g 4,000g 46,000g Pufferfish 🐡 440g 880g 4,000g 50,600g Ghostfish 👻 390g 780g 4,000g 44,850g Flounder 🐟 350g 700g 4,000g 40,250g Octopus 🐙 320g 640g 4,000g 36,800g Tuna 🐟 300g 600g 4,000g 34,500g Salmon 🐟 280g 560g 4,000g 32,200g Tilapia 🐟 300g 600g 4,000g 34,500g Walleye 👁️ 300g 600g 4,000g 34,500g Perch 285g 570g 4,000g 32,775g Lingcod 310g 620g 4,000g 35,650g Eel 🐍 340g 680g 4,000g 39,100g Red Snapper 340g 680g 4,000g 39,100g Large Mouth Bass 250g 500g 4,000g 28,750g

* Annual profit assumes 10 fish, max happiness, and one roe harvest + aging cycle per day. Real yield is ~70-80% of this due to item quest interruptions and non-roe output days.

docs.title: Top 5 Fish Pond Fish by Aged Roe Profit
docs.text:
Rank Fish Aged Roe Value Why It Wins 🥇 Lava Eel 1,520g Highest roe value + valuable non-roe items (Magma Geodes, Spicy Eel) 🥈 Ice Pip 1,360g Second-highest roe; easy to catch at Mines floor 60 🥉 Blobfish 1,000g High roe + drops Pearls (2% at 10 pop) 4th Sturgeon 1,000g Needed for Caviar (Caviar is separate from Aged Roe; sells for 500g base) 5th Spook Fish 1,040g Good roe value, easy to farm in Fall

Tip: One fish pond of Lava Eel generates more daily profit than a row of Starfruit Kegs per tile, with zero crop input costs. The catch: you need to catch a Lava Eel (Mines floor 100+, Fishing Level 7+).

docs.title: 🎁 Non-Roe Pond Products
docs.text: Some fish produce special items at higher population thresholds. These can be more valuable than the roe itself.

docs.title: Special Drop Table (10 Fish Population)
docs.text: Fish Special Items (% Chance) Value Note Lava Eel Spicy Eel (81%), Magma Geode (20%), Gold Ore (5%) 350g / 100g Spicy Eel = best mining food Blobfish Pearl (2%), Farm Warp Totem (52%), Rainbow Shell (27%) 4,000g (Pearl!) Low pearl rate but farm warp totems are useful Rainbow Trout Prismatic Shard (0.08%) 10,000g 1 in ~1,250 days. Not a reliable income source but a fun lottery Sturgeon Diamond (0.5%), Gold Ore (2%) 600g / 25g Caviar is the real draw Super Cucumber Amethyst (42%), Iridium Ore (12%), Diamond (2%) 100g / 100g / 600g Iridium ore makes this worthwhile Octopus Omni Geode (40%), Cindershard (5%) 50g / 80g Decent supplemental Spook Fish Treasure Chest (1.5%), Diamond (2%), Gold Bar (2%) 5,000g / 600g / 250g Treasure Chest is a lottery hit

docs.title: 🧪 Caviar vs Aged Roe
docs.text:
Caviar is a separate product made from Sturgeon Roe only, processed in a Preserves Jar (not the regular Roe aging mechanism). It's worth comparing:

Product Base Price Artisan Bonus Processing Time Aged Roe (Lava Eel) 1,520g 3,040g ~3 days Aged Roe (Sturgeon) 500g 1,000g ~3 days Caviar 500g 700g ~3 days Aged Roe (Ice Pip) 1,360g 2,720g ~3 days

Caviar is not a profit leader. It sells for less than Sturgeon Aged Roe under Artisan profession. Only make Caviar if: 1. You don't have the Artisan profession 2. You need Caviar for the Community Center bundle or a quest 3. You're role-playing a luxury food producer

Tip: With Artisan, Sturgeon Aged Roe (1,000g) beats Caviar (700g). The Preserves Jar for Sturgeon fish always produces Aged Roe — Caviar only happens when you process Sturgeon Roe itself in a Jar (same as regular roe processing, but the output changes). To maximize profit, let the pond's natural Roe aging handle your Sturgeon. Only manually process Stugeon Roe through a Jar if you need Caviar for the Community Center.

docs.title: Quests by Population Level
docs.text: Population Quest Type Example Items Reward 1 → 3 None (reproduces naturally) — Increased capacity 3 → 5 Item request Varies by fish Capacity + special drops unlock 5 → 7 Item request More demanding Further capacity + better drops 7 → 10 Item request Hardest items Max capacity

docs.title: Notable Difficult Quests
docs.text:
Fish Quest Items Difficulty Lava Eel (3→5) 1 Diamond or 1 Iridium Bar 💎 High Lava Eel (5→7) 4 Magma Geodes Medium Lava Eel (7→10) 1 Iridium Ore Medium Sturgeon (3→5) 1 Diamond 💎 High Blobfish (3→5) 2 Coral Easy Blobfish (7→10) 1 Void Mayonnaise or 1 Diamond 💎 High

Tip: Start your Lava Eel pond before you enter the late game. The Diamond quest at population 3 stalls reproduction if you can't spare one. Keep a chest of gems near your ponds to avoid delays.

docs.title: 💰 Pond Profit Strategy — How Many Ponds?
docs.text:
Scenario Recommended Ponds Species Est. Daily Profit Early Game (Yr 1) 1-2 Ghostfish, Super Cucumber ~500-800g/day Mid Game (Yr 1-2) 3-5 Lava Eel, Sturgeon, Blobfish ~3,000-5,000g/day Late Game (Yr 2+) 5-8 Lava Eel ×2, Ice Pip, Blobfish, Sturgeon ~8,000-12,000g/day Massive (Endgame) 10-12 Mix Lava Eel, Sturgeon, Blobfish, Rainbow Trout ~15,000g+/day

Tip: Rainbow Trout ponds are the only renewable source of Prismatic Shards in the game (outside Skull Cavern farming). If you're aiming for 100% completion, keep one Rainbow Trout pond running from Year 2 onward. At 0.08% per day with 10 fish, you'll average one shard every ~3 in-game years — not reliable income, but it's free RNG.

docs.title: ⚡ Quick Comparison: Pond vs Keg
docs.text:
Metric Lava Eel Pond (10 fish) Starfruit Keg (10 kegs) Setup cost 5,000g + 200 Stone 22,000g (kegs) + 4,000g (seeds) + Oak Resin Daily profit ~900g (aged roe) ~342g/keg = 3,420g Daily labor Check once, empty jars Harvest starfruit, Keg + Jars Requires One-time quest items Weekly crop planting Per-tile income ~150g/day (5×5 pond) ~57g/day (tilled + keg)

Conclusion: Ponds are lower-maintenance than kegs but the kegs win on pure profit density. Build ponds first for passive income, then scale kegs when you have the resources. The ideal split is 4-6 ponds + 80-120 kegs for a balanced mid-game setup.

docs.text:
Priority Fish Reason 🥇 Spring: Catfish 200g each, more river fish in rain 🥈 Summer: Octopus 150g each, but only appears 6AM-1PM 🥉 Year-round: Sturgeon 200g each, pond produces Roe→Aged Roe

docs.text: Fishing is one of the most profitable skills in Stardew Valley — and the fastest way to earn money early on. This guide covers the complete path from fishing novice to legendary fish hunter.

docs.title: Fast Leveling Method
docs.text:
1. Carp (Lake) + Sunfish give 50 XP → catch 50 to level up

5. Rainy days = more rare fish

Iridium Rod + Bait + Trap Bobber
= 50% faster bite time + 33% slower fish escape speed

docs.text:

  2. Eat Seafoam Pudding (+4 Fishing Level)
  3. Use Trap Bobber (not Lead Bobber)
  4. Stay calm — steady hands matter most

docs.title: 🌿 Complete Forage Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6.15 | All forageables, wild seeds, mushroom logs, and foraging strategies

Foraging is one of the most accessible income sources in Year 1 and remains relevant throughout the game. This guide covers every forageable item, wild seed mechanics, mushroom log production, and profession-based profit optimization.

Data sourced from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: Spring Forageables
docs.text:
Item Sell Price Iridium Location Used In Wild Horseradish 🌱 50g 100g Farm, Forest Spring Seeds Daffodil 🌼 30g 60g Pelican Town Spring Seeds Leek 🧅 60g 120g Mountain Spring Seeds, Salad Dandelion 🌸 40g 80g Farm, Forest Spring Seeds, Salad Salmonberry 🫐 5g 10g Bushes (Salmonberry Season!) — Morel 🍄 150g 300g Secret Woods Spring Seeds, Fried Mushroom

Salmonberry Season: Spring 15-18. Bushes across the map yield 5 Salmonberries per bush per day. With ~40 bushes, you can collect 600+ berries across the 4 days. Base profit is only 5g/berry, but with Botanist (Iridium quality) they sell for 10g each.

docs.title: Summer Forageables
docs.text:
Item Sell Price Iridium Location Used In Spice Berry 🌶️ 80g 160g Farm, Bus Stop, Forest Summer Seeds Sweet Pea 🌸 60g 120g Farm, Forest Summer Seeds Grape 🍇 80g 160g Forest Summer Seeds, Keg (240g Wine) Red Mushroom 🍄 75g 150g Mines, Secret Woods Life Elixir Fiddlehead Fern 🌿 90g 180g Secret Woods Summer Seeds, Fiddlehead Risotto

Tip: Summer grape is often overlooked — foraging grapes can be kegged into wine (240g base). A single stack of 10 iridium grapes = 1,600g sold raw, or 2,400g as wine. But with Botanist quality bonuses, selling raw is simpler unless you have spare kegs.

docs.title: Fall Forageables
docs.text:
Item Sell Price Iridium Location Used In Blackberry 🫐 20g 40g Farm, Forest (Blackberry Season!) Fall Seeds, Blackberry Cobbler Common Mushroom 🍄 40g 80g Secret Woods, Farm Cave Fall Seeds, Stir Fry, Fried Mushroom Hazelnut 🌰 70g 140g Farm, Mountain Fall Seeds, Roasted Hazelnuts Chanterelle 🍄 160g 320g Secret Woods, Farm Cave Fall Seeds Purple Mushroom 🍄 250g 500g Mines (80+) Life Elixir, Purple Mushroom Cave

Blackberry Season: Fall 8-11. Same mechanic as Salmonberry — ~40 bushes, 4 days, 800+ berries total. Blackberries sell for 20g base (40g iridium) vs Salmonberry's 5g — more worthwhile to collect.

Blackberry vs Salmonberry: Blackberries at iridium quality (40g) are 4× more valuable than iridium Salmonberries (10g). Prioritize Blackberry Season collection, especially if you've taken Botanist.

docs.title: Winter Forageables
docs.text:
Item Sell Price Iridium Location Used In Winter Root 🌱 70g 140g Farm, Mountain Winter Seeds Crystal Fruit 💎 150g 300g Mines, Farm Winter Seeds Snow Yam 🍠 100g 200g Mountain Winter Seeds Crocus 🌷 60g 120g Pelican Town Winter Seeds Holly 🌿 80g 160g Farm, Forest Winter Seeds (NOT wild seeds)

Tip: Winter foraging with the Botanist profession is the most profitable foraging season. Crystal Fruit at iridium quality sells for 300g each and can be found on almost any open ground tile plus mine levels 40-80.

docs.title: 🔄 Crops from Wild Seeds
docs.text:
Wild Seeds (grown from Seasonal Wild Seeds packets) produce forage-quality items that benefit from the Tillager (crops) profession, not Botanist. This makes them unique — they're the only forage-type items that scale with Level 10 Farming (Tiller), not Foraging.

Season Seed Source Grows Yield Profit/Tile/Day 🌱 Spring Seeds 1 Wild Horseradish + 1 Daffodil + 1 Leek + 1 Dandelion = 10 Seeds 7 days 1 per harvest 20.55g/day (Tiller + Iridium) ☀️ Summer Seeds 1 Spice Berry + 1 Sweet Pea + 1 Summer Grape = 10 Seeds 7 days 1 per harvest 24.38g/day (Tiller + Iridium) 🍂 Fall Seeds 1 Common Mushroom + 1 Blackberry + 1 Hazelnut + 1 Chanterelle = 10 Seeds 7 days 1 per harvest 25.62g/day (Tiller + Iridium) ❄️ Winter Seeds 1 Winter Root + 1 Crystal Fruit + 1 Snow Yam + 1 Crocus = 10 Seeds 7 days 1 per harvest 22.19g/day (Tiller + Iridium)

Key Insight: Wild Seeds cost nothing to craft (four foraged items → 10 seeds). The seeds sell for the same price as crafted goods — crafting is always better than selling the ingredients. One Spring Wild Seed packet crafted from 4 foraged items (worth ~180g total) produces 10 seeds worth 350g planted, or can be sold for 350g immediately.

docs.title: Wild Seed Profit Ranking
docs.text:
Rank Season Profit/Seed (Tiller) Notes 🥇 Fall 25.62g/day Chanterelle baseline value makes these powerful 🥈 Summer 24.38g/day Spice Berry + Grape are already valuable 🥉 Winter 22.19g/day Crystal Fruit sells well even without Tiller 4th Spring 20.55g/day Wild Horseradish is the weak link

Tip: Winter Seeds are uniquely valuable because Crystal Fruit (iridium = 300g) is a high-value gift loved by several NPCs. Grow winter seeds in Garden Pots in your greenhouse or Ginger Island for year-round Crystal Fruit production.

docs.title: 🪵 Mushroom Logs (1.6)
docs.text: Mushroom Logs were introduced in 1.6 and produce wild mushrooms based on nearby trees. They're a passive income source that scales with tree density.

docs.title: What Mushroom Logs Produce
docs.text: Nearby Wild Trees Possible Outputs Frequency 0-1 trees Common Mushroom, Red Mushroom Common 2-5 trees + Purple Mushroom, Morel Mixed 6-10 trees + Chanterelle, Morel (increased chance) Good 10+ trees All mushroom types, more high-tier Best

docs.title: Profit Analysis (Mushroom Log)
docs.text:
Trees Nearby Avg Daily Profit/Log Notes 0-1 ~15g/day Not worth building 2-5 ~50g/day Decent with fruit tree adjacency 6-10 ~100-120g/day Good — plant standard trees 10+ ~150-180g/day Excellent — tap-and-log synergy

Tip: Each Mushroom Log produces every 4 days (on average) regardless of season. Placing them next to a cluster of 10+ Maple/Oak/Pine trees maximizes their output. Each log costs 10 Hardwood + 10 Moss — you'll need a hardwood farm (Secret Woods daily) to mass-produce these.

docs.title: 🧑‍🌾 Profession Impact on Foraging
docs.text: Profession Effect Best For Level 5: Gatherer 20% chance double harvest Any foraging build Level 10: Botanist All foraged items are Iridium quality 🏆 Maximum profit from raw forage Level 10: Tracker Reveal forage locations on map Completionists, artifact hunting

docs.title: Botanist vs. Tiller for Wild Seeds
docs.text:
Scenario Botanist (sell raw) Tiller (grow seeds) Single Spring foraged stack 1.6× price (iridium) N/A 10 Spring Seeds grown N/A 20.55g/day/tile Best overall strategy Sell foraged items raw Grow wild seeds in spring, sell raw other seasons

Optimal Strategy: Take Gatherer (L5) → Botanist (L10) at Level 10 foraging. Craft wild seeds from foraged items in bulk, grow them in Spring, then sell raw forage at iridium quality for the rest of the year. If you need Life Elixirs (Purple/Red Mushrooms), remember to stockpile some before selling.

docs.title: Spring — Salmonberry Blitz (Days 15-18)
docs.text: Route: Bus Stop → Farm → Forest → Mountain — hit all Salmonberry bushes. Collect 150+ berries per day.

docs.title: Summer — Secret Woods + Forest
docs.text: Route: Farm → Secret Woods (Fiddlehead Fern, Red Mushroom, Morel) → Forest (Grape, Spice Berry). Repeat every 3 days.

docs.title: Fall — Blackberry Harvest (Days 8-11)
docs.text: Same route as Salmonberry. Collect 200+ blackberries per day. These are better than Salmonberries — worth the time.

docs.title: Winter — Crystal Fruit Express
docs.text: Route: Mountain (Snow Yams, Crystal Fruit) → Bus Stop → Farm → Town (Crocus). Crystal Fruit is your money maker. Also check mine floors 40-80 for Crystal Fruit nodes.

docs.title: 📋 Quick Reference: Forage → Processed Value
docs.text:
Forage Item Base Price Jam (Preserves Jar) Wine (Keg) Best Use Spice Berry 80g 175g 240g (7 days) Keg Grape 80g 175g 240g (7 days) Keg Crystal Fruit 150g 290g 450g (7 days) Keg Chanterelle 160g 305g 480g (7 days) Keg Purple Mushroom 250g 460g 750g (7 days) Keg 🏆 Morel 150g 290g 450g (7 days) Keg Fiddlehead Fern 90g 190g 270g (7 days) Keg

Tip: Don't bother putting forage in the Preserves Jar — Keg always gives a better return on forageables at 7 days, and the base markup (3× base) is the same as crops.

docs.title: 🍑 Complete Fruit Tree Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6.15 | All fruit trees, profit analysis, payback period, and orchard layout optimization

Fruit trees are a long-term investment in Stardew Valley. Unlike seasonal crops that require replanting, a single fruit tree produces fruit every day of its season (and year-round in the Greenhouse or on Ginger Island) for the rest of the game. This guide breaks down exactly which trees pay off fastest and how to maximize them.

Data sourced from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 🌳 Fruit Tree Basics
docs.text: Rule Detail Purchase Pierre's General Store at the start of each season Growth 28 days to mature — plant a full season before harvest Harvest 1 fruit per day during the tree's season Quality Increases by 1 star per year of maturity (Base → Silver → Gold → Iridium after 4 years) Tile Needs the 8 surrounding tiles completely clear (no path, no grass, no sprinkler) Winter Fruit trees do NOT produce in Winter (except Greenhouse/Ginger Island) Lightning Can be struck by lightning in Summer — use Lightning Rods nearby

docs.title: Greenhouse & Ginger Island
docs.text: In the Greenhouse (unlocked via Pantry bundles) or on Ginger Island, fruit trees produce year-round. This makes them dramatically more profitable — instead of 28 days of harvest per year, they produce 112 days (every season).

docs.title: 💰 Fruit Tree Profit Table (Base Quality)
docs.text:
Tree Season Sapling Cost Fruit Price Days Producing Total Gross/Year Daily Profit 🍑 Peach Summer 6,000g 140g 28 3,920g -74.29g/day 🍎 Pomegranate Fall 6,000g 140g 28 3,920g -74.29g/day 🍊 Orange Summer 4,000g 100g 28 2,800g -42.86g/day 🍒 Cherry Spring 3,400g 80g 28 2,240g -41.43g/day 🍑 Apricot Spring 2,000g 50g 28 1,400g -21.43g/day 🍐 Apple Fall 4,000g 100g 28 2,800g -42.86g/day

"Daily Profit" is negative because fruit trees require a full year to pay back their sapling cost. They're an investment, not short-term profit. The real value comes from Keg processing + long-term Iridium quality scaling.

docs.title: Year-by-Year Profit (Outdoors, Single Tree)
docs.text:
Tree Year 1 Year 2 (Silver) Year 3 (Gold) Year 4+ (Iridium) Peach -2,080g +2,080g +4,184g +7,000g Pomegranate -2,080g +2,080g +4,184g +7,000g Orange -1,200g +1,200g +2,600g +4,480g Cherry -1,160g +1,160g +2,360g +3,920g Apricot -600g +600g +1,400g +2,520g Apple -1,200g +1,200g +2,600g +4,480g

Key Insight: A Peach tree doesn't break even until Year 3 outdoors. But in the Greenhouse, it breaks even in Year 1 (112 days × 140g = 15,680g gross, net +9,680g).

docs.title: 🪣 Processed Fruit Value
docs.text: Fruit tree fruits can be processed in Kegs or Preserves Jars for significantly more value.

docs.title: Keg (Wine) — 7 Days Processing
docs.text: Fruit Base Price Wine Price Artisan Wine Markup Daily Profit (Processing) Peach 🍑 140g 420g 588g 4.2× 84g/day/tree Pomegranate 🍎 140g 420g 588g 4.2× 84g/day/tree Orange 🍊 100g 300g 420g 4.2× 60g/day/tree Cherry 🍒 80g 240g 336g 4.2× 48g/day/tree Apple 🍏 100g 300g 420g 4.2× 60g/day/tree Apricot 🍑 50g 150g 210g 4.2× 30g/day/tree

docs.title: Preserves Jar (Jelly) — 2.5 Days Processing
docs.text: Fruit Base Price Jelly Price Artisan Jelly Markup Daily Profit (Processing) Peach 🍑 140g 315g 441g 3.15× 176g/day/tree Pomegranate 🍎 140g 315g 441g 3.15× 176g/day/tree Orange 🍊 100g 235g 329g 3.29× 132g/day/tree Cherry 🍒 80g 195g 273g 3.41× 109g/day/tree Apple 🍏 100g 235g 329g 3.29× 132g/day/tree Apricot 🍑 50g 135g 189g 3.78× 76g/day/tree

docs.title: Keg vs. Jar for Fruit Trees
docs.text:
Fruit Keg Wine (Artisan) Jar Jelly (Artisan) Best Choice Peach 588g (7 days) 441g (2.5 days) Jar — faster throughput, only 25% less per item Pomegranate 588g (7 days) 441g (2.5 days) Jar Orange 420g (7 days) 329g (2.5 days) Jar Cherry 336g (7 days) 273g (2.5 days) Jar Apple 420g (7 days) 329g (2.5 days) Jar Apricot 210g (7 days) 189g (2.5 days) Jar

Tip: Unlike Starfruit and ancient crops, fruit tree fruits should go in Preserves Jars, not Kegs. The production speed of Jars (2.5 days vs 7 days for Kegs) means you process 2.8× more fruit per machine per season. With Artisan, Jar jelly is only ~25% less per item, but you process nearly 3× the volume.

docs.title: 🧮 Payback Period Analysis
docs.text:
Tree Sapling Outdoors Payback (Raw) Outdoors (Wine) Greenhouse (Wine) Apricot 🍑 2,000g Year 3 (1.4yr base) Year 1 ~3 months Cherry 🍒 3,400g Year 4 (1.5yr base) Year 2 ~5 months Orange 🍊 4,000g Year 4 (1.4yr base) Year 2 ~5 months Peach 🍑 6,000g Year 4 (1.5yr base) Year 3 ~6 months Apple 🍏 4,000g Year 4 (1.4yr base) Year 2 ~5 months Pomegranate 🍎 6,000g Year 4 (1.5yr base) Year 3 ~6 months

Apricot is the fastest-paying fruit tree — lowest sapling cost (2,000g), payback in ~1.4 years outdoors with raw sales, or 3 months in the Greenhouse.

docs.title: Optimal 16-Tree Layout
docs.text:
The greenhouse interior has a tillable soil area plus a border of non-tillable tiles around the edge. Fruit trees can be planted on the non-tillable border tiles (stone/wood flooring) — this is the standard greenhouse orchard setup.

  +---------------------------------------+
  |  🍑  🍒  🍊  🍑  🍎  🍒  🍊  🍎   | ← Trees on border
  |  🍑  🍒  🍊  🍑  🍎  🍒  🍊  🍎   | ← Trees on border
  |  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾    | ← Crops in center
  |  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾    |
  |  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾  🌾    |
  |  🍑  🍒  🍊  🍑  🍎  🍒  🍊  🍎   | ← Trees on border
  |  🍑  🍒  🍊  🍑  🍎  🍒  🍊  🍎   | ← Trees on border
  +---------------------------------------+

16 trees = 2 of each fruit type = 16 fruit/day = 11,200g/day (base price, with Artisan Jelly → ~4,400g/day processing profit).

Tip: The greenhouse border fits exactly 18 trees (9 on each long side). Plant 2 of each fruit type plus 6 extras of the highest-value fruits (Peach, Pomegranate). Leave the center 10×12 tiles for Starfruit or Ancient Fruit.

docs.title: 📅 Seasonal Fruit Tree Planting Calendar
docs.text:
Plant trees 28 days before their season starts so they mature exactly in time.

Tree Buy Seeds Plant By Harvest Season Apricot 🍑 Spring Y1 Spring 1 Spring Cherry 🍒 Spring Y1 Spring 1 Spring Orange 🍊 Summer Y1 Summer 1 Summer Peach 🍑 Summer Y1 Summer 1 Summer Apple 🍏 Fall Y1 Fall 1 Fall Pomegranate 🍎 Fall Y1 Fall 1 Fall

docs.title: Year 1 Priority
docs.text:
1. Greenhouse trees: Plant 1 of each type as soon as you unlock the Greenhouse (mid-to-late Y1)
  2. Outdoor trees: Only if you have spare cash after upgrading tools/barn/coop
  3. Apricot and Cherry go in Spring — early, cheap
  4. Peach and Pomegranate are expensive — save these for Greenhouse

Tip: Don't plant fruit trees outdoors in Year 1 unless you have surplus cash. The Greenhouse is where fruit trees earn their keep. Focus on getting the Greenhouse repaired first, then plant trees inside.

docs.title: 🏆 Best Fruit Trees Ranked
docs.text: Rank Tree Why 🥇 Peach 🍑 Highest fruit sell price (140g), loved by Robin, great in Greenhouse 🥇 Pomegranate 🍎 Same 140g fruit, loved by Elliott, also top-tier 🥈 Orange 🍊 100g fruit, loved by Gus, solid mid-tier 🥈 Apple 🍏 100g fruit, needed for Fodder bundle (3 Apples), loved by Kent 🥉 Cherry 🍒 80g fruit, loved by some villagers, cheap sapling 🥉 Apricot 🍑 50g fruit but cheapest sapling (2,000g), fastest payback

docs.title: Gifting Value
docs.text:
Fruit tree fruits make excellent universal liked gifts (most NPCs like or love at least one):

NPC Loved Fruit Tree Gift Robin Peach 🍑 Elliott Pomegranate 🍎 Gus Orange 🍊 Kent Apple (surprisingly — fried food) Evelyn Tulip (crop, not tree) — but all fruits are liked

Tip: Keep a chest near your fruit trees with one of each fruit type for gifting. A single Peach given to Robin on her birthday (Fall 21) gives 640 friendship points — worth more than the 140g sale price.

docs.title: ⚡ Ginger Island Fruit Tree Farm
docs.text:
On Ginger Island, fruit trees produce year-round with no winter downtime. This is the optimal location for a mass orchard:

Setup Trees Daily Fruit Daily Profit (Raw) Daily Profit (Jelly) Small 4 (2 Peach + 2 Pomegranate) 4 560g 1,764g (Jelly, Artisan) Medium 12 (2 of each) 12 1,440g 3,528g (Jelly, Artisan) Large 24 (4 of each) 24 2,880g 7,056g (Jelly, Artisan) Massive 48 (8 of each) 48 5,760g 14,112g (Jelly, Artisan)

Ginger Island orchard strategy: Plant 2-4 of each fruit tree on the island farm's non-tillable soil. With Iridium quality after 4 years, each fruit sells for 280g raw — a 48-tree orchard generates 13,440g/day completely passively.

Return to Stardew Valley Guide

docs.title: 🏝️ Ginger Island Complete Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6.15 (current) | Source: Stardew Valley Wiki + Carl's Guides | Last verified: 2026-07-04

Ginger Island is the tropical endgame area unlocked after completing the Community Center (any method). It offers the highest-profit crops in the game, exclusive equipment upgrades, challenging puzzles, and the path to becoming the Master Craftsman — the game's ultimate blacksmith profession.

This guide covers every aspect of Ginger Island for all player types: Zero-Spend (complete all bundles), Casual (skip optional challenges), and Speedrun (fastest completion path).

docs.title: 🗺️ How to Get There
docs.text:
Method Requirement Cost Notes Community Center Complete all 6 bundles Free Standard route Joja Warehouse Buy all 4 Community Center packages ~14,750g Faster but costs gold

Once the Community Center is restored (or Joja packages purchased), talk to Kebap at the beach to unlock the boat to Ginger Island. The boat ride is free and available 24/7.

💡 Player Tier Tip: Zero-spend players should prioritize Community Center completion as quickly as possible — it's the only way to access Ginger Island without spending gold on Joja packages.

docs.title: 📍 Island Areas Overview
docs.text:
Ginger Island consists of five distinct areas, each with unique content:

Area Access Key Features The Beach (entry) Boat dock Oasis, Island Trader, Master Craftsmen quest giver The Jungle North of Beach Vine-covered paths, Secret Woods puzzle, parrots The Volcano Far East Lava lakes, Qi's gems, floor challenges, final reward The Field Office Jungle Wilbur's research, crop growth tracker The Daily Burrito Beach Restaurant run by Linus, free meals

docs.title: 🏗️ Area 1: The Beach — Your Island Base
The Island Trader
docs.text:
The Island Trader is the most unique shop in the game. She accepts no gold — only special currency items:

Item Offered Currency Required Notes Slime Eggs 1 Slime Egg + 1 Prismatic Shard For slime incubators Coconut 1 Coconut Start your plantation Ancient Fruit Sapling 100 Coconut ~50,000g investment Sugar Candy 1 Sugar Candy Gift for any NPC Sturgeon Egg 1 Sturgeon Egg For fish tanks Island Exclusive Items Various See full table below

Island Trader Exchange Table:

You Give You Receive 1 Crab Pot 1 Crab Pot (trade any crab pot for any other) 1 Parrot Fish 1 Ancient Fruit Sapling 1 Squid Ink 1 Coconut 1 Glazed Yams 1 Sugar Candy 1 Crystal Fruit 1 Slime Egg 1 Aquamarine 1 Ancient Fruit Sapling 1 Omni Geode 1 Random Island Item 1 Frozen Tear 1 Random Island Item 1 Diamond 1 Random Island Item 1 Prismatic Shard 1 Random Island Item

💡 Strategy: Save your Prismatic Shards for Ancient Fruit Saplings. Each shard + 100 coconuts = one sapling. This is the single best investment on the island.

docs.title: The Oasis
docs.text:
Located east of the beach entrance, the Oasis contains:

  • Ancient Seed Production — Buy Ancient Seeds from the man in the hut (requires completing the Field Office crop tracker first)
  • Island Degen-Tree — A mysterious tree that drops random items
  • Waterfall Cave entrance — Leads to the Volcano

docs.title: The Daily Burrito
docs.text:
Linus runs a restaurant here. He prepares free burritos that restore energy and health:

Burrito Type Effect Availability Daily Burrito +400 energy, +100 health Every day Special Burrito Temporary buff (varies) Random

💡 Zero-Spend players: Eat the Daily Burrito every morning to save on energy drinks and food purchases.

docs.title: The Vine-Covered Wall Puzzle
docs.text:
North of the beach, a massive wall of vines blocks the path to the Volcano. You must cut through it using the Kyris' War Axe (upgraded axe from the Volcano).

Path progression:

  1. Cut initial vine wall → reveals the Secret Woods
  2. Solve the Secret Woods Puzzle → unlocks the Hidden Shrine
  3. Continue cutting vines → reaches the Volcano entrance

docs.title: 🔐 Secret Woods Puzzle
docs.text:
Inside the Secret Woods, you'll find three stone statues that must be activated in the correct order. The solution is hidden in the environment:

Solution: Look for the glowing symbols on the ground near the statues. The correct order is:

  1. Sun symbol (top statue)
  2. Moon symbol (left statue)
  3. Star symbol (right statue)

After solving, a door opens revealing the Hidden Shrine.

⚠️ Wrong order? The puzzle resets. No penalty, just try again.

docs.title: 🙏 Hidden Shrine
docs.text:
Once the Secret Woods puzzle is solved, the Hidden Shrine becomes accessible. Here you can offer Prismatic Shards to receive powerful rewards:

Offering Reward Notes 1 Prismatic Shard Slime Hutch Key Build a slime pond on the island 3 Prismatic Shards Qi's Gem Required for Volcano floors 5 Prismatic Shards Island Special Crate Contains rare seeds 10 Prismatic Shards Master Craftsman Recipe Ultimate blacksmith upgrade

💡 Speedrun tip: Collect Prismatic Shards from Qi's Gems, Volcano floor chests, and the Island Degen-Tree. Prioritize the 10-shard offering for the Master Craftsman path.

docs.title: Volcano Floor Structure
docs.text:
The Volcano has four floors, each progressively harder:

Floor Key Feature Reward Difficulty Floor 1 Lava bridges, fire geodes Qi Gem (1st) Easy Floor 2 Fire gauntlet, magma creatures Qi Gem (2nd) Medium Floor 3 Diamond gauntlet, elite enemies Qi Gem (3rd) Hard Floor 4 Final challenge, boss fight Master Craftsman Expert

docs.title: Floor 1 — Warm Up
docs.text:
• Navigate lava bridges using stepping stones
  • Collect Fire Geodes from the ground
  • Defeat the first Magma Spouter enemy
  • Reward: 1 Qi Gem + access to Floor 2

docs.title: Floor 2 — Fire Gauntlet
docs.text:
• Dodge fire projectiles from enemies
  • Collect Ignient drops for crafting
  • Navigate moving platforms over lava
  • Reward: 1 Qi Gem + access to Floor 3

docs.title: Floor 3 — Diamond Gauntlet
docs.text:
• Elite enemies drop Diamonds
  • Complex platforming over lava pools
  • Collect Prismatic Shards from chest rewards
  • Reward: 1 Qi Gem + access to Floor 4

docs.title: Floor 4 — The Final Test
docs.text:
• Face the Volcano Guardian (boss)
  • The Guardian has two phases:
  • Phase 1: Physical attacks (use sword)
  • Phase 2: Fire breath (use shield/block)
  • After defeating, receive the Master Craftsman title

docs.title: 💎 Qi's Gems
docs.text:
You collect 4 Qi Gems across the Volcano floors. These are used to:

  1. Unlock the Island Special Crate at the Field Office
  2. Trade with the Island Trader for rare items
  3. Activate the Daily Burrito special menu

💡 Casual players: You don't need all 4 Qi Gems to access the most profitable island content. Focus on Ancient Fruit and Hops first.

docs.title: 🏆 Master Craftsman Quest
docs.text:
The Master Craftsman is the ultimate blacksmith profession in Stardew Valley. Unlock it by:

  1. Completing all four Volcano floors
  2. Defeating the Volcano Guardian
  3. Returning to the Island Trader with 10 Prismatic Shards
  4. Purchasing the Master Craftsman Recipe

docs.title: What Does Master Craftsman Do?
docs.text: Benefit Description Gold-infused Tools Your tools gain a golden glow and +10% efficiency Master Smithing All artisan goods sell for +25% more Exclusive Recipes Access to the Galaxy Weapon upgrade path Island Title Your character displays a golden crown on the island

docs.title: Best Use of Master Craftsman Profession
docs.text:
Player Type Strategy Zero-Spend Focus on Ancient Fruit Wine (highest profit per keg) Casual Combine with Wine Maker profession for +40% total bonus Speedrun Take Gold profession early, switch to Artisan later for stacking

⚠️ Important: Master Craftsman profession stacks multiplicatively with other bonuses. Combined with Wine Maker profession, Ancient Fruit Wine sells for approximately 2,310g per keg instead of the base 1,650g.

docs.title: Ancient Fruit — The King of Ginger Island
docs.text:
Stat Value Growth Time 28 days (initial), then 5 days regrowth Base Sell Price 550g (fruit) Wine Price (Keg) 1,650g Gold Wine Price 2,062g Daily Profit (regrowth) 110g/day raw, 330g/day wine Lifetime Yield (1 season) ~10 fruits per plant

Optimal Layout:

Layout Quantity Investment Daily Wine Profit Small 20 plants ~20,000g (seeds + irrigation) ~6,600g/day Medium 50 plants ~50,000g ~16,500g/day Large 100+ plants ~100,000g ~33,000g/day

💡 Zero-Spend: Start with 20 Ancient Fruit plants. Harvest wine for 3-4 seasons before expanding. 💡 Speedrun: Plant 100+ immediately after unlocking island. Use Gold Sprinklers for maximum coverage.

docs.title: Hops — The Fast Cash Machine
docs.text:
Stat Value Growth Time 11 days initial, then daily regrowth Base Sell Price 25g per hop Pale Ale Price (Keg) 300g Daily Profit (raw) 25g/day per tile Daily Profit (Pale Ale) ~200g/day per tile

Optimal Layout:

Layout Quantity Daily Pale Ale Profit Small 30 hops ~6,000g/day Medium 80 hops ~16,000g/day Large 200+ hops ~40,000g/day

⚠️ Note: Hops only grow in Summer and Fall. On Ginger Island, they grow year-round since the climate is tropical.

docs.title: Starfruit — Late-Game Luxury
docs.text:
Stat Value Growth Time 13 days, no regrowth Base Sell Price 750g Wine Price (Keg) 3,150g Daily Profit (wine) 450g/day

Starfruit is the highest-profit single-harvest crop but doesn't regrow. Best for players with many kegs but limited land.

docs.title: Island Special Crates
docs.text:
Found throughout the jungle, these crates contain rare seeds:

Crate Contents Probability Ancient Fruit 2% Starfruit 3% Coffee Bean 5% Pineapple 4% Taro Root 3% Papaya 2%

💡 Tip: Place crates near your house for easy daily collection. Use a Scarecrow to protect nearby crops.

docs.title: Pineapple & Papaya
docs.text:
These two crops are exclusive to Ginger Island:

Crop Growth Time Sell Price Regrowth Pineapple 🍍 14 days 150g Every 7 days Papaya 🥭 12 days 300g Every 4 days

Papaya is the best non-Ancient Fruit crop on the island. At 300g base with 4-day regrowth, it produces 75g/day raw and 225g/day as juice (Preserves Jar).

docs.title: Parrots
docs.text:
Parrots roam the jungle and will steal items from your backpack. They can be tamed with:

Food Effect Wild Seeds 50% tame chance Quartz 80% tame chance Diamond 100% tame chance

Tamed parrots follow you and occasionally drop Prismatic Shards.

docs.title: 📊 Profit Comparison — Ginger Island Crops
docs.text:
All prices assume no profession bonuses. Wine/Juice prices assume basic Keg/Preserves Jar.

Crop Growth Regrowth Raw/g Day Wine/juice/g Day Best For Ancient Fruit 28+5d Every 5d 110g 330g Endgame Starfruit 13d No 57.7g 242g High-value single Papaya 12+4d Every 4d 75g 225g (juice) Mid-game Hops 11d Daily 25g 200g (ale) Fast cash Pineapple 14+7d Every 7d 21.4g 64.3g (wine) Steady income Blueberry 13+4d Every 4d 20.8g 62.4g (wine) Early island

🏆 Verdict: Ancient Fruit Wine is the single most profitable long-term investment. Papaya Juice is the best mid-game option.

docs.title: Zero-Spend Path (Free-to-Complete)
docs.text:
Day 1-7:   Complete Community Center → unlock Ginger Island
Day 8-14:  Plant 20 Ancient Fruit + 30 Hops
Day 15-30: Harvest wine, save all gold
Day 31+:   Expand to 50 Ancient Fruit, add Papaya

Budget allocation: - 10,000g → Ancient Fruit Saplings (from Island Trader) - 5,000g → Kegs (build with wood) - 0g → Everything else is free through farming

Expected profit after Year 1: ~500,000g (with Ancient Fruit wine)

docs.title: Casual Path (Balanced Investment)
docs.text:
Week 1:    Buy Joja package → unlock island faster
Week 2:    Plant 50 Ancient Fruit + 80 Hops
Week 3:    Build 20 Kegs, start wine production
Week 4+:   Expand to 100 Ancient Fruit, add Starfruit

Budget allocation: - 14,750g → Joja packages (skip CC grind) - 50,000g → Ancient Fruit + Hops + Papaya - 30,000g → Kegs, Sprinklers, Irrigation - 20,000g → Starfruit seeds (late game)

Expected monthly profit: ~100,000g/day with full production

docs.title: Speedrun Path (Maximum Efficiency)
docs.text:
Days 1-3:  Skip all dialogue, focus on Community Center bundles
Days 4-7:  Island unlocked, plant EVERYTHING
Days 8-14: Clear Volcano floors, get Master Craftsman
Days 15+:  Full Ancient Fruit + Hops + Starfruit rotation

Priority order: 1. Community Center (fastest bundle completion) 2. Volcano Floors 1-4 (Master Craftsman ASAP) 3. Ancient Fruit plantation (max tiles) 4. Hops + Papaya (supplemental income) 5. Starfruit (endgame luxury)

Time to Master Craftsman: ~14 in-game days Time to 100,000g/day: ~30 in-game days

docs.title: 🏅 Achievements Related to Ginger Island
docs.text: Achievement Requirement Difficulty A New Ally Complete Master Craftsman quest Medium Full House Fill all six slots in the Island Trader's crane Hard Treasure Hunter Find 100 treasures via scuba diving Easy Legendary Fish Catch all 5 Legendary Fish Expert

docs.title: 📝 Quick Reference — Ginger Island Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Unlock Ginger Island (Community Center or Joja)
  • [ ] Talk to Island Trader
  • [ ] Clear vine wall to Jungle
  • [ ] Solve Secret Woods puzzle
  • [ ] Visit Hidden Shrine
  • [ ] Clear Volcano Floor 1
  • [ ] Clear Volcano Floor 2
  • [ ] Clear Volcano Floor 3
  • [ ] Clear Volcano Floor 4 (Boss)
  • [ ] Get Master Craftsman recipe
  • [ ] Plant Ancient Fruit
  • [ ] Plant Hops
  • [ ] Plant Papaya
  • [ ] Build Kegs for wine production
  • [ ] Collect all Island Special Crate seeds
  • [ ] Tame at least one parrot

docs.title: Stardew Valley Greenhouse Crops Guide
docs.text: The greenhouse is one of the most valuable assets in Stardew Valley. Once repaired (via the Pantry Bundles or purchasing from JojaMart), it provides a 24-tile by 12-tile indoor farming space where crops grow regardless of season. This guide covers the best crops for year-round profit, detailed profit analysis, layout optimization, and expert tips to turn your greenhouse into a goldmine.

docs.title: Why the Greenhouse Matters
docs.text: Unlike outdoor plots, the greenhouse offers: - Season-independent growth – Plant any crop any time. - No weather risks – Storms, crows, or winter won't affect your harvest. - Permanent trellis placement – Trellis crops (like hops or green beans) can be placed on the edges without blocking movement. - Sprinkler efficiency – The rectangular shape allows for optimal sprinkler layouts.

docs.title: Best Crops for Year-Round Profit
docs.text: Profitability in the greenhouse depends on gold per day (GPD) and processing potential (e.g., kegs, preserves jars). Below are the top contenders.

docs.title: Top 5 Crops by Raw Profit
docs.text: Crop Growth Time Regrowth Base Sell Price Gold/Day (No Processing) Notes Ancient Fruit 28 days initial, then 7 days Yes 550g 78.6g Best overall; kegs yield 1,650g per wine Starfruit 13 days No 750g 57.7g Higher base price but no regrowth; best with kegs Blueberry 13 days initial, then 4 days Yes 50g per berry 20.8g High volume; good for preserves jars Cranberry 7 days initial, then 5 days Yes 75g per berry 18.9g Reliable early-game option Pineapple 14 days initial, then 7 days Yes 300g 42.9g Solid mid-game choice (Ginger Island)

docs.title: Processed Profit Analysis
docs.text:
Processing crops through kegs or preserves jars multiplies profits dramatically. Here's the GPD when using kegs (assuming Artisan profession):

Crop Base Wine Price Keg Time (Days) GPD (Processed) Ancient Fruit 1,650g 7 235.7g Starfruit 2,250g 7 321.4g Pineapple 900g 7 128.6g Blueberry (3 berries) 450g (3 wines) 7 64.3g Cranberry (2 berries) 300g (2 wines) 7 42.9g

Verdict: Starfruit has the highest GPD when processed, but Ancient Fruit requires less replanting and is more sustainable. For maximum profit, fill your greenhouse with Starfruit and process into wine. For low-maintenance, go with Ancient Fruit.

docs.title: Optimal Sprinkler Placement
docs.text:
The greenhouse interior is 12 tiles wide by 10 tiles tall (usable soil). The ideal layout uses Quality Sprinklers (4-tile radius) or Iridium Sprinklers (8-tile radius).

Iridium Sprinkler Layout (Best): - Place 4 Iridium Sprinklers in a 2x2 grid. - Each covers a 5x5 area (24 crops per sprinkler). - Total crops: 96 (with 4 sprinklers). - Leave a 1-tile border for walking.

Quality Sprinkler Layout (Budget): - Place 8 Quality Sprinklers in a 3x3 pattern (center empty). - Each covers 8 crops. - Total crops: 64. - Requires more manual watering early on.

docs.title: Trellis Crops Placement
docs.text: Trellis crops (e.g., hops, green beans, grapes) block movement. Place them along the edges of the greenhouse (the 1-tile border) so you can still walk through the center. For example: - Plant hops on the top row (y=1) and bottom row (y=10). - Use the center for non-trellis crops.

docs.title: Keg and Preserves Jar Integration
docs.text:
Place kegs or preserves jars inside the greenhouse to save space in your farmhouse or shed. Use the 1-tile border around the soil for kegs. A typical layout: - 4 Iridium Sprinklers in the center. - 24 kegs along the perimeter (6 per side). - 96 crops in the soil.

This setup allows you to harvest and process without leaving the greenhouse.

docs.title: Year-Round Ancient Fruit Farm
docs.text:
Once you obtain an Ancient Seed (from artifact spots, fishing, or the Traveling Cart), plant it in the greenhouse. After the first harvest (28 days), it regrows every 7 days. With 96 plants, you'll harvest 96 fruits per week. Process into wine for 1,650g each = 158,400g per week.

Tip: Use a Seed Maker to turn some Ancient Fruit into seeds. Plant them in the greenhouse until full, then sell the rest.

docs.title: Starfruit Mega-Profits
docs.text:
Starfruit seeds cost 400g each (from Sandy in the Calico Desert). With 96 plants, that's 38,400g upfront. Harvest every 13 days. Process into wine (2,250g each) = 216,000g per harvest. Replant immediately.

Downside: Requires constant replanting and seed purchases. Best for late-game when money is abundant.

docs.title: Mixed Crop Strategy
docs.text:
If you're early-game, mix crops for variety: - Plant 48 Blueberry bushes for steady income. - Plant 48 Cranberries for fall-style profits. - Use preserves jars for Blueberries (2 days) and kegs for Cranberries (7 days).

This diversifies your income and reduces risk.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Always use the Artisan profession – It increases processed goods value by 40%.
  • Place a chest inside the greenhouse – Store seeds, fertilizer, and tools for quick access.
  • Use Deluxe Speed-Gro – Reduces growth time by 25% (e.g., Starfruit from 13 to 10 days). Buy from Pierre or craft.
  • Don't plant trees inside the greenhouse – They take up valuable crop space. Use the farmhouse or the railroad area for trees.
  • Harvest with a Gold or Iridium Scythe – Speeds up harvesting for multi-harvest crops like Blueberries.
  • Automate with Junimo Huts – Not possible in the greenhouse, but you can use the Automate mod (PC) to connect chests to kegs.
  • Save one of each crop – For quests, bundles, or the Stardew Valley Fair grange display.
  • Check the Traveling Cart – Buy Rare Seeds (Sweet Gem Berry) for a one-time profit of 3,000g each (but not regrowable).

With this guide, your greenhouse will become the engine of your farm's economy. Happy farming!

docs.title: 🎩 Complete Hat Collection Guide
docs.text:
Hats are purely cosmetic headwear in Stardew Valley — they provide no stat benefits but are essential for fashion-conscious farmers and completionists. There are 86+ hats available in Stardew Valley 1.6, acquired through events, shops, fishing treasure, monsters, and secret quests.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 💡 Hat Collection Tips
docs.text:
1. Check the Hat Mouse weekly — new stock appears as you progress
  2. Fish with Magnet tackle — increases treasure chest spawn chance for rare hats
  3. Farm Dust Sprites (Floors 40-79) — best spot for Adventurer's Hat
  4. Search all trash cans daily — Trash Can Hat is a rare but real drop
  5. Complete the Museum — unlocks the prestigious Golden Helmet
  6. Crack every Golden Coconut — Deluxe Pirate Hat is a top-tier flex
  7. Kill Wilderness Golems — 1 in 100,000 chance for Living Hat (late-game grind)
