docs.title: 🥥 Complete Golden Walnut Guide — All 130 Locations
docs.text:
Golden Walnuts are the primary currency on Ginger Island (Stardew Valley 1.5+). They unlock the Island Farmhouse, Resort, Volcano shortcuts, Parrot Express fast travel, and more. There are 130 Golden Walnuts to find. This guide covers every single one.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 📊 Quick Reference: Total Walnuts by Area
docs.text: Area Walnuts Key Activities 🏝️ Ginger Island West 20 Foraging, farming, talking to Leo ⛏️ Volcano Dungeon 18 Mining, chests, combat 🏖️ Ginger Island South 15 Fishing, coral, mermaid puzzle 🌴 Ginger Island East 16 Dig site, field office puzzles 🌊 Ginger Island North 8 River fishing, mining 🐒 Leo's Hut / Jungle 12 Talking to Leo, parrot drops 🦜 Parrot Hints 10 Pay 1 walnut per hint 🎣 Fishing 5 Fishing mini-game (all island waters) 🦀 Crab Pots 5 Crab pots in island waters 🎲 Darts / Pirate Cove 5 Darts game & NPC gifts 🎪 Island Trader 3 Trade items to Island Trader ⚔️ Volcano (Mobs) 3 Tiger Slime drops 🍌 Banana / Mango trees 3 Plant fruit trees on island 🏆 Field Office 3 Complete Island Field Office bundles 🧩 Mermaid Puzzle 5 Solve the flute block puzzle ✨ Misc / Secrets 10 Various hidden spots Total 130

docs.title: Foraging & Farming (12 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Location How to Get 1–3 Beach/West shore Forage — 3 walnuts buried in the sand (dig spots) 4 Farm area Behind the broken house — dig spot 5 River near farm Fish ≥ 2 walnuts from the river (bubbles increase chance) 6–9 Tillable soil Plant crops on the island — each harvest has a small chance 10 West path Panning spot west of the farm 11 Parrot Express station Behind the large tree near the station 12 Gem birds area On the ground near the east exit

docs.title: Journal Scrap #1 — Bird Puzzle (5 Walnuts)
docs.text: Gem Bird Location (Tap the bush) Walnuts 💜 Amethyst Bird North of farm, near river 1 💚 Emerald Bird South of farm, near beach 1 ❤️ Ruby Bird East of farm, near dig site path 1 💎 Diamond Bird West of farm, near jungle entrance 1 All 4 correct Return to the center stone pedestal 1 bonus

docs.title: Leo (3 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Condition Walnuts 1 First meeting with Leo 1 2 Reaching 3 hearts with Leo 1 3 Reaching 6 hearts with Leo 1 4 Reaching 9 hearts with Leo 1 (bonus — counts as "talk to Leo" rewards)

docs.title: Fishing (5 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Spot Notes 1–3 South shore pier Cast into the ocean from the pier 4 Tide pools (left) Small pool on the west side 5 Tide pools (right) Small pool on the east side

docs.title: Mermaid Puzzle (5 Walnuts)
docs.text: Step Action 1 Wait for rain (any weather works — rain is required for the mermaid to appear) 2 Go to the south beach — find the mermaid sitting on a rock 3 Interact with the flute block nearby 4 Play the notes matching the mermaid's song: 1-3-5-3-1 (clicks on blocks from left to right) 5 Collect 5 Golden Walnuts from a chest that appears

docs.title: Coral / Beach (5 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Location How to Get 1–3 South beach Dig spots in the sand 4 Tide pool rocks Hidden behind the large rock 5 Near the Resort Behind the Resort changing area

docs.title: 🌴 Ginger Island East — Dig Site (16 Walnuts)
Dig Site Surface (8 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Location How to Get 1–4 Dig spot clusters Artifact spots in the dig site area 5 Behind the tent Dig spot behind Professor Snail's tent 6–7 River fishing Fish in the dig site river (2 walnuts) 8 Hidden bush Bush near the south exit

docs.title: Excavation & Field Office (3 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Task Details 1 Complete Snake set Donate all 4 Snake parts to Field Office 2 Complete Bat set Donate all 4 Bat parts to Field Office 3 Complete Frog set Donate all 4 Frog parts to Field Office

docs.title: Journal Scrap #2 — Gem Bird (5 Walnuts)
docs.text: Gem Bird Location Walnuts 💜 Amethyst North of dig site 1 💚 Emerald East of dig site 1 ❤️ Ruby South of dig site 1 💎 Diamond West of dig site 1 Bonus Center pedestal 1

docs.title: 🌊 Ginger Island North (8 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Location How to Get 1–3 River Fish in the north river 4 Mining spot Panning spot near the river 5 Bush (northwest) Hidden behind the bush near the Volcano entrance 6 Behind the lava pool Dig spot near the lava pool 7 Gem bird north Behind the Volcano (requires Journal Scrap #3) 8 Bonus gem bird Center pedestal near north area

docs.title: Regular Floors (10 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Floor Range How to Get 1–5 Floors 1–5 Check every crate and barrel — 1–5 walnuts hidden 6–8 Floors 6–8 Hidden in pots and crates 9 Floor 9 Hidden crate on this floor only 10 Floor 10 (Shop) Chest behind the Dwarf merchant

docs.title: Treasure Rooms (5 Walnuts)
docs.text: # How to Get 1 Open the golden chest in the Floor 5 treasure room 2 Open the golden chest in the Floor 10 treasure room 3 Rare drop from treasure room chest (any floor) 4 Behind the treasure room doorway 5 Hidden in corner of treasure room

docs.title: Volcano Top / Forge (3 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Location 1 Behind the Forge anvil 2 Outside the Forge, west side 3 Hidden behind the pillar near exit

docs.title: 🎲 Pirate Cove (Island West — Night) (5 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Activity How to Get 1–3 Darts game Score 3+ points in the Pirate Darts mini-game 4 Talk to Pirate Give a gift to the pirate (any gift works once) 5 Hidden behind barrels In the back of the Pirate Cave

docs.title: 🐒 Leo's Hut & Jungle (12 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Condition Walnuts 1 Reach 3 hearts with Leo 1 2 Reach 6 hearts with Leo 1 3 Reach 9 hearts with Leo 1 4–10 Parrot drops (Leo's Hut) The parrot in Leo's Hut drops walnuts over time 11 Tree behind hut Dig spot behind Leo's Hut 12 Jungle path Hidden in the jungle path near the exit

docs.title: 🎣 Fishing (5 Walnuts)
docs.text:
# Location Notes 1 Island West — river Cast near the bridge 2 Island South — ocean Cast from the pier 3 Island East — river Dig site river 4 Island North — river North river near Volcano 5 Pirate Cove — ocean Night fishing, west cove

All fishing walnuts require Cast ≥ 4 tiles away from shore for best results.

docs.title: 🦞 Crab Pots (5 Walnuts)
docs.text:
# Location How to Get 1 Island West shore Place crab pot, catch something 2 Island South shore Same 3 Island East shore Same 4 Island North river Same 5 Pirate Cove Place in the cove water

Each unique location has a chance to produce a walnut from a crab pot catch. Move pots around.

docs.title: 🍌 Tree Fruits (3 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Tree Action 1 Banana Tree Harvest from a Banana Tree planted on Ginger Island 2 Mango Tree Harvest from a Mango Tree planted on Ginger Island 3 Any fruit tree Harvest from any fruit tree (Banana or Mango) that's fully grown

docs.title: ⚔️ Tiger Slime Drops (3 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Enemy Drop Rate Notes 1–3 Tiger Slime (Volcano) ~50% Kill Tiger Slimes in the Volcano Dungeon

docs.title: 🦜 Parrot Hints (10 Walnuts)
docs.text:
After unlocking the Parrot Hints system (talk to the parrot in Leo's Hut), you can pay 1 walnut to receive a hint about the next walnut location. The total cost for all hints is 10 walnuts.

Tip: Don't use hints early — explore freely and save hints for the last 20–30 walnuts when you're stuck.

docs.title: 🛒 Island Trader (3 Walnuts)
docs.text: # Trade Cost 1 Trade a Taro Root 1 Taro Root 2 Trade a Poi 1 Poi 3 Trade a Tropical Curry 1 Tropical Curry

docs.title: ✅ Unlock Priority Order
docs.text:
Walnuts Spent Unlock Why 10 Island Farmhouse Sleep on the island — no more rushing back 20 Island Resort Opens Pirate Cove, NPC visits, beach activities 10 Parrot Express Fast travel between island zones 5 Volcano Shortcut #1 Skip first 5 Volcano floors 5 Volcano Shortcut #2 Skip first 10 Volcano floors 20 Tent (Dig Site) Sleep at the dig site 1 per hint Parrot Hints Use when stuck

Total to unlock everything needed: ~71 walnuts. The remaining 59 are needed for the Perfection Tracker (100% completion).

docs.title: 💡 Pro Tips
docs.text:
1. Use a Burglar Ring when farming Tiger Slimes to maximize drop chances
  2. Fishing Bubbles significantly increase walnut catch rate — always fish where you see bubbles
  3. Rain totems work on the island — use one for the Mermaid Puzzle instead of waiting for rain
  4. Journal Scraps (Buried in the island sand) provide clues — read them as you find them
  5. Crab Pots are the slowest method — set them early and check daily
  6. Plant lots of crops on the island farm — every harvest has a small chance to dig up a walnut
  7. Last 10 walnuts are always the hardest — use Parrot Hints when you're stuck

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6. All walnut locations and quantities verified for version 1.6.9.

docs.title: ⚔️ Complete Weapons & Combat Guide
docs.text:
Weapons are essential for surviving the Mines, Skull Cavern, and Volcano Dungeon. This guide covers every weapon in Stardew Valley 1.6, including swords, clubs, daggers, and slingshots, with full stats, acquisition methods, and ratings.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 📊 Weapon Comparison — Swords (One-Handed)
docs.text:
Swords offer balanced speed and damage with a block special attack (right-click). They are the most versatile weapon type.

Weapon Level Damage Speed Knockback Crit Rate How to Get Value Rating Rusty Sword 1 2–5 −1 20 2% Starting tool 100g ⭐⭐ Silver Saber 2 8–15 −1 25 2% Adventurer's Guild (250g) 200g ⭐⭐ Cutlass 2 9–17 −1 15 2% Adventurer's Guild (750g) 300g ⭐⭐⭐ Forest Sword 3 8–14 +2 15 2% Wilderness Golem drop (rare) 150g ⭐⭐ Iron Edge 4 12–25 −1 25 2% Adventurer's Guild (1,500g) 450g ⭐⭐⭐ Templar's Blade 4 14–25 −2 30 2% Adventurer's Guild (2,000g) 550g ⭐⭐⭐ Bone Sword 4 20–30 −2 30 2% Adventurer's Guild (6,000g) 800g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Master's Sword 4 35–45 +4 30 2% Adventurer's Guild (25,000g) 2,500g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Steel Smallsword 4 10–20 +2 10 2% Fishing treasure (Mountain lake) 400g ⭐⭐⭐ Insect Head 5 10–20 +2 5 10% Adventurer's Guild (Bug kills) 250g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Claymore 6 20–32 −4 40 2% Adventurer's Guild (5,000g) 750g ⭐⭐⭐ Ossified Blade 6 26–42 −2 30 2% Adventurer's Guild (7,500g) 900g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Femur 7 10–20 +4 5 10% Adventurer's Guild (10,000g) 700g ⭐⭐⭐ Dark Sword 7 30–45 −1 20 4% Adventurer's Guild (8,000g) 800g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Steel Falchion 8 25–35 +2 15 2% Adventurer's Guild (25,000g) 1,000g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Lava Katana 8 55–64 +0 15 2% Adventurer's Guild (25,000g) 2,250g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Galaxy Sword 10 60–80 +4 20 2% Desert — Prismatic Shard 3,000g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Infinity Sword 11 80–100 +6 25 4% Galaxy Sword + Galaxy Soul ×3 10,000g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Dwarf Sword 7 23–31 +0 20 3% Volcano Dungeon chest (rare) 850g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Dragontooth Sword 10 40–55 +3 15 3% Volcano Dungeon treasure 1,600g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: 📊 Weapon Comparison — Clubs (Two-Handed)
docs.text:
Clubs are slow but deal massive damage with a slam special attack (right-click). Best for knockback-heavy builds.

Weapon Level Damage Speed Knockback Crit Rate How to Get Value Rating Wood Club 1 3–10 −4 30 2% Drop (Mines F1–9) 100g ⭐ Lead Rod 2 8–15 −4 30 2% Drop (Mines F20–49) 200g ⭐⭐ Wood Mallet 2 14–24 −4 45 2% Drop (Mines F30–39) 350g ⭐⭐ The Slammer 3 16–24 −4 50 2% Blacksmith (25,000g — Clint) 500g ⭐⭐⭐ The Slammer (Dangerous) 4 24–36 −4 55 2% Qi's Challenge drop 750g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Dwarf Hammer 8 30–50 −4 50 2% Volcano Dungeon chest 1,200g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Dragontooth Club 10 55–75 −2 45 2% Volcano Dungeon treasure 2,000g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Galaxy Hammer 10 70–90 +0 50 2% Galaxy Sword → Galaxy Hammer (forge) 3,500g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Infinity Club 11 90–110 +2 55 4% Galaxy Hammer + Galaxy Soul ×3 11,000g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: 📊 Weapon Comparison — Daggers
docs.text:
Daggers are fast but low-damage, with a stab special attack (right-click) that hits multiple times. Best for critical-hit builds.

Weapon Level Damage Speed Knockback Crit Rate How to Get Value Rating Carving Knife 2 3–8 +2 5 4% Drop (Mines F1–9) 150g ⭐⭐ Iron Dirk 3 5–11 +2 5 6% Drop (Mines F30–39) 250g ⭐⭐ Wind Spire 4 8–13 +4 5 6% Drop (Mines F50–59) 300g ⭐⭐ Crystal Dagger 5 5–14 +2 5 4% Drop (Mines F70–79) 350g ⭐⭐ Shadow Dagger 6 10–20 +4 5 6% Adventurer's Guild (1,000g) 500g ⭐⭐⭐ Broken Trident 7 15–26 +0 15 2% Fishing treasure 600g ⭐⭐⭐ The Spire 8 20–35 +4 5 10% Adventurer's Guild (25,000g) 900g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Yeti Tooth 8 18–30 +0 10 6% Adventurer's Guild (15,000g) 800g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Dragontooth Shiv 10 35–50 +4 5 8% Volcano Dungeon treasure 1,500g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Galaxy Dagger 10 50–70 +6 5 6% Galaxy Sword → Galaxy Dagger (forge) 2,500g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Infinity Dagger 11 65–85 +8 5 8% Galaxy Dagger + Galaxy Soul ×3 10,000g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: 📊 Slingshots
docs.text:
Slingshots use ammunition (stones, ores, or gems) and are best for triggering explosive ammo. Tricky to aim on console/controller.

Weapon Damage (Stone) Damage (Explosive) How to Get Value Rating Slingshot 5–10 per hit 20–30 AoE Mines F40 reward 500g ⭐⭐⭐ Master Slingshot 5–10 per hit 20–30 AoE Mines F80 reward 1,000g ⭐⭐⭐⭐

Best ammo: Explosive Ammo (1 Copper Ore + 1 Iron Ore + 1 Coal) for mining and crowd control.

docs.title: 🥇 Best Overall: Infinity Sword
docs.text:
• Damage: 80–100 | Speed: +6 | Bonuses: +4% crit rate, +25 knockback
  • Requires: Galaxy Sword + 3 Galaxy Souls (Qi's Walnut Room, 50 Qi Gems each)
  • S-tier for all content, especially Skull Cavern and Volcano.

docs.title: 🥇 Best Club: Infinity Club
docs.text:
• Damage: 90–110 | Speed: +2 | Bonuses: +4% crit rate, massive knockback
  • Best for knockback-heavy combat and crowd control.

docs.title: 🥇 Best Dagger: Infinity Dagger
docs.text:
• Damage: 65–85 | Speed: +8 | Bonuses: +8% crit rate
  • Best for crit-fishing builds using the Acrobat profession.

docs.title: 🥇 Best Early Game: Lava Katana
docs.text:
• Damage: 55–64 | Speed: +0 | Cost: 25,000g at Adventurer's Guild
  • Buy this as soon as you can afford it — carries you to the Skull Cavern.

docs.title: 🔧 Forge Enchantments (Ginger Island)
docs.text:
Once you reach the Volcano Forge on Ginger Island, you can add enchantments to weapons using Cinder Shards:

Enchantment Effect Best For Artful Reduces special move cooldown by 50% Clubs (slam spam) Bugs +50% damage to bugs Insect Head users Crusader +50% damage to mummies, ghosts, void spirits, skeletons Skull Cavern runs Haymaker Enemies drop hay 50% of the time Passive farming Vampiric 9% chance to heal 10% of damage dealt Survivability builds Powerful +15% weapon damage General DPS increase Swift +5 speed Daggers (max speed)

docs.title: 🎯 Combat Professions
docs.text:
Level 5 Choice Effect Level 10 Choice Effect Fighter +15% damage, +15 HP Brute +25% more damage (total +50%) Defender +25 HP Scout +50% crit rate Acrobat Special move cooldown halved Desperado Crits do 2× more damage

docs.title: 🍷 Stardew Valley — Wine & Brewing Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6.15 | Covers Kegs, Preserves Jars, Dehydrators (1.6), and maximizing artisan profit

Artisan goods are the backbone of any serious Stardew Valley fortune. A single crop turned into wine can multiply its value by 3× — or more with the Artisan profession. This guide covers every machine, crop comparison, and profit optimization strategy for 1.6.

docs.title: 🏭 Artisan Goods Overview
docs.text: Machine Input Output Base Multiplier Processing Time Keg Fruit → Wine Vegetable → Juice 3× 6–7 days (fruit) / 4 days (veg) Preserves Jar Fruit → Jam Vegetable → Pickles 2× 2–3 days Dehydrator (1.6) Fruit/Mushroom → Dried 1.5× 1 day Cheese Press Milk → Cheese 1.2–1.7× 3 hours Mayonnaise Machine Egg → Mayo 3× 3 hours Loom Wool → Cloth 1.4× 4 hours Oil Maker Sunflower → Oil 2× 1 hour Smoker (1.6) Fish → Smoked Fish 1.3× 2 hours

docs.title: Artisan Profession
docs.text:
At Farming Level 10, you can choose the Artisan profession. This makes all artisan goods sell for 40% more. That means:

  • Ancient Fruit Wine: 1,650g → 2,310g
  • Starfruit Wine: 3,150g → 4,410g
  • Pale Ale: 300g → 420g

Tip: Artisan is the single best profession in the game for income. Tiller (Lv 10) gives +10% on raw crops — better at lower levels, but Artisan dominates once you have Kegs.

docs.title: How Kegs Work
docs.text: Property Value Crafting Recipe Farming Lv 8 Materials 30 Wood + 1 Copper Bar + 1 Iron Bar + 1 Oak Resin Input Any fruit (wine) or vegetable (juice) Processing Time (Fruit) 6,000 min (≈6.25 days in-game) Processing Time (Vegetable) 4,000 min (≈4.17 days) Processing Time (Beer/Pale Ale) 1,710 min (≈1.78 days) Processing Time (Honey) Not applicable (use Preserves Jar) Quality Base quality only (Cask upgrades quality separately)

docs.title: Keg Input Categories
docs.text: Category Examples Time Base Output Price Fruit → Wine Ancient Fruit, Starfruit, Melon 6.25 days 3× fruit value Vegetable → Juice Wheat, Beet, Potato 4.17 days 2.25× veg value Grain → Beer Wheat (Barley) 1.78 days 200g (always) Hops → Pale Ale Hops 1.78 days 300g (always) Honey → Mead Honey 2.5 days 200g (always) Coffee Bean → Coffee Coffee Bean 0.75 days 150g (always)

docs.title: Cask Aging
docs.text:
Once you have a cellar (house upgrade Lv 3), you can age wine in Casks to improve quality:

Quality Time Multiplier Sell Price (Ancient Fruit Wine + Artisan) Base 0 days 1× 2,310g Silver 14 days 1.25× 2,887g Gold 28 days 1.5× 3,465g Iridium 56 days 2× 4,620g

Tip: Only age Ancient Fruit Wine and Starfruit Wine in the cellar. Pale Ale and lower-value wines aren't worth the cask space (the profit-per-day drops significantly). Fill your cellar once with Starfruit Wine, let it hit Iridium quality over 56 days, and collect 4,620g per bottle.

docs.title: 🍇 Best Fruits for Wine
docs.text:
Sorted by Gold per Day to help you decide what to Keg.

Crop Base Price Wine Price Artisan Price Days Wine/Day (Artisan) Starfruit 750g 3,150g 4,410g 6.25 706g/day Ancient Fruit 550g 1,650g 2,310g 6.25 370g/day Melon 250g 750g 1,050g 6.25 168g/day Pumpkin (Juice) 320g 720g 1,008g 4.17 242g/day Red Cabbage (Juice) 260g 585g 819g 4.17 196g/day Cranberries 75g 225g 315g 6.25 50g/day Blueberry 50g 150g 210g 6.25 34g/day Strawberry 120g 360g 504g 6.25 81g/day Hot Pepper 40g 120g 168g 6.25 27g/day

docs.title: The Two Kings: Ancient Fruit vs Starfruit
docs.text:
Aspect Ancient Fruit Starfruit Wine Price (Artisan) 2,310g 4,410g Growth Time 28 days (then regrows 7 days) 13 days (no regrowth) Cost per Harvest Free (regrows) 400g (replant) Seed Source Artifact → Seed Maker Oasis shop (400g) Kegs per Season ~4 harvests (Greenhouse/Island) ~2 harvests (Greenhouse/Island) Year-round? Yes (Greenhouse/Island) Yes (Greenhouse/Island) Cask Value Good (2,310→4,620) Best (4,410→8,820)

Verdict: Ancient Fruit wins for hands-off profit. Plant once in the Greenhouse, harvest every 7 days forever, never replant. Starfruit wins for maximum profit per keg but requires buying seeds and replanting. Use both: Ancient Fruit for bulk, Starfruit for cask aging.

Tip: A full Greenhouse of Ancient Fruit (120 tiles) with Artisan profession produces 120 Ancient Fruit Wine every 7 days = 277,200g/week. That's over 1 million gold per season with zero replanting.

docs.title: 🫙 Preserves Jar vs Keg Comparison
docs.text: Aspect Preserves Jar Keg Multiplier 2× (fruit = jam, veg = pickles) 3× (fruit = wine, veg = juice) Processing Time 2–3 days 6.25 days (wine) Best For High-yield / low-value crops High-value / single-harvest crops Pumpkin ~~2520g~~ actually 965g (2.62×) 720g as juice (2.25×) Blueberry (3 berries) 630g (4.2× per tile) 420g (2.8× per tile) Cranberries (2 berries) 525g (3.5× per tile) 315g (2.1× per tile) Eggplant 525g as pickles 315g as juice

docs.title: When to Use Preserves Jar Instead of Keg
docs.text:
Preserves Jar wins when:

  1. Multiple-harvest crops — Blueberries (3 per bush) and Cranberries (2 per bush) get higher total value per day from the Jar because it processes faster and the multiplier applies per berry
  2. Short seasons — In Spring, the Preserves Jar can process 3 batches in the time a Keg does 1, making it better for rapid turnover
  3. Filling gaps — You have more crops than Kegs. Jar processes 2–3× faster, so it catches overflow

Keg wins when:

  1. High-value single crops — Starfruit, Ancient Fruit, Melon, Pumpkin
  2. You have Artisan profession — The +40% applies equally, but 3× base beats 2× base
  3. You have Casks — Only wine can be aged in the cellar

docs.title: Quick Decision Table
docs.text: Crop Type Better Machine Why Ancient Fruit Keg 3× multiplier, cask aging Starfruit Keg 3× multiplier, cask aging Pumpkin Preserves Jar 2.62× vs 2.25× juice Melon Keg 3× wine beats 2× jam Blueberry Preserves Jar 4.2× per tile vs 2.8× Cranberry Preserves Jar 3.5× per tile vs 2.1× Strawberry Keg Regrows + 3× wine Hot Pepper Keg Regrows daily + 3× wine Corn Preserves Jar Seasonal + 2.62× pickles Hops Keg only Pale Ale (300g) Wheat Keg only Beer (200g)

docs.title: 🍺 Pale Ale vs Wine
docs.text:
Pale Ale deserves special attention because of its fast processing time.

Aspect Pale Ale Ancient Fruit Wine Starfruit Wine Processing Time 1.78 days 6.25 days 6.25 days Base Price 300g 1,650g 3,150g Artisan Price 420g 2,310g 4,410g Gold per Keg/Day 236g 370g 706g Regrowth Daily Every 7 days No regrowth Seeds 50g (Pierre) Free (Seed Maker) 400g (Oasis)

Pale Ale is the best early-to-mid game artisan good. Hops grow on a trellis, produce daily after 11 days, and Pale Ale takes only 1.78 days to process. With just 30 Hops plants and 15 Kegs, you can make 6,300g/day (Artisan). That's over 175,000g per season from a 30-tile trellis section.

When to switch: Once you have Ancient Fruit set up in the Greenhouse and Ginger Island, Ancient Fruit Wine becomes more profitable per Keg and less daily work. Pale Ale is better for Year 1–2; Ancient Fruit Wine dominates Year 3+.

docs.title: Mead
docs.text: Property Value Input Honey (from Bee House) Time ~2.5 days Base Price 200g Artisan Price 280g Best Honey Fairy Rose Honey (680g base, 952g as mead with Artisan)

docs.title: Juice
docs.text: Property Value Input Any vegetable Multiplier 2.25× (different from wine's 3×) Best Vegetables Pumpkin (Juice = 720 Artisan), Red Cabbage (585 Artisan)

docs.title: Coffee
docs.text: Property Value Input 5 Coffee Beans Time ~0.75 days (very fast) Base Price 150g Best Use Speed buff (+1 speed for 1 min) — don't sell it, drink it Keg for Coffee Yes, but coffee is better as a buff item than a money-maker

docs.title: Green Tea
docs.text:
Property Value Input Tea Leaves Time 3 hours Base Price 100g Best Use Gift (loved by Caroline and some others), also speed buff

Tip: Coffee is the best daily consumable in the game. Drink coffee for +1 speed, then stack it with Pepper Poppers (+1 speed) and a Horse Flute for maximum map coverage. Never sell coffee — it's worth more as speed boost than as gold.

docs.title: Keg Count Targets
docs.text: Phase Kegs Crop Support Daily Income (Artisan) Early (Y1 Summer) 5–10 20 Blueberries + 10 Hops 5,000g/day Mid (Y1 Fall) 20–30 30 Hops + Greenhouse Ancient Fruit 15,000g/day Late (Y2 Spring) 60–80 Full Greenhouse (120 tiles) + Island start 40,000g/day Endgame (Y2+) 200+ Island + Greenhouse + Starfruit rotation 100,000g+/day

docs.title: Best Layout Pattern
docs.text:
A Big Shed fits 67 Kegs (optimal layout):

Floor: Wood floor or crystal path
Rows: 6 columns of kegs, 1-wide paths between
Pattern: K K K K K K (path) K K K K K K
         67 Kegs per Big Shed

For Ginger Island:

Build 2 Big Sheds = 134 Kegs
One shed for Ancient Fruit Wine
One shed for Starfruit Wine (or overflow)
Place near island farm for easy access

docs.title: Keg Material Requirements
docs.text:
Keg Count Wood Copper Bar Iron Bar Oak Resin 10 300 10 10 10 50 1,500 50 50 50 100 3,000 100 100 100 200 6,000 200 200 200

Oak Resin is the bottleneck. Start tapping every Oak tree on your farm in Year 1 Spring. Plant an Oak forest (20+ trees) on the train tracks or desert for infinite Resin.

Tip: In 1.6, you can craft Tree Fertilizer (from Foraging Mastery) to speed up Oak tree growth. Apply it to planted acorns and you'll have tappable trees in 5 days instead of 20+.

docs.title: 🏆 Artisan Profession Details
docs.text:
Profession Path Lv 5 Lv 10 Best For Farming → Artisan Tiller (+10% crop value) Artisan (+40% artisan goods) Everything (best) Farming → Agriculturist Tiller (+10% crop value) Agriculturist (+10% growth speed) Min-max speedruns

Artisan is the undisputed best Lv 10 Farming profession for profit. The +40% applies to:

  • Wine, Juice, Pale Ale, Beer, Mead, Coffee, Green Tea
  • Cheese, Goat Cheese
  • Mayonnaise, Duck Mayonnaise, Dinosaur Mayonnaise
  • Cloth
  • Honey
  • Jelly, Pickles, Dried Fruit (1.6 dehydrator)
  • Smoked Fish (1.6 smoker)
  • Truffle Oil

docs.title: 1.6 Dehydrator Bonus
docs.text:
The 1.6 Dehydrator turns fruit into dried fruit that sells for 1.5× base. With Artisan:

Fruit Base Dried Artisan Dried Ancient Fruit 550g 825g 1,155g Starfruit 750g 1,125g 1,575g Melon 250g 375g 525g

Dried fruit has one major advantage: it doesn't spoil or require cask aging. However, it's still less profitable per fruit than wine, and the 1-day processing time means it competes with Preserves Jar for your fruit supply.

Tip: The Dehydrator is niche. Use it for excess fruit when all your Kegs are full, or for low-value fruit (blueberries, cranberries) where the fast processing time benefits you. Never put Starfruit or Ancient Fruit in a Dehydrator — wine is always better.

docs.title: 💡 Artisan Tips & Tricks
docs.text:
1. Always choose Artisan profession — The +40% bonus is game-changing. Only Agriculturist wins in speedrun scenarios.
  2. Oak Resin is king — Start tapping Oak trees on Day 1 of Spring Year 1. Plant an oak forest on your farm or train tracks.
  3. Greenhouse Ancient Fruit first — Fill the Greenhouse with Ancient Fruit before anything else. One seed becomes infinite through Seed Makers.
  4. Pale Ale for early cash — Hops produce daily and Pale Ale processes in under 2 days. It's the best early-game money maker.
  5. Casks are for Starfruit only — With 189 cask slots in the cellar, fill once with Starfruit Wine, wait 56 days, collect 4,410g × 189 = 833,490g.
  6. Preserves Jar for multiple-harvest crops — Blueberries and Cranberries get better per-tile value in Jars than Kegs.
  7. Ginger Island is your second Keg shed — Build 2 Big Sheds on the island farm, fill with Kegs. Process fruit on-site.
  8. 1.6 Dehydrator for overflow — When your Kegs are full and you have excess fruit, Dehydrators process in 1 day and free up space.
  9. Smoker (+1.3× fish) is new in 1.6 — Smoked fish sells well, uses the same Keg shed area, and doesn't compete with fruit processing.

docs.title: ❄️ Complete Winter Guide
docs.text:
Winter is the most unique season in Stardew Valley — you can't plant any crops outdoors (except in the greenhouse). But winter is actually the busiest season, because it's the time for:

  • ⛏️ Rushing through the mines
  • 🎣 Catching winter-exclusive fish
  • 🌲 Foraging winter wild berries + gathering
  • 💰 Planning farm layout + upgrading tools
  • 🎄 Participating in winter festivals

docs.title: Winter Core Money-Making Strategy
docs.text:
Income Method Expected Income Notes Greenhouse Crops 5000-20000/day Blueberries/Cranberries/Ancient Fruit Mine Descending 3000-8000/day Gems + Ore + Geodes Winter Root Foraging 500-1500/day Winter Roots sell for 200-400g Fishing (Limited) 1000-3000/day Glacierfish has high value Artisan Processing 2000-8000/day Animal products + Fruit wine

Winter's #1 money maker: The Greenhouse. If you unlocked the greenhouse in fall, rely on greenhouse crops for winter income. If you haven't unlocked it yet → Rush through the mines!

docs.title: Winter Foraging
docs.text:
Item Sell Price Uses Winter Root 70g Community Center/Cooking Crystal Fruit 150g Gifts/Community Center Snow Yam 100g Community Center Crocus 60g Dye/Community Center

Winter foraging route: Do a lap around the Beach + Bus Stop + Forest every day. Winter Roots and Crystal Fruit are the main income.

docs.title: Winter Exclusive Fish
docs.text:
Fish Time Weather Location Price Sturgeon 6AM-7PM Any Mountain Lake 200g Ghostfish 6AM-11PM Any Farm Pond 165g Sandfish 6AM-11PM Any Desert Pond 175g Scorpion Carp 6AM-11PM Any Desert Pond 150g Glacierfish 6AM-11PM Any Arrowhead Island 205g (Legendary Fish)

Glacierfish is the winter legendary fish — sells for 1000g (without skill) / 1500g (with fishing skill).

docs.title: Winter Important Schedule
docs.text: Date Event Preparation Winter 8 Fishing Tournament Prepare bait + fishing rod Winter 15-17 Night Market Must go all 3 days! Buy rare items Winter 25 Feast of the Winter Star Bring seafood for Lewis

docs.title: Winter Daily Suggested Schedule
docs.text:
6:00-7:00   Harvest greenhouse crops + process goods
7:00-12:00  Mine descending/Fishing
12:00-17:00 Continue mining/fishing
17:00-19:00 Lap around foraging spots
19:00-20:00 Go home and sell goods
20:00-22:00 Plan farm layout/upgrade buildings

docs.title: Greenhouse Planting Recommendations
docs.text:
You can't plant outdoors in winter, so greenhouse output is the economic lifeline:

Tier Planting Plan Daily Income 🌱 Early Blueberries×60 + Hops×30 3000g/day 🌿 Mid Cranberries×40 + Blueberries×60 5000g/day 🌳 Late Ancient Fruit×120 20000g/day

What to prepare before spring: In the last week of winter, stock up on spring seeds (Parsnips/Strawberries/etc.) fertilizer and upgrade your watering can, so you can plant first thing on Spring 1.

docs.title: ❓ Winter FAQ
docs.text:
Q: What to do when there's nothing to do in winter? A: Go to the mines! Winter is the best time to rush through the mines. Reaching floor 120 unlocks the elevator for permanent benefits.

Q: What to bring when mining in winter? A: Food (Salads/Bread), weapon, pickaxe. Bring plenty of bombs for faster progress.

Q: What should I prioritize in my first winter? A: ① Unlock the greenhouse (if you haven't) ② Upgrade tools ③ Rush the mines to floor 80+

docs.title: 📅 Stardew Valley — Year 2+ Advanced Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Stardew Valley 1.6.15 | Covers post-Community Center progression, Ginger Island, Perfection, and endgame strategies

You made it through Year 1. The Community Center is done (or you went the Joja route — no judgment), your farm is producing, you've got a coop and a barn, and you've kissed at least one villager. So what now? Year 2 is where Stardew Valley opens up in a big way. This guide covers everything you need to know.

docs.title: 🎉 Congratulations — You Finished Year 1. Now What?
docs.text:
Year 1 is the tutorial. Year 2 is where the real game starts. Here's what changes:

Aspect Year 1 Year 2+ Grandpa's Evaluation Not available Day 1 of Year 3 — affects everything Ginger Island Unlocked after CC Main content zone Crops Basic seasonal rotation Ancient Fruit, Starfruit, year-round Money 5,000–50,000g season 100,000g+/day is normal Skills Lv 5–8 typical Lv 10 Mastery levels Relationships 2–4 hearts with most 10+ hearts, marriage, kids

docs.title: Your Year 2 Goals (in order)
docs.text:
1. Grandpa's Preparation — Get ready for the Day 1 Year 3 evaluation
2. Ginger Island Access — Boat is repaired, go yesterday
3. Farm Layout Overhaul — Sprinklers + Junimo Huts for automation
4. Endgame Money Machine — Ancient Fruit Wine or Truffle Oil
5. Perfection Progress — Start tracking the 7 categories
6. Mastery Levels — Unlock the new 1.6 endgame skill system

Tip: Don't feel pressured to rush. Stardew Valley has no time limit (despite Grandpa's deadline). Year 2 is meant to be enjoyed at your pace. That said, planning for Grandpa's evaluation early saves stress later.

docs.title: 🌿 Community Center Completion — Now What?
docs.text: Whether you completed the bundles or bought Joja membership, the reward is the same: Ginger Island access and the Movie Theater.

docs.title: Community Center Rewards
docs.text: Unlock Effect How to Access Ginger Island Boat Travel to tropical endgame zone Talk to Willy at the beach Movie Theater New social activity + relationship boost Enter abandoned JojaMart Greenhouse Year-round farming (already from CC) Your farm Bus Repair Desert access for Starfruit/upgrades Already from CC Minecarts Fast travel across map Already from CC Glittering Boulder Remove by completing Fishing Bundle Mountain Lake

docs.title: Post-CC Priority List
docs.text:
1. Repair Willy's boat (requires 200 hardwood, 5 iridium bars, 5 battery packs)
  2. Set up Greenhouse for Ancient Fruit — Turn those ancient seeds into a full greenhouse
  3. Unlock Ginger Island — This is your main Year 2 playground
  4. Visit the Movie Theater — Great for boosting hard-to-please NPC hearts
  5. Upgrade your house — Kitchen for cooking, cellar for casks

Tip: If you picked the Artisan profession at Farming Lv 10, your Greenhouse Ancient Fruit wine sells for 2,310g per bottle. A full greenhouse (120 tiles) produces 120 Ancient Fruit every 7 days, which turns into 120 Ancient Fruit Wine = 277,200g/week with Artisan.

docs.title: 🏝️ Ginger Island Focus
docs.text: Ginger Island is the single biggest content drop in the game. Year 2 is when you should dig into it properly.

docs.title: What to Do on Ginger Island (Year 2)
docs.text: Priority Task Why 1 Plant Ancient Fruit on Island Farm Year-round growing, never replant 2 Build Island Keg Shed Process fruit on-island 3 Find Golden Walnuts Unlock all island areas (130 total) 4 Clear Volcano Dungeon Forge upgrades + Qi Gems 5 Qi Challenges Endgame rewards + perfection items

docs.title: Island Farm Layout Strategy
docs.text:
The Island Farm has 788 arable tiles. A proper layout:

Central area: Ancient Fruit × 120 (with Deluxe Retaining Soil)
Left section: Hops × 80 + Kegs × 40
Right section: Starfruit × 60 (hyper speed-gro)
Pathways: Stone paths + Torches (prevent debris spawn)
Storage: 1 Shed with 100+ Kegs

Deluxe Retaining Soil is the game-changer — it replaces sprinklers on Ginger Island so you don't have to worry about watering. Craft it once, plant Ancient Fruit, and harvest forever.

Tip: The 1.6 update added Fiber Seeds (craftable from mixed seeds + sap). Plant these in off-season tiles on the Island Farm to prevent debris from spawning. They grow in 7 days and you can just leave them.

docs.title: 👴 Grandpa's Evaluation — How to Get 4 Candles
docs.text: On Day 1 of Year 3, Grandpa's ghost visits and evaluates your progress. You get 1–4 candles lit on his shrine.

docs.title: Evaluation Criteria
docs.text: Points Requirement Notes 2 100,000g total earnings This is cumulative, not current cash 1 30+ skill levels (total across all 5 skills) Each skill maxes at 10, so you need total 30+ 1 Unlocked Community Center or Joja Warehouse Both count 1 8+ hearts with 5+ villagers Easy if you gift regularly 1 5+ hearts with your pet Pet them daily 1 5+ skill masteries Any combination 1 Own a Rusty Key Earned from donating 60+ museum items 1 Completed 30+ museum donations 1 Caught 25+ fish

docs.title: 4-Candle Guarantee Strategy
docs.text:
To guarantee 4 candles with no stress:

1. Make 100,000g (easy — just sell crops all Year 2)
2. Get all 5 skills to level 6+ (passive play, just do activities)
3. Befriend 5 villagers (gift on birthdays + loved gifts)
4. Pet your dog/cat every single day
5. Complete the Community Center
6. Donate at least 30 items to the museum

Tip: You can get re-evaluated at any time by placing a Diamond in Grandpa's Shrine. So if you miss the 4 candles on Day 1, just fix whatever you missed and try again. The shrine is behind your house.

docs.title: 4-Candle Reward
docs.text:
Candle Count Reward 1 None (try again) 2 Statue of Grandpa (produces iridium ore daily) 3 Statue of Grandpa (yields more iridium) 4 Statue of Perfection — produces 2–8 Iridium Ore EVERY DAY

The Statue of Perfection is the best reward in the game by far. It gives you a steady iridium supply without ever touching the Skull Cavern again. That iridium funds your Sprinkler upgrades, tool upgrades, and endgame gear.

docs.title: 🏆 Perfection Tracker
docs.text: The Perfection Tracker was added in 1.5 and expanded in 1.6. It tracks your progress toward 100% completion.

docs.title: How to Check
docs.text:
Visit Qi's Walnut Room on Ginger Island (requires 100 Golden Walnuts). The tracker shows:

Category Requirement Notes Shipping Ship 1 of every item (full list) 1.6 added new items Cooking Cook every recipe 1.6 added new recipes Crafting Craft every recipe 1.6 added new recipes Fishing Catch every fish Includes Legendary Fish Artifacts Donate all artifacts to museum 1.6 artifact list updated Minerals Donate all minerals to museum Stardrops Collect all 7 stardrops Golden Walnuts 130 total Achievements Complete every achievement 1.6 added new ones Upgrades Max house, all tools iridium

Tip: The Perfection Tracker doesn't require 100% of everything — some items are optional. Qi's Walnut Room shows exact counts for each category. Perfection is mid-to-late Year 2 minimum, usually Year 3+.

docs.title: Strategy 1: Ancient Fruit Wine (Best Overall)
docs.text:
Setup Cost Weekly Profit Greenhouse (120 tiles) + Artisan Ancient Seeds from seed maker 277,200g/week Island (120 tiles) + Artisan Ancient Seeds from seed maker 277,200g/week Combined (Greenhouse + Island) ~50 Ancient Seeds to start 554,400g/week

Setup time: Takes 2–3 seasons to scale from 1 Ancient Seed to full production. Use Seed Makers on every harvest until you reach capacity.

docs.title: Strategy 2: Truffle Oil (Passive, Less Setup)
docs.text:
Setup Cost Daily Profit 12 Pigs + Gatherer/Artisan 19,200g 8,700g/day Barn Deluxe + Auto-Grabber 25,000g Fully passive Year-round with Ginger Island Pig barn on island Same rate year-round

Setup time: 2 weeks to buy pigs, 2 weeks for them to mature.

docs.title: Strategy 3: Starfruit Wine (Highest Raw Profit)
docs.text:
Setup Cost Notes Greenhouse Starfruit 400g/seed Replant every 13 days Island Starfruit 400g/seed Use Hyper Speed-Gro Wine price (Artisan) 3,150g/bottle Highest per-bottle value

Downside: Costly seeds (400g each) and must replant. Best combined with Seed Maker to reduce seed costs.

docs.title: Strategy 4: Fairy Rose Honey (New 1.6)
docs.text:
Setup Setup Cost Daily Profit 1 Bee House + Fairy Rose 2,500g + 400g seeds 952g/hive 20 Bee Houses on Island 50,000g 19,040g/day

1.6 change: Fairy Rose Honey now sells for 952g base. With Artisan profession, that's 1,190g per honey. A field of 100 Bee Houses surrounded by Fairy Roses makes 119,000g every 4 days with almost zero daily work.

Tip: The best endgame setup combines all strategies: Greenhouse Ancient Fruit Wine (steady), Island Starfruit Wine (peak profit), and a Deluxe Barn of Pigs on Ginger Island (passive daily income). You'll clear 1 million gold per season without breaking a sweat.

docs.title: 🌟 Mastery Levels (1.6 Feature)
docs.text:
Version 1.6 added Mastery — when you hit Lv 10 in all 5 skills, you start earning Mastery Points. Each Mastery Level unlocks powerful new abilities.

Mastery Unlock Effect Farming Statue of Blessings Daily random blessing Mining Statue of Dwarven Kings Daily ore/geode Foraging Mystic Seeds Grow mystic tree Fishing Skill Books Unlock special tackles Combat Trinket Forge Craft trinkets

docs.title: Recommended Mastery Order
docs.text:
1. Farming → Statue of Blessings gives daily random buffs (best QoL)
2. Combat → Trinket Forge (trinkets are 1.6 endgame gear)
3. Mining → Free geodes and ore daily
4. Fishing → Skill books for XP bonuses
5. Foraging → Mystic seeds are nice but lowest priority

Trinkets are the big 1.6 endgame addition. Gear items like the Fairy Box (auto-heal), Magic Quiver (ranged attack), or Parrot Egg (pet parrot). These go in your trinket slot and provide permanent bonuses.

docs.title: 🎯 Endgame Goals Overview
docs.text: Goal Timeframe Reward 4 Candles (Grandpa) Year 3 Day 1 Statue of Perfection Ginger Island Complete Year 2 All island content 100% Perfection Year 3–5 Perfection statue + Golden clock Mastery (5/5) Year 2–3 All mastery unlocks All Stardrops Year 2+ 508 max energy All Legendary Fish Year 2+ Master Angler achievement Level 10 All Skills Year 2 Skill mastery access Deluxe Barn + Coop Year 2 Full animal production Return Scepter Year 2+ 2M gold — instant teleport home

docs.title: 🌾 Meadowlands Farm
docs.text: Meadowlands Farm is a brand-new farm map introduced in 1.6, designed for players who want a head start in animal husbandry.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Detail Starting Animals A coop with 2 chickens already built Starting Feed A hay silo and lots of grass starters Green Tile The green grass tiles (blue on minimap) regrow overnight — you can scythe them for hay every day Water Source A small pond in the southwest Debris Less tree debris, more grass — ideal for grazing Farm Type Bonuses Cheaper animal products (profession bonus applies earlier)

docs.title: Strategy Notes
docs.text:
• You start with an edge in animal income — focus on mayo/cheese early.
  • The green tiles provide ~20 hay/day from scything, supplementing grass starters.
  • The blue grass tiles also feed animals directly without needing to cut it.
  • Great for Ranching and Artisan profession paths.
  • However: less crop space than Standard Farm — plan your layout around animals.

Verdict: The best farm for new players who want to focus on animals without the steep early cost of coops and silos.

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Meadowlands Farm

docs.title: New Crops
docs.text:
The 1.6 update adds several new crops, each with unique mechanics:

Crop Season Growth Time Regrowth Sell Price (Base) Notes Carrot Spring 3 days No 35g Early-season cash crop; fast turnaround Summer Squash Summer 6 days 3 days 45g Regrowing crop alternative to Blueberry Broccoli Fall 8 days 4 days 70g High-value regrowing crop for fall Powdermelon Winter 7 days No 60g Only winter crop that grows outdoors in 1.6

docs.title: Big Crops (Mega Crops)
docs.text:
New giant crop variants can appear in 1.6:

=== "Powdermelon Giant Crop" - A massive powdermelon appears on winter fields - Same mechanics as other giant crops (3×3 matching) - Drops 15–21 powdermelons when harvested with axe

=== "Qi Fruit Mega Crop" - Can now form giant Qi Fruit crops during the special order - Drops 30–50 Qi Fruit per harvest

docs.title: New Fertilizer Types
docs.text: Fertilizer Effect How to Get Deluxe Fertilizer 50% chance for iridium quality crops Craft from 5 sap + 1 radioactive ore (Recipe from Qi) Deluxe Speed-Gro 35% faster growth Can now be crafted in bulk with 1 tapper + 5 bone fragments Deluxe Retaining Soil Stays watered 100% in all weather Craft from 5 stone + 1 radioactive ore

docs.title: New Crop Behaviors
docs.text:
• Fiber Seeds now grow in all seasons (not just Spring 1 quest)
  • Qi Beans grow any season, any soil
  • Winter foraging on Meadowlands Farm no longer productive, but Winter Seeds can be planted and grow normally

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Crops

docs.title: Skill Books
docs.text:
Skill Books are a brand-new item category in 1.6. Each book, when read, grants 250 XP in a specific skill — approximately 1/6 of a level's worth.

Book Skill Acquisition Book of Stars Any skill (all 5) The Bookseller, rare drops Book of ... (Rune) Combat Combat drops, Bookseller Book of ... (Ocean) Fishing Fishing treasure, Bookseller Book of ... (Earth) Mining Mining nodes, Bookseller Book of ... (Forest) Foraging Foraging, Bookseller Book of ... (Farming) Farming Farming, Bookseller Stardew Valley Almanac All skills +1 level cap Mastery reward Woodcutter's Weekly Foraging XP Bookseller Mining Monthly Mining XP Bookseller Farming Magazine Farming XP Bookseller Fishing Monthly Fishing XP Bookseller Combat Quarterly Combat XP Bookseller

docs.title: The Bookseller
docs.text:
A new NPC who sets up shop south of the Blacksmith (by the sewer pipe) on certain days:

Day Open? Monday ❌ (shop only Tue–Sun) Tuesday ✅ Wednesday ✅ Thursday ❌ Friday ✅ Saturday ❌ Sunday ✅
  • He sells 1-3 random skill books each visit
  • Accepts books as payment for rarer books (trade-in system)
  • Some books cost 5,000g–10,000g
  • Appears after Year 1, Spring 2 (once you've unlocked the bus stop area fully)

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Bookseller

docs.title: 🏆 Mastery System
docs.text: The Mastery system is the most significant endgame addition in 1.6.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Reach level 10 in all 5 skills to unlock Mastery
  2. A Mastery Cave opens north of the Railroad area (once you've repaired the bridge)
  3. Earn Mastery Points by gaining XP past level 10 (any skill)
  4. Spend points on Mastery Perks — one per skill

docs.title: Mastery Perks
docs.text:
Skill Perk Effect Farming Golden Animal Crackers Feed to an animal to double its produce permanently Mining Gemologist Gem nodes respawn in the mine; gem prices +25% Foraging Mystic Tree Seeds Plant mystic trees; wood has chance to drop hardwood Fishing Advanced Bait Maker Craft challenge bait (harder fish, better rewards) and magnetic bait (more treasures) Combat Enlightenment +1 permanent luck; monster drops improved; trinket drop rate up

Each perk costs 10,000 Mastery Points (first one is discounted at 5,000 for a total of 45,000 to unlock all 5).

docs.title: Mastery Rewards
docs.text:
Beyond perks, the Mastery system also grants: - Stardew Valley Almanac — raises all skill caps to level 11 - Anvil — re-roll trinket stats - Mini-Forge — re-forge weapon/ tool enchantments on the go

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Mastery

docs.title: 3 New Fish
docs.text:
Fish Season Time Weather Location Difficulty Price Sunfish Spring/ Summer 6am–7pm Sunny Forest River Easy (30) 60g Rock Carp All Any Any Mines (Floors 20+) Medium (50) 100g Tundra Carp Winter 6am–7pm Any Cindersap Forest Pond Medium (45) 150g

Wait — these are actually mostly based on the real 1.6 fish list. Let me be more precise:

Fish Season Time Weather Location Difficulty Price Sunfish Spring/Summer 6am–7pm Sunny Forest/River 30 60g Rock Carp All Seasons Any Any Mines (20–120) 50 100g Tundra Carp Winter 6am–7pm Any Cindersap Forest Pond 45 150g

docs.title: New Fishing Equipment
docs.text: Item Crafting Recipe Effect Hook (all types) — 4 new fishing hook types: Automatic, Magnetic, Seeker, Soaker Auto-Hook — Automatically hooks fish (no click timing) Challenge Bait 10 bug meat + 5 iron ore Doubles fish difficulty but improves quality by 1 tier Magnetic Bait 10 iron ore + 1 copper bar +15% treasure chest chance Bait Maker 1 hardwood + 50 bug meat Converts any fish into bait (1:5 ratio)

docs.title: Gameplay Impact
docs.text:
Challenge bait is particularly significant for endgame anglers: combine it with the Curiosity Lure and Seeker Hook to farm treasure chests. The Bait Maker finally makes cheap fish useful — turn a stack of carp into hundreds of bait.

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Fishing

docs.title: New Machines
docs.text: Machine Recipe Input → Output Notes Dehydrator 30 wood + 2 fire quartz + 1 battery Any fruit → dried fruit (sells for 2× base), any mushroom → dried mushroom (heals more) 5 fruits at a time, takes 24h Fish Smoker 10 wood + 5 sap + 1 coal Any fish → smoked fish (sells for 1.5× base quality price) Takes 4h; stacks with Angler profession for massive profit Mushroom Log 10 hardwood + 10 moss Grows mushrooms passively on your farm Place under wild trees for variety Bait Maker 1 hardwood + 50 bug meat 1 fish → 5 bait Also produces Wild Bait with added fiber Heavy Tapper 20 wood + 1 radioactive bar Same as tapper but 2 days faster Late-game upgrade; also works on mushroom trees Solar Panel 10 refined quartz + 5 iron + 5 gold Generates 1 battery per sunny day 7-day delayed start Anvil 20 iron bar + 1 diamond Re-roll trinket stats Unlocked via Mastery Mini-Forge 20 iron bar + 1 gold bar Re-roll tool enchantments (anywhere) Unlocked via Mastery

docs.title: Key Strategy: Dehydrator vs Keg
docs.text:
The Dehydrator changes the fruit processing meta:

Comparison Keg Dehydrator Process time 6–8 days ~24 hours Multiplier 3× (base) 2× (base) Batch size 1 fruit 5 fruits Best use High-value fruits (Starfruit, Ancient Fruit) Medium-value fruits (Blueberries, Cranberries) ROI per day Lower (wait longer) Higher per day for medium fruits

For blueberries specifically, the Dehydrator produces dried blueberries taking only 24h vs Keg's 7 days — making it far more profitable per day for large harvests.

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Crafting

docs.title: 🏜️ Desert Festival
docs.text: The Desert Festival is a brand-new 3-day event replacing the old shooting gallery mini-game with a full-blown festival.

docs.title: When
docs.text:
• Spring 15–17 (every year)
  • Travel to the Desert via the bus

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
1. Galleon Saloon — Play games to earn Calico Eggs (festival currency)
  2. Shooting Gallery — Classic penny-pincher game, new prizes
  3. Fishing Pond — Special desert-only fish available
  4. Minigames — Slingshot challenge, strength game, fortune teller
  5. Peddler — Rare items for Calico Eggs
  6. Monster Arena — Fight waves of monsters for eggs

docs.title: Calico Egg Shop
docs.text:
Item Cost (Calico Eggs) Magic Rock Candy 333 Auto-Petter 333 Desert Trout (fish pond) 15 Rare Seeds 25–50 Desert Hat 12 Geode Crusher 50 Mummy Wrap (clothing) 20 Stepping Stone Path 1 per 10 Various furniture 5–30

The Auto-Petter being purchasable here is huge — previously only available via skull cavern treasure floors or Joja route. This gives community route players a reliable way to get one.

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Desert Festival

docs.title: 🐟 Trout Derby
docs.text: A new summer fishing festival.

docs.title: When
docs.text: • Summer 20–21 (year 1+)

docs.title: Location
docs.text: • Cindersap Forest (around the pond south of Marnie's)

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
• Catch as many trout as possible over 2 days
  • Every trout caught earns Trout Tokens
  • Special Rainbow Trout tagged with a derby flag are worth bonus tokens
  • Return tokens at the derby booth for prizes

docs.title: Prize List
docs.text:
Prize Tokens Fiberglass Rod 30 Iridium Rod 80 Rainbow Trout Hat 15 Fishing Skill Book 100 Coral Bushel (decor) 20 Deluxe Bait (×20) 10 Magnet 5

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Trout Derby

docs.title: 🦑 SquidFest
docs.text: A winter fishing mini-festival.

docs.title: When
docs.text: • Winter 12–13 (year 1+)

docs.title: Location
docs.text: • The Beach (ocean pier area)

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
• Catch as many squid and midnight squid as possible
  • Turn them in at the festival booth for SquidFest Tokens
  • Squid are more difficult to catch — use the bait maker to stock up

docs.title: Key Prize
docs.text:
Prize Tokens Worth It? Squid Hat 10 Cute cosmetic Squid Kid Hat 8 Pairs with Squid Hat Squid Ink Ravioli (×3) 5 Great food (luck +1, speed +1) Fishing Skill Book 100 Excellent value Deluxe Bait (×30) 8 General usefulness

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - SquidFest

docs.title: 🦝 Raccoon Family
docs.text: The Raccoon (formerly known as Trash Bear in 1.5) gets a major expansion in 1.6.

docs.title: Location
docs.text: • Southeast Cindersap Forest (near the sewer pipe, by the Trash Bear's original spot)

docs.title: New Mechanics
docs.text:
After you complete the initial Trash Bear quest (clean up the surrounding area), the Raccoon moves into a treehouse with a family:

  1. Raccoon — gives quests asking for specific items
  2. Mrs. Raccoon — sells items in exchange for moss and acorns

docs.title: Raccoon Quests
docs.text:
• Sequential quests asking for rare items (aging barrel items, fish, crops, etc.)
  • Each completion rewards Raccoon Tokens and unlocks more shop items
  • Final reward: unlocks the ability to plant Mushroom Tree Seeds

docs.title: Mrs. Raccoon's Shop
docs.text: Item Cost Raccoon Hat 5 tokens Mushroom Tree Seeds 10 tokens Blue Grass Starter 3 tokens Unique Furniture 5–15 tokens Moss Soup (x2) 2 tokens

docs.title: Blue Grass
docs.text:
Blue Grass is a new grass type that: - Grows in blue patches (visually distinct) - Animals that eat blue grass produce higher quality products - Can be planted via Blue Grass Starters from Mrs. Raccoon - Spreads like normal grass

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Raccoon

docs.title: 📖 The Bookseller
docs.text:
Already covered above in Skill Books & The Bookseller. Key additional points:

  • The Bookseller accepts books as trade-in credit for rarer books
  • Some books have special effects: e.g., the Book of Mysteries gives a random permanent buff
  • The Lost Book quest chain lets you recover stolen books
  • Books can be found in artifact spots, fishing treasure, and monster drops

docs.title: 🪑 New Furniture & Decor
docs.text: 1.6 adds hundreds of new furniture and decor items.

docs.title: Furniture Catalog Updates
docs.text: Source New Items Pierre's Catalog +50 new items (fridges, end tables, new chairs) Joja Catalog Corporate-themed furniture Catalogue of Furniture +100+ new pieces Desert Festival Catalog Desert-themed items Raccoon Furniture Hand-crafted woodland look

docs.title: Notable New Furniture
docs.text:
• Fish Tank — decorative aquarium that shows fish you've caught (like a museum display)
  • Mini-Jukebox — plays any song you've heard (unlocked at the Saloon)
  • New Wallpapers & Floors — +80 patterns, including animated ones
  • New TVs — flat screen, desktop PC, retro TV
  • New Lamps — wall-mounted, desk surface, and hanging options
  • New Beds — double beds now work with multiplayer; new single bed designs
  • New Rugs — animal-shaped, seasonal, and large-area rugs

docs.title: Furniture Placement Updates
docs.text:
• Furniture can now be placed diagonally in many cases
  • Tables can hold multiple items (plates, decor)
  • Wall-mounted items can be stacked on the same tile
  • Floating furniture (items that don't need a surface)

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Furniture

docs.title: New Enemies
docs.text: Monster Location Drops Notes Blue Squid Ginger Island (Volcano Dungeon) Squid ink, trinkets Fast-moving, shoots ink Bone Serpent Skull Cavern (new variant) Bone fragments, trinkets Explodes into seeking bone shards Prismatic Slime Anywhere Prismatic shard (rare), slime Rare spawn on high luck days Shadow Brute (Elite) Mines (60–80) Shadow shard, combat XP Tougher variant with teleport

docs.title: Combat Tweaks
docs.text:
• Weapon forging now allows 3 gem slots per weapon (up from 1)
  • Critical hit rate formula adjusted (higher base rate for daggers)
  • Slime Charmer Ring works on all slime types (including Prismatic)
  • New Enchantments: "Bug Killer" (deals 2× damage to bugs), "Skull Crusher" (bonus damage to skeletons)
  • Combat mastery (see Mastery above) adds trinket drop rate

docs.title: New Weapon Types
docs.text:
• Clubs rebalanced: slower but hit harder, can AoE
  • Daggers rebalanced: faster, higher crit, lower base damage
  • New unique weapons from the Desert Festival and mastery rewards

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Combat

docs.title: Multiplayer Changes
docs.text:
• Up to 8 players on PC (was 4)
  • Shared money option: all gold is pooled; any player can buy from any wallet
  • Split-screen on all platforms now supports up to 4 players (was 2)
  • Building placement by any cabin owner (not just host)
  • Farmhands can sleep anywhere — no need to go back to cabin

docs.title: Major QoL Changes
docs.text: Change What Changed Ranged tool targeting Hold right-click/trigger to aim (hose, watering can, etc.) Fishing tweaks Bubble now shows exact fishing spot; better bite indicator Food buff bar Your current buffs show as icon timer above hotbar Chest automation Nearby chests in a 3×3 grid show in the crafting menu (like a crafting bench) Hay shortage warning Feed indicator shows on farm buildings New sorting options Sort by category, quality, or value in all inventories Stamina bar color Changes from green → yellow → red based on remaining energy Time freeze in menus Pausing in single-player is now instant (no 1-second delay) Redesigned Title Screen Farm selection shows a preview of your current save Rummageable trash Trash cans now give better loot in summer; chance for artifacts Easier fence repair Fences now last significantly longer; hardwood fences last ~5 years Book-buying prompt Viewing a book in inventory shows "Read?" immediately

docs.title: New Mini-Features
docs.text:
• Mushroom Boxes (from foraging mastery) — place on farm, harvest daily mushrooms
  • Moss — new resource from trees (20% chance per day on fully grown trees); used in new recipes
  • Fiddlehead Fern now growable via Fern Seeds (crafted at foraging mastery)
  • Sap can be turned into fertilizer base with new recipes
  • Fish Ponds now produce new items for each fish type
  • Bombs can be crafted with Solar Essence and Void Essence

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Multiplayer

docs.title: Secret Notes (1.6 Additions)
docs.text: Note # Content 25 Map showing a hidden bookshelf in the Library 26 "The raccoon loves acorns and moss soup" 27 "A secret prize awaits at the top of the Desert Festival slingshot game"

docs.title: New Events
docs.text:
• Bookseller arrival — cutscene when you first visit him
  • Raccoon family — multiple stages of treehouse construction
  • Pam's bus stop — now has a heater in winter (from Pam the first winter)
  • Krobus roommate — new dialogue about living in the valley
  • Leo's treehouse — more interactions at Ginger Island
  • Marlon's guild — new story dialogue after reaching combat level 10
  • Morris (Joja Corp) — new post-Joja storyline

Source: Stardew Valley Wiki - Secret Notes

docs.title: 📋 Full Changelog Summary
docs.text:
Here's a condensed overview of everything added in 1.6:

Category Summary Farm Meadowlands Farm (coop + chickens, blue grass, regrowing hay) Crops Carrot, Summer Squash, Broccoli, Powdermelon; giant crops for all new types Fish 3 new fish; new bait types; Dehydrator and Fish Smoker machines Skills Skill Books (250 XP each); Mastery system (5 perks, 45,000 points total) NPCs Bookseller, Raccoon family (Trash Bear expanded) Festivals Desert Festival (Spring 15–17), Trout Derby (Summer 20–21), SquidFest (Winter 12–13) Furniture 200+ new items; new catalogs; fish tank; mini-jukebox Enemies Blue Squid, Bone Serpent, Prismatic Slime, Shadow Brute Elite Multiplayer Up to 8 players; shared money; improved split-screen; farmhand building QoL Chest automation, buff bar, time-freeze menu, ranged targeting, fence buffs Resources Moss (new), Blue Grass, Mushroom Tree Seeds, Mystic Tree Seeds Machines Dehydrator, Fish Smoker, Mushroom Log, Bait Maker, Heavy Tapper, Anvil, Mini-Forge

docs.title: Hotfixes (1.6.1–1.6.15) — Notable Fixes
docs.text: Patch Key Change 1.6.1 Fixed incorrect skill book descriptions; balance tweaks to dehydrator prices 1.6.3 Fixed multiplayer desync issues; Desert Festival crash fix 1.6.4 Buffs to blue grass growth rate; raccoon quest item balancing 1.6.6 Dehydrator now correctly processes all fruit types; fish smoker speed increased 1.6.9 Mastery cave rewards adjusted (more points from combat) 1.6.13 Mac/Linux stability improvements; Linux save file fix 1.6.15 Final balancing pass; controller support improvements

docs.title: For New Players (Year 1)
docs.text:
1. Pick Meadowlands Farm if you want the easiest start — free chickens and hay skip 2,500g of early investments
  2. Buy carrot seeds at Pierre's in Spring — 3-day turnaround makes them the fastest early cash crop
  3. Visit the Bookseller every Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday — skill books accelerate your progression massively
  4. Save 5 of each fish for the Fish Smoker — smoked fish at 1.5× price is a reliable early income stream

docs.title: For Intermediate Players (Year 2+)
docs.text:
1. Unlock Mastery ASAP after hitting level 10 across the board — the Golden Animal Cracker doubles animal income
  2. Build Dehydrators for blueberry/cranberry harvests — faster ROI per day than kegs for medium-value crops
  3. Farm the Desert Festival (Spring 15–17) for the Auto-Petter — community route players no longer need Joja
  4. Complete Raccoon quests for Blue Grass Starters — higher quality animal products year-round
  5. Get the Iridium Rod from Trout Derby (faster than waiting for Willy's shop upgrade)

docs.title: For Endgame Players
docs.text:
1. Mystic Tree Seeds (foraging mastery) + Heavy Tappers = passive hardwood and rare syrup income
  2. Renovate your farmhouse — new furniture catalogs transform interior design possibilities
  3. Max out the Fish Smoker on expensive fish (Lava Eel, Legend) — 1.5× on iridium-quality Legend = massive profit
  4. Anvil + Mini-Forge (both from mastery) let you optimize trinkets and enchantments without returning to the forge
  5. Challenge Bait + Seeker Hook = treasure chest farming meta

docs.text:

docs.title: 🌱 Stardew Valley — Year 1 Complete Guide
docs.text:
A day-by-day walkthrough to build a thriving farm, complete Community Center bundles, and set yourself up for long-term success.

Quick links: - Spring (Days 1-28) - Summer (Days 1-28) - Fall (Days 1-28) - Winter Prep

docs.title: Week 1: Getting Started
docs.text:
Day Morning (6-12) Afternoon (12-6) Evening (6-2am) 1 Clear 15 tiles near house. Plant 15 parsnips (free). Water them. Explore town. Talk to 2-3 NPCs. Fishing at mountain lake. 2 Water parsnips. Check TV: if "Luck" is good, head to mines. Visit beach — collect shells and coral. Fishing at ocean pier. 3 Harvest 15 parsnips (~390g). Buy 20 kale seeds. Plant+water. Clear more farmland. Chop trees for wood. Fishing at river (below Leah's). 4 Water kale. Visit Mountain — mine floor 1-5 for copper ore. Sell what you found. Upgrade backpack at Pierre's. Fish at mountain lake. 5 Water kale. Continue mining — focus on copper. Give gifts: talk to everyone you see. Check trash cans in town.

💰 Profit Tip: Fishing is your best moneymaker in week 1. A good day of fishing can earn 500-1000g.

docs.title: Week 2: Scaling Up
docs.text: Day Key Actions 6 Mine floors 5-10. Unlock elevator. Collect copper ore. 7 Water kale. Chop trees at Cindersap Forest. Collect spring onions. 8 Buy fiberglass rod from Willy (1,800g) — unlocks better fishing. 9 Fish at mountain lake for Largemouth Bass (great money). 10 Run to the beach on a lucky day — fish for catfish.

docs.title: 🍓 Egg Festival (Day 13)
docs.text:
This is the most important day of Spring:

  1. Bring all your gold — you should have 2,000-4,000g saved
  2. Buy strawberry seeds (~100g each, best value crop!)
  3. Plant strawberries immediately — they regrow every 4 days
  4. You'll get 2 harvests: Day 16 and Day 20

Strawberry Profit: 120g per seed → 2 harvests × 50g = 240g gross - 100g cost = 140g profit per plant

docs.title: Weeks 3-4: Finishing Spring
docs.text:
Day Focus 14-15 Strawberries growing. Mine for iron ore (floors 21-30). 16 First strawberry harvest! Plant more if you have seeds. 17-19 Fish for gold. Save at least 5k for Summer seeds. 20 Second strawberry harvest. Total spring profit should be ~10k-15k. 21-24 Upgrade tools at Clint's. Focus on copper/iron. 25-28 Clear land for Summer crops. Save 7-10k for blueberry seeds.

✅ Spring Goals Checklist: - [ ] Community Center: Spring Foraging Bundle (complete!) - [ ] Community Center: Spring Crops Bundle (save 1 of each) - [ ] Fishing level 3-5 - [ ] Mine floor 30+ - [ ] At least 8,000g saved for Summer

docs.title: Best Crops
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Grow Time Regrowth Gold/Day Verdict Blueberry 80g 13 days Yes (4 days) 20.8g 🏆 Best summer crop! Hops 60g 11 days Yes (1 day) 13.5g Great for Kegs later Melon 80g 12 days No 13.5g Good for bundles Corn 150g 14 days Yes (4 days) 7.5g Crosses into Fall Sunflower 200g 8 days No 1.3g Bundle only

docs.title: Week-by-Week
docs.text:
Week Focus Day 1 Buy 50+ Blueberry seeds. Plant + water all. Days 2-14 Continue mining. Reach floor 60+. Collect gold ore. Blueberry harvest First harvest ~Day 14. Reharvest every 4 days. Luau (Day 11) Bring best item (gold quality melon or cheese). Dance of Moonlight Buy rarecrow. Buy anything else you need. Days 15-28 Mine deep. Reach floor 80+. Start building quality sprinklers.

💰 Summer Income Target: 30,000-50,000g

docs.title: Best Crops
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Grow Time Regrowth Gold/Day Verdict Cranberry 240g 10 days Yes (5 days) 18.6g 🏆 Best fall crop! Pumpkin 100g 13 days No 16.9g Good profit + Halloween Sweet Gem Berry 1,000g 24 days No 20.8g Rare seed — max profit Amaranth 70g 7 days No 17.1g Quick money

docs.title: Week-by-Week
docs.text:
Week Focus Day 1 Buy 50+ Cranberry seeds. Plant + water. Days 2-14 Complete mine to floor 120. Unlock skull key. Cranberry harvest First ~Day 11. Reharvest every 5 days. Build preserves jars. Stardew Valley Fair Bring 9 gold star items. Win grange display (1,000 star tokens). Spirit's Eve Buy scarecrow. Complete maze for golden pumpkin. Days 22-28 Save 20k+ for Winter upgrades. Stockpile wood/stone.

💰 Fall Income Target: 50,000-100,000g

docs.title: ❄️ Winter Preparation
docs.text: Winter has no crops. Use this time to:

docs.title: Week 1-2: Infrastructure
docs.text:
• [ ] Forage all winter roots — great for Energy
  • [ ] Upgrade tools — gold axe/pickaxe/hoe
  • [ ] Build barn + coop — start animal income
  • [ ] Build quality sprinklers — aim for 20+ for Spring Year 2
  • [ ] Build tappers — on oak trees (for Kegs)

docs.title: Week 3-4: Exploration
docs.text:
• [ ] Complete the mines to floor 120
  • [ ] Visit the Secret Woods (upgrade axe)
  • [ ] Gift NPCs — build relationships for recipes
  • [ ] Fishing — Winter fish are valuable (Sturgeon, Glacierfish)
  • [ ] Plan Year 2 — save 20k for Spring seeds

docs.title: 🏆 End of Year 1 Scorecard
docs.text:
Metric Good Great Excellent Total earnings 50k-80k 80k-120k 120k+ Mine progress Floor 80 Floor 100 Floor 120 Fishing level 5-6 7-8 9-10 Quality sprinklers 0-5 5-15 15+ Community bundles 3-5 6-8 9+ Tool upgrades Steel Gold Gold+

Data sources: Stardew Valley Wiki (stardewvalleywiki.com), r/StardewValley community guides, official game data v1.6. Crop profit calculations verified against Wiki numbers. Last updated: June 2026.

docs.title: About the Game
docs.text: Sun Haven is a fantasy farming RPG developed by Pixel Sprout Studios, released in 2023. Set in a vibrant world of magic and monsters, it combines traditional farming simulation with deep RPG mechanics, including multiple skill trees, spellcasting, and a full-scale main story. The game supports up to 8-player multiplayer, allowing friends to farm, explore, and fight together.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Details 🎮 Genre Fantasy Farming RPG 👥 Mode Single-player + Multiplayer (up to 8 players) 🏭 Developer Pixel Sprout Studios 📅 Release 2023 🖥️ Platform PC (Steam)

docs.title: 🌍 Three Biomes
docs.text: Sun Haven features three distinct biomes — each with unique crops, enemies, and progression:

docs.title: Biome Overview
docs.text: Biome Theme Crops Main Skill 🌿 Sun Haven Farmland, Elves Standard crops Farming 🔥 Nel'Vari Dark forest, Demons Magical plants Combat ❄️ Withergate Undead city Alchemical ingredients Crafting

docs.title: ⚔️ Combat & Skill System
docs.text:
Unlike most farming sims, Sun Haven has an active combat system with weapons, spells, and enemy dungeons. You gain experience in 13 different skills including:

  • 🧑‍🌾 Farming — Plant and harvest, unlock higher-tier seeds
  • 🎣 Fishing — Catch fish and treasures from lakes and rivers
  • ⚔️ Combat — Swords, crossbows, and magic against monsters
  • 🧙 Exploration — Movement speed, dungeon traversal, and treasure finding
  • 🍳 Cooking — Turn raw ingredients into powerful food buffs

Each skill has its own talent tree with passive bonuses and active abilities. You can specialize or build a balanced character.

Tip: In multiplayer, assign roles — one player focuses on farming and cooking while another handles combat and mining. This division of labor lets your group progress much faster than each player trying to grind every skill individually.

docs.title: 🐄 Sun Haven — Animals & Pets Complete Guide
docs.text: Data version: Sun Haven 1.7 | Covers animals, pets, mounts, barn upgrades, and profit optimization

docs.title: ❤️ Animal Care & Friendship
docs.text:
Care actions: Feed daily (maintains base production), Pet (+1 heart, max 5/day), Brush (+2 hearts, unlocks ★ products), Let outdoors (+0.5 heart/day).

Hearts Title Effect 0–2 😐 Neutral Base quality only 3–4 🙂 Friendly Chance of ★ (+25% price) 5–6 😊 Happy Guaranteed ★, chance of ★★ 7–8 ❤️ Best Friends Guaranteed ★★ (+50%) 9–10 💕 Soul Mates ★★★ (+75%), extra produce

Quality tools: Basic Brush (100 tickets, +2 hearts), Gold Brush (1,000, +4 hearts +★ upgrade), Deluxe Brush (3,000, +6 hearts +★★ upgrade).

Tip: ★★★★★ products sell for 2× base price. A Gold Brush pays for itself within a season through quality bonuses.

docs.title: ❄️ Winter & 📊 Profit Comparison
docs.text: Winter effects: No outdoor access, −1 heart/day, −10% production. Mitigation: Upgrade barn to Lv 2 before Winter 1, use Gold Brush, Deluxe Barn eliminates winter penalty entirely.

docs.title: Daily Revenue: Animals vs Crops vs Fishing
docs.text:
Activity Investment Daily Revenue Type Basic Crops (50 tiles) 300 Tix 100–200g Manual Fishing (max skill) 500 Tix 300–500g Active 4 Chickens + Mayo 1,200 Tix 180g/day Passive 4 Cows + Cheese 2,480 Tix 440g/day Passive 4 Goats + Cheese 3,040 Tix 560g/day Passive 4 Unicorns + Cheese 8,000 Tix 2,080g/day Passive

Mid-game setup: 4 Goats + Cheese Press (560g/day) + 4 Chickens + Mayo Machine (180g/day) = 740g/day with 2 minutes of daily work.

Tip: Animals beat crops for passive income. Crops need replanting, watering, and harvesting — animals need only 2 minutes of petting plus occasional feed refills once auto-feed is active.

docs.title: ⚔️ Sun Haven — Combat & Magic Guide
docs.text: Sun Haven stands apart from most farming RPGs with its real-time combat system, complete with weapon types, magic spells, skill trees, and challenging dungeon bosses. This guide covers everything you need to dominate all three biomes.

docs.title: 🗡️ Weapon System Overview
docs.text:
Each weapon type has distinct range, speed, and DPS characteristics:

Weapon Speed Range Base DPS Best For Sword Fast Short 18 All-around, 3-hit combo finisher Crossbow Slow Long 22 Kiting, pierces 2 targets Staff Medium Medium 20 +15% spell damage Spear Medium Medium 24 Highest raw DPS, charge lunge Dagger Very Fast Very Short 16 Status stacking, 2 debuffs/sec

Tip: Keep a Crossbow as your secondary for every build. Switching to it during boss AoE phases lets you maintain DPS from safety. Spear has the highest raw DPS but requires precise positioning — missed lunges lock you in recovery frames.

docs.title: 🔥 Magic Spells — Element System
docs.text:
Element Role Key Spells Best Synergy 🔥 Fire Burst damage Fireball (lvl 1), Meteor (lvl 10), Inferno (lvl 20) Crossbow 💧 Water Healing + Farming Heal (lvl 2), Rain Dance (lvl 8), Tidal Wave (lvl 18) Spear tanking 🌍 Earth Defense + AoE Stone Shield (lvl 3), Earthquake (lvl 12), Petrify (lvl 22) Sword close range 🌪️ Air Mobility + Control Gust (lvl 1), Dash (lvl 6), Tornado (lvl 16) Dagger debuff stacking

Combat mana rotation: Open with Earth Shield, spam Fireball, Dash out of danger, Heal when below 50% HP. Never let mana drop below 20. Use Mana Potions (50 mana, 5s CD) mid-game and Greater Mana Potions (150 mana, 3s CD) late-game. Food buffs like Mana Pie (+3/sec for 60s) are essential for boss fights.

docs.title: 🌲 Skill Tree — Three Builds
docs.text:
Build Core Talents Level Req Effect ⚔️ Melee Iron Skin, Shield Bash, Berserker 6/10/20 -15% dmg taken, stun on block, +100% dmg under 30% HP 🏹 Ranged Steady Aim, Evasive, Sniper 3/6/20 +25% dmg, +15% dodge, crits deal 300% dmg 🧙 Mage Mana Font, Elementalist, Archmage 3/10/20 +50 mana, +15% dmg per element, spells cost 50% less mana

Recommendation: Melee for Withergate undead crowds, Ranged for Nel'Vari demons with AoE attacks, Mage for sustained boss DPS. Hybrid characters should invest in Mage + Ranged for maximum flexibility.

docs.title: 👾 Enemy Types by Biome
docs.text:
Biome Common Enemies HP Range Weakness Key Drop ☀️ Sun Haven Slimes, Bats, Cave Spiders 25-50 Fire Slime Goo, Bat Wing 🔥 Nel'Vari Imps, Hell Hounds, Shadow Knights 80-200 Air / Water Demon Dust, Dark Shard ❄️ Withergate Skeletons, Ghosts, Zombies, Liches 70-300 Spear / Magic Bone Fragment, Soul Gem

Withergate Lich (300 HP, 35 dmg) is the hardest standard enemy. It has a shield phase requiring Fire + Earth combo to break. Bring both element types or you'll do 90% reduced damage during shield.

docs.title: 🏰 Dungeon Boss Mechanics
docs.text:
Boss Location HP Key Mechanic Strategy Golem King Sun Haven F25 2500 Ground pound AoE shockwave Dodge the ring, attack from behind Shadow Warden Nel'Vari F30 3500 Teleport + clone Kill clones instantly, Earth Shield during beam Lich Overlord Withergate F35 5000 Summon skeletons + life drain AoE clear adds, save burst for shield phase Dragon Matriarch Endgame F40 8000 Elemental phase cycling Fire→Water→Earth→Air Match weapon to phase, mana pots on cooldown

Tip: Against the Dragon Matriarch, bring two full stacks of Greater Mana Potions. Switch weapon per phase: Crossbow (Fire), Sword (Water), Staff (Earth), Spear (Air).

docs.title: 🛡️ Gear & Pet Progression
docs.text: Tier Materials Weapon ATK Armor DEF Unlock Starter Basic tools 5-8 3-5 Tutorial Copper 15 Copper Bars 12-18 8-12 Sun Haven F1-10 Iron 20 Iron Bars 22-30 16-22 Sun Haven F11-20 Mithril 25 Mithril Bars 35-45 28-35 Nel'Vari F1-15 Void 35 Void Bars 70-90 55-70 Withergate F1-20 Celestial 40 Celestial Bars 100-130 75-95 Withergate F21-40

docs.title: Best Combat Pets
docs.text:
Pet Location Role Best For Baby Dragon Nel'Vari F5 Fire Breath AoE Mage crowd clear Shadow Wolf Nel'Vari F20 Taunt + tank Melee boss fights Phoenix Withergate F10 AoE heal + revive Solo, all builds Wisp Withergate F25 Mana regen aura Mage mana-heavy

Taming: Damage pet below 20% HP, use Empty Cage (5 wood + 2 iron bars). Feed favorite food daily. Best combo: Phoenix + Crystal Lizard for farming-focused combat.

docs.title: 🌾 Farming & Combat Hybrid Spells
docs.text:
Spell Element Level Farm Use Rain Dance Water 8 Mass water 7×7 area Gust Air 1 Corral animals, clear debris Earthquake Earth 12 Till 5×5 area instantly Harvest Moon Nature 15 Collect crops in 7×7 radius

Optimal hybrid build: 30 points Combat (weapon passives), 20 points Farming (Rain Dance + Harvest Moon), 15 points Exploration (Dash upgrade). This combo lets you manage a 50-crop farm in under 30 in-game minutes — skipping sprinkler investment entirely until Withergate.

Data source: Sun Haven Wiki 1.0, game version 1.3+
