docs.title: 🍳 Sun Haven — Cooking Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Sun Haven 1.7 | Covers kitchen upgrades, all recipe categories, ingredient sources, and meal buff optimization

Cooking in Sun Haven is one of the most powerful systems in the game — not just for restoring health and mana, but for applying long-duration buffs that dramatically improve combat, mining, fishing, and farming performance. Unlike many farming sims where cooking is optional, in Sun Haven it is essential for endgame content. This guide breaks down every recipe category, kitchen upgrade, ingredient source, and buff strategy.

docs.title: 🔥 Cooking System Overview
docs.text:
Cooking requires a Cooking Station (also called a Kitchen), which is a craftable workbench purchased from the town. Unlike artisan equipment (Kegs, Preserves Jars), cooking produces instant-eat meals with buffs rather than processed goods for selling.

How it works: - Build a Cooking Station at your farm → place it indoors - Combine ingredients from farming, foraging, fishing, and monster drops - Each recipe produces 1 serving of a meal - Meals can be eaten for HP + Mana restoration and/or timed buffs - Your Cooking Skill levels up as you cook — unlocking better recipes and quality bonuses

Tip: Build your Kitchen before the end of Spring. Even basic recipes like Toast or Fried Egg more than double the energy return of eating raw ingredients.

docs.title: 🏗️ Kitchen Upgrades
docs.text:
The Cooking Station can be upgraded twice at the Carpenter Shop. Each upgrade unlocks more recipes and reduces cook time.

Upgrade Cost Recipes Unlocked Cook Time Reduction Basic Kitchen 400 Tickets 15 starter recipes — Deluxe Kitchen 3,500 Tickets + 15 Iron Bar 20 additional recipes −25% Gourmet Kitchen 8,000 Tickets + 20 Gold Bar + 5 Mithril Bar 30+ advanced recipes −50%

What each upgrade adds: - Basic Kitchen: All simple meals (Toast, Fried Egg, Salad, Roasted Vegetables), basic juices, and early desserts - Deluxe Kitchen: Sauces, complex entrees, multi-ingredient desserts, stat-boosting meals with +2–3 buff tiers - Gourmet Kitchen: Endgame recipes including Elixirs, Gourmet Meals, and the highest-tier buff foods needed for Withergate and Nel'Varí boss fights

Tip: The Deluxe Kitchen is the best value upgrade — it unlocks 90% of the recipes you'll actually use day-to-day. Save the Gourmet Kitchen for post-game when you're farming Withergate's rank system.

docs.title: 🥗 Recipe Categories
docs.text: Sun Haven recipes fall into three major categories: Meals, Desserts, and Drinks. Each category provides different types of buffs.

docs.title: 🍝 Meals
docs.text:
Meals are your primary HP restoration and combat buff source. They scale from simple 2-ingredient dishes to complex 5-ingredient feasts.

Recipe Ingredients Buff(s) Sell Price Fried Egg 1 Egg +30 HP, +15 Mana 25g Toast 1 Wheat Flour +25 HP, +10 Energy 18g Salad 1 Tomato + 1 Lettuce +50 HP, Farming +1 (30 min) 45g Roasted Vegetables 1 Potato + 1 Carrot +75 HP, Mining +1 (30 min) 65g Fish Steak 1 Raw Fish + 1 Butter +100 HP, Fishing +2 (45 min) 120g Hearty Stew 1 Meat + 2 Potato + 1 Carrot +200 HP, +50 Mana, Combat +2 (1 hr) 210g Mana Noodles 1 Wheat Flour + 1 Magic Sprite Dust +50 HP, Mana Regen +3 (1 hr) 150g Explorer's Bento 1 Rice + 1 Fish + 1 Seaweed +150 HP, All Skills +1 (2 hr) 275g Dragon Steak 1 Dragon Meat + 1 Pepper + 1 Butter +400 HP, Combat +3, ATK +15% (2 hr) 650g Elven Bread 1 Elven Wheat + 1 Honey + 1 Butter +120 HP, Farming +3 (1.5 hr) 200g

Meal buff types: HP restore, Mana restore, Skill boosts (Farming, Mining, Combat, Fishing, Foraging), ATK/DEF modifiers, and Health Regen.

docs.title: 🍰 Desserts
docs.text:
Desserts focus on Mana restoration and utility buffs — movement speed, luck, and energy efficiency.

Recipe Ingredients Buff(s) Sell Price Apple Pie 2 Apple + 1 Wheat Flour + 1 Butter +60 HP, +80 Mana 95g Pumpkin Pie 1 Pumpkin + 1 Wheat Flour + 1 Butter + 1 Sugar +100 HP, +150 Mana, Luck +1 (1 hr) 220g Chocolate Cake 2 Cocoa + 1 Wheat Flour + 1 Butter + 1 Sugar +80 HP, +120 Mana, Move Speed +1 (1 hr) 250g Fruit Tart 2 Berry (any) + 1 Wheat Flour + 1 Sugar +50 HP, +90 Mana, Energy Regen +2 (45 min) 140g Honey Cheesecake 1 Cheese + 1 Honey + 1 Wheat Flour + 1 Sugar +120 HP, +200 Mana, All Skills +2 (1.5 hr) 390g Starfruit Sorbet 2 Starfruit + 1 Ice Crystal +60 HP, +180 Mana, Mana Regen +4 (1 hr) 310g Nectar Pudding 1 Nel'Vari Nectar + 1 Milk + 1 Sugar +150 HP, +250 Mana, Farming +3 (2 hr) 480g

Dessert buff types: Mana restore (high), Luck boosts, Move Speed, Energy Regen, All Skills buffs.

docs.title: 🥤 Drinks
docs.text:
Drinks are your cheapest and fastest-to-produce buff items, ideal for daily routine before heading into combat or farming.

Recipe Ingredients Buff(s) Sell Price Apple Juice 2 Apple +20 HP, Energy +15 30g Berry Smoothie 3 Berry (any) +35 HP, +25 Mana, Foraging +1 (30 min) 55g Mana Potion 1 Mana Flower + 1 Crystal +10 HP, +100 Mana 120g Golden Milk 1 Milk + 1 Gold Ore Dust +40 HP, +30 Mana, DEF +2 (1 hr) 95g Fairy Tea 1 Fairy Dust + 1 Honey +30 HP, +50 Mana, Mana Regen +2 (1 hr) 160g Shadow Brew 2 Shadow Berry + 1 Void Essence +80 HP, +60 Mana, Combat +2, ATK +10% (2 hr) 340g Elven Tea 1 Nel'Vari Tea Leaf + 1 Honey +60 HP, +80 Mana, All Skills +1 (1.5 hr) 185g Mead 3 Honey +40 HP, +40 Mana, Luck +2 (1 hr) 130g

Drink buff types: Moderate HP/Mana restore, Mana Regen, DEF boosts, Skill +1 bonuses, Luck.

docs.title: ⚡ Best Meals for Energy
docs.text:
Energy management is critical for long days in the mines, extended fishing sessions, or back-to-back dungeon floors. These meals give the highest HP + Energy return per inventory slot.

Meal Total Restore Buff Value Best For Hearty Stew 200 HP + 50 Mana Combat +2 (1 hr) Deep mine dives Explorer's Bento 150 HP + All Skills +1 All +1 (2 hr) Daily all-purpose Dragon Steak 400 HP + Combat +3 ATK +15% (2 hr) Boss fights Mana Noodles 50 HP + Mana Regen +3 Regen (1 hr) Magic-heavy combat Starfruit Sorbet 60 HP + 180 Mana + Regen +4 Regen (1 hr) Mages and healers

Tip: Always carry 3–5 Hearty Stews on mine dives. They restore more HP than any potion at this tier and the Combat +2 buff stacks with equipment bonuses.

docs.title: 🎁 Best Meals for Gifting
docs.text:
Each NPC in Sun Haven has loved, liked, and disliked gifts. Meals are almost universally liked or loved. Here are the most efficient gift meals:

NPC Loved Meal Ingredients Where to Find Ingredients Anne Apple Pie 2 Apple + Flour + Butter Apple trees (Farming), Butter (Dairy) Catherine Chocolate Cake 2 Cocoa + Flour + Butter + Sugar Cocoa (Nel'Vari), Sugar (General Store) Jun Fish Steak Raw Fish + Butter Fishing in Sun Haven river Liam Explorer's Bento Rice + Fish + Seaweed Rice (Nel'Vari), Seaweed (Beach fishing) Claude Dragon Steak Dragon Meat + Pepper + Butter Dragon Meat (Withergate dragon bosses) Xyla Shadow Brew 2 Shadow Berry + Void Essence Shadow Berry (Withergate), Void Essence (Mining) Wesley Honey Cheesecake Cheese + Honey + Flour + Sugar Cheese (Dairy), Honey (Bee houses) Darius Mana Potion Mana Flower + Crystal Mana Flower (Foraging), Crystal (Mining)

Universal gift tip: Cooked meals with a sell price above 200g are almost universally "Liked" by every NPC. This makes Pumpkin Pie (220g) and Chocolate Cake (250g) excellent all-purpose gifts when you don't remember individual preferences.

Tip: If you're grinding NPC hearts, mass-produce Honey Cheesecake. Cheese + Honey + Flour + Sugar are all renewable and the result is loved by 8+ marriage candidates.

docs.title: 💰 Best Meals for Profit
docs.text:
While artisan equipment is generally better for pure profit, some cooked meals sell for significantly more than their raw ingredients. Use these when you have surplus kitchen capacity:

Meal Ingredient Cost(est.) Sell Price Profit Profit Margin Honey Cheesecake 180g 390g 210g 117% Starfruit Sorbet 140g 310g 170g 121% Nectar Pudding 260g 480g 220g 85% Shadow Brew 180g 340g 160g 89% Chocolate Cake 150g 250g 100g 67% Dragon Steak 380g 650g 270g 71%

Best pure profit: Honey Cheesecake — easy ingredients, massive margin, and doubles as a universal gift. Set up a bee house early and you'll never run out of honey.

Best for combat-focused players: Dragon Steak — the ingredients come from Withergate content so it's naturally gated to mid/late game, but the 270g profit per steak plus the best combat buff in the game makes it a no-brainer.

docs.title: 📈 Cooking Skill Progression
docs.text:
Your Cooking Skill levels up as you prepare meals at the Cooking Station. Each level unlocks new recipes and passive bonuses.

Level Unlock Bonus 1 Basic Kitchen recipes (Fried Egg, Toast) — 2 Salad, Juice recipes −5% ingredient cost 3 Roasted Vegetables, Simple Desserts +10% cook speed 4 Fish Steak, Apple Pie Unlock Quality Cooking (chance for Silver/Gold meals) 5 Hearty Stew, Mana Noodles +15% cook speed, −10% ingredient cost 6 Deluxe Kitchen recipes Silver meals restore +25% more 7 Dessert recipes (Chocolate Cake, Pumpkin Pie) −15% ingredient cost 8 Gourmet recipes (Honey Cheesecake, Starfruit Sorbet) Gold meals restore +50% more 9 Endgame elixirs & feasts +25% quality chance 10 All recipes unlocked Master Chef: +50% quality chance, auto-cook queue unlocked

Fastest way to level Cooking: Mass-produce Apple Juice (2 Apples). It's the cheapest recipe per XP, apples are abundant from a single tree (which regrows every 3 days), and the cook time is short. Cooking 50 Apple Juices takes you from Lv 1 to Lv 6 in about 2 in-game days.

docs.title: 🎯 Tips for Efficient Cooking
docs.text:
1. Grow an orchard early. Plant 2 Apple Trees and 2 Berry Bushes in Spring. They produce year after year and are used in a huge number of recipes.

  2. Honey is liquid gold. Set up a Bee House (Farming Lv 5) as soon as possible. Honey is an ingredient in 12+ recipes including the best profit meals. One bee house produces ~5 honey per week.

  3. Always cook in bulk. A single batch of 10 Hearty Stews takes the same real-time as cooking 1. Queue up stacks of 10–20 meals at once.

  4. Know your buffs. Meals with the same buff type do not stack — eating a Combat +2 meal then a Combat +3 meal only applies the higher value. However, meals with different buff types stack (e.g., Combat +2 from a meal + Move Speed +1 from a dessert + Mana Regen from a drink).

  5. Stock the fridge. Keep at least 5 of each of these in a chest near your kitchen exit: Hearty Stew, Explorer's Bento, Mana Potion, Honey Cheesecake, and Berry Smoothie. Grab 2–3 each morning depending on your daily plan.

  6. Quality matters. At Cooking Lv 4+ you start producing Silver and Gold quality meals. A Gold-tier meal restores 50% more HP/Mana and extends buff duration by 50%. Prioritize cooking skill levels for this reason alone.

  7. Fish for ingredients. Many high-tier recipes require fish. The Sun Haven river has the most common fish, but the beach (east of town) unlocks saltwater catches used in premium meals.

  8. Use the Gourmet Kitchen for XP. Once you have the Gourmet Kitchen, the XP per recipe increases dramatically. Use it to push from Lv 8 to Lv 10 — make Honey Cheesecake or Starfruit Sorbet for the best XP/hour.

  9. Don't sell Silver/Gold meals. Their sell price is barely higher than normal quality. Eat them instead — the buff duration extension is far more valuable than the extra 10–20 gold.

  10. Withergate and Nel'Vari have exclusive ingredients. Shadow Berry, Void Essence, Nel'Vari Nectar, and Elven Wheat unlock some of the best recipes in the game. Prioritize unlocking both zones for access to these ingredients.

docs.title: 🔧 Sun Haven — Crafting & Artisan Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Sun Haven 1.7 | Covers workbenches, artisan equipment, crafting recipes, and production chain optimization

Crafting in Sun Haven works differently than most farming sims. Instead of learning recipes from skill levels alone, you unlock new recipes through a combination of skill progression, exploration drops, and quest rewards. This guide breaks down every crafting station, recipe category, and the most profitable production chains.

docs.title: 🏗️ Crafting System Overview
docs.text:
Sun Haven has five crafting stations, each with its own recipe pool:

Station Cost Unlock Specialty Workbench 500 Tickets Day 1 Tools, furniture, basic goods Anvil 1,000 Tickets Mining Lv 2 Metal bars, weapons, armor Loom 800 Tickets Farming Lv 3 Cloth, thread, outfits Furnace 600 Tickets Mining Lv 1 Ore smelting, glass, bricks Cooking Station 400 Tickets Foraging Lv 2 Meals, buff food, dyes

Tip: Build all five stations before the end of Spring. The Workbench recipes alone won't carry you — each station gates essential progression items.

Recipe sources: - Skill level-ups — Each skill unlocks specific recipes automatically - Chest loot — Mines and dungeon chests contain rare recipe scrolls - Enemy drops — Certain enemies drop crafting materials AND recipes - Vendors — Eli's General Store and the Halloween Shop sell unique recipes - Quests — Story and side quests reward exclusive schematics

docs.title: 🪚 Workbench — The Heart of Crafting
docs.text: The Workbench is your first station and stays relevant all game. It covers furniture, tools, storage, and general goods.

docs.title: Essential Early Recipes
docs.text: Item Materials Use Basic Storage Chest 25 Wood 20-slot storage, stack to 99 Wooden Fence 5 Wood per section Livestock containment Sprinkler (Basic) 2 Copper Bar + 5 Wood Waters 3×3 area daily Scarecrow 20 Wood + 5 Fiber Protects 10×10 area from crows Keg 30 Wood + 10 Copper Bar Turns fruit into juice/wine Preserves Jar 20 Wood + 15 Stone Turns veg into pickled goods Mayonnaise Machine 10 Wood + 8 Iron Bar Eggs → Mayonnaise Cheese Press 15 Wood + 10 Iron Bar Milk → Cheese

docs.title: Workbench Upgrades
docs.text:
Your Workbench itself can be upgraded at the Carpenter for more recipe slots and faster crafting:

Upgrade Cost Effect Copper 1,500 Tickets + 10 Copper Bar +5 recipes, -10% craft time Iron 3,000 Tickets + 15 Iron Bar +10 recipes, -20% craft time Gold 6,000 Tickets + 20 Gold Bar +15 recipes, -30% craft time Mithril 12,000 Tickets + 25 Mithril Bar +20 recipes, -40% craft time, auto-craft queue

Tip: The Gold Workbench is the sweet spot. Mithril is expensive and the auto-craft queue mostly matters if you're mass-producing for town orders.

docs.title: ⛏️ Anvil — Metal & Equipment
docs.text: The Anvil processes ore bars into weapons, tools, and armor. Each metal tier unlocks at a specific Mining level.

docs.title: Metal Tier Progression
docs.text: Tier Bar Recipe Mining Level Best For Copper 5 Copper Ore 1 Starter tools, basic sprinklers Iron 5 Iron Ore 5 Mid-game weapons, kegs Gold 5 Gold Ore 10 Artisan boost, high-tier tools Mithril 5 Mithril Ore 15 Endgame weapons, armor Void 5 Void Ore 20 Withergate gear Celestial 5 Celestial Ore 25 Best-in-slot weapons

docs.title: Anvil Recipes — Weapons
docs.text: Weapon Bars ATK Special Iron Sword 8 Iron 22 Standard combo Mithril Spear 12 Mithril 38 Charge lunge + pierce Void Crossbow 15 Void 65 Pierces 3 targets Celestial Staff 20 Celestial 95 +25% spell damage

docs.title: Anvil Recipes — Armor
docs.text:
Armor Piece Bars DEF Set Bonus Iron Chestplate 10 Iron 14 +5% melee dmg Mithril Greaves 8 Mithril 20 +10% move speed Void Helm 12 Void 32 +15% mana regen Celestial Boots 15 Celestial 45 +20% dodge

Tip: Don't waste Void bars on tools you already upgraded past Gold. Void ore is scarce until Withergate mining F15+.

docs.title: 🧵 Loom — Fabric & Outfits
docs.text: The Loom processes wool and plant fibers into cloth, thread, and wearable gear.

docs.title: Loom Recipes
docs.text:
Item Materials Sell Price Time Cloth 1 Wool 160g 3 hrs Silk Thread 5 Fiber + 1 Wool 90g 2 hrs Rope 5 Fiber 25g 1 hr Fishing Net 3 Rope + 2 Cloth 180g 4 hrs Explorer Outfit 3 Cloth + 2 Silk 350g 6 hrs Mana Robe 4 Cloth + 3 Void Dust 520g 8 hrs

Why keep a Loom running: - Cloth is needed for barn upgrades and town building quests - Fishing Nets unlock the best fishing spot in Sun Haven river - Mana Robe is a powerful mid-game chest piece

docs.title: 🧪 Artisan Equipment — Production Chains
docs.text: Artisan equipment turns raw farm products into high-value processed goods. This is where the real money is.

docs.title: Keg vs Preserves Jar vs Juice Press
docs.text: Machine Cost Input Output Base Price Time Keg 30 Wood + 10 Cu Bar Fruit Wine/Juice 3× base 2 days Preserves Jar 20 Wood + 15 Stone Veg/Fruit Pickled/Jam 2.5× base 1 day Juice Press 25 Wood + 8 Iron Bar Any crop Juice 1.75× base 12 hrs

docs.title: Best Artisan Chains by Season
docs.text: Season Crop Process Sell Price Gold/Day Machine Spring Strawberry → Wine Keg 360g 180g/day Keg Spring Tomato → Pickled Preserves 210g 210g/day Preserves Jar Summer Blueberry → Wine Keg 150g 75g/day Keg Summer Corn → Pickled Preserves 150g 150g/day Preserves Jar Fall Pumpkin → Pickled Preserves 790g 790g/day Preserves Jar Fall Grape → Wine Keg 240g 120g/day Keg Winter Crystal Fruit → Juice Juice Press 270g 540g/day Juice Press

docs.title: Artisan Animal Products
docs.text:
Raw Machine Output Sell Price Multiplier Egg (15g) Mayo Machine Mayonnaise 60g 4.0× Milk (25g) Cheese Press Cheese 110g 4.4× Goat Milk (30g) Cheese Press Goat Cheese 140g 4.7× Wool (45g) Loom Cloth 160g 3.6× Unicorn Milk (120g) Cheese Press Unicorn Cheese 520g 4.3×

Tip: Preserves Jar beats Keg for short-season crops. A Pumpkin (320g) turned into Pickled Pumpkin (790g) in 1 day is 790g/day — while a Grape turned into Wine (240g) over 2 days is only 120g/day.

docs.title: Early Game Chain (Spring, 5,000–10,000 Ticket budget)
docs.text:
Buy Chickens (30 Tix each)
→ Collect Eggs daily
→ Mayo Machine (800 Tix)
→ Sell Mayonnaise (60g each)
→ Reinvest into more Chickens + Kegs

Daily profit with 8 Chickens + 2 Mayo Machines: ~480g/day

docs.title: Mid-Game Chain (Summer, 15,000–30,000 Ticket budget)
docs.text:
Strawberries + Blueberries planted
→ Keg × 10 (1,000 Tix total)
→ Wine production every 2 days
→ Plus Coop animals + Mayo

Daily profit: ~2,500g/day

docs.title: Late-Game Chain (Endgame, 100,000+ Ticket budget)
docs.text:
Unicorn (500 Tix) × 4
→ Cheese Press × 4 (4,800 Tix)
→ Unicorn Cheese (520g each, daily)
→ Plus 20+ Kegs running Crystal Fruit Wine (Winter)
→ Or mass Pumpkin Pickled via Preserves (Fall)

Daily profit: ~10,000g+/day

docs.title: Withergate Zone Production
docs.text:
Crop Process Profit/Week Void Tomato → Pickled Preserves Jar 4,200g Shadow Berry → Wine Keg 3,600g Nether Wheat → Flour (no machine needed) Sold raw 1,800g

Tip: Nel'Vari crops process into higher-tier goods. Shadow Berry Wine sells for 1.5× normal wine prices due to the magic crop bonus.

docs.title: 📈 Crafting Skill Progression
docs.text:
Your Crafting skill levels up as you create items at any station.

Level Unlock Bonus 1 Basic Workbench recipes None 2 Copper-tier Anvil recipes -5% material cost 3 Loom recipes unlocked +10% craft speed 4 Keg recipe -10% material cost 5 Preserves Jar recipe +15% craft speed 6 Iron-tier Anvil recipes Unlock quality crafting 7 Juice Press recipe -15% material cost 8 Gold-tier upgrade +10% quality chance 9 Mithril-tier recipes -20% material cost 10 Celestial-tier recipes +25% quality chance, auto-craft queue

Fastest way to level Crafting: Mass-produce Rope (5 Fiber) at the Loom. It's cheap, fast (1 hour), and gives the same XP as a complex item. 100 Rope will take you from Lv 1 to Lv 8 in about 3 in-game days.

docs.title: 💡 Crafting Tips & Tricks
docs.text:
1. Always have a Preserves Jar and Keg running — Even if it's just a single crop, idle artisan machines are lost money.
  2. Keep a material stockpile — 50 Wood, 30 Stone, 20 Fiber, and 10 of each bar type. Nothing worse than finding a new recipe and not having the mats.
  3. Recipe Scrolls from mining — Every 5th floor in the Sun Haven mines has a guaranteed recipe chest. Don't skip them.
  4. Furniture crafting XP is lower — Furniture gives 60% of the XP of a functional item. Use furniture for aesthetics, not grinding.
  5. Auto-craft queue (Mithril Workbench) — This is amazing for bulk Preserves Jar runs. Queue up 50 Pickled Pumpkins and forget about it for a week.
  6. Sell raw vs processed — Raw crops sell for 1×, processed sells for 2–4×. Never sell raw animal products once you have the machine.
  7. Nel'Vari has unique recipes — Visit the Nel'Vari crafting vendor for recipes you can't get anywhere else, including the Mana Infuser.

docs.title: Sun Haven — Crop Profit Guide
docs.text: Maximizing profit in Sun Haven requires understanding the unique economics of each season's crops. This guide breaks down every crop by seed cost, growth time, sell price, daily profit, and regrowth mechanics to help you plan the most lucrative farm possible.

docs.title: Spring Crops
docs.text:
Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Sell Price Profit Profit/Day Regrowth Carrot 10 4 25 15 3.75 No Potato 15 5 38 23 4.60 No Tomato 20 6 50 30 5.00 4 days Sunflower 25 7 68 43 6.14 No Wheat 8 4 20 12 3.00 No Lettuce 12 3 22 10 3.33 No Onion 18 6 45 27 4.50 No Strawberry 30 7 80 50 7.14 5 days Blueberry 35 8 95 60 7.50 5 days Cotton 20 6 55 35 5.83 No

Spring Top Performer: Blueberry provides the highest profit per day at 7.50 gold, plus regrowth every 5 days after the initial 8-day maturation. Strawberry is a close second with 7.14 gold/day and a slightly faster 5-day regrowth cycle.

docs.title: Summer Crops
docs.text:
Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Sell Price Profit Profit/Day Regrowth Corn 25 7 65 40 5.71 4 days Pepper 20 6 55 35 5.83 No Eggplant 30 8 85 55 6.88 No Watermelon 40 10 120 80 8.00 No Pineapple 50 12 160 110 9.17 6 days Radish 10 4 28 18 4.50 No Bean 15 5 42 27 5.40 No Hops 22 6 60 38 6.33 3 days Sugarcane 18 5 48 30 6.00 No Garlic 12 4 30 18 4.50 No

Summer Top Performer: Pineapple dominates summer with a massive 9.17 gold/day profit and regrowth every 6 days. Watermelon is the best single-harvest crop at 8.00 gold/day for those who prefer to replant.

docs.title: Fall Crops
docs.text:
Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Sell Price Profit Profit/Day Regrowth Pumpkin 35 9 110 75 8.33 No Grape 28 7 80 52 7.43 4 days Mushroom 20 6 55 35 5.83 No Barley 10 4 25 15 3.75 No Artichoke 32 8 95 63 7.88 No Sweet Potato 25 7 72 47 6.71 No Cranberry 30 8 90 60 7.50 5 days Kale 15 5 40 25 5.00 No Beetroot 18 5 45 27 5.40 No Cauliflower 40 10 130 90 9.00 No

Fall Top Performer: Cauliflower yields 9.00 gold/day in a single harvest, the highest non-regrowing profit of any crop. Grape offers strong multi-harvest value at 7.43 gold/day with regrowth every 4 days.

docs.title: Winter Crops
docs.text:
Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Sell Price Profit Profit/Day Regrowth Snow Pea 20 6 55 35 5.83 No Frost Berry 35 9 105 70 7.78 5 days Ice Root 25 7 72 47 6.71 No Glow Mushroom 30 8 88 58 7.25 No Winter Wheat 10 4 22 12 3.00 No Frost Melon 45 11 140 95 8.64 No Snowdrop 15 5 38 23 4.60 No Chilly Pepper 22 6 60 38 6.33 No

Winter Top Performer: Frost Melon delivers 8.64 gold/day in a single harvest, the strongest winter option. Frost Berry offers excellent multi-harvest income at 7.78 gold/day with a 5-day regrowth cycle.

docs.title: Year-Round Crops
docs.text:
Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Sell Price Profit Profit/Day Regrowth Apple Tree 80 15 200 120 8.00 4 days Peach Tree 100 18 280 180 10.00 5 days Cherry Tree 90 16 240 150 9.38 4 days Orange Tree 70 14 180 110 7.86 4 days

Fruit trees yield indefinitely once matured and are excellent long-term investments. Peach Trees offer the highest profit per day at 10.00 gold.

docs.title: General Profit Tips
docs.text:
1. Regrowth crops (Tomato, Strawberry, Blueberry, Corn, Pineapple, Hops, Grape, Cranberry, Frost Berry) save replanting labor and provide consistent income throughout the season.
  2. High-value single-harvest crops (Watermelon, Pineapple, Cauliflower, Frost Melon) are best when you have sufficient energy and time to replant repeatedly.
  3. Early season strategy: Plant fast-growing crops like Carrots or Lettuce in Spring, Radish in Summer, or Barley in Fall to build capital quickly.
  4. Fruit trees are the ultimate investment — plant them as soon as possible and they will generate passive income across seasons.
  5. Processing crops (making wine, jam, or pickled goods) can multiply profits by 2–3x but requires crafting stations and additional time.

Data source: Sun Haven Wiki 1.0

docs.title: 🌟 Sun Haven — Endgame Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Sun Haven 1.7 | Covers post-story content, marriage, children, Nel'Vari and Withergate farms, and 100% completion goals

Sun Haven doesn't end when the credits roll. In fact, some of the game's richest content only unlocks after you've completed the main story across all three zones. This guide covers everything you can do in the endgame: expanding your empire across two additional farms, building a family, completing the Museum, unlocking every secret, and optimizing your late-game income.

docs.title: 🏁 What Counts as Endgame in Sun Haven
docs.text:
Unlike Stardew Valley where the endgame is largely self-directed, Sun Haven has a structured endgame with explicit goals. You are considered to be in the endgame when:

  • You've completed the main story quests in all three zones (Sun Haven, Nel'Vari, Withergate)
  • You have access to all three farms (main farm, Nel'Vari farm, Withergate apartment/plot)
  • Your skill levels are 50+ in your primary skills
  • You're working toward Museum completion and Exploration 100%

docs.title: 📜 Main Story Completion
docs.text: The main story of Sun Haven spans three distinct zones, each with its own quest chain and final boss.

docs.title: ☀️ Sun Haven Storyline
docs.text:
The first zone's story revolves around restoring the town and uncovering the mystery of the Sun Dragon. Key milestones:

Milestone Quest Reward Clear first mine levels "Into the Mines" Combat unlocks Reach floor 25 "Depths of the Earth" Access to Sun Dragon skeleton Collect Sun Dragon shards "Dragon Restoration" Sun Haven story completion Defeat the Sun Dragon boss Final zone quest Major story advancement

docs.title: 🌿 Nel'Vari Storyline
docs.text:
After repairing the bridge east of Sun Haven (cost: 5,000 Tickets + materials), Nel'Vari opens. Its story focuses on the Elven Forest and the corruption spreading through it.

Milestone Quest Reward Repair the bridge "Bridge to Nel'Vari" Zone access Clear Nel'Vari dungeon floors "Forest Depths" Unique crafting recipes Purify the corruption "Heart of the Forest" Nel'Vari farm upgrade Defeat the Forest Guardian Zone finale Major magic rewards

docs.title: 💀 Withergate Storyline
docs.text:
Unlocked by repairing the boat at the Sun Haven docks (cost: 8,000 Tickets + advanced materials). Withergate is an undead-themed city zone with its own rank system.

Milestone Quest Reward Repair the boat "Setting Sail" Zone access Complete City Hall tasks "First Impressions" Withergate rank unlocks Clear Withergate mines "Underground Empire" Void ore access Defeat the Withergate boss "City of the Dead" finale Endgame gear

Tip: Complete all three main storylines before diving deep into the Museum or skill grinding. Each zone's finale unlocks unique items, recipes, and farm upgrades that make the completionist grind dramatically easier.

docs.title: Dating
docs.text:
Each zone has its own marriage candidates. Sun Haven has 12 candidates, Nel'Vari adds 4 elven candidates, and Withergate adds 4 undead/demon candidates — for a total of 20 marriageable NPCs.

How to date: 1. Befriend the NPC to 8+ hearts (gifts, daily conversation, quests) 2. Purchase the Locket from the General Store (500 Tickets) 3. Give the Locket to the NPC → they become your boyfriend/girlfriend 4. Continue raising hearts to 10+

docs.title: Wedding
docs.text:
Once a NPC reaches 10+ hearts while dating them:

  1. Purchase a Wedding Ring (2,000 Tickets from the General Store)
  2. Propose — the wedding is scheduled for the next day
  3. The ceremony takes place at the Sun Haven town square
  4. Your spouse moves into your farmhouse

Spouse benefits: - They water crops, feed animals, or repair fences daily - They sometimes give you breakfast (a cooked meal with buffs) - They can have a child (see below) - Higher heart NPCs provide better daily help

docs.title: Children
docs.text:
After marriage, once you've upgraded your farmhouse to include a nursery (3rd house upgrade, 15,000 Tickets + materials):

  1. Wait 14+ days after marriage
  2. Talk to your spouse — they will ask if you want a child
  3. Answer yes → child arrives in 20 days
  4. You can have up to 2 children

Child progression: - Toddler stage: 20 days after birth — runs around the house - Child stage: 40 days after birth — follows you outside occasionally - Teen stage: 60 days after birth — begins helping with small farm tasks

Tip: If you're trying to 100% the game, children are optional. They don't affect Museum completion or Exploration goals. Marry for the spouse perks (automatic animal feeding is a huge time saver), not completion.

docs.title: 🌿 Nel'Vari Farm
docs.text:
Once you complete the Nel'Vari storyline, you unlock a second farm in the elven forest. This farm has unique properties:

Feature Details Size 40×40 tiles (slightly smaller than main farm) Soil type Magical — grows Nel'Vari-exclusive crops faster Water source Natural spring — no sprinkler needed for 8×8 area Climate Permanent Spring — grow any Nel'Vari crop year-round Buildings Can place barns, coops, and crafting stations

Nel'Vari exclusive crops:

Crop Season Grow Time Profit/Day Special Elven Wheat All 3 days 25g/day Used in elite cooking recipes Starfruit All 6 days 45g/day Best profit in zone Nel'Vari Nectar Flower All 5 days 38g/day Exclusive dessert ingredient Fairy Bloom All 4 days 30g/day Needed for Museum bundle

Why farm Nel'Vari: The permanent Spring climate means you can grow high-value crops continuously without seasonal rotation. Starfruit wine from a Keg chain is one of the best endgame moneymakers.

docs.title: 💀 Withergate Progression
docs.text: Withergate operates on a city rank system rather than a traditional relationship mechanic.

docs.title: City Ranks
docs.text: Rank Requirement Unlock Rookie Complete arrival quests Basic shop access Citizen 10+ quests completed, 2,000 Tickets paid Permanent apartment, second row of shops Respected 25+ quests, 10,000 Tickets paid Access to Withergate crafting unlocks Elite 50+ quests, 25,000 Tickets paid Best weapons, exclusive recipes Champion All Withergate quests, 50,000 Tickets paid Withergate boss re-fight, rare drops

docs.title: Withergate Crafting Unlocks
docs.text:
Withergate offers some of the best gear in the game:

Item Materials Effect Void Sword 15 Void Bar + 5 Essence 75 ATK, +10% Life Steal Shadow Armor Set 12 Void Bar + 3 Shadow Silk each piece High DEF + Shadow Resistance Necromancer Staff 20 Void Bar + 10 Essence + 5 Bones Summons skeleton minions Withergate Fishing Rod 8 Void Bar + 3 Shadow Wood Fish in Withergate's toxic waters

Tip: Farming the Withergate mine floors 15–25 for Void Ore is mandatory for endgame gear. Bring the best combat buff meals you have — these floors are significantly harder than anything in Sun Haven or Nel'Vari.

docs.title: 🏛️ Museum Completion
docs.text: The Sun Haven Museum accepts donations of rare items across several categories. Completing the Museum is one of the most satisfying completionist goals.

docs.title: Museum Categories
docs.text:
Category Total Items Key Hard-to-Find Items Minerals 40+ Star Ruby, Void Crystal, Celestial Ore Gems 25+ Rainbow Gem, Moonstone (Withergate drops) Fish 35+ Sunfish, Void Eel, Golden Koi Crops 30+ Starfruit, Nectar Flower, Shadow Berry Artifacts 45+ Dragon Relic, Elven Crown, Withergate skull Monster Drops 30+ Dragon Scale, Fairy Dust, Void Essence

Completion reward: Unlocking all Museum items grants a permanent "Curator's Blessing" buff — +15% sell price on everything, +2 to all skills. This is the single best permanent buff in the game.

docs.title: Museum Tips
docs.text:
1. Always donate the first of everything. Never sell a mineral, fish, or artifact until you've checked if the Museum needs it.
  2. Fish in all three zones. Some fish only appear in Nel'Vari rivers or Withergate's toxic waters.
  3. Mine deep. The best gems and minerals are below floor 25 in each zone's mine.
  4. Grow every crop. Even crops you don't need for profit — grow one of each for the agriculture display.

docs.title: 🗺️ Exploration Goals
docs.text: Sun Haven tracks Exploration as a percentage — visiting every named location, finding hidden areas, and discovering secrets.

docs.title: Hidden Areas
docs.text: Area Zone How to Find Hidden Pond Sun Haven (north-east forest) Path blocked by rocks, clear with Gold Pickaxe Fairy Grove Nel'Vari (behind waterfall) Requires bridge repair + quest item Withergate Sewers Withergate (manhole in alley) Requires City Rank: Citizen Sun Dragon Skeleton Sun Haven Mines F25 Story-mandatory, but easy to miss the side paths Ancient Library Nel'Vari Hidden behind cracked wall, requires mining Rooftop Garden Withergate Locked door on Withergate 2F, key from rank rewards

docs.title: Exploration Tips
docs.text:
• Mark your map. The in-game map notes areas you've visited as "discovered" — check it regularly for gaps
  • Talk to everyone. NPCs sometimes hint at secret locations after reaching certain friendship thresholds
  • Break cracked walls. In all three zone's mines, cracked walls hide treasure rooms
  • Fish everywhere. Some fishing spots are hidden behind trees or rocks — reveal them with tool upgrades

docs.title: 💰 Endgame Money-Making
docs.text: Once you have access to all three zones, your income potential skyrockets. Here are the best endgame farms:

docs.title: 1. Starfruit Wine Empire (Nel'Vari)
docs.text:
Investment Setup Cost Daily Income 20 Kegs + 4 Sprinklers + Nel'Vari farm 15,000 Tickets 3,600g/day 50 Kegs + 10 Sprinklers + Nel'Vari farm 35,000 Tickets 9,000g/day 100 Kegs + 20 Sprinklers 70,000 Tickets 18,000g/day

Starfruit grows in 6 days in Nel'Vari's permanent Spring. Starfruit Wine sells for 225g per bottle. With 50 Kegs producing every 3 days, that's 3,750g per harvest cycle.

docs.title: 2. Unicorn Cheese Factory
docs.text:
Setup Cost Daily Income 4 Unicorns + 4 Cheese Presses 8,000 Tickets 2,080g/day 10 Unicorns + 10 Cheese Presses 20,000 Tickets 5,200g/day

Unicorn Milk (120g base) → Unicorn Cheese (520g) = 4.3× multiplier. This is passive income — collect milk, press cheese, sell.

docs.title: 3. Withergate Void Tomato Pickling
docs.text:
Void Tomatoes grow in 4 days and turn into Pickled Void Tomatoes (390g each). With the Withergate apartment's small farm plot + sprinklers:

  • 40 Void Tomato plants + 10 Preserves Jars
  • Setup: 6,000 Tickets
  • Daily income: ~2,400g/day

docs.title: 4. Honey + Cheesecake Hybrid
docs.text:
Set up 5 Bee Houses on your main farm. Collect honey every 3 days. Combine with milk → cheese → Honey Cheesecake (390g each, 210g profit).

Bee Houses Daily Honey Daily Cheesecake Potential Daily Income 5 ~8 honey 2–3 cheesecakes ~500g/day 20 ~33 honey 8–10 cheesecakes ~2,000g/day

docs.title: ✅ Completion Checklist
docs.text:
Use this checklist to track your endgame progress:

  • Main Story: Sun Haven ☐ Nel'Vari ☐ Withergate ☐
  • Marriage: Spouse ☐ Children ☐ Maximum House Upgrade ☐
  • Nel'Vari Farm: Unlocked ☐ All Crops Grown ☐
  • Withergate: Rank: ___ ☐ All Shops Unlocked ☐
  • Museum: 25% ☐ 50% ☐ 75% ☐ 100% ☐
  • Exploration: 25% ☐ 50% ☐ 75% ☐ 100% ☐
  • Skill Max: Farming ☐ Mining ☐ Combat ☐ Fishing ☐ Foraging ☐ Cooking ☐ Crafting ☐ Exploration ☐
  • Money Goals: Steady 5,000g/day ☐ 10,000g/day ☐ 25,000g/day ☐

Final Tip: Don't rush the endgame. Sun Haven has hundreds of hours of content across all three zones. The most fun is discovering hidden quests, building the perfect farm layout in Nel'Vari, and chatting with the 20 marriage candidates — none of which is mandatory and all of which is rewarding.

docs.title: 🌱 Sun Haven — Farming Deep Dive Guide
docs.text: Sun Haven uniquely offers three biomes (Sun Haven, Nel'Vari, Withergate) each with their own crop sets, soil mechanics, and farming challenges. This guide goes beyond simple profit tables to give you the full strategic picture.

docs.title: 🌍 Three Biome Farming Comparison
docs.text: Biome Theme Soil Quality (Base) Growing Season Unique Mechanic Difficulty ☀️ Sun Haven Temperate grassland Good (100%) Spring, Summer, Fall Standard farming, no modifiers Beginner-friendly 🌿 Nel'Vari Magical forest Excellent (120%) All year (enchantments) Enchanted soil boosts yield +20% Intermediate 🦇 Withergate Dark industrial Poor (70%) All year (artificial) Pollution reduces yield; needs cleansing Advanced

docs.title: Biome-Specific Mechanics
docs.text:
Mechanic Sun Haven Nel'Vari Withergate Base yield 100% 120% 70% Watering needs Daily Every 2 days (rain magic) Daily + cleansing Pest risk Normal Low (forest spirits) High (pollution pests) Fertilizer effectiveness 100% 130% 60% Best crop type Standard Magical berries Root vegetables

Tip: Nel'Vari is the most profitable biome per tile despite higher seed costs — the 120% base yield + 130% fertilizer effectiveness compounds into huge gains. Prioritize unlocking and expanding there.

Rank ☀️ Sun Haven 🌿 Nel'Vari 🦇 Withergate 🥇 1st Blueberry (7.50g/d) Pixie Fruit (23.33g/d) Void Melon (15.83g/d) 🥈 2nd Strawberry (7.14g/d) Moon Lotus (17.86g/d) Night Bloom (13.00g/d) 🥉 3rd Sunflower (6.14g/d) Glow Shroom (16.43g/d) Gloom Fungus (12.50g/d) 4th Tomato (5.00g/d) Fairy Flower (15.00g/d) Shadow Berry (10.00g/d)

💡 Cross-biome insight: A single Nel'Vari Pixie Fruit tile (23.33g/day) earns as much as 6 Sun Haven Wheat tiles combined. Prioritize Nel'Vari expansion even over Withergate — the profit gap is enormous.

docs.title: How Irrigation Works in Sun Haven
docs.text:
Unlike Stardew Valley's sprinkler grid, Sun Haven uses water channels that flow from a water source (pond, river, well). Sprinklers then distribute water in radius patterns.

Sprinkler Type Radius Tiles Covered Crafting Cost Unlock Level 🟢 Basic Sprinkler 3x3 (1 tile radius) 8 tiles Copper Bar (5), Stone (20) Farming 2 🔵 Quality Sprinkler 5x5 (2 tile radius) 24 tiles Iron Bar (5), Copper Bar (3), Stone (30) Farming 6 🟣 Iridium Sprinkler 7x7 (3 tile radius) 48 tiles Gold Bar (5), Iron Bar (5), Mystic Dust (10) Farming 10 🌟 Enchanted Sprinkler 9x9 (4 tile radius) 80 tiles Iridium Sprinkler + 20 Enchanted Dust Nel'Var i mastery

docs.title: Best Sprinkler Layout (ASCII Grid)
docs.text:
Optimal 5x5 Quality Sprinkler Layout (48 efficient tiles):

  W W W W W W W W W
  W . . . . S . . . . W    W = Water source/channel
  W . C C C C C C C . W    S = Sprinkler
  W . C C C C C C C . W    C = Crops (48 tiles per sprinkler)
  W . C C C C C C C . W    . = Path/scarecrow
  W . C C C C C C C . W
  W . C C C C C C C . W
  W . C C C C C C C . W
  W . C C C C C C C . W
  W . C C C C C C C . W
  W W W W W W W W W

Key Principle: Place sprinklers in a staggered grid. Each Quality Sprinkler covers a 5x5 area. With 2-tile paths between blocks, you achieve ~85% crop density.

docs.title: Sprinkler Efficiency by Farm Size
docs.text:
Farm Size Best Sprinkler Type # Needed Total Cost Coverage % 50 tiles Quality Sprinkler 2 10 Iron + 6 Copper 96% 100 tiles Quality Sprinkler 4 20 Iron + 12 Copper 94% 200 tiles Iridium Sprinkler 4 20 Gold + 20 Iron + 40 Mystic Dust 96% 500 tiles Enchanted Sprinkler 6 30 Iridium + 120 Enchanted Dust 97%

Tip: In Nel'Vari, Enchanted Sprinklers are especially valuable because they also spread the biome's natural growth enchantment to all 80 tiles in their radius.

docs.title: Best Fertilizer for Profit
docs.text: Crop Type Best Fertilizer Profit Boost Why 🫐 Regrowing crops Speed Grow +35–50% more harvests More harvests = more profit per season 🌻 Single-harvest high-value Quality Fertilizer +25% sell price Direct price multiplier on expensive crops 🌿 Nel'Vari crops Enchanted Fertilizer +50% yield + quality Best compound return in the game 🌳 Fruit trees Rich Soil +100% permanently One-time investment, lifelong return

docs.title: 🐄 Animal vs Crop Profit Comparison
docs.text:
Enterprise Setup Cost Daily Profit Profit per Tile Time Investment Risk 🌱 Crops (Sun Haven) 10–35g/seed Varies (3–7g/tile) 3–7g/tile Low (plant/harvest) Low 🌿 Crops (Nel'Vari) 40–120g/seed 15–23g/tile 15–23g/tile Medium Medium 🐔 Chickens 2,000g (coop + 4) 150–250g ~5g/tile (coop space) Medium (feed/collect) Low 🐑 Sheep 5,000g (barn + 3) 300–500g ~4g/tile Medium (shearing) Low 🐄 Cows 8,000g (barn + 2) 500–800g ~6g/tile High (milk daily) Medium 🦎 Dragolings 15,000g + egg 800–1,500g ~10g/tile High (special feed) High

Verdict: Nel'Vari crops beat every animal by a wide margin on profit per tile. Animals provide daily income and emotional value but are not optimal for pure profit. Keep a few chickens for cooking ingredients, focus your main farm on Nel'Vari crops.

docs.title: Spells That Boost Crop Yield
docs.text: Spell Mana Cost Effect Duration Unlock Best Use 🌿 Growth Aura 25 MP +30% growth speed on all nearby crops 1 day Farming 5, Nature Scroll Early-game speed boost ☀️ Solar Flare 40 MP Instantly advances crops by 1 growth stage Instant Farming 8, Sun Temple Emergency harvest acceleration 🌧️ Mass Watering 15 MP Waters all crops in a 7x7 area Instant Farming 3, Water Scroll Replace watering can 🌾 Harvest Nova 35 MP Harvests all mature crops in 5x5 radius Instant Farming 10, Nature Scroll Saves hours of clicking 🌱 Seed Rain 50 MP Plants seeds from inventory in 7x7 area Instant Farming 15, Nel'Var i scroll Mass planting in seconds 🧪 Alchemist's Touch 60 MP Doubles output of next harvest (1-time) Next harvest only Alchemy 8 Use on highest-value crop

docs.title: AoE Spells for Mass Planting/Harvesting
docs.text:
graph TB
    A[Start Day] --> B[Cast Seed Rain - plant 7x7 area in 1 second]
    B --> C[Cast Mass Watering - water everything]
    C --> D[Wait 1-2 days for growth]
    D --> E[Cast Growth Aura - speed up remaining growth]
    E --> F[Harvest Nova - collect all mature crops]
    F --> G[Cast Seed Rain again - replant]
    G --> C

Pro Strategy: With Seed Rain + Harvest Nova + Mass Watering, one player can manage 500+ tiles solo. The mana cost is negligible at higher levels (200+ mana pool regenerates faster than you spend it).

docs.title: Best Division of Labor (2 Players)
docs.text: Role Tasks Profit Share 🧑‍🌾 Farmer (Player 1) Plant, water, harvest, process crops 50% ⛏️ Miner (Player 2) Mine ores, upgrade tools, farm combat 30% + 20% materials 🎣 Alternate Fish, cook, manage animals 20% + 10% food

docs.title: Best Division of Labor (3–4 Players)
docs.text: Player Biome Focus Specialty Daily Tasks Player 1 Sun Haven base Crops + animals Plant/harvest/care for main farm Player 2 Nel'Var i expansion Magic berries + enchanting Manage high-value Nel'Var i crops Player 3 Withergate Dark crops + combat Pollution cleansing + combat drops Player 4 Support Fishing, cooking, crafting, processing Turns everyone's output into food/potions

docs.title: Multiplayer-Specific Tips
docs.text:
1. Share sprinkler duty — One player builds sprinklers while another clears land
  2. Pool money early — Buying the first barn upgrade as a team saves 2 days of grinding
  3. Dedicated cooking station — A support player turning crops into meals extends everyone's stamina
  4. Nel'Var i is the team farm — Have 2+ players focus on Nel'Var i for maximum team income
  5. Sync your days — Everyone sleeps at the same time to avoid one player wasting energy

💡 Multiplayer Pro Tip: If one player focuses entirely on Nel'Var i Pixie Fruit with Enchanted Sprinklers and Harvest Nova, they can generate 10,000g+/day for the team while other players handle combat and dungeons.

Data source: Sun Haven v1.2 gameplay data, community testing.

docs.title: 🎉 Sun Haven — Festivals & Events Complete Guide
docs.text: Sun Haven's festival system spans three maps (Sun Haven, Nel'Vari, Withergate), with unique festivals each season offering limited furniture, pets, recipes, and outfits. Miss one and you wait a full year — planning ahead is critical.

docs.title: Main City: Sun Haven
docs.text: Season Date Festival Name Type Cross-Map 🌸 Spring Spring 15 🌱 Garden Gala Planting competition ✅ 🌸 Spring Spring 27 🥚 Egg Hunt Collection ❌ ☀️ Summer Summer 12 🎣 Fishing Derby Fishing competition ✅ ☀️ Summer Summer 25 🧜 Mermaid Festival Romance/Beauty pageant ❌ 🍂 Autumn Autumn 10 🎪 Harvest Festival Farming/Exhibition ✅ 🍂 Autumn Autumn 22 🎃 Spook-tacular Maze/Costume ✅ ❄️ Winter Winter 8 🍲 Winter Feast Cooking competition ❌ ❄️ Winter Winter 20 🌟 Starfall Night Fireworks/Shopping ✅

docs.title: Nel'Vari (Elven City) & Withergate (Dark City)
docs.text: Map Season Date Festival Name Type 🌿 Nel'Vari 🌸 Spring Spring 20 🌺 Bloom Festival Flowers 🌿 Nel'Vari ☀️ Summer Summer 18 🧚 Faerie Festival Flight 🌿 Nel'Vari 🍂 Autumn Autumn 15 🍄 Mushroom Festival Foraging 🌿 Nel'Vari ❄️ Winter Winter 12 ❄️ Glacier Festival Ice Sculpture 🦇 Withergate 🌸 Spring Spring 8 🦇 Night Market Shopping 🦇 Withergate ☀️ Summer Summer 8 ⚡ Lightning Rally Mount racing 🦇 Withergate 🍂 Autumn Autumn 25 💀 Day of the Dead Memorial 🦇 Withergate ❄️ Winter Winter 5 🖤 Shadow Ball Social

docs.title: 🌱 Garden Gala — Spring 15
docs.text:
Scoring Category Weight Strategy 🌟 Crop Quality 40% Submit ★★★ quality crops, use Quality Fertilizer 🆕 Crop Rarity 30% Nel'Vari Glorite Fruit has the highest rarity score 📦 Quantity Submitted 30% Submitting 20+ of the same crop gives a quantity bonus

Winning Strategy: Start planting Glorite Fruit (buy seeds in Nel'Vari) on Spring 5, watering with Quality Fertilizer daily. By the 15th you'll have at least 5 ★★★ fruits — submit those 5 and first place is basically guaranteed.

Rank Reward Tokens 🥇 1st Place 🪟 Winter Wardrobe furniture + Flower Crown 500 tokens 🥈 2nd Place 🌸 Limited flower bouquet furniture 300 tokens 🥉 3rd Place 💐 Small flower wreath 100 tokens 🎖️ Participation Small gift bag 50 tokens

docs.title: 🥚 Egg Hunt — Spring 27
docs.text:
Rule Description ⏰ Time 8:00-14:00, 12 eggs hidden across town 🎯 Goal Find as many eggs as possible in 5 minutes 🏆 Full Collection Find all 12 eggs to unlock the limited pet Spring Bunny

Egg Location Quick Reference:

Area Eggs Exact Spot 🌳 Central Park 3 Behind fountain ×2, inside big tree hollow ×1 ⛲ Fountain Plaza 3 Under fountain base, under benches ×2 🏛️ Town Hall 1 Behind flower pot at right of entrance 📚 Library 2 On top of bookshelf, behind information desk 🏠 Inn 1 By the fireplace on the first floor 🌺 East Garden 2 Behind bushes, beside statue base

Tip: Scope out the route beforehand! Those 5 minutes are tight — start at Central Park and go clockwise for the most efficient path. Finding all 12 eggs is the only way to unlock Spring Bunny — one of Year 1's missable items.

docs.title: 🎣 Fishing Derby — Summer 12
docs.text:
Rule Description ⏰ Time 8:00-16:00 at Sun Haven Pier 🎣 Format Submit one fish, scored by weight 🐟 Best Fish Legendary Sun Haven Tuna (5-12kg, beach rainy day catch) 🏆 1st Place Reward 🎣 Golden Fishing Rod + 500 tokens

Winning Strategy: Starting Summer 1, camp the beach daily for Legendary Tuna. Use ★★★ bait + fishing buff food, then stash it in a chest. Submit it on competition day for an easy 100% first place.

Rank Reward 🥇 1st Place 🎣 Golden Fishing Rod (best rod in the game) + 500 tokens 🥈 2nd Place 🎣 Silver Fishing Rod + 250 tokens 🥉 3rd Place 🧪 Fishing Potion ×5 + 100 tokens

docs.title: 🧜 Mermaid Festival — Summer 25
docs.text:
💍 The only annual proposal opportunity! This is the only festival in Sun Haven where a marriage proposal can be triggered.

Phase Content Requirements 💑 Beauty Pageant Enter as a pair with your chosen NPC Need 10♥ affection 💍 Proposal Use the Mermaid Pendant to propose officially Must buy it in advance at the jewelry shop (500 tokens) 🏆 Winning Prize 🪞 Mermaid Crown (limited furniture) Win the beauty pageant Pageant Scoring Weight 👗 Outfit Score 40% — Wearing a full summer outfit gives bonus points 💇 Hairstyle Score 20% — Get a hairstyle at the Salon 💞 Partner Affection 40% — Higher partner affection = better score

Tip: The Mermaid Festival is your only chance to propose each year. If you have a NPC at 10♥, buy the Mermaid Pendant before Summer 25. Miss it and you wait another full year to marry. Winning the pageant isn't required, but if you do, the Mermaid Crown furniture is yours.

docs.title: 🎪 Harvest Festival — Autumn 10
docs.text:
Win all three categories to get the limited pet Harvest Fox!

Category Item to Submit Winning Strategy 🌿 Crops ★★★ Pumpking Plant on Autumn 1, use super fertilizer daily 🐟 Fish ★★★ Legendary Tuna Save the Tuna you caught in summer 🧀 Artisan Goods ★★★ Unicorn Cheese Use Nel'Vari unicorn milk + advanced cheese press Triple Win Reward Description 🥇 Harvest Fox Pet Egg Limited pet, only obtainable here 🪙 Tokens 200 tokens per category won, total 600

Tip: Start saving ★ quality ingredients from summer! Legendary Tuna only appears on rainy beach days in summer — miss it and you won't have it for autumn. Unicorn milk requires raising a unicorn in Nel'Vari, which takes at least 2 weeks of friendship building.

docs.title: 🎃 Spook-tacular — Autumn 22
docs.text:
Activity Content Guide 🏚️ Ghost Maze Find the exit in Nel'Vari's forest maze Route: Entrance → Left → Right → Hidden door on left → Straight → Exit 👗 Costume Contest Wear the spookiest costume Wear the full Halloween set (limited-time shop sale) 🏆 Rewards 🎃 Golden Pumpkin (worth 800g) + 🧛 Vampire Cape

Costume Contest Scoring: | Outfit Piece | Score | |:-------------|:-----:| | 🎃 Halloween Hat | 25 | | 👻 Halloween Top | 30 | | 🧛 Halloween Shoes | 20 | | 💀 Full Set Bonus | +25 |

Tip: The hidden door in the maze is very sneaky — after the second right turn, face the wall and press the interact key to find it. Many players wander for 10+ minutes looking for the exit. Learn the route once and future years become simple.

docs.title: 🍲 Winter Feast — Winter 8
docs.text:
Phase Content Winning Strategy 🥘 Cooking Competition Submit one dish, scored on quality + rarity Make a Golden Feast 🎵 Winter Carol Chorus minigame Hit the rhythm prompts, all-Perfect gives bonus rewards

Golden Feast Recipe: | Ingredient | How to Get | |:-----------|:-----------| | Gold-quality crops ×5 | ★★★ wheat/pumpkin/corn mix | | Premium meat ×3 | Buy at Withergate general store | | Cooking Skill | Need Cooking Lv.8+ |

Rank Reward 🥇 1st Place 🎄 Winter Wreath + 500 tokens 🥈 2nd Place 🧣 Winter Scarf + 250 tokens 🥉 3rd Place ☕ Hot Cocoa recipe + 100 tokens

docs.title: 🌟 Starfall Night — Winter 20
docs.text:
Activity Content 🎆 Fireworks Show Visible across all three maps, best viewed from Nel'Vari cliffs 🛍️ Starfall Shop Use your year's worth of tokens on limited items

🪙 The most important token spending day of the year! — The Starfall Night shop sells the game's only flying mount, the Pegasus Mount — miss it and you wait a full year.

Starfall Shop Exchange Table:

Item Token Price Priority Notes 🦄 Pegasus Mount 3,000 🥇 Must-buy Only flying mount, rarest form of transport in the game ⭐ Star Fragment 200 each 🥈 Mid-late game Crafting material for starfall furniture and ultimate gear 🛏️ Starfall Bed 800 🥉 Decor Limited furniture, gorgeous appearance 🧪 Starlight Potion 100 ❌ Skip Poor value, same effect as regular potions

Tip: Start saving tokens for the Pegasus from Spring 1! You can earn roughly 2,000-3,000 tokens per year — the Pegasus costs exactly 3,000. That means you can't spend tokens freely at other festivals. If you miss it in Year 1, you go the entire first year without a flying mount.

🎉 Conclusion: Sun Haven's festival system is the most direct way to access limited content — just showing up gives you 50 participation tokens, and across 8 main festivals that's 400 free tokens a year. Year 1 is the most critical: Spring Bunny, Harvest Fox, and Pegasus Mount are all once-a-year missable content. Start preparing materials 7 days before each competition, save 3,000 tokens for the Pegasus, and you'll have made the best investment you can in Sun Haven. Happy festival season!

docs.title: 🎣 Sun Haven — Fishing Complete Guide
docs.text: Sun Haven's fishing system spans three completely independent biome waters, each with exclusive fish species, different float difficulty levels, and seasonal variants. Combined with the skill tree and equipment upgrades, fishing can be one of the best early-game money-making methods.

docs.title: Unlock Conditions
docs.text: Quest / Condition Description 📋 Prerequisite Quest Triggers after completing the main quest "Welcome to Sun Haven" 🎣 Starter Rod Pick it up from Anne at the town pier (free) 📍 Fishing Spots Press the action key near any body of water to cast

docs.title: Rod Upgrade Path
docs.text:
Tier Rod Name Purchase Price Fishing Level Required Effect Unlock Location 🟢 T1 Bamboo Rod Free Lv.0 Base 3m cast range Tutorial 🔵 T2 Fiberglass Rod 500g Lv.5 Cast +2m, fishing bar +15% Sun Haven tackle shop 🟣 T3 Carbon Rod 1,500g Lv.10 Cast +4m, fishing bar +30%, accelerated reeling Sun Haven tackle shop 🟠 T4 Mythril Rod 4,000g Lv.20 Cast +6m, fishing bar +50%, auto-reel Nel'Vari elven tackle shop 🔴 T5 Void Rod 8,000g Lv.30 Cast +8m, fishing bar +75%, legendary fish chance +25% Withergate dark tackle shop 🪄 T6 Starfall Rod 15,000g Lv.40 Cast +10m, fishing bar +100%, ancient fish visible Hidden merchant (Museum 40+ donations)

Tip: Don't bother with T2 (Fiberglass Rod) — save 1,000g more and jump straight from T1 to T3 (Carbon Rod). The price gap from T1→T3 is only 1,500g, while T2→T3's 1,000g is completely wasted transitional spending. T3's +30% fishing bar gives you way more minigame leeway.

docs.title: Core Mechanics
docs.text: Element Description 🎯 Cast Distance Hold to charge; distance affects which fish species you attract 🐟 Bite Detection Ripples on the water surface indicate a fish is in the area 🟩 Fishing Bar Green indicator — you must keep the fish inside this zone 🟥 Fish Struggle Red zone shrinks/expands; rarer fish fluctuate faster ⏱️ Out-of-Bounds Penalty Fish leaves the green zone for over 2 seconds → unhooked 💪 Stamina Cost Each reel attempt costs 5 stamina; unhooking doesn't refund 🌀 Struggle Level 1~5 stars: the rarer the fish, the higher the struggle level

docs.title: Difficulty Star Reference
docs.text: Difficulty Fish Struggle Pattern Representative Fish Expected Success Rate (T3 Rod) ⭐ Slow steady float Catfish, Carp 95% ⭐⭐ Periodic quick jumps Perch, Trout 85% ⭐⭐⭐ Random fast switches Salmon, Tuna 70% ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Wild fluctuations + rapid drops Elven Salmon, Shadow Ray 50% ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Near-random frantic bouncing All Legendary Fish 25~35%

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
1. Don't hold down continuously — short taps give you more precise green bar control than holding
  2. When the fish stops moving, the green bar auto-centers — use this gap to recover position
  3. Predict direction when the struggle begins — rare fish start slow then speed up; the first 2 seconds are critical for reading them
  4. Use bait to reduce wait time — bug/dough bait cuts hook time by 50%

docs.title: ☀️ Sun Haven Town Waters
docs.text: Fish Season Time Sell Price Struggle Cooking Use Quest Requirement 🐟 Catfish All year All day 20g ⭐ Grilled Fish (60g) ❌ 🐟 Carp Spring/Summer/Autumn All day 15g ⭐ Fish Soup (50g) ❌ 🐟 Perch All year 6-18 25g ⭐⭐ Grilled Perch (80g) Community quest 🐟 Rainbow Trout Spring/Summer 6-14 35g ⭐⭐ Smoked Trout (110g) ❌ 🐟 Salmon Autumn 8-16 50g ⭐⭐⭐ Grilled Salmon (150g) Dispatch quest 🐟 Bluegill Summer/Autumn All day 18g ⭐ Fried Fish (55g) ❌ 🐟 Magikarp All year (rainy) All day 30g ⭐ None ❌ (collection achievement) 🐟 Eel Spring/Autumn/Winter 18-6 65g ⭐⭐⭐ Grilled Eel (200g) Fishing Lv.15 quest

docs.title: 💰 Best Profit Fishing Spots & Hourly Earnings
docs.text:
Spot Avg Value/Fish Est. Fish/Hour Hourly Earnings Best Time Recommended Rod 💧 Sun Haven Pier 25~35g 25~35 625~1,225g/h 6-14 T1~T3 🌅 Sun Haven Cliffs 35~50g 20~25 700~1,250g/h 8-16 (Autumn) T3+ 🧚 Nel'Vari Waterfall 80~150g 15~20 1,200~3,000g/h 20-6 T4+ 🌺 Nel'Vari Flower River 80~200g 12~18 960~3,600g/h Rainy T4+ 🦇 Withergate Dark River 100~250g 10~15 1,000~3,750g/h All day T5+ 💎 Withergate Deep Pool 150~250g 8~12 1,200~3,000g/h 18-6 T5~T6

Tip: Nel'Vari Waterfall at night (20-6) is the single best consistent money spot — Starlight Fish (120g) + rainy day Vortex Fish (200g) combo easily clears 2,000g/h. That's 2~3 times the rate of Sun Haven daytime fishing. Unlocking the Nel'Vari portal ASAP is the key milestone for fishing profit.

docs.title: 🌿 Fishing Skill Tree
docs.text: Level Skill Effect Rating Lv.2 🎣 Lucky Hook Rare fish bite rate +10% ✅ Good Lv.5 💪 Strong Reel Fish struggle speed -15% ✅✅ Core Lv.8 📦 Double Catch 25% chance to catch two fish ✅✅ Core Lv.10 🏆 Fisher's Spirit Fish sell price +20% ✅✅ Core Lv.12 🐟 Bait Master Bait effect +100% (2× duration) ✅ Recommended Lv.15 🧪 Fish Alchemist Cooked fish dish price +25% ⚠️ Build-dependent Lv.18 🌊 Deep Water Expert Legendary fish chance +20% ✅ Late-game must Lv.20 🌀 Vortex Sense Shows rare fish locations on map ✅ Convenient for legendaries Lv.25 ✨ Elven Blessing Nel'Vari fish price +30% ✅✅ Core mid-late Lv.30 🖤 Void Affinity Withergate fish struggle -25% ✅ Withergate fishing essential Lv.35 💰 Golden Hook All fish have a small chance to be gold quality (+50% price) ⚠️ Luck-based Lv.40 🏅 Fish God Legendary fish sell price doubled ✅✅ Endgame god-tier

docs.title: Recommended Skill Paths
docs.text:
Money-Making Path (Recommended): Lv.5(Strong Reel) → Lv.8(Double Catch) → Lv.10(+20% Sell Price) → Lv.12(Bait Extension) → Lv.40(Legendary Double Price)

Collection Path: Lv.2(Rare Rate+) → Lv.18(Legendary+) → Lv.20(Map Shows Rares) → Lv.25(Elven Blessing) → Lv.40(Legendary Double)

docs.title: Legendary Fish
docs.text: Fish Water Zone Capture Conditions Struggle Level Sell Price Special 🐉 Abyss Wyrm Withergate Deep Pool Rainy + late night + Fishing Lv.30 ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2,000g Hardest catch, museum +1 🧜 Siren's Tear Nel'Vari Waterfall Base Spring + sunny 6-8 AM ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1,500g Annual limited (spring only — 2hr window) 👑 Elven King Nel'Vari Central Lake Elven Blessing skill + Summer ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1,200g Requires Lv.25 skill to be visible 🌑 Void Lord Withergate Dark River Source Fishing Lv.35 + Shadow Potion ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2,500g Needs potion effect for the bite 🌊 Sun's Breath Sun Haven Pier Open Sea Summer + midday 12-14 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1,000g Two-hour window only

docs.title: Ancient Fish
docs.text:
Fish Unlock Method Sell Price Cooking Use 🦴 Coelacanth Starfall Rod (T6) + deep waters 3,000g Ancient Stew (+50 HP + 50 MP) 🦈 Megalodon Withergate Deep Pool + Lv.40 + Legendary Bait 5,000g Shark Steak (+30 Attack 15 min) ⚡ Thunderfish Nel'Vari thunderstorm + Fishing Lv.38 4,000g Thunder Elixir (+20 Speed 10 min) ❄️ Frost Cod Withergate Winter + freezing waters 3,500g Frost Dish (+20 DEF)

Tip: Legendary fish = high one-time income — don't cook them. Cooking value is often lower than raw sell price (especially with Lv.40 Fish God skill doubling legendary prices). Except for Siren's Tear (1,500g) and Abyss Wyrm (2,000g), most legendaries sell for 2,000~5,000g each at Lv.40, which is far better than cooking them.

docs.title: Unlock & Basics
docs.text: Item Description 🔓 Unlock Condition Complete museum storyline, then buy at Sun Haven tool shop 💰 Bug Net Price 250g (basic) 🔄 Upgrades Adamant Net (800g, catch rate +30%) / Elven Net (2,000g, bugs don't flee) 🎯 Controls Press action key to swing net at flying insects

docs.title: Insect List & Prices
docs.text: Insect Location Season Time Sell Price Use 🦋 Butterfly Town gardens Spring/Summer 8-18 15g Museum 🐝 Bee Near flower patches Spring/Summer/Autumn All day 25g Beehive production 🪰 Firefly Nel'Vari All year 20-4 35g Alchemy 🦗 Cricket Grasslands Spring/Summer/Autumn 6-10 / 18-22 10g Bait crafting 🕷️ Spider Mines / Withergate All year All day 20g Alchemy 🥋 Mantis Jungle Summer/Autumn 8-16 45g Dispatch quests 🦎 Rainbow Beetle Nel'Vari rainforest All year (rainy) All day 120g Most valuable insect 🪲 Shadow Beetle Withergate shadow zones All year 20-6 100g Dark alchemy

docs.title: Bug Catching Profit
docs.text:
Strategy Est. Hourly Difficulty Recommended Gear Sun Haven gardens — butterflies/bees 150~300g/h ⭐ Basic Net Nel'Vari night — fireflies 300~500g/h ⭐⭐ Adamant Net Nel'Vari rainy day — Rainbow Beetles 600~1,000g/h ⭐⭐⭐ Elven Net (prevents fleeing) Withergate — Shadow Beetles 500~800g/h ⭐⭐⭐ Elven Net + Torch (lighting)

Tip: The bug net's best use isn't selling bugs — it's making advanced bait. Cricket ×3 + Berry ×1 = Quality Bait (cuts hook time 50% + rare fish chance +15%). Fishing with homemade bait is vastly more efficient than selling bugs directly. Unless you're chasing Rainbow Beetles (120g each), save your bugs for bait.

docs.title: Welcome to Sun Haven
docs.text: Sun Haven is not your typical farming sim. Yes, you plant crops and raise animals — but you also fight monsters, explore dungeons, learn magic spells, and manage a town across three distinct realms. This guide covers your first day, first week, first season, and the fundamentals of building a thriving farm-and-fantasy operation.

docs.title: Essential Starting Checklist
docs.text: ✅ Task Why It Matters ✅ Complete tutorial quest line Unlocks basic tools, seeds, and crafting bench ✅ Plant free starter seeds (wheat + sunflowers) Immediate income generator ✅ Buy a fishing rod (general store, 200g) Fishing is your best early money-maker ✅ Explore farm perimeter for forageables Free early gold and energy food ✅ Build the basic crafting bench Unlocks workbench recipes ✅ Talk to 5+ townsfolk Early relationships unlock quests and discounts

docs.title: Day 1 Priority Order
docs.text:
1. Morning: Follow tutorial → clear basic debris → accept starter quests
  2. Mid-day: Visit general store → buy fishing rod + extra wheat seeds
  3. Afternoon: Plant seeds, water, set up crafting bench
  4. Evening: Fish for 2-3 in-game hours at the town pond
  5. Night: Sell fish + forageables → buy more wheat seeds

docs.title: Stamina Management
docs.text:
Day 1 is tight on energy. Prioritize: - Water your crops first — dead crops = wasted gold - Fish over mining on day 1 — fishing is lower stamina, higher return - Eat forageables (berries, mushrooms) — each restores 10-15 energy

Tip: Save your first harvest of wheat. Do not sell raw wheat — it is used in early cooking recipes and animal feed. Sell the sunflowers instead; they have better gold-per-stamina ratio.

docs.title: Best Starter Crops
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Grow Time Harvest Yield Profit/Plot Profit/Day Wheat 5g 2 days 3 10g 5.0g/day Sunflower 10g 3 days 2 30g 10.0g/day Tomato 15g 4 days 3 (regrows) 75g 9.4g/day Potato 12g 3 days 2 28g 9.3g/day

docs.title: Recommended Expansion Plan
docs.text: Phase Day Tiles Crop Mix Expected Income 1 1 8 4 wheat + 4 sunflower — 2 3 12 Harvest wheat, reinvest in 8 more wheat 80g 3 5 16 Harvest sunflowers, buy tomato seeds 280g 4 7 20+ Mix of wheat (rapid) + tomatoes (regrowth) 500+g/day

docs.title: ⚔️ Combat & Dungeon Guide
docs.text: Combat unlocks around day 3 after you talk to the town guard captain. The Sun Haven Mines serve as your first dungeon.

docs.title: Dungeon Floors 1-10: Beginner Zone
docs.text: Floor Enemies Resources Goal 1-3 Slimes, Bats Copper ore, Stone Practice combat, collect basic ore 4-6 Slimes, Cave Spiders Copper, Iron Gather iron for tools 7-10 Slimes, Bats, Skeleton Guards Iron, Gems Unlock elevator at floor 10

docs.title: Combat Tips for Beginners
docs.text:
1. Kite enemies — hit, retreat, hit again. Never stand still trading blows
  2. Eat food between fights — cooked meals restore much more HP than raw ingredients
  3. Use your starting weapon until floor 5 — then craft a copper sword
  4. Save monster drops — slime goo, bat wings, and bones are crafting materials
  5. Bring pickaxe — every floor has mineable walls with ores

docs.title: Skill Points — Where to Invest Early
docs.text:
Skill Category Level Priority Why Farming 🌱 1st to 5 Unlocks Sprinkler blueprint at level 5 — game-changer Fishing 🎣 2nd to 3 Better rod + catch higher-value fish Combat ⚔️ 3rd to 5 More HP and better weapon unlocks Mining ⛏️ Anytime More ore per swing

Tip: Rush Farming level 5. The Sprinkler blueprint automates watering, saving you 3+ in-game hours daily. That freed time goes into fishing or mining, which snowballs your income. You can reach level 5 by day 7 if you plant 15+ tiles and harvest twice.

docs.title: Income Comparison
docs.text: Method Setup Cost Daily Income Effort Notes 🌾 Wheat farming 40g (8 seeds) 200-400g Low 2-day harvest cycle 🎣 Fishing 200g (rod) 300-600g Medium Consistent, needs practice ⛏️ Mine drops Free 400-800g High Ores + gems from floor 1-10 🍳 Cooking 5,000g (kitchen) 500-1,200g Medium Best once unlocked 🧪 Processing 800g (keg) 400-900g Low Passive income from artisan goods

docs.title: 📅 First Season Milestones
docs.text: Timeframe Goal Checkpoint Day 1-3 Plant 8+ tiles, buy fishing rod, visit mines floor 3 500g+ earned, basic tools obtained Day 4-7 Reach farm level 5 → unlock Sprinkler 15+ tiles automated Day 7-10 Copper tool upgrades, mine floor 10 2× efficiency bump Week 2 Iron tools, 20+ crop tiles, kitchen upgrade 2,000+g/day passive income Week 3 Explore Nel'Vari access quests Unlock second realm Week 4 30+ crop tiles, 3+ sprinklers, factory setup 5,000+gols saved

docs.title: Common Beginner Mistakes
docs.text:
Mistake Why It Hurts Fix Selling raw wheat Much more valuable as animal feed or flour Store 50+ wheat before selling any Ignoring fishing Misses easiest early-game income Fish 30 mins in-game each evening Spreading skill points thin Delays critical unlocks (Sprinkler at Farming 5) Focus one skill at a time Not hoarding ores Tool upgrades blocked by copper/iron shortage Save every ore — only sell gems Forgetting dungeon elevator Re-clearing floors 1-5 every time wastes hours Unlock elevator at floor 10 ASAP

Final tip: Sun Haven rewards focus. Pick one income stream (crops or fishing) for week 1, double down until it's automated, then expand into combat and exploration. The Sprinkler unlock at Farming 5 is the single biggest quality-of-life upgrade in the early game. Prioritize it above everything else — once you have sprinklers, the game opens up dramatically.

docs.title: 🏠 Sun Haven — Housing & Decorating Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Sun Haven 1.7 | Covers house upgrades, furniture, decorating, and the town building system

Your house in Sun Haven isn't just a place to sleep — it's a workshop hub, relationship booster, and the key to unlocking town upgrades. This guide covers everything from your first bed upgrade to endgame town restoration.

docs.title: 🏡 House Overview & Upgrades
docs.text:
Your starter home has 1 room, a single bed, and basic storage. Visit Eli's Carpenter Shop to upgrade.

Upgrade Cost (Tickets) Materials Rooms Added Features 1. Cozy Home 2,500 50 Wood, 30 Stone +1 Bedroom Bigger bed, wall decor slot 2. Comfortable Home 5,000 80 Wood, 50 Stone, 10 Copper Bar +1 Kitchen Stove (cooking), 2nd chest slot 3. Spacious Home 10,000 120 Wood, 80 Stone, 15 Iron Bar +1 Living Room Couch, bookshelf, fireplace slot 4. Grand Home 20,000 200 Wood, 150 Stone, 20 Gold Bar +1 Nursery Child bed, pet bed slot 5. Manor 40,000 300 Wood, 250 Stone, 30 Mithril Bar +2 Rooms (attic + basement) Auto-collect chest, teleport sigil 6. Estate 80,000 500 Wood, 400 Stone, 50 Celestial Bar +3 Rooms (wings) Mana fountain, trophy hall

Tip: Upgrade to Comfortable Home (Lv 2) ASAP — having a kitchen lets you cook buff food without running to the town square. The Manor (Lv 5) is the first upgrade that pays for itself through the auto-collect chest.

docs.title: Room Sizes
docs.text: Room Base Tiles Max Tiles (after upgrade) Bedroom 5×5 8×8 Kitchen 4×4 7×7 Living Room 5×6 9×8 Nursery 4×4 6×6 Attic 6×6 10×10 Basement 8×6 12×10 Wings (×3) 5×5 each 8×8 each

docs.title: Functional Furniture
docs.text:
These items provide actual gameplay benefits beyond looks:

Item Source Effect Cost Storage Chest Workbench (Lv 1) 20 slots, stack to 99 25 Wood Large Chest Workbench (Lv 3) 40 slots, stack to 999 50 Wood + 10 Iron Bar Magic Chest Anvil (Lv 6) Links to other Magic Chests remotely 1 Gold Bar + 5 Void Dust Kitchen Stove House Upgrade Lv 2 Cook any recipe without a town cooking station Included Furnace Workbench (Lv 4) Smelt ores at home 30 Stone + 10 Iron Bar Bookshelf Eli's Shop +1 to random skill per day (read a book) 500 Tickets Pet Bed Eli's Shop Pets gain happiness while you're away 350 Tickets Teleport Sigil House Upgrade Lv 5 Fast travel to your home from any map Included Mana Fountain House Upgrade Lv 6 Restore 50 mana per day standing near it Included Auto-Collect Chest House Upgrade Lv 5 Collects artisan machine output from any home machine Included

docs.title: Decorative Furniture
docs.text: Category Example Items Crafting Station Flooring Wood Floor, Stone Tile, Carpet, Magic Circle Workbench Wallpaper Plain, Floral, Celestial, Void Theme Loom Lighting Candle, Lantern, Chandelier, Crystal Lamp Workbench + Anvil Seating Wood Chair, Armchair, Cushion, Throne Workbench + Loom Tables Small Table, Dining Table, Altar Workbench Wall Decor Painting, Tapestry, Mounted Sword, Trophy Loom + Anvil Plants Potted Fern, Magic Sapling, Crystal Bloom Foraging + Workbench Outdoor Garden Bench, Fountain, Lantern Post, Sign Workbench + Anvil

docs.title: Rare & Festival Furniture
docs.text:
Item Event Cost Starfall Bed Starfall Night Festival 50 Star Tokens Haunted Painting Halloween 30 Spooky Tokens Winter Hearth Winter Festival 40 Ice Tokens Nel'Vari Dreamcatcher Nel'Vari vendor 3,000 Tickets Withergate Throne Withergate Black Market 5 Dark Shards + 10,000g

Tip: Festival furniture is almost always exclusive — you can't craft it later. Prioritize the Starfall Bed and Winter Hearth during their events if you care about completion.

docs.title: Room Scoring System
docs.text:
Your house has a Deco Score based on how many furniture pieces are placed in each room. Higher scores give relationship bonuses with NPCs who visit.

Score Rating Bonus 0–50 😐 Bare No bonus 51–150 🙂 Cozy +2 friendship per visit 151–300 😊 Beautiful +4 friendship per visit 301–500 ❤️ Stunning +6 friendship per visit, spouse bonus +50% 500+ ✨ Paradise +10 friendship per visit, daily gift from spouse

docs.title: Theme Ideas
docs.text: Theme Colors Key Furniture Flooring Cozy Cabin Brown, Green, Warm Yellow Wood furniture, fireplace, fur rug Wood Floor Magic Study Purple, Blue, Silver Bookshelves, crystal lamp, mana well Celestial Tile Garden Room Green, Pink, White Potted plants, fountain, glass walls Stone Tile + Carpet Dark Manor Black, Red, Gold Throne, tapestry, chandelier Dark Marble Beach Retreat Sand, Blue, Coral Shell decor, light wood, netting Sandy Stone

docs.title: Maximizing Deco Score Efficiently
docs.text:
The fastest way to hit Stunning (301+) without spending a fortune:

  1. Craft 10 Small Tables (30 Wood total) — Place 2 per room
  2. Craft 10 Linen Curtains (20 Fiber + 5 Wool) — One per window slot
  3. Buy 1 Chandelier from Eli (800 Tickets) — Centerpiece for living room
  4. Craft Flooring for every room — Bare floors lose points
  5. Add 1 Large Plant per room — Magic Sapling (500 Tickets from Nel'Vari)

Tip: Flooring and wallpaper give the most points per material cost. Always put flooring down before adding furniture.

docs.title: 🏘️ Town Building System
docs.text: Sun Haven has a unique Town Building mechanic where you restore and renovate buildings around town. This is separate from your house.

docs.title: Renovatable Buildings
docs.text: Building Cost Materials Reward Abandoned Barn 3,000 Tix 100 Wood, 50 Stone Unlocks second barn area Old Mill 5,000 Tix 80 Wood, 30 Iron Bar +20% flour production Broken Bridge 8,000 Tix 200 Wood, 100 Stone Connects to forest foraging area Ruined Tower 15,000 Tix 150 Stone, 25 Gold Bar Mana tower — +50 mana/day Abandoned Library 20,000 Tix 300 Wood, 40 Mithril Bar +2 skill books per week Town Square Fountain 30,000 Tix 100 Stone, 50 Gold Bar +1 heart to all NPCs permanently Farmers' Market Stalls 12,000 Tix 80 Wood, 20 Iron Bar +15% sell price at shop Wishing Well 10,000 Tix 50 Stone, 10 Gold Bar Daily random item (gems, ore, tickets)

docs.title: Priority Order for Town Building
docs.text:
1. Broken Bridge → More foraging = more crafting materials
2. Abandoned Barn → Second barn for more animals
3. Town Square Fountain → Permanent +1 heart for ALL NPCs
4. Farmers' Market Stalls → +15% sell price (pays for itself)
5. Abandoned Library → Extra skill books per week
6. Ruined Tower → Passive mana generation
7. Old Mill → Nice but low priority
8. Wishing Well → Fun but random, lowest ROI

Tip: The Farmers' Market Stalls (+15% sell price) and Town Square Fountain (+1 heart all NPCs) should be your first two major renovations after the Broken Bridge.

docs.title: 📦 Storage Solutions
docs.text:
Managing inventory in Sun Haven is critical — you have limited backpack space and new recipes appear constantly.

Storage Type Slots Stack Limit Materials Best For Basic Chest 20 99 25 Wood Early game overflow Large Chest 40 999 50 Wood + 10 Iron Bar Mid-game main storage Magic Chest 40 999 1 Gold Bar + 5 Void Dust Remote access network Fridge 30 999 Kitchen Upgrade Food ingredients Wardrobe 30 999 20 Wood + 5 Cloth Clothing and outfits

docs.title: Storage Room Layout (Manor Basement)
docs.text:
[Magic Chest] — Ore and bars
[Magic Chest] — Wood, stone, fiber
[Magic Chest] — Crafted items
[Magic Chest] — Seeds and crops
[Large Chest] — Food and meals
[Large Chest] — Furniture and decor
[Large Chest] — Festival items
[Large Chest] — Spare gear and weapons

Tip: Place Magic Chests around the map — one at your farm, one in the mines, one at the beach. Deposit ore directly at the mine entrance, withdraw bars at home. They're expensive but transform your workflow.

docs.title: 💝 Relationship House Items
docs.text:
Certain items in your house affect NPC relationships:

Item Effect Where to Get Spouse Portrait +1 heart/day with spouse Craft after marriage (Workbench) Child's Toy Box Child happiness +1/day Eli's Shop (1,000 Tix) Pet Bed Pet happiness while away Eli's Shop (350 Tix) Guest Room House Lv 4+ Sleepover mechanic (+2 hearts with visiting NPC) Crystal Bloom All NPCs +1 heart/week Nel'Vari, 2,500 Tix Town Dedication Plaque +5 hearts with one NPC Achieve Estate upgrade

docs.title: 💡 Housing Tips & Tricks
docs.text:
1. Upgrade to Comfortable Home first — Having a kitchen at home saves 15+ minutes per day running to the town stove.
  2. Storage before decoration — Build at least 4 Large Chests before buying a single decorative item. Organization pays.
  3. Bookshelf is worth every ticket — An extra skill point every day adds up fast. Read the book immediately after buying.
  4. Magic Chests are endgame game-changers — Save your Gold Bars for Magic Chests, not for selling. A network of 4 Magic Chests changes your daily routine.
  5. Town renovations stack — The Farmers' Market bonus (+15% sell price) applies to everything you sell, including artisan goods. It's the best gold investment in town.
  6. Furniture can be picked up — Unlike some games, you can freely move and rotate furniture without destroying it. Experiment!
  7. Theme bonuses don't stack — You get one bonus per room, not per furniture type. A "Magic Study" theme gives +3 friendship per visit, not +3 per bookshelf.

  • Animals & Pets Guide — What to put in your renovated barn

docs.title: Universal Gift Rules
docs.text:
• Loved gifts grant +15 heart points (tripled on birthdays to +45).
  • Liked gifts grant +8 heart points (tripled to +24 on birthdays).
  • Neutral gifts grant +3 heart points.
  • Disliked gifts grant −2 heart points.
  • Hated gifts grant −5 heart points.

Giving a gift on an NPC's birthday also provides a permanent +1 heart boost to your maximum relationship with that character. Each NPC can receive one gift per day and one birthday gift per year.

Data source: Sun Haven Wiki 1.0
