docs.title: 📅 Sun Haven — Seasonal Strategy Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Sun Haven 1.7 | Season-by-season breakdown for all three zones

Sun Haven runs on a four-season calendar similar to Stardew Valley, but Winters here aren't a dead zone — there's always something profitable to do. This guide covers each season in detail, plus the unique zones Nel'Vari and Withergate.

docs.title: 🌍 Season System Overview
docs.text:
Each season lasts 28 days (4 weeks of 7 days). Crops are season-specific unless grown in Nel'Vari or on your farm with season-extending magic.

Season Weather Temp Effect Special Event 🌸 Spring Rain 40% of days Perfect for crops Spring Festival (Day 15) ☀️ Summer Sun 70%, Storms 15% Crops grow +10% faster Summer Solstice (Day 21) 🍂 Fall Wind 30%, Rain 30% Foraging yields +1 per bush Harvest Festival (Day 14) ❄️ Winter Snow 60%, Clear 20% Crops on farm grow −25% slower Starfall Night (Day 25)

Tip: Winter's crop penalty only applies to outdoor farm tiles. Your greenhouse, Nel'Vari farm, and indoor plots are unaffected. Plan accordingly.

docs.title: 🌸 Spring — The Fresh Start
docs.text: Spring is all about building your foundation. Your farm is clearing out, tool upgrades are starting, and every ticket matters.

docs.title: Best Spring Crops
docs.text:
Crop Cost (per seed) Growth Regrowth Sell Price Gold/Day Strawberry 80g 8 days Every 4 days 120g 15g/day (raw) Tomato 50g 6 days Every 3 days 80g 13.3g/day (raw) Potato 40g 6 days No 100g 10g/day (raw) Green Bean 30g 10 days Every 3 days 60g 5.5g/day (raw) Carrot 20g 5 days No 45g 5g/day (raw)

Processed Spring Profits:

Crop → Product Machine Sell Price Gold/Day Strawberry → Wine Keg 360g 180g/day Tomato → Pickled Preserves Jar 210g 210g/day Potato → Juice Juice Press 175g 175g/day

Tip: Strawberry is the king of Spring if you have Kegs. With 20 Strawberry plants + 5 Kegs you'll make 3,600g every 4 days from wine alone.

docs.title: Spring Priorities
docs.text:
1. Day 1–5: Clear farm debris, plant 30 Carrots for quick cash, build Workbench
  2. Day 5–10: Upgrade Hoe to Copper, plant Strawberries, build a Keg
  3. Day 10–15: Save tickets, buy a Chicken and Mayo Machine
  4. Day 15: Spring Festival — buy Starfall seeds if available
  5. Day 15–20: Expand crop area to 50+ tiles, build second Keg
  6. Day 20–28: Focus on Mining — reach floor 15 for Copper/Iron ores

docs.title: Spring Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Clear all farm debris (rocks, weeds, logs)
  • [ ] Plant 30+ Carrots for early cash
  • [ ] Build Workbench, Furnace, and Anvil
  • [ ] Transition to Strawberries by Day 8
  • [ ] Buy 1 Chicken + Mayo Machine
  • [ ] Upgrade Pickaxe to Copper
  • [ ] Attend Spring Festival (Day 15)
  • [ ] Reach mine floor 15

docs.title: ☀️ Summer — The Growth Season
docs.text: Summer's +10% growth speed makes it the most productive farming season. Crops mature faster and storms provide free watering.

docs.title: Best Summer Crops
docs.text:
Crop Cost Growth Regrowth Sell Price Gold/Day Blueberry 60g 8 days Every 4 days 100g 12.5g/day (raw) Corn 50g 10 days Every 3 days 90g 9g/day (raw) Melon 100g 10 days No 250g 15g/day (raw) Hot Pepper 40g 8 days Every 3 days 70g 8.8g/day (raw) Sunflower 30g 6 days No 80g 8.3g/day (raw)

Processed Summer Profits:

Crop → Product Machine Sell Price Gold/Day Blueberry → Wine Keg 300g 150g/day Corn → Pickled Preserves Jar 225g 225g/day Melon → Juice Juice Press 438g 438g/day

Tip: Melon Juice is the highest gold-per-day crop in Summer. One Juice Press processing a melon every 12 hours makes 438g/day — better than Kegging Starfruit in some other games.

docs.title: Summer Priorities
docs.text:
1. Day 1–5: Plant 20+ Blueberries, 10 Melons, build 3 Kegs
  2. Day 5–10: Upgrade Barn to Lv 2 (auto-feed), buy a second animal
  3. Day 10–15: Build Cheese Press if you have a Cow/Goat
  4. Day 15–20: Expand to 60+ crop tiles, build 3 Preserves Jars
  5. Day 21: Summer Solstice festival
  6. Day 21–28: Mining focus — aim for floor 25+, stockpile Iron

docs.title: Summer Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Plant 20+ Blueberries and 10 Melons
  • [ ] Build 3+ Kegs and 2+ Preserves Jars
  • [ ] Upgrade Barn to Lv 2 (auto-feed)
  • [ ] Buy at least 1 Cow for steady milk → cheese
  • [ ] Stockpile Wood and Stone for Fall preparations
  • [ ] Attend Summer Solstice
  • [ ] Reach mine floor 25

docs.title: 🍂 Fall — The Harvest Season
docs.text: Fall brings the highest-value single crops — Pumpkins are the standout. This is also the best time to push deep into the mines before Winter.

docs.title: Best Fall Crops
docs.text:
Crop Cost Growth Regrowth Sell Price Gold/Day Pumpkin 320g 12 days No 790g 39g/day (raw) Grape 80g 10 days Every 4 days 120g 12g/day (raw) Sweet Potato 60g 8 days Every 3 days 90g 11.3g/day (raw) Eggplant 50g 7 days Every 5 days 85g 8.5g/day (raw) Amaranth 40g 6 days No 95g 9.2g/day (raw)

Processed Fall Profits:

Crop → Product Machine Sell Price Gold/Day Pumpkin → Pickled Preserves Jar 1,580g 1,580g/day Grape → Wine Keg 360g 180g/day Sweet Potato → Juice Juice Press 158g 158g/day

Pumpkin is the single most profitable crop in Sun Haven. A single Preserves Jar processing one Pumpkin per day makes 1,580g/day. With 10 jars, that's 15,800g/day.

docs.title: Fall Priorities
docs.text:
1. Day 1–5: Plant 10+ Pumpkins, build 5 Preserves Jars
  2. Day 5–10: Plant Grapes for Kegging, build 5 Kegs
  3. Day 10–14: Stockpile Wood heavily — you'll need it for Winter prep
  4. Day 14: Harvest Festival — buy exclusive recipes
  5. Day 15–20: Push to mine floor 35+, collect Gold Ore
  6. Day 20–28: Final crop harvest, convert everything to artisan goods

docs.title: Fall Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Plant 10+ Pumpkins on Day 1
  • [ ] Build 5+ Preserves Jars
  • [ ] Plant Grapes for Keg processing
  • [ ] Attend Harvest Festival
  • [ ] Stockpile 500+ Wood for Winter
  • [ ] Upgrade Pickaxe to Gold
  • [ ] Reach mine floor 35+
  • [ ] Save at least 15,000 Tickets for Winter investments

docs.title: ❄️ Winter — The Mining Season
docs.text: Winter penalizes outdoor crops (−25% growth), so focus shifts to mining, fishing, and animal products. Your greenhouse and any indoor plots are your only crop zones.

docs.title: Winter Crops (Greenhouse / Indoor Only)
docs.text:
Crop Cost Growth Regrowth Sell Price Gold/Day Crystal Fruit 120g 7 days Every 5 days 180g 18g/day (raw) Snow Root 80g 6 days No 140g 10g/day (raw) Frost Berry 100g 10 days Every 4 days 150g 10.7g/day (raw)

Processed Winter Profits:

Crop → Product Machine Sell Price Gold/Day Crystal Fruit → Juice Juice Press 315g 315g/day Frost Berry → Wine Keg 450g 225g/day

docs.title: Winter Priorities (Non-Farming)
docs.text:
Winter is the time to push endgame content.

Activity Why Target Mining No crop distractions Reach floor 50+ (Mithril/Void ores) Fishing Winter fish are valuable Complete fish collection Combat Dungeon clearing Defeat Golem King and Shadow Warden Town Buildings Catch up on renovations Broken Bridge + Fountain House Upgrades Save and upgrade Reach Spacious or Grand Home Animal Focus Passive income all season 8+ animals with full artisan setup

docs.title: Winter Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Move all crops to greenhouse / indoor plots
  • [ ] Plant Crystal Fruit for Juice Press production
  • [ ] Focus on mining — reach floor 50+
  • [ ] Complete animal barn upgrades to Lv 3+
  • [ ] Upgrade house to at least Spacious Home
  • [ ] Complete Broken Bridge renovation
  • [ ] Build Town Square Fountain
  • [ ] Fish for Winter-exclusive species
  • [ ] Attend Starfall Night (Day 25)
  • [ ] Prepare crop plan for next Spring

Tip: Don't ignore animals in Winter. With a Lv 3 barn (auto-pet + auto-feed), animals produce at full rate regardless of weather. 8 Unicorns with Cheese Presses make 4,160g/day with zero effort.

docs.title: Nel'Vari (Unlocked mid-Summer)
docs.text:
Accessible via the portal in the Sun Haven forest. Nel'Vari has its own season cycle and crops.

Nel'Vari Crop Season Growth Sell Price Special Shadow Berry Any 8 days 200g +25% wine value Nether Wheat Any 6 days 60g Used for special breads Void Tomato Any 7 days 150g Best for Preserves Jar Moon Melon Any 12 days 500g Highest base price

Nel'Vari priority: Unlock as soon as you can (around Day 15 of Summer). Start with 10 Void Tomatoes for Preserves Jar processing.

docs.title: Withergate (Unlocked late-Fall)
docs.text:
Accessible via train from Sun Haven station (required 20,000 Tickets ticket). Harder enemies, better ores.

Withergate Focus Why Target Void Ore mining Best bars for endgame gear Floor 10+ of Withergate mines Demon Dust farming Needed for Celestial crafting Imps and Shadow Knights Withergate exclusive recipes Best consumables in game Cooking Station upgrades Black Market Rare items and furniture Save 20,000+ Tickets

Tip: Don't rush Withergate in your first year. Focus on Sun Haven and Nel'Vari until you have at least Mithril-tier gear. Withergate enemies hit hard and have 200+ HP.

docs.title: Month-by-Month Priority
docs.text: Month #1 Priority #2 Priority Gold Target Spring 1 Clear farm, plant Carrots Build Workbench + Furnace 5,000 Spring 2 Strawberries + Kegs Buy Chicken 10,000 Spring 3 Expand crops, mine Barn upgrade Lv 2 15,000 Spring 4 Save for Summer Reach mine F20 20,000 Summer 1 Blueberries + Melons Build 3+ Kegs 30,000 Summer 2 Add animals Build Cheese Press 45,000 Summer 3 Expand to 60+ tiles Nel'Vari unlock 60,000 Summer 4 Mine for Gold Ore Preserves Jar x5 80,000 Fall 1 Pumpkins + Preserves Build 5 more jars 120,000 Fall 2 Grapes + Kegs Stockpile Wood 160,000 Fall 3 Push mine F35+ Upgrade Pickaxe 200,000 Fall 4 Convert all to artisan Save for Winter 250,000 Winter 1 Greenhouse crops Animal focus 280,000 Winter 2 Push mine F50+ Town renovations 320,000 Winter 3 Fishing collection House upgrade 360,000 Winter 4 Prep for Spring/NG+ Starfall Night 400,000

docs.title: Year 2+ Strategy
docs.text:
Once you've completed one year, you can start a New Game+ or continue building your empire:

  1. Max all skills — Get Crafting, Mining, and Combat to Level 10
  2. Complete all renovations — All 8 town buildings restored
  3. All house upgrades — Reach Estate (Lv 6)
  4. Luxury animal farm — All 4 Unicorns with Deluxe barn
  5. Withergate endgame — Celestial gear, Dragon Matriarch defeat

Tip: In New Game+, you keep your house upgrades and skill levels. Focus on story quests you missed the first time around — some quests are season-locked.

docs.text:

  • Housing Guide — Town renovation details
  • Animals & Pets Guide — Winter animal strategies
  • Combat & Magic Guide — Winter dungeon targets

Data sources: Sun Haven Wiki 1.0, in-game testing, game version 1.3+. Crop values current as of patch 1.6.

docs.title: 🗺️ Sun Haven — Zone & Map Guide
docs.text:
Data version: Sun Haven 1.7 | Covers all 3 major zones, how to unlock them, key locations in each area, and the fast travel system

Sun Haven is a farming RPG with a sprawling world split across three distinct zones, each with its own theme, shops, farmable land, NPCs, dungeons, and story arcs. Understanding how to navigate these zones — and more importantly, how to unlock them efficiently — is critical to progressing through the game. This guide breaks down every zone, its key locations, and the travel systems that connect them.

docs.title: 🌍 Three Zones Overview
docs.text:
Zone Theme How to Unlock Key Features ☀️ Sun Haven Human/demon/monster fantasy town Starting zone Main farm, Sun Haven Mines, Beach, General Store 🌿 Nel'Vari Elven forest sanctuary Repair bridge (5,000 Tickets + materials) Second farm, Nel'Vari mine, unique crops/animals 💀 Withergate Undead industrial city Repair boat (8,000 Tickets + advanced materials) City rank system, Withergate mines, unique gear

Tip: Don't rush to unlock both zones immediately. Focus on Sun Haven first until you have Gold-tier tools and at least 10,000 Tickets in savings. Nel'Vari is accessible earlier (around Summer Year 1) but Withergate should wait until you have strong combat gear.

docs.title: ☀️ Sun Haven — The Main Area
docs.text: Sun Haven is your home base. It's the largest zone by surface area and contains the most shops, NPCs, and daily activities.

docs.title: Key Locations
docs.text: Location Description Highlights Your Farm Starting farm plot (30×40 tiles) Expandable, upgradable farmhouse, barns/coops Town Square Center of Sun Haven Bulletin board, festivals, marriage ceremonies General Store (Eli's) Main shop Seeds, tools, furniture, basic goods (open 9 AM–6 PM) Carpenter Shop Building upgrades House upgrades, farm buildings, sprinkler crafting Blacksmith (Liam's) Tool and weapon upgrades Tool upgrades cost tickets + bars Clinic (Dr. Cheng's) Healing and buffs HP/Mana restoration, antidotes for poison Tavern (Anne's) Social hub Buy food, meet NPCs, accept side quests Sun Haven Mines 40-floor dungeon 5 ore types, gems, monster drops, boss at F40 Beach (East) Fishing and foraging Saltwater fish, coral, shells, beach forageables Museum Donation center 200+ items to donate, Curator's Blessing reward Bus Stop Fast travel hub Travel to other zones (unlocked later)

docs.title: Sun Haven Mines
docs.text:
The mines are your primary source of ores, gems, and combat experience. The 40 floors are divided into tiers:

Floors Ores Enemies Key Drops 1–10 Copper, Stone Slimes, Bats Copper Ore, Bat Wings 11–20 Iron, Gold Skeletons, Cave Spiders Iron Ore, Spider Silk 21–30 Gold, Mithril Ghosts, Golems Mithril Ore, Soul Shards 31–40 Mithril, Celestial Dark Elves, Bosses Celestial Ore, Dragon Scales

Elevator: Unlocked at floor 10, 20, 30, and 40 — allows instant travel to any unlocked floor.

Tip: Always bring a Hearty Stew (or 3) when diving below floor 20. The enemies hit harder and the healing from food is more efficient than potions for long runs.

docs.title: 🌿 Nel'Vari — The Elven Forest
docs.text: Nel'Vari is a lush, magical forest zone populated by elves, fairies, and magical creatures. It has a completely different aesthetic and economic system from Sun Haven.

docs.title: How to Unlock Nel'Vari
docs.text:
1. Complete the "Bridge to Nel'Vari" quest (appears after reaching Farming Lv 5 or completing early story)
  2. Gather the required materials:
  3. 5,000 Tickets
  4. 200 Wood + 100 Stone + 50 Iron Bar
  5. Pay the Carpenter — the bridge east of Sun Haven is rebuilt in 3 days
  6. Cross the bridge to enter Nel'Vari

docs.title: Key Locations in Nel'Vari
docs.text: Location Description Highlights Nel'Vari Town Center Elven village hub Unique shops, elven NPCs, daily quests Nel'Vari Farm Second farm plot (40×40) Permanent Spring climate, unique crops Nel'Vari General Store Elven seed shop Elven Wheat, Starfruit, Nectar Flower seeds Nel'Vari Crafting Shop Unique recipes Loom upgrades, elven armor, magic-infused tools Nel'Vari Mine 25-floor dungeon Magic ore, fairy drops, forest-themed enemies Fairy Pond Hidden area behind waterfall Rare fish, Fairy Dust foraging Elven Library Knowledge center Crafting recipe scrolls, lore books

docs.title: Unique Nel'Vari Crops
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Grow Time Harvest Profit/Day Elven Wheat 20g 3 days 2–3 15g/day Starfruit 60g 6 days 1 45g/day Nel'Vari Nectar Flower 45g 5 days 1 38g/day Fairy Bloom 35g 4 days 1 30g/day

docs.title: Unique Nel'Vari Animals
docs.text:
• Elven Chicken (1,200g) — Lays golden eggs (sell for 40g each, higher than regular eggs)
  • Magical Cow (2,500g) — Produces enchanted milk (80g base, used in elven cooking)
  • Fairy Moth (3,000g) — Produces Fairy Dust every 3 days (key crafting ingredient)

Tip: Nel'Vari's permanent Spring climate means you can plant Starfruit year-round and never worry about season changes. This makes it the ideal location for a dedicated Keg-wine operation.

docs.title: 💀 Withergate — The Undead City
docs.text: Withergate is an industrial, gothic city inhabited by undead, demons, and monsters. It's the hardest zone to unlock but also offers the highest-end gear, recipes, and income opportunities.

docs.title: How to Unlock Withergate
docs.text:
1. Complete the "Setting Sail" quest (appears after reaching Combat Lv 10 or completing Sun Haven story)
  2. Gather materials for the boat repair:
  3. 8,000 Tickets
  4. 50 Mithril Bar + 20 Cloth + 10 Rope + 5 Magic Sprite Dust
  5. The boat at Sun Haven docks is repaired in 5 days
  6. Sail to Withergate from the Sun Haven dock

docs.title: Key Locations in Withergate
docs.text: Location Description Highlights Withergate Docks Arrival point Boat back to Sun Haven, dock forageables Withergate City Center Urban hub Multiple shops, city hall, rank-up NPC Withergate Apartment Your Withergate home Small farm plot on the roof Withergate General Store Zone shop Shadow Berry seeds, Void Tomato seeds Withergate Blacksmith Void-tier gear Best weapons and armor in the game Withergate Mines 30-floor dungeon Void Ore, Shadow gems, skeleton enemies Rooftop Garden Exclusive area Key from high city rank, rare plants Sewer Entrance Hidden dungeon Post-game content, toughest enemies

docs.title: Withergate City Rank System
docs.text:
Unlike Nel'Vari which opens fully at once, Withergate gates content behind a city rank progression:

Rank Requirement Unlock Rookie Complete arrival quests Basic shop access, apartment Citizen 10 quests + 2,000 Tickets Second shop row, sewer entrance Respected 25 quests + 10,000 Tickets Rooftop Garden, exclusive crafting Elite 50 quests + 25,000 Tickets Best weapon recipes Champion All quests + 50,000 Tickets Boss re-fight, rare drops, ultimate title

docs.title: Unique Withergate Items
docs.text:
• Shadow Berry — Exclusive crop, used in Shadow Brew (best combat buff drink)
  • Void Tomato — High-value crop for pickling
  • Void Ore — Found in Withergate mines F15+, used for best gear
  • Shadow Silk — Monster drop, used for Shadow Armor
  • Withergate Fishing Rod — Required to fish in Withergate's toxic waters

Tip: City rank quests are the fastest way to advance — prioritize the bulletin board in the City Center. Each rank-up quest chain unlocks new shops with items you can't get anywhere else.

docs.title: 🚀 Fast Travel System
docs.text: Sun Haven has a robust fast travel system that makes navigating the three zones much more manageable.

docs.title: Warp Points
docs.text:
Warp points are glowing crystals found in key locations. Once activated, you can teleport between them instantly.

Warp Point Zone How to Unlock Farm Portal Sun Haven Active from day 1 Town Square Sun Haven Walk to town square Beach Sun Haven Walk to beach area Mine Entrance Sun Haven Walk to mines entrance Nel'Vari Entrance Nel'Vari Cross bridge after repair Nel'Vari Town Nel'Vari Walk to town center Nel'Vari Mine Nel'Vari Walk to mine entrance Withergate Docks Withergate Arrive by boat Withergate Center Withergate Walk to city center Withergate Mine Withergate Walk to mine entrance

docs.title: Bus System
docs.text:
The Bus Stop near the Town Square allows paid travel between zones:

Route Cost Travel Time Sun Haven → Nel'Vari 50 Tickets Instant (loading screen) Nel'Vari → Sun Haven 50 Tickets Instant Sun Haven → Withergate 100 Tickets Instant (requires boat repair) Withergate → Sun Haven 100 Tickets Instant

docs.title: Returning to Sun Haven
docs.text:
You can return to Sun Haven from any zone at any time via: - Warp point from the zone's entrance portal - Bus stop in Nel'Vari - Boat from Withergate docks - Recall spell (learned late-game from Museum reward)

Tip: Unlock all warp points as soon as you enter a new zone. The farm portal alone won't cover you — having the town center warp active saves 5 minutes of walking every single day.

docs.title: Hidden Areas & Secrets
docs.text: Secret Zone How to Discover Sun Haven Hidden Pond Sun Haven (NE woods) Break rocks blocking path (Gold Pickaxe required) Fairy Grove Nel'Vari Go behind the waterfall in the NE corner Withergate Sewers Withergate Manhole in alley near city center — requires Citizen rank Ancient Library Nel'Vari Cracked wall behind the main building (Mining Lv 5+) Sun Dragon Ribcage Sun Haven Mines F25 Side path on floor 25, contains rare artifact Rooftop Garden Withergate 2F Locked door, requires Respected rank key Secret Beach Cave Sun Haven Beach Hidden behind large rock at low tide (morning only)

docs.title: General Exploration Tips
docs.text:
1. Always enter every building. Some shops and NPC houses aren't marked on the map at first — entering them adds them to your discovery log.
  2. Break every cracked wall. In all three zone's mines, cracked walls conceal treasure rooms with chests containing rare recipes, gems, and artifacts.
  3. Fish in every body of water. Different fish spawn in different zones, and some fish are needed for Museum completion. Don't assume the Sun Haven river is the same as the Nel'Vari river.
  4. Forage widely. Each zone has unique forageables. Withergate's docks have exclusive items not found anywhere else.
  5. Talk to NPCs. Many secret locations are revealed through NPC dialogue. Befriending certain characters (especially the Mayor, the Librarian, and the Innkeeper) reveals map markers for hidden areas.
  6. Map completion percentage is tracked in the pause menu. If you're below 100% for any zone, check for undiscovered warp points and unvisited buildings.
  7. Use the Quick Travel feature from the menu (Y on keyboard, or the map icon). This lets you instantly travel to any unlocked warp point without walking to the physical crystal.

  • Cooking Guide — Zone-specific ingredients and recipes

  • Combat & Magic Guide — Dungeon strategies for each zone

docs.title: Stardew Valley Mods Guide
docs.text: Mods can completely transform your Stardew Valley experience — from visual upgrades to massive content expansions. Here's our curated collection of the best mods, organized by category.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. Install SMAPI (Stardew Modding API) — Download here
  2. Extract SMAPI to your Stardew Valley folder
  3. Run install.exe on Windows (or terminal on Mac/Linux)
  4. Launch the game through SMAPI (a new shortcut will be created)

docs.title: Installing a Mod
docs.text:
1. Download the mod from Nexus Mods
  2. Extract the ZIP file
  3. Copy the mod folder into Stardew Valley/Mods/
  4. Launch the game via SMAPI

docs.title: Mod Manager (Recommended)
docs.text: Stardrop — an open-source, cross-platform mod manager: - Auto-install and update mods - Enable/disable mods with one click - Manage mod profiles (different sets for different saves)

docs.title: 🎨 Visual & UI
docs.text: Mod Description Link UI Info Suite 2 Shows experience bars, NPC locations, crop calendar, and more Nexus Visible Fish See fish swimming in the water before you cast Nexus Diverse Stardew Valley New character portraits and sprites Nexus Seasonal Outfits NPCs change clothes with the seasons Nexus Better Artisan Goods Shows which artisan goods are most profitable Nexus

docs.title: 📦 Starter Mod Pack
docs.text:
For new players, install these 5 mods first:

  1. SMAPI — Required for all mods
  2. UI Info Suite 2 — See everything you need
  3. Lookup Anything — Never search the wiki again
  4. Chests Anywhere — Organize your storage
  5. Automate — Let machines work while you explore

This pack gives you quality-of-life improvements without changing the core game experience.

docs.title: ⚠️ Important Notes
docs.text:
• Back up your save files before installing mods
  • Check mod compatibility — some mods don't work together
  • Update regularly — game updates can break mods
  • SMAPI tells you which mods need updates when you launch
  • Multiplayer: All players need the same mods installed

🔗 All mods listed are from Nexus Mods. Always download from trusted sources.

docs.title: Crop Profit Calculator
docs.text: 📊 Calculator 📖 Quick Guide ❓ FAQ   # Crop Profit Calculator  Welcome to the **Stardew Valley Crop Profit Calculator** — an interactive tool to help you maximize your farming profits.          Farming Level:               Tiller Profession:               Artisan Profession:               Fertilizer:        None Basic Fertilizer Quality Fertilizer Deluxe Fertilizer Calculate Crop Season Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Price Harvests Total Revenue Total Profit Profit/Day   ## 📖 Quick Guide  1. **Set your Farming Level** — Higher level = more crops harvested 2. **Toggle professions** — Tiller (10% more crops) or Artisan (40% more artisan goods) 3. **Choose fertilizer** — Basic/Quality/Deluxe/Speed-Gro affects harvest quality 4. **Select a crop** — Compare spring/summer/fall crops side by side 5. **Read the results** — Gold/day tells you which crop is most profitable  **Pro tip:** Blueberries (summer) and Cranberries (fall) are the top earners in early-game. Switch to Starfruit or Ancient Fruit for end-game max profit.     ## ❓ FAQ  **Q: Why is Gold/Day more important than total profit?** A: Total profit ignores how many days the crop takes to grow. Gold/Day accounts for the growing time, so you can compare crops fairly.  **Q: Does quality fertilizer increase profit?** A: Yes — higher quality crops sell for more. Quality fertilizer increases the chance of silver/gold/iridium quality harvests.  **Q: Should I use Speed-Gro or Quality Fertilizer?** A: It depends. Speed-Gro lets you get more harvests per season, which beats quality for multi-harvest crops. Quality fertilizer is better for single-harvest crops with high base value.   #results { margin: 20px 0; } #profitChart { margin-top: 20px; border-radius: 12px; }   ---  > 🛒 [**Buy Stardew Valley on Steam**](https://store.steampowered.com/app/413150/) — Support the developer!

Feature What You Can Change 🧑 Player Name, appearance, gender, farm type 💰 Money Starting gold, earnings 🎒 Inventory Items, tools, weapons ⛏️ Skills Farming, Mining, Foraging, Fishing, Combat levels 💞 Relationships Friendship hearts with NPCs 🏠 Buildings House upgrades, farm buildings

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
1. Find your save file:
  2. Windows: %AppData%/StardewValley/Saves/
  3. macOS: ~/.config/StardewValley/Saves/

  4. Linux: ~/.local/share/StardewValley/Saves/

  5. Back up your save — always make a copy before editing!

  6. Upload to the editor — drag and drop the file

  7. Edit what you want — change money, skills, inventory

  8. Download and replace your save file

  9. Launch the game — your changes will be loaded

docs.title: 🌴 Coral Island — Crop Profit Calculator & Farm Planner
docs.text: This guide provides the data and methodology behind Coral Island's crop economy. Use the tables below to plan your farm layout, compare artisan processing margins, and build a season-by-season crop rotation that maximizes profit per tile.

docs.title: Profit Formula
docs.text:
Profit per Day (PPD) = (Sell Price × Quality Multiplier − Seed Cost) ÷ Growth Days

For regrowing crops, we calculate across an entire season (28 days):

PPD (Regrow) = [(Sell Price × Quality × Harvests) − Seed Cost] ÷ 28
Harvests = 1 + floor((28 − GrowthDays) ÷ RegrowInterval)

docs.title: Quality Multipliers
docs.text:
Quality Tier Price Multiplier How to Get ⚪ Base 1.0x (100%) No fertilizer ⚪⚪ Silver 1.25x Basic Fertilizer ⚪⚪⚪ Gold 1.50x Quality Fertilizer, high farming level 🪐 Osmium 2.00x Deluxe Fertilizer, max farming level

Note: All PPD values below assume base (100%) quality. Actual profit with Quality Fertilizer can be 25–50% higher.

docs.text:
Tiles Crop Strategy 100 tiles (greenhouse) ☕ Coffee Bean Plant Day 1, harvest every 2 days Crafting focus 🪣 Kegs & Preserve Jars Build 20+ kegs for next Spring Upgrades ⛏️ Tool upgrades Osmium watering can + pickaxe

Winter: Passive coffee income (~30,000g/month) + prep for Year 2

💡 Year 2 Strategy: By Year 2, replace Strawberries with Coffee Beans in the greenhouse. Dedicate 60 tiles to Grapes in Fall for mass wine production. With 20+ kegs running constantly, aim for 300,000g+ per season.

docs.title: 🏛️ Stardew Valley Community Center Bundle Tracker
docs.text: Track your Community Center bundle progress in Stardew Valley 1.6. Check off items as you donate them — progress saves automatically in your browser.

docs.title: 📖 How to Use
docs.text:
1. Click any item checkbox to mark it as donated to the bundle
  2. Progress auto-saves in your browser (localStorage)
  3. Bundle progress shows as X/Y — when all items in a bundle are checked, it turns green
  4. Room progress bars at the top show per-room completion
  5. Overall progress bar tracks your total Community Center completion

docs.title: 💡 Tips
docs.text:
• Don't rush the Quality Crops bundle — you need Gold-quality Parsnips, Melons, Pumpkins, and Corn. Use Basic/Quality Fertilizer!
  • The Fish Tank requires multiple seasons — check which fish are available each season using the Fish Tracker
  • The Bulletin Board has many cooking recipes — save your ingredients!
  • The Vault requires a total of 42,500g — start saving early
  • 1.6 additions: Legendary Fish II bundles and the Children's Bundle with new 1.6 items (Raisins, Pear, etc.)

docs.title: 🏆 Rewards

docs.title: 🧑‍🌾 Stardew Valley Crop Profit Comparison
docs.text:
Compare every crop in Stardew Valley 1.6 side-by-side. Sort by any column, filter by season, regrowth, or artisan products, and select 2–4 crops for a detailed profit comparison.

Data source: Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 🔍 Filters
docs.text: 🔎 Search 🌸 Season All Seasons 🌸 Spring ☀️ Summer 🍂 Fall ❄️ Winter 🌿 Greenhouse/Any 🏝️ Ginger Island  Regrowing only      🏭 Artisan All Has Artisan Product No Artisan Product

docs.title: 📋 Crop Comparison Table
docs.text: 🔍 🌱 Name ↕ 🌸 Season ↕ 💰 Seed Cost ↕ ⏱️ Grow ↕ 🔄 Regrow ↕ ⭐ Base ↕ 🥈 Silver ↕ 🥇 Gold ↕ 💎 Iridium ↕ 🏭 Artisan ↕ 📈 Profit/Day ↕

docs.title: 📊 Compare Mode
docs.text: Select 2–4 crops to compare side-by-side (checkboxes on left).

docs.title: 💡 Tips
docs.text:
• Profit/Day is calculated assuming a standard 28-day season. For regrowing crops, it accounts for the initial grow time + multiple harvests.
  • Free seeds (Seed Spots crops: Carrot, Summer Squash, Broccoli, Powdermelon) have zero seed cost — profit is pure income.
  • Quality prices: Silver = 1.25×, Gold = 1.5×, Iridium = 2× base price.
  • Artisan column shows the most profitable artisan product. Fruits make Wine (3×), Vegetables make Juice (2.25×) or Pickles (2× + 50g).
  • Greenhouse crops (Ancient Fruit, Cactus Fruit, Pineapple, Taro Root, Tea Leaves) can be grown year-round — profit/day gets even better with continuous harvests.
  • Starfruit at 750g base and 2250g as Wine is unmatched for single-harvest profit, and Hops with Pale Ale at 300g daily is the Keg efficiency king.
  • Sweet Gem Berry (83.33g/day) is the highest profit-per-day crop but requires Rare Seeds from the Traveling Cart.

docs.title: 📝 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Quality prices (Silver/Gold/Iridium) are calculated from base price using the standard multipliers.
  • Artisan prices shown reflect the base artisan product price before the Artisan profession (+40%) buff.
  • Coffee Beans: 5 beans brew 1 Coffee. The seed cost shown (2500g) is for a single bean purchased at the Traveling Cart.
  • Qi Fruit, Fiber, and Moss Soup Plant are special crops not typically grown for profit.

Data source: Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.text: 🎣 Fish Season Location Time Weather Difficulty Price Bundle

docs.title: 🎯 Difficulty Guide
docs.text: Badge Difficulty Description ★★★ 90+ Legendary — need high fishing level + trap bobber ★★ 70-89 Hard — need iridium rod + bait ★ 50-69 Medium — fiberglass rod is enough (none) 0-49 Easy — even bamboo pole works

docs.title: 💡 Tips
docs.text:
• Fishing skill increases catch bar size and reduces bite time
  • Iridium Rod + Trap Bobber makes hard fish much catchable
  • Level 10 + Dish O' The Sea (+3) maxes your fishing temporarily
  • Curiosity Lure (Qi's Walnut Room) increases chance of rare fish
  • Rain = Catfish, Walleye, Eel, Shad become available
  • Ginger Island fish are available year-round
  • Legendary fish can only be caught once per save file!
  • Use the Weather filter to find rain-specific fish, or Caught/Uncaught toggle to see only what's missing

docs.title: 🗺️ Important Floor Resources
docs.text: Floor Resource Notes 5 📜 Dwarf Scroll I Dig spots / monster drops 10 🛗 Elevator First elevator stop 15 📜 Dwarf Scroll II Monster drops 21 🪙 Gold Ore starts appearing Common from floor 80+ 41 ❄️ Frost Dungeon / Ghosts Frost Jelly, Ghostfish fishing 51 📜 Dwarf Scroll III Monster drops 61 📜 Dwarf Scroll IV Monster drops 71 👻 Ghosts common Ghost + Carbon Ghost 81 💎 Iridium & Slimes Iridium Ore starts, Purple Slimes 100 🛗 Last elevator Final checkpoint 110 🔑 Skull Key earned Unlocks Skull Cavern 120 🏆 Mines Complete Final chest with Stardrop

docs.title: 📝 Usage Instructions
docs.text:
1. Click a mine floor in the grid to mark your progress. Click any floor up to your current max.
  2. Jump to floor using the input box for quick navigation.
  3. Skull Cavern — enter your deepest floor reached (text input, no limit).
  4. Monster kills — use +1 / +10 / -1 / -10 buttons to track kills per monster type. Click ✏️ to enter a custom number.
  5. Guild rewards — automatically updates as you track kills. Met thresholds show ✅.
  6. Progress bar — tracks overall completion combining Mines progress and monster kills.
  7. All data saves automatically in your browser (localStorage).

docs.title: 🌱 Stardew Valley Crop Profit Planner
docs.text:
Select a season, fertilizer, and your skills to see the most profitable crops ranked by profit per day. Batch-select crops to compare total investment vs. return.

Season 🌸 Spring ☀️ Summer 🍂 Fall ❄️ Winter 🌿 All Seasons 🏠 Greenhouse (Any) 🧪 Fertilizer None Basic Fertilizer Quality Fertilizer Deluxe Fertilizer Speed-Gro (+10%) Deluxe Speed-Gro (+25%) Hyper Speed-Gro (+33%) Professions +40% artisan goods +10% crop sell price +10% growth speed 📦 Batch Summary Selected: 0 crops Seed Cost: 0g Total Income: 0g 📈 Net Profit: 0g ✓ Crop Seed Cost Growth Regrow Base Sell Harvests Total Income Profit/Day Fertilizer Notes

docs.title: 📖 How to Use
docs.text:
1. Select a season to see which crops are available
  2. Choose a fertilizer to see how it affects growth time and profit
  3. Toggle professions (Artisan, Tiller, Agriculturist) for accurate calculations
  4. Check the boxes next to crops to see batch totals (seed cost, income, profit)
  5. Sort by profit/day — the table is automatically ranked highest to lowest

docs.title: 💡 Profit Per Day Explained
docs.text:
Profit/Day = (Total Income − Seed Cost) ÷ Total Days

  • Single-harvest crops: Total days = growth time
  • Multi-harvest (regrowing): Total days = initial growth + (harvests − 1) × regrow interval
  • Fertilizer effects: Speed-Gro reduces growth time; Quality fertilizers boost sell price
  • Artisan bonus: Crops with artisan processing (wine, juice, beer, etc.) get +40% with the Artisan profession

docs.title: 🌾 Fertilizer Comparison💬 Data accuracy
docs.text:
Fertilizer Effect Best For Basic Fertilizer +~10% avg sell price (quality boost) Early game, cheap Quality Fertilizer +~25% avg sell price Mid-game crops Deluxe Fertilizer +~50% avg sell price High-value single harvests Speed-Gro −10% growth time Regrowing crops, more harvests Deluxe Speed-Gro −25% growth time Maximizing harvest count Hyper Speed-Gro −33% growth time Min-maxing rare seeds

All data verified against Stardew Valley 1.6 wiki. Found an error? Let us know.
